sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।हते दुर्योधने चैव हते सैन्ये च सर्वशः ।धृतराष्ट्रो महाराजः श्रुत्वा किमकरोन्मुने ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,hate duryodhane caiva hate sainye ca sarvaśaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭro mahārājaḥ śrutvā kimakaronmune.
english: Janamejaya said: 'O Sage (mune), when Duryodhana was killed and the army was completely destroyed, what did King Dhṛtarāṣṭra do after hearing (this)?'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: 'O Sage (mune), when Duryodhana was killed and the army was completely destroyed, what did King Dhṛtarāṣṭra do after hearing (this)?'

sanskrit: तथैव कौरवो राजा धर्मपुत्रो महामनाः ।कृपप्रभृतयश्चैव किमकुर्वत ते त्रयः ॥२॥
transliteration: tathaiva kauravo rājā dharmaputro mahāmanāḥ ,kṛpaprabhṛtayaścaiva kimakurvata te trayaḥ.
english: Similarly, what did the great-minded Kuru king (Yudhiṣṭhira), the son of Dharma (dharma), do? And what did those three, Kṛpa and others (Aśvatthāman and Kṛtavarman), do?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. Similarly, what did the great-minded Kuru king (Yudhiṣṭhira), the son of Dharma (dharma), do? And what did those three, Kṛpa and others (Aśvatthāman and Kṛtavarman), do?

sanskrit: अश्वत्थाम्नः श्रुतं कर्म शापश्चान्योन्यकारितः ।वृत्तान्तमुत्तरं ब्रूहि यदभाषत संजयः ॥३॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmnaḥ śrutaṁ karma śāpaścānyonyakāritaḥ ,vṛttāntamuttaraṁ brūhi yadabhāṣata saṁjayaḥ.
english: I have heard about Ashvatthama's deed (karma) and the reciprocal curse. Please narrate the subsequent events, as you, Sañjaya, recounted them.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. I have heard about Ashvatthama's deed (karma) and the reciprocal curse. Please narrate the subsequent events, as you, Sañjaya, recounted them.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।हते पुत्रशते दीनं छिन्नशाखमिव द्रुमम् ।पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्तं धृतराष्ट्रं महीपतिम् ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,hate putraśate dīnaṁ chinnaśākhamiva drumam ,putraśokābhisaṁtaptaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ mahīpatim.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: (He approached) King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who was miserable after his hundred sons had been killed, like a tree with its branches severed, and completely tormented by grief for his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: (He approached) King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, who was miserable after his hundred sons had been killed, like a tree with its branches severed, and completely tormented by grief for his sons.

sanskrit: ध्यानमूकत्वमापन्नं चिन्तया समभिप्लुतम् ।अभिगम्य महाप्राज्ञः संजयो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥५॥
transliteration: dhyānamūkatvamāpannaṁ cintayā samabhiplutam ,abhigamya mahāprājñaḥ saṁjayo vākyamabravīt.
english: Having fallen into a silent stupor of contemplation (dhyāna) and overwhelmed by anxiety, the highly intelligent Sañjaya approached (King Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Having fallen into a silent stupor of contemplation (dhyāna) and overwhelmed by anxiety, the highly intelligent Sañjaya approached (King Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and spoke these words.

sanskrit: किं शोचसि महाराज नास्ति शोके सहायता ।अक्षौहिण्यो हताश्चाष्टौ दश चैव विशां पते ।निर्जनेयं वसुमती शून्या संप्रति केवला ॥६॥
transliteration: kiṁ śocasi mahārāja nāsti śoke sahāyatā ,akṣauhiṇyo hatāścāṣṭau daśa caiva viśāṁ pate ,nirjaneyaṁ vasumatī śūnyā saṁprati kevalā.
english: Why do you grieve, great king? There is no help in sorrow. O lord of men, eighteen Akṣauhiṇī armies have been slain. This earth is now deserted, empty, and solitary.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. Why do you grieve, great king? There is no help in sorrow. O lord of men, eighteen Akṣauhiṇī armies have been slain. This earth is now deserted, empty, and solitary.

sanskrit: नानादिग्भ्यः समागम्य नानादेश्या नराधिपाः ।सहितास्तव पुत्रेण सर्वे वै निधनं गताः ॥७॥
transliteration: nānādigbhyaḥ samāgamya nānādeśyā narādhipāḥ ,sahitāstava putreṇa sarve vai nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Having assembled from various directions, kings from different lands, together with your son, all indeed met their end.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Having assembled from various directions, kings from different lands, together with your son, all indeed met their end.

sanskrit: पितॄणां पुत्रपौत्राणां ज्ञातीनां सुहृदां तथा ।गुरूणां चानुपूर्व्येण प्रेतकार्याणि कारय ॥८॥
transliteration: pitṝṇāṁ putrapautrāṇāṁ jñātīnāṁ suhṛdāṁ tathā ,gurūṇāṁ cānupūrvyeṇa pretakāryāṇi kāraya.
english: Perform the funeral rites for fathers, sons, grandsons, relatives, friends, and also for teachers, in due order.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. Perform the funeral rites for fathers, sons, grandsons, relatives, friends, and also for teachers, in due order.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तच्छ्रुत्वा करुणं वाक्यं पुत्रपौत्रवधार्दितः ।पपात भुवि दुर्धर्षो वाताहत इव द्रुमः ॥९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tacchrutvā karuṇaṁ vākyaṁ putrapautravadhārditaḥ ,papāta bhuvi durdharṣo vātāhata iva drumaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that pathetic statement, the unconquerable one, afflicted by the killing of his sons and grandsons, fell to the ground like a tree struck by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that pathetic statement, the unconquerable one, afflicted by the killing of his sons and grandsons, fell to the ground like a tree struck by the wind.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।हतपुत्रो हतामात्यो हतसर्वसुहृज्जनः ।दुःखं नूनं भविष्यामि विचरन्पृथिवीमिमाम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,hataputro hatāmātyo hatasarvasuhṛjjanaḥ ,duḥkhaṁ nūnaṁ bhaviṣyāmi vicaranpṛthivīmimām.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "With my sons killed, my ministers killed, and all my well-wishers killed, I will surely wander this earth in sorrow."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "With my sons killed, my ministers killed, and all my well-wishers killed, I will surely wander this earth in sorrow."

sanskrit: किं नु बन्धुविहीनस्य जीवितेन ममाद्य वै ।लूनपक्षस्य इव मे जराजीर्णस्य पक्षिणः ॥११॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu bandhuvihīnasya jīvitena mamādya vai ,lūnapakṣasya iva me jarājīrṇasya pakṣiṇaḥ.
english: What use is life to me today, now that I am deprived of my kinsmen? I am like a bird with clipped wings, worn out by old age.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. What use is life to me today, now that I am deprived of my kinsmen? I am like a bird with clipped wings, worn out by old age.

sanskrit: हृतराज्यो हतसुहृद्धतचक्षुश्च वै तथा ।न भ्राजिष्ये महाप्राज्ञ क्षीणरश्मिरिवांशुमान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: hṛtarājyo hatasuhṛddhatacakṣuśca vai tathā ,na bhrājiṣye mahāprājña kṣīṇaraśmirivāṁśumān.
english: My kingdom snatched away, my friends killed, and my eyes blinded - indeed, I shall not shine, O greatly wise one, like the sun with diminished rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. My kingdom snatched away, my friends killed, and my eyes blinded - indeed, I shall not shine, O greatly wise one, like the sun with diminished rays.

sanskrit: न कृतं सुहृदां वाक्यं जामदग्न्यस्य जल्पतः ।नारदस्य च देवर्षेः कृष्णद्वैपायनस्य च ॥१३॥
transliteration: na kṛtaṁ suhṛdāṁ vākyaṁ jāmadagnyasya jalpataḥ ,nāradasya ca devarṣeḥ kṛṣṇadvaipāyanasya ca.
english: The words of my well-wishers were not heeded - neither those spoken by Jamadagni's son (Paraśurāma), nor by the divine sage Nārada, nor by Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. The words of my well-wishers were not heeded - neither those spoken by Jamadagni's son (Paraśurāma), nor by the divine sage Nārada, nor by Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa).

sanskrit: सभामध्ये तु कृष्णेन यच्छ्रेयोऽभिहितं मम ।अलं वैरेण ते राजन्पुत्रः संगृह्यतामिति ॥१४॥
transliteration: sabhāmadhye tu kṛṣṇena yacchreyo'bhihitaṁ mama ,alaṁ vaireṇa te rājanputraḥ saṁgṛhyatāmiti.
english: In the assembly, Krishna declared to me what was best: 'Enough of this enmity for you, O King; let your son be embraced!'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. In the assembly, Krishna declared to me what was best: 'Enough of this enmity for you, O King; let your son be embraced!'

sanskrit: तच्च वाक्यमकृत्वाहं भृशं तप्यामि दुर्मतिः ।न हि श्रोतास्मि भीष्मस्य धर्मयुक्तं प्रभाषितम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tacca vākyamakṛtvāhaṁ bhṛśaṁ tapyāmi durmatiḥ ,na hi śrotāsmi bhīṣmasya dharmayuktaṁ prabhāṣitam.
english: And because I, being foolish, did not follow that advice, I now grieve intensely. Indeed, I was not a listener to what Bhishma had said, which was endowed with natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. And because I, being foolish, did not follow that advice, I now grieve intensely. Indeed, I was not a listener to what Bhishma had said, which was endowed with natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य च तथा वृषभस्येव नर्दतः ।दुःशासनवधं श्रुत्वा कर्णस्य च विपर्ययम् ।द्रोणसूर्योपरागं च हृदयं मे विदीर्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya ca tathā vṛṣabhasyeva nardataḥ ,duḥśāsanavadhaṁ śrutvā karṇasya ca viparyayam ,droṇasūryoparāgaṁ ca hṛdayaṁ me vidīryate.
english: And having heard of Duryodhana, roaring like a bull, and the slaying of Duḥśāsana, and the reversal of Karna's fortune, and the eclipse (demise) of Drona who was like the sun, my heart is being torn apart.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. And having heard of Duryodhana, roaring like a bull, and the slaying of Duḥśāsana, and the reversal of Karna's fortune, and the eclipse (demise) of Drona who was like the sun, my heart is being torn apart.

sanskrit: न स्मराम्यात्मनः किंचित्पुरा संजय दुष्कृतम् ।यस्येदं फलमद्येह मया मूढेन भुज्यते ॥१७॥
transliteration: na smarāmyātmanaḥ kiṁcitpurā saṁjaya duṣkṛtam ,yasyedaṁ phalamadyeha mayā mūḍhena bhujyate.
english: O Sanjaya, I do not remember any past misdeed of my own self (ātman) for which this consequence is being experienced by me, a foolish man, here today.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. O Sanjaya, I do not remember any past misdeed of my own self (ātman) for which this consequence is being experienced by me, a foolish man, here today.

sanskrit: नूनं ह्यपकृतं किंचिन्मया पूर्वेषु जन्मसु ।येन मां दुःखभागेषु धाता कर्मसु युक्तवान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: nūnaṁ hyapakṛtaṁ kiṁcinmayā pūrveṣu janmasu ,yena māṁ duḥkhabhāgeṣu dhātā karmasu yuktavān.
english: Surely, I must have committed some wrong in previous lives (janmasu), by which Providence (dhātā) has engaged me in sorrowful circumstances (karmasu).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. Surely, I must have committed some wrong in previous lives (janmasu), by which Providence (dhātā) has engaged me in sorrowful circumstances (karmasu).

sanskrit: परिणामश्च वयसः सर्वबन्धुक्षयश्च मे ।सुहृन्मित्रविनाशश्च दैवयोगादुपागतः ।कोऽन्योऽस्ति दुःखिततरो मया लोके पुमानिह ॥१९॥
transliteration: pariṇāmaśca vayasaḥ sarvabandhukṣayaśca me ,suhṛnmitravināśaśca daivayogādupāgataḥ ,ko'nyo'sti duḥkhitataro mayā loke pumāniha.
english: The decline of my age, the destruction of all my relatives, and the loss of my friends and allies – all these have come upon me by the decree of fate. What other man in this world is more miserable than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. The decline of my age, the destruction of all my relatives, and the loss of my friends and allies – all these have come upon me by the decree of fate. What other man in this world is more miserable than I?

sanskrit: तन्मामद्यैव पश्यन्तु पाण्डवाः संशितव्रतम् ।विवृतं ब्रह्मलोकस्य दीर्घमध्वानमास्थितम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tanmāmadyaiva paśyantu pāṇḍavāḥ saṁśitavratam ,vivṛtaṁ brahmalokasya dīrghamadhvānamāsthitam.
english: Therefore, let the Pāṇḍavas behold me today, firm in my vows, as I stand upon the long, opened path leading to the world of Brahmā (brahmaloka).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. Therefore, let the Pāṇḍavas behold me today, firm in my vows, as I stand upon the long, opened path leading to the world of Brahmā (brahmaloka).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्य लालप्यमानस्य बहुशोकं विचिन्वतः ।शोकापहं नरेन्द्रस्य संजयो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasya lālapyamānasya bahuśokaṁ vicinvataḥ ,śokāpahaṁ narendrasya saṁjayo vākyamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: To that king, who was lamenting and deeply pondering his great sorrow, Saṃjaya spoke words that removed his grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. Vaiśampāyana said: To that king, who was lamenting and deeply pondering his great sorrow, Saṃjaya spoke words that removed his grief.

sanskrit: शोकं राजन्व्यपनुद श्रुतास्ते वेदनिश्चयाः ।शास्त्रागमाश्च विविधा वृद्धेभ्यो नृपसत्तम ।सृञ्जये पुत्रशोकार्ते यदूचुर्मुनयः पुरा ॥२२॥
transliteration: śokaṁ rājanvyapanuda śrutāste vedaniścayāḥ ,śāstrāgamāśca vividhā vṛddhebhyo nṛpasattama ,sṛñjaye putraśokārte yadūcurmunayaḥ purā.
english: O king, cast away your sorrow. You have heard the definitive doctrines of the Vedas and various traditional texts (śāstras) from the elders, O best among kings. (These are similar to what) the sages formerly told Sañjaya, who was afflicted by the grief (śoka) for his son.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. O king, cast away your sorrow. You have heard the definitive doctrines of the Vedas and various traditional texts (śāstras) from the elders, O best among kings. (These are similar to what) the sages formerly told Sañjaya, who was afflicted by the grief (śoka) for his son.

sanskrit: तथा यौवनजं दर्पमास्थिते ते सुते नृप ।न त्वया सुहृदां वाक्यं ब्रुवतामवधारितम् ।स्वार्थश्च न कृतः कश्चिल्लुब्धेन फलगृद्धिना ॥२३॥
transliteration: tathā yauvanajaṁ darpamāsthite te sute nṛpa ,na tvayā suhṛdāṁ vākyaṁ bruvatāmavadhāritam ,svārthaśca na kṛtaḥ kaścillubdhena phalagṛddhinā.
english: O king, when your son was seized by the arrogance born of youth, the words of well-wishers, though spoken, were not heeded by you. And by that greedy one, craving for rewards, no self-interest was ever achieved.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. O king, when your son was seized by the arrogance born of youth, the words of well-wishers, though spoken, were not heeded by you. And by that greedy one, craving for rewards, no self-interest was ever achieved.

sanskrit: तव दुःशासनो मन्त्री राधेयश्च दुरात्मवान् ।शकुनिश्चैव दुष्टात्मा चित्रसेनश्च दुर्मतिः ।शल्यश्च येन वै सर्वं शल्यभूतं कृतं जगत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tava duḥśāsano mantrī rādheyaśca durātmavān ,śakuniścaiva duṣṭātmā citrasenaśca durmatiḥ ,śalyaśca yena vai sarvaṁ śalyabhūtaṁ kṛtaṁ jagat.
english: Your minister was Duḥśāsana, and Rādhā's son (Karṇa) was evil-minded. Śakuni was also wicked, and Citrasena was ill-intentioned. And Śalya, by whom indeed the entire world was made like a dart (śalya).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 24. Your minister was Duḥśāsana, and Rādhā's son (Karṇa) was evil-minded. Śakuni was also wicked, and Citrasena was ill-intentioned. And Śalya, by whom indeed the entire world was made like a dart (śalya).

sanskrit: कुरुवृद्धस्य भीष्मस्य गान्धार्या विदुरस्य च ।न कृतं वचनं तेन तव पुत्रेण भारत ॥२५॥
transliteration: kuruvṛddhasya bhīṣmasya gāndhāryā vidurasya ca ,na kṛtaṁ vacanaṁ tena tava putreṇa bhārata.
english: The words of Bhīṣma, the elder of the Kurus, of Gāndhārī, and of Vidura were not heeded by your son, O descendant of Bharata.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 25. The words of Bhīṣma, the elder of the Kurus, of Gāndhārī, and of Vidura were not heeded by your son, O descendant of Bharata.

sanskrit: न धर्मः सत्कृतः कश्चिन्नित्यं युद्धमिति ब्रुवन् ।क्षपिताः क्षत्रियाः सर्वे शत्रूणां वर्धितं यशः ॥२६॥
transliteration: na dharmaḥ satkṛtaḥ kaścinnityaṁ yuddhamiti bruvan ,kṣapitāḥ kṣatriyāḥ sarve śatrūṇāṁ vardhitaṁ yaśaḥ.
english: No intrinsic nature (dharma) was properly honored by anyone, with everyone constantly declaring, 'This is war!' As a result, all the warriors (kṣatriya) were destroyed, and the glory of the enemies increased.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 26. No intrinsic nature (dharma) was properly honored by anyone, with everyone constantly declaring, 'This is war!' As a result, all the warriors (kṣatriya) were destroyed, and the glory of the enemies increased.

sanskrit: मध्यस्थो हि त्वमप्यासीर्न क्षमं किंचिदुक्तवान् ।धूर्धरेण त्वया भारस्तुलया न समं धृतः ॥२७॥
transliteration: madhyastho hi tvamapyāsīrna kṣamaṁ kiṁciduktavān ,dhūrdhareṇa tvayā bhārastulayā na samaṁ dhṛtaḥ.
english: Indeed, you yourself remained neutral (madhyastha) and did not speak anything appropriate. You, as the leader (dhūrdhara), did not bear the burden impartially, like with a balance.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 27. Indeed, you yourself remained neutral (madhyastha) and did not speak anything appropriate. You, as the leader (dhūrdhara), did not bear the burden impartially, like with a balance.

sanskrit: आदावेव मनुष्येण वर्तितव्यं यथा क्षमम् ।यथा नातीतमर्थं वै पश्चात्तापेन युज्यते ॥२८॥
transliteration: ādāveva manuṣyeṇa vartitavyaṁ yathā kṣamam ,yathā nātītamarthaṁ vai paścāttāpena yujyate.
english: From the very beginning, a person should act appropriately so that they are not later associated with regret (paścāttāpa) concerning a past matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 28. From the very beginning, a person should act appropriately so that they are not later associated with regret (paścāttāpa) concerning a past matter.

sanskrit: पुत्रगृद्ध्या त्वया राजन्प्रियं तस्य चिकीर्षता ।पश्चात्तापमिदं प्राप्तं न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥२९॥
transliteration: putragṛddhyā tvayā rājanpriyaṁ tasya cikīrṣatā ,paścāttāpamidaṁ prāptaṁ na tvaṁ śocitumarhasi.
english: O King, because you longed for a son (putragṛddhyā) and wished to do what was dear to him, you have incurred this regret (paścāttāpa). Therefore, you ought not to grieve.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 29. O King, because you longed for a son (putragṛddhyā) and wished to do what was dear to him, you have incurred this regret (paścāttāpa). Therefore, you ought not to grieve.

sanskrit: मधु यः केवलं दृष्ट्वा प्रपातं नानुपश्यति ।स भ्रष्टो मधुलोभेन शोचत्येव यथा भवान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: madhu yaḥ kevalaṁ dṛṣṭvā prapātaṁ nānupaśyati ,sa bhraṣṭo madhulobhena śocatyeva yathā bhavān.
english: One who sees only the honey and does not perceive the precipice, such a person, ruined by greed for honey, surely grieves, just as you do.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 30. One who sees only the honey and does not perceive the precipice, such a person, ruined by greed for honey, surely grieves, just as you do.

sanskrit: अर्थान्न शोचन्प्राप्नोति न शोचन्विन्दते सुखम् ।न शोचञ्श्रियमाप्नोति न शोचन्विन्दते परम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: arthānna śocanprāpnoti na śocanvindate sukham ,na śocañśriyamāpnoti na śocanvindate param.
english: One who does not grieve obtains wealth; one who does not grieve finds happiness. One who does not grieve attains prosperity; one who does not grieve finds the supreme (param).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 31. One who does not grieve obtains wealth; one who does not grieve finds happiness. One who does not grieve attains prosperity; one who does not grieve finds the supreme (param).

sanskrit: स्वयमुत्पादयित्वाग्निं वस्त्रेण परिवेष्टयेत् ।दह्यमानो मनस्तापं भजते न स पण्डितः ॥३२॥
transliteration: svayamutpādayitvāgniṁ vastreṇa pariveṣṭayet ,dahyamāno manastāpaṁ bhajate na sa paṇḍitaḥ.
english: One who himself ignites a fire and then wraps it with a garment, and subsequently suffers mental distress from being burned, such a person is not truly wise.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 32. One who himself ignites a fire and then wraps it with a garment, and subsequently suffers mental distress from being burned, such a person is not truly wise.

sanskrit: त्वयैव ससुतेनायं वाक्यवायुसमीरितः ।लोभाज्येन च संसिक्तो ज्वलितः पार्थपावकः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tvayaiva sasutenāyaṁ vākyavāyusamīritaḥ ,lobhājyena ca saṁsikto jvalitaḥ pārthapāvakaḥ.
english: Indeed, this fire of Arjuna (Pārtha) has been ignited by you and your son; it has been fanned by the wind of your words and drenched with the ghee of greed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 33. Indeed, this fire of Arjuna (Pārtha) has been ignited by you and your son; it has been fanned by the wind of your words and drenched with the ghee of greed.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्समिद्धे पतिताः शलभा इव ते सुताः ।तान्केशवार्चिर्निर्दग्धान्न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥३४॥
transliteration: tasminsamiddhe patitāḥ śalabhā iva te sutāḥ ,tānkeśavārcirnirdagdhānna tvaṁ śocitumarhasi.
english: Your sons have fallen into that blazing fire like moths. You should not grieve for them, for they have been consumed by the flames of Keśava.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 34. Your sons have fallen into that blazing fire like moths. You should not grieve for them, for they have been consumed by the flames of Keśava.

sanskrit: यच्चाश्रुपातकलिलं वदनं वहसे नृप ।अशास्त्रदृष्टमेतद्धि न प्रशंसन्ति पण्डिताः ॥३५॥
transliteration: yaccāśrupātakalilaṁ vadanaṁ vahase nṛpa ,aśāstradṛṣṭametaddhi na praśaṁsanti paṇḍitāḥ.
english: And, O King, the face you bear, stained with tears, is a sight not sanctioned by the scriptures; truly, wise persons do not commend such a thing.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 35. And, O King, the face you bear, stained with tears, is a sight not sanctioned by the scriptures; truly, wise persons do not commend such a thing.

sanskrit: विस्फुलिङ्गा इव ह्येतान्दहन्ति किल मानवान् ।जहीहि मन्युं बुद्ध्या वै धारयात्मानमात्मना ॥३६॥
transliteration: visphuliṅgā iva hyetāndahanti kila mānavān ,jahīhi manyuṁ buddhyā vai dhārayātmānamātmanā.
english: For indeed, like sparks (of fire), these (emotions of grief and anger) consume human beings. Give up this sorrow with your intellect (buddhi), and uphold your own self (ātman) by your own self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 36. For indeed, like sparks (of fire), these (emotions of grief and anger) consume human beings. Give up this sorrow with your intellect (buddhi), and uphold your own self (ātman) by your own self (ātman).

sanskrit: एवमाश्वासितस्तेन संजयेन महात्मना ।विदुरो भूय एवाह बुद्धिपूर्वं परंतप ॥३७॥
transliteration: evamāśvāsitastena saṁjayena mahātmanā ,viduro bhūya evāha buddhipūrvaṁ paraṁtapa.
english: Thus consoled by that great-souled Sañjaya, Vidura again spoke, with thoughtful deliberation, O tormentor of foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 1 Verse 37. Thus consoled by that great-souled Sañjaya, Vidura again spoke, with thoughtful deliberation, O tormentor of foes.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततोऽमृतसमैर्वाक्यैर्ह्लादयन्पुरुषर्षभम् ।वैचित्रवीर्यं विदुरो यदुवाच निबोध तत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato'mṛtasamairvākyairhlādayanpuruṣarṣabham ,vaicitravīryaṁ viduro yaduvāca nibodha tat.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Now, listen to what Vidura spoke, delighting Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the foremost among men (puruṣa), with words like nectar."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Now, listen to what Vidura spoke, delighting Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the foremost among men (puruṣa), with words like nectar."

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।उत्तिष्ठ राजन्किं शेषे धारयात्मानमात्मना ।स्थिरजङ्गममर्त्यानां सर्वेषामेष निर्णयः ॥२॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,uttiṣṭha rājankiṁ śeṣe dhārayātmānamātmanā ,sthirajaṅgamamartyānāṁ sarveṣāmeṣa nirṇayaḥ.
english: Vidura said: "Arise, O King! Why do you sleep? Sustain your self (ātman) by your own self (ātman). This is the decree for all fixed, mobile, and mortal beings."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. Vidura said: "Arise, O King! Why do you sleep? Sustain your self (ātman) by your own self (ātman). This is the decree for all fixed, mobile, and mortal beings."

sanskrit: सर्वे क्षयान्ता निचयाः पतनान्ताः समुच्छ्रयाः ।संयोगा विप्रयोगान्ता मरणान्तं हि जीवितम् ॥३॥
transliteration: sarve kṣayāntā nicayāḥ patanāntāḥ samucchrayāḥ ,saṁyogā viprayogāntā maraṇāntaṁ hi jīvitam.
english: All accumulations end in destruction; all elevations end in a fall; all unions end in separation; and indeed, life ends in death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. All accumulations end in destruction; all elevations end in a fall; all unions end in separation; and indeed, life ends in death.

sanskrit: यदा शूरं च भीरुं च यमः कर्षति भारत ।तत्किं न योत्स्यन्ति हि ते क्षत्रियाः क्षत्रियर्षभ ॥४॥
transliteration: yadā śūraṁ ca bhīruṁ ca yamaḥ karṣati bhārata ,tatkiṁ na yotsyanti hi te kṣatriyāḥ kṣatriyarṣabha.
english: O Bhārata, when Yama seizes both the brave and the timid, why then, O foremost of kṣatriyas, should these warriors (kṣatriya) not indeed fight?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. O Bhārata, when Yama seizes both the brave and the timid, why then, O foremost of kṣatriyas, should these warriors (kṣatriya) not indeed fight?

sanskrit: अयुध्यमानो म्रियते युध्यमानश्च जीवति ।कालं प्राप्य महाराज न कश्चिदतिवर्तते ॥५॥
transliteration: ayudhyamāno mriyate yudhyamānaśca jīvati ,kālaṁ prāpya mahārāja na kaścidativartate.
english: One who does not fight perishes, while one who fights lives. O great king, when the appointed time is reached, no one can escape or overcome it.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. One who does not fight perishes, while one who fights lives. O great king, when the appointed time is reached, no one can escape or overcome it.

sanskrit: न चाप्येतान्हतान्युद्धे राजञ्शोचितुमर्हसि ।प्रमाणं यदि शास्त्राणि गतास्ते परमां गतिम् ॥६॥
transliteration: na cāpyetānhatānyuddhe rājañśocitumarhasi ,pramāṇaṁ yadi śāstrāṇi gatāste paramāṁ gatim.
english: And you should not grieve for these kinsmen slain in battle, O king. If the scriptures are indeed authoritative, they have attained the supreme destination.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. And you should not grieve for these kinsmen slain in battle, O king. If the scriptures are indeed authoritative, they have attained the supreme destination.

sanskrit: सर्वे स्वाध्यायवन्तो हि सर्वे च चरितव्रताः ।सर्वे चाभिमुखाः क्षीणास्तत्र का परिदेवना ॥७॥
transliteration: sarve svādhyāyavanto hi sarve ca caritavratāḥ ,sarve cābhimukhāḥ kṣīṇāstatra kā paridevanā.
english: Indeed, all of them were diligent in their personal study (svādhyāya), and all had observed their sacred vows. All of them faced the enemy and perished, so what cause for lamentation is there concerning them?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. Indeed, all of them were diligent in their personal study (svādhyāya), and all had observed their sacred vows. All of them faced the enemy and perished, so what cause for lamentation is there concerning them?

sanskrit: अदर्शनादापतिताः पुनश्चादर्शनं गताः ।न ते तव न तेषां त्वं तत्र का परिदेवना ॥८॥
transliteration: adarśanādāpatitāḥ punaścādarśanaṁ gatāḥ ,na te tava na teṣāṁ tvaṁ tatra kā paridevanā.
english: They appeared from the unmanifested and returned again to the unmanifested. They do not belong to you, nor do you belong to them; so what cause for lamentation is there regarding that?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. They appeared from the unmanifested and returned again to the unmanifested. They do not belong to you, nor do you belong to them; so what cause for lamentation is there regarding that?

sanskrit: हतोऽपि लभते स्वर्गं हत्वा च लभते यशः ।उभयं नो बहुगुणं नास्ति निष्फलता रणे ॥९॥
transliteration: hato'pi labhate svargaṁ hatvā ca labhate yaśaḥ ,ubhayaṁ no bahuguṇaṁ nāsti niṣphalatā raṇe.
english: Even if killed, one attains heaven; and by killing, one attains fame. For us, both outcomes are highly meritorious; there is no failure in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. Even if killed, one attains heaven; and by killing, one attains fame. For us, both outcomes are highly meritorious; there is no failure in battle.

sanskrit: तेषां कामदुघाँल्लोकानिन्द्रः संकल्पयिष्यति ।इन्द्रस्यातिथयो ह्येते भवन्ति पुरुषर्षभ ॥१०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ kāmadughāँllokānindraḥ saṁkalpayiṣyati ,indrasyātithayo hyete bhavanti puruṣarṣabha.
english: Indra will bestow upon them the worlds that fulfill all desires. Indeed, these men become the guests of Indra, O best among men (puruṣarṣabha).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. Indra will bestow upon them the worlds that fulfill all desires. Indeed, these men become the guests of Indra, O best among men (puruṣarṣabha).

sanskrit: न यज्ञैर्दक्षिणावद्भिर्न तपोभिर्न विद्यया ।स्वर्गं यान्ति तथा मर्त्या यथा शूरा रणे हताः ॥११॥
transliteration: na yajñairdakṣiṇāvadbhirna tapobhirna vidyayā ,svargaṁ yānti tathā martyā yathā śūrā raṇe hatāḥ.
english: Mortals do not attain heaven by means of rituals (yajñaiḥ) accompanied by generous fees (dakṣiṇāvadbhiḥ), nor by ascetic practices (tapas), nor by knowledge, in the same way that heroes killed in battle do.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. Mortals do not attain heaven by means of rituals (yajñaiḥ) accompanied by generous fees (dakṣiṇāvadbhiḥ), nor by ascetic practices (tapas), nor by knowledge, in the same way that heroes killed in battle do.

sanskrit: मातापितृसहस्राणि पुत्रदारशतानि च ।संसारेष्वनुभूतानि कस्य ते कस्य वा वयम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: mātāpitṛsahasrāṇi putradāraśatāni ca ,saṁsāreṣvanubhūtāni kasya te kasya vā vayam.
english: Thousands of mothers and fathers, and hundreds of sons and wives, have been experienced in cycles of transmigration (saṃsāra). To whom do they belong, and to whom do we belong?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. Thousands of mothers and fathers, and hundreds of sons and wives, have been experienced in cycles of transmigration (saṃsāra). To whom do they belong, and to whom do we belong?

sanskrit: शोकस्थानसहस्राणि भयस्थानशतानि च ।दिवसे दिवसे मूढमाविशन्ति न पण्डितम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: śokasthānasahasrāṇi bhayasthānaśatāni ca ,divase divase mūḍhamāviśanti na paṇḍitam.
english: Thousands of causes for sorrow and hundreds of causes for fear afflict a foolish person day after day, but they do not affect a wise one.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. Thousands of causes for sorrow and hundreds of causes for fear afflict a foolish person day after day, but they do not affect a wise one.

sanskrit: न कालस्य प्रियः कश्चिन्न द्वेष्यः कुरुसत्तम ।न मध्यस्थः क्वचित्कालः सर्वं कालः प्रकर्षति ॥१४॥
transliteration: na kālasya priyaḥ kaścinna dveṣyaḥ kurusattama ,na madhyasthaḥ kvacitkālaḥ sarvaṁ kālaḥ prakarṣati.
english: O best of the Kurus, no one is dear to Time, nor is anyone hateful to it. Time is never neutral; Time eventually draws everything away.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. O best of the Kurus, no one is dear to Time, nor is anyone hateful to it. Time is never neutral; Time eventually draws everything away.

sanskrit: अनित्यं जीवितं रूपं यौवनं द्रव्यसंचयः ।आरोग्यं प्रियसंवासो गृध्येदेषु न पण्डितः ॥१५॥
transliteration: anityaṁ jīvitaṁ rūpaṁ yauvanaṁ dravyasaṁcayaḥ ,ārogyaṁ priyasaṁvāso gṛdhyedeṣu na paṇḍitaḥ.
english: Life, beauty, youth, the accumulation of wealth, health, and living with loved ones are all impermanent. A wise person should not cling to these.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. Life, beauty, youth, the accumulation of wealth, health, and living with loved ones are all impermanent. A wise person should not cling to these.

sanskrit: न जानपदिकं दुःखमेकः शोचितुमर्हसि ।अप्यभावेन युज्येत तच्चास्य न निवर्तते ॥१६॥
transliteration: na jānapadikaṁ duḥkhamekaḥ śocitumarhasi ,apyabhāvena yujyeta taccāsya na nivartate.
english: You alone should not grieve over a general public misfortune. Even if one were to be joined with utter annihilation, that misfortune would still not cease for him.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. You alone should not grieve over a general public misfortune. Even if one were to be joined with utter annihilation, that misfortune would still not cease for him.

sanskrit: अशोचन्प्रतिकुर्वीत यदि पश्येत्पराक्रमम् ।भैषज्यमेतद्दुःखस्य यदेतन्नानुचिन्तयेत् ।चिन्त्यमानं हि न व्येति भूयश्चापि विवर्धते ॥१७॥
transliteration: aśocanpratikurvīta yadi paśyetparākramam ,bhaiṣajyametadduḥkhasya yadetannānucintayet ,cintyamānaṁ hi na vyeti bhūyaścāpi vivardhate.
english: If one perceives an opportunity for valor or action, one should counteract (the situation) without grieving. The remedy for sorrow is not to dwell on it. For when it is dwelled upon, it certainly does not pass away; instead, it increases even further.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. If one perceives an opportunity for valor or action, one should counteract (the situation) without grieving. The remedy for sorrow is not to dwell on it. For when it is dwelled upon, it certainly does not pass away; instead, it increases even further.

sanskrit: अनिष्टसंप्रयोगाच्च विप्रयोगात्प्रियस्य च ।मनुष्या मानसैर्दुःखैर्युज्यन्ते येऽल्पबुद्धयः ॥१८॥
transliteration: aniṣṭasaṁprayogācca viprayogātpriyasya ca ,manuṣyā mānasairduḥkhairyujyante ye'lpabuddhayaḥ.
english: Humans of limited understanding are afflicted by mental sorrows both from contact with what is undesirable and from separation from what is dear.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. Humans of limited understanding are afflicted by mental sorrows both from contact with what is undesirable and from separation from what is dear.

sanskrit: नार्थो न धर्मो न सुखं यदेतदनुशोचसि ।न च नापैति कार्यार्थात्त्रिवर्गाच्चैव भ्रश्यते ॥१९॥
transliteration: nārtho na dharmo na sukhaṁ yadetadanuśocasi ,na ca nāpaiti kāryārthāttrivargāccaiva bhraśyate.
english: There is no material gain (artha), no righteous conduct (dharma), and no happiness (sukha) in this matter over which you grieve. Moreover, not only does it not cease to exist by your grieving, but one also deviates from practical goals and indeed from the three aims of human life (trivarga).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. There is no material gain (artha), no righteous conduct (dharma), and no happiness (sukha) in this matter over which you grieve. Moreover, not only does it not cease to exist by your grieving, but one also deviates from practical goals and indeed from the three aims of human life (trivarga).

sanskrit: अन्यामन्यां धनावस्थां प्राप्य वैशेषिकीं नराः ।असंतुष्टाः प्रमुह्यन्ति संतोषं यान्ति पण्डिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: anyāmanyāṁ dhanāvasthāṁ prāpya vaiśeṣikīṁ narāḥ ,asaṁtuṣṭāḥ pramuhyanti saṁtoṣaṁ yānti paṇḍitāḥ.
english: Ordinary people, having achieved various successive stages of wealth, and being discontented, become greatly deluded; whereas the wise attain (true) contentment.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. Ordinary people, having achieved various successive stages of wealth, and being discontented, become greatly deluded; whereas the wise attain (true) contentment.

sanskrit: प्रज्ञया मानसं दुःखं हन्याच्छारीरमौषधैः ।एतज्ज्ञानस्य सामर्थ्यं न बालैः समतामियात् ॥२१॥
transliteration: prajñayā mānasaṁ duḥkhaṁ hanyācchārīramauṣadhaiḥ ,etajjñānasya sāmarthyaṁ na bālaiḥ samatāmiyāt.
english: One should eliminate mental suffering with wisdom and physical suffering with medicines. This is the power of true knowledge (jñāna); one should not be equated with the ignorant.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 21. One should eliminate mental suffering with wisdom and physical suffering with medicines. This is the power of true knowledge (jñāna); one should not be equated with the ignorant.

sanskrit: शयानं चानुशयति तिष्ठन्तं चानुतिष्ठति ।अनुधावति धावन्तं कर्म पूर्वकृतं नरम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: śayānaṁ cānuśayati tiṣṭhantaṁ cānutiṣṭhati ,anudhāvati dhāvantaṁ karma pūrvakṛtaṁ naram.
english: Past actions (karma) follow a person who is lying down, follow a person who is standing, and run after a person who is running.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 22. Past actions (karma) follow a person who is lying down, follow a person who is standing, and run after a person who is running.

sanskrit: यस्यां यस्यामवस्थायां यत्करोति शुभाशुभम् ।तस्यां तस्यामवस्थायां तत्तत्फलमुपाश्नुते ॥२३॥
transliteration: yasyāṁ yasyāmavasthāyāṁ yatkaroti śubhāśubham ,tasyāṁ tasyāmavasthāyāṁ tattatphalamupāśnute.
english: In whatever state a person performs good or bad deeds, in that very state they experience the corresponding result.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 2 Verse 23. In whatever state a person performs good or bad deeds, in that very state they experience the corresponding result.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।सुभाषितैर्महाप्राज्ञ शोकोऽयं विगतो मम ।भुय एव तु वाक्यानि श्रोतुमिच्छामि तत्त्वतः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,subhāṣitairmahāprājña śoko'yaṁ vigato mama ,bhuya eva tu vākyāni śrotumicchāmi tattvataḥ.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "O greatly wise one, this sorrow of mine has been removed by your excellent sayings. But I indeed wish to hear more words, relating to the true essence (tattva) of things."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: "O greatly wise one, this sorrow of mine has been removed by your excellent sayings. But I indeed wish to hear more words, relating to the true essence (tattva) of things."

sanskrit: अनिष्टानां च संसर्गादिष्टानां च विवर्जनात् ।कथं हि मानसैर्दुःखैः प्रमुच्यन्तेऽत्र पण्डिताः ॥२॥
transliteration: aniṣṭānāṁ ca saṁsargādiṣṭānāṁ ca vivarjanāt ,kathaṁ hi mānasairduḥkhaiḥ pramucyante'tra paṇḍitāḥ.
english: How indeed, are the wise truly liberated (mokṣa) from mental sufferings here, considering they arise from association with undesirable things and avoidance of desirable ones?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. How indeed, are the wise truly liberated (mokṣa) from mental sufferings here, considering they arise from association with undesirable things and avoidance of desirable ones?

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।यतो यतो मनो दुःखात्सुखाद्वापि प्रमुच्यते ।ततस्ततः शमं लब्ध्वा सुगतिं विन्दते बुधः ॥३॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,yato yato mano duḥkhātsukhādvāpi pramucyate ,tatastataḥ śamaṁ labdhvā sugatiṁ vindate budhaḥ.
english: Vidura said: "From whatever sorrow or even happiness the mind is freed, by that very liberation, having attained peace, the wise person finds a good destination."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. Vidura said: "From whatever sorrow or even happiness the mind is freed, by that very liberation, having attained peace, the wise person finds a good destination."

sanskrit: अशाश्वतमिदं सर्वं चिन्त्यमानं नरर्षभ ।कदलीसंनिभो लोकः सारो ह्यस्य न विद्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: aśāśvatamidaṁ sarvaṁ cintyamānaṁ nararṣabha ,kadalīsaṁnibho lokaḥ sāro hyasya na vidyate.
english: O best among men, when contemplated, this entire world (loka) is impermanent. The world (loka) is like a plantain tree; indeed, it possesses no essence.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. O best among men, when contemplated, this entire world (loka) is impermanent. The world (loka) is like a plantain tree; indeed, it possesses no essence.

sanskrit: गृहाण्येव हि मर्त्यानामाहुर्देहानि पण्डिताः ।कालेन विनियुज्यन्ते सत्त्वमेकं तु शोभनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: gṛhāṇyeva hi martyānāmāhurdehāni paṇḍitāḥ ,kālena viniyujyante sattvamekaṁ tu śobhanam.
english: Indeed, the wise (paṇḍitāḥ) declare that the bodies of mortals are like houses; they are destroyed by time. However, only the inner being (sattva) is truly beautiful.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. Indeed, the wise (paṇḍitāḥ) declare that the bodies of mortals are like houses; they are destroyed by time. However, only the inner being (sattva) is truly beautiful.

sanskrit: यथा जीर्णमजीर्णं वा वस्त्रं त्यक्त्वा तु वै नरः ।अन्यद्रोचयते वस्त्रमेवं देहाः शरीरिणाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: yathā jīrṇamajīrṇaṁ vā vastraṁ tyaktvā tu vai naraḥ ,anyadrocayate vastramevaṁ dehāḥ śarīriṇām.
english: Just as a person, having discarded a worn-out or even an unworn garment, desires another piece of clothing, so too are bodies for those who are embodied.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. Just as a person, having discarded a worn-out or even an unworn garment, desires another piece of clothing, so too are bodies for those who are embodied.

sanskrit: वैचित्रवीर्य वासं हि दुःखं वा यदि वा सुखम् ।प्राप्नुवन्तीह भूतानि स्वकृतेनैव कर्मणा ॥७॥
transliteration: vaicitravīrya vāsaṁ hi duḥkhaṁ vā yadi vā sukham ,prāpnuvantīha bhūtāni svakṛtenaiva karmaṇā.
english: O son of Vichitravirya, indeed, creatures in this world experience either suffering or happiness, entirely due to their own actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. O son of Vichitravirya, indeed, creatures in this world experience either suffering or happiness, entirely due to their own actions (karma).

sanskrit: कर्मणा प्राप्यते स्वर्गं सुखं दुःखं च भारत ।ततो वहति तं भारमवशः स्ववशोऽपि वा ॥८॥
transliteration: karmaṇā prāpyate svargaṁ sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ ca bhārata ,tato vahati taṁ bhāramavaśaḥ svavaśo'pi vā.
english: O descendant of Bharata, heaven, happiness, and sorrow are obtained through (karma) action. Therefore, one carries that burden, whether helpless or even self-controlled.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. O descendant of Bharata, heaven, happiness, and sorrow are obtained through (karma) action. Therefore, one carries that burden, whether helpless or even self-controlled.

sanskrit: यथा च मृन्मयं भाण्डं चक्रारूढं विपद्यते ।किंचित्प्रक्रियमाणं वा कृतमात्रमथापि वा ॥९॥
transliteration: yathā ca mṛnmayaṁ bhāṇḍaṁ cakrārūḍhaṁ vipadyate ,kiṁcitprakriyamāṇaṁ vā kṛtamātramathāpi vā.
english: And just as an earthen pot, placed on the potter's wheel, perishes - whether it is still in the process of being made or has just been completed -
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. And just as an earthen pot, placed on the potter's wheel, perishes - whether it is still in the process of being made or has just been completed -

sanskrit: छिन्नं वाप्यवरोप्यन्तमवतीर्णमथापि वा ।आर्द्रं वाप्यथ वा शुष्कं पच्यमानमथापि वा ॥१०॥
transliteration: chinnaṁ vāpyavaropyantamavatīrṇamathāpi vā ,ārdraṁ vāpyatha vā śuṣkaṁ pacyamānamathāpi vā.
english: Whether something is cut, or being planted, or taken down/picked; whether it is wet or dry, or being ripened/cooked.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. Whether something is cut, or being planted, or taken down/picked; whether it is wet or dry, or being ripened/cooked.

sanskrit: अवतार्यमाणमापाकादुद्धृतं वापि भारत ।अथ वा परिभुज्यन्तमेवं देहाः शरीरिणाम् ॥११॥
transliteration: avatāryamāṇamāpākāduddhṛtaṁ vāpi bhārata ,atha vā paribhujyantamevaṁ dehāḥ śarīriṇām.
english: Whether it is being taken down from cooking, or taken out (from the pot), O Bhārata; or even being consumed – in this manner are the bodies of embodied beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. Whether it is being taken down from cooking, or taken out (from the pot), O Bhārata; or even being consumed – in this manner are the bodies of embodied beings.

sanskrit: गर्भस्थो वा प्रसूतो वाप्यथ वा दिवसान्तरः ।अर्धमासगतो वापि मासमात्रगतोऽपि वा ॥१२॥
transliteration: garbhastho vā prasūto vāpyatha vā divasāntaraḥ ,ardhamāsagato vāpi māsamātragato'pi vā.
english: Whether (one is) in the womb, or born, or only a few days old; whether half a month old, or just one month old.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. Whether (one is) in the womb, or born, or only a few days old; whether half a month old, or just one month old.

sanskrit: संवत्सरगतो वापि द्विसंवत्सर एव वा ।यौवनस्थोऽपि मध्यस्थो वृद्धो वापि विपद्यते ॥१३॥
transliteration: saṁvatsaragato vāpi dvisaṁvatsara eva vā ,yauvanastho'pi madhyastho vṛddho vāpi vipadyate.
english: Whether one year old, or even two years old; even while in youth, or in middle age, or old  -  one perishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. Whether one year old, or even two years old; even while in youth, or in middle age, or old  -  one perishes.

sanskrit: प्राक्कर्मभिस्तु भूतानि भवन्ति न भवन्ति च ।एवं सांसिद्धिके लोके किमर्थमनुतप्यसे ॥१४॥
transliteration: prākkarmabhistu bhūtāni bhavanti na bhavanti ca ,evaṁ sāṁsiddhike loke kimarthamanutapyase.
english: Indeed, due to actions (karma) from previous lives, beings come into existence and cease to exist. Why then do you lament in this naturally existing world?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. Indeed, due to actions (karma) from previous lives, beings come into existence and cease to exist. Why then do you lament in this naturally existing world?

sanskrit: यथा च सलिले राजन्क्रीडार्थमनुसंचरन् ।उन्मज्जेच्च निमज्जेच्च किंचित्सत्त्वं नराधिप ॥१५॥
transliteration: yathā ca salile rājankrīḍārthamanusaṁcaran ,unmajjecca nimajjecca kiṁcitsattvaṁ narādhipa.
english: Just as, O King (rājan), some creature moves about in the water for sport, now surfacing and now submerging, O Lord of men (narādhipa).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. Just as, O King (rājan), some creature moves about in the water for sport, now surfacing and now submerging, O Lord of men (narādhipa).

sanskrit: एवं संसारगहनादुन्मज्जननिमज्जनात् ।कर्मभोगेन बध्यन्तः क्लिश्यन्ते येऽल्पबुद्धयः ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁsāragahanādunmajjananimajjanāt ,karmabhogena badhyantaḥ kliśyante ye'lpabuddhayaḥ.
english: In this way, those who are of small intellect (alpa-buddhayaḥ), being bound by the experience of their actions (karma-bhoga), suffer through emerging and submerging from the thicket of worldly existence (saṃsāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. In this way, those who are of small intellect (alpa-buddhayaḥ), being bound by the experience of their actions (karma-bhoga), suffer through emerging and submerging from the thicket of worldly existence (saṃsāra).

sanskrit: ये तु प्राज्ञाः स्थिताः सत्ये संसारान्तगवेषिणः ।समागमज्ञा भूतानां ते यान्ति परमां गतिम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ye tu prājñāḥ sthitāḥ satye saṁsārāntagaveṣiṇaḥ ,samāgamajñā bhūtānāṁ te yānti paramāṁ gatim.
english: But those wise ones who are established in truth (satya), seeking the end of worldly existence (saṃsāra), and aware of the coming together and separation of beings, they attain the supreme goal.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. But those wise ones who are established in truth (satya), seeking the end of worldly existence (saṃsāra), and aware of the coming together and separation of beings, they attain the supreme goal.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।कथं संसारगहनं विज्ञेयं वदतां वर ।एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं तत्त्वमाख्याहि पृच्छतः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,kathaṁ saṁsāragahanaṁ vijñeyaṁ vadatāṁ vara ,etadicchāmyahaṁ śrotuṁ tattvamākhyāhi pṛcchataḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O best among speakers, how can one understand the profound and intricate nature of transmigration (saṃsāra)? I wish to hear about this; please explain the truth to me who inquires."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O best among speakers, how can one understand the profound and intricate nature of transmigration (saṃsāra)? I wish to hear about this; please explain the truth to me who inquires."

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।जन्मप्रभृति भूतानां क्रियाः सर्वाः शृणु प्रभो ।पूर्वमेवेह कलले वसते किंचिदन्तरम् ॥२॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,janmaprabhṛti bhūtānāṁ kriyāḥ sarvāḥ śṛṇu prabho ,pūrvameveha kalale vasate kiṁcidantaram.
english: Vidura said: "O lord, listen to me concerning all the actions of beings from the very beginning of their existence. Even at the embryonic stage, a subtle distinction (antara) is already present."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. Vidura said: "O lord, listen to me concerning all the actions of beings from the very beginning of their existence. Even at the embryonic stage, a subtle distinction (antara) is already present."

sanskrit: ततः स पञ्चमेऽतीते मासे मांसं प्रकल्पयेत् ।ततः सर्वाङ्गसंपूर्णो गर्भो मासे प्रजायते ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa pañcame'tīte māse māṁsaṁ prakalpayet ,tataḥ sarvāṅgasaṁpūrṇo garbho māse prajāyate.
english: Then, after the fifth month, the embryo forms flesh. Subsequently, the fetus, complete with all its limbs, is born (usually in the ninth month).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. Then, after the fifth month, the embryo forms flesh. Subsequently, the fetus, complete with all its limbs, is born (usually in the ninth month).

sanskrit: अमेध्यमध्ये वसति मांसशोणितलेपने ।ततस्तु वायुवेगेन ऊर्ध्वपादो ह्यधःशिराः ॥४॥
transliteration: amedhyamadhye vasati māṁsaśoṇitalepane ,tatastu vāyuvegena ūrdhvapādo hyadhaḥśirāḥ.
english: The fetus dwells amidst impurity, coated with flesh and blood. Then, indeed, by the force of the vital air (or birth wind), it assumes a position with its feet upwards and head downwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. The fetus dwells amidst impurity, coated with flesh and blood. Then, indeed, by the force of the vital air (or birth wind), it assumes a position with its feet upwards and head downwards.

sanskrit: योनिद्वारमुपागम्य बहून्क्लेशान्समृच्छति ।योनिसंपीडनाच्चैव पूर्वकर्मभिरन्वितः ॥५॥
transliteration: yonidvāramupāgamya bahūnkleśānsamṛcchati ,yonisaṁpīḍanāccaiva pūrvakarmabhiranvitaḥ.
english: Having entered the birth canal, and being associated with previous actions (karma), one experiences many difficulties, even from the compression of the womb.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. Having entered the birth canal, and being associated with previous actions (karma), one experiences many difficulties, even from the compression of the womb.

sanskrit: तस्मान्मुक्तः स संसारादन्यान्पश्यत्युपद्रवान् ।ग्रहास्तमुपसर्पन्ति सारमेया इवामिषम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tasmānmuktaḥ sa saṁsārādanyānpaśyatyupadravān ,grahāstamupasarpanti sārameyā ivāmiṣam.
english: Released from that (womb-related suffering) and from the cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra), he then perceives other afflictions. Astrological influences (grahāḥ) approach him, just as dogs approach a piece of meat.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. Released from that (womb-related suffering) and from the cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra), he then perceives other afflictions. Astrological influences (grahāḥ) approach him, just as dogs approach a piece of meat.

sanskrit: ततः प्राप्तोत्तरे काले व्याधयश्चापि तं तथा ।उपसर्पन्ति जीवन्तं बध्यमानं स्वकर्मभिः ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāptottare kāle vyādhayaścāpi taṁ tathā ,upasarpanti jīvantaṁ badhyamānaṁ svakarmabhiḥ.
english: Thereafter, in a later period, diseases also approach him in the same way, while he is living and being bound by his own actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. Thereafter, in a later period, diseases also approach him in the same way, while he is living and being bound by his own actions (karma).

sanskrit: बद्धमिन्द्रियपाशैस्तं सङ्गस्वादुभिरातुरम् ।व्यसनान्युपवर्तन्ते विविधानि नराधिप ।बध्यमानश्च तैर्भूयो नैव तृप्तिमुपैति सः ॥८॥
transliteration: baddhamindriyapāśaistaṁ saṅgasvādubhirāturam ,vyasanānyupavartante vividhāni narādhipa ,badhyamānaśca tairbhūyo naiva tṛptimupaiti saḥ.
english: O king, various misfortunes come upon him, who is distressed and bound by the snares of the senses, which are sweet due to attachment. And even when repeatedly bound by these, he never attains satisfaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. O king, various misfortunes come upon him, who is distressed and bound by the snares of the senses, which are sweet due to attachment. And even when repeatedly bound by these, he never attains satisfaction.

sanskrit: अयं न बुध्यते तावद्यमलोकमथागतम् ।यमदूतैर्विकृष्यंश्च मृत्युं कालेन गच्छति ॥९॥
transliteration: ayaṁ na budhyate tāvadyamalokamathāgatam ,yamadūtairvikṛṣyaṁśca mṛtyuṁ kālena gacchati.
english: This person does not realize that he has already arrived in the realm of Yama. And being dragged by the messengers of Yama, he eventually succumbs to death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. This person does not realize that he has already arrived in the realm of Yama. And being dragged by the messengers of Yama, he eventually succumbs to death.

sanskrit: वाग्घीनस्य च यन्मात्रमिष्टानिष्टं कृतं मुखे ।भूय एवात्मनात्मानं बध्यमानमुपेक्षते ॥१०॥
transliteration: vāgghīnasya ca yanmātramiṣṭāniṣṭaṁ kṛtaṁ mukhe ,bhūya evātmanātmānaṁ badhyamānamupekṣate.
english: Whatever desirable or undesirable things are uttered by the mouth of one whose intellect (dhī) is dominated by speech, he himself again and again disregards how he is being bound by his own actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. Whatever desirable or undesirable things are uttered by the mouth of one whose intellect (dhī) is dominated by speech, he himself again and again disregards how he is being bound by his own actions.

sanskrit: अहो विनिकृतो लोको लोभेन च वशीकृतः ।लोभक्रोधमदोन्मत्तो नात्मानमवबुध्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: aho vinikṛto loko lobhena ca vaśīkṛtaḥ ,lobhakrodhamadonmatto nātmānamavabudhyate.
english: Alas, people are deceived and subjugated by greed. Maddened by greed, anger, and arrogance, one does not understand their own true self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. Alas, people are deceived and subjugated by greed. Maddened by greed, anger, and arrogance, one does not understand their own true self (ātman).

sanskrit: कुलीनत्वेन रमते दुष्कुलीनान्विकुत्सयन् ।धनदर्पेण दृप्तश्च दरिद्रान्परिकुत्सयन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: kulīnatvena ramate duṣkulīnānvikutsayan ,dhanadarpeṇa dṛptaśca daridrānparikutsayan.
english: He takes delight in his noble lineage, all the while criticizing those of ignoble birth. And, arrogant due to the pride of wealth, he despises the poor.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. He takes delight in his noble lineage, all the while criticizing those of ignoble birth. And, arrogant due to the pride of wealth, he despises the poor.

sanskrit: मूर्खानिति परानाह नात्मानं समवेक्षते ।शिक्षां क्षिपति चान्येषां नात्मानं शास्तुमिच्छति ॥१३॥
transliteration: mūrkhāniti parānāha nātmānaṁ samavekṣate ,śikṣāṁ kṣipati cānyeṣāṁ nātmānaṁ śāstumicchati.
english: He calls others fools, but he does not examine himself (ātman). And he imparts instruction to others, but he does not wish to discipline himself (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. He calls others fools, but he does not examine himself (ātman). And he imparts instruction to others, but he does not wish to discipline himself (ātman).

sanskrit: अध्रुवे जीवलोकेऽस्मिन्यो धर्ममनुपालयन् ।जन्मप्रभृति वर्तेत प्राप्नुयात्परमां गतिम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: adhruve jīvaloke'sminyo dharmamanupālayan ,janmaprabhṛti varteta prāpnuyātparamāṁ gatim.
english: In this transient world of living beings, he who lives from birth onwards, observing the natural law (dharma), will attain the supreme destination.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. In this transient world of living beings, he who lives from birth onwards, observing the natural law (dharma), will attain the supreme destination.

sanskrit: एवं सर्वं विदित्वा वै यस्तत्त्वमनुवर्तते ।स प्रमोक्षाय लभते पन्थानं मनुजाधिप ॥१५॥
transliteration: evaṁ sarvaṁ viditvā vai yastattvamanuvartate ,sa pramokṣāya labhate panthānaṁ manujādhipa.
english: O lord of men, whoever, having indeed known all this, adheres to the truth (tattva), he obtains the path to final liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. O lord of men, whoever, having indeed known all this, adheres to the truth (tattva), he obtains the path to final liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।यदिदं धर्मगहनं बुद्ध्या समनुगम्यते ।एतद्विस्तरशः सर्वं बुद्धिमार्गं प्रशंस मे ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,yadidaṁ dharmagahanaṁ buddhyā samanugamyate ,etadvistaraśaḥ sarvaṁ buddhimārgaṁ praśaṁsa me.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "Please describe to me in detail this entire intellectual path (buddhimārga) by which this profound subject of natural law (dharma) is comprehended."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: "Please describe to me in detail this entire intellectual path (buddhimārga) by which this profound subject of natural law (dharma) is comprehended."

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।अत्र ते वर्तयिष्यामि नमस्कृत्वा स्वयंभुवे ।यथा संसारगहनं वदन्ति परमर्षयः ॥२॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,atra te vartayiṣyāmi namaskṛtvā svayaṁbhuve ,yathā saṁsāragahanaṁ vadanti paramarṣayaḥ.
english: Vidura said: Having offered obeisance to the Self-existent One, I will now explain to you how the great sages (paramarṣi) describe the profound wilderness of existence (saṃsāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. Vidura said: Having offered obeisance to the Self-existent One, I will now explain to you how the great sages (paramarṣi) describe the profound wilderness of existence (saṃsāra).

sanskrit: कश्चिन्महति संसारे वर्तमानो द्विजः किल ।वनं दुर्गमनुप्राप्तो महत्क्रव्यादसंकुलम् ॥३॥
transliteration: kaścinmahati saṁsāre vartamāno dvijaḥ kila ,vanaṁ durgamanuprāpto mahatkravyādasaṁkulam.
english: A certain twice-born man (dvija), living in this vast cycle of existence (saṃsāra), arrived at a formidable forest, densely populated by large carnivorous creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. A certain twice-born man (dvija), living in this vast cycle of existence (saṃsāra), arrived at a formidable forest, densely populated by large carnivorous creatures.

sanskrit: सिंहव्याघ्रगजाकारैरतिघोरैर्महाशनैः ।समन्तात्संपरिक्षिप्तं मृत्योरपि भयप्रदम् ॥४॥
transliteration: siṁhavyāghragajākārairatighorairmahāśanaiḥ ,samantātsaṁparikṣiptaṁ mṛtyorapi bhayapradam.
english: It was completely surrounded on all sides by extremely dreadful creatures, resembling lions, tigers, and elephants, with enormous jaws, inspiring fear even in Death itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. It was completely surrounded on all sides by extremely dreadful creatures, resembling lions, tigers, and elephants, with enormous jaws, inspiring fear even in Death itself.

sanskrit: तदस्य दृष्ट्वा हृदयमुद्वेगमगमत्परम् ।अभ्युच्छ्रयश्च रोम्णां वै विक्रियाश्च परंतप ॥५॥
transliteration: tadasya dṛṣṭvā hṛdayamudvegamagamatparam ,abhyucchrayaśca romṇāṁ vai vikriyāśca paraṁtapa.
english: Upon seeing that, his heart became filled with extreme agitation, and indeed, his hairs stood on end and there were bodily distortions, O tormentor of enemies (parantapa).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. Upon seeing that, his heart became filled with extreme agitation, and indeed, his hairs stood on end and there were bodily distortions, O tormentor of enemies (parantapa).

sanskrit: स तद्वनं व्यनुसरन्विप्रधावनितस्ततः ।वीक्षमाणो दिशः सर्वाः शरणं क्व भवेदिति ॥६॥
transliteration: sa tadvanaṁ vyanusaranvipradhāvanitastataḥ ,vīkṣamāṇo diśaḥ sarvāḥ śaraṇaṁ kva bhavediti.
english: He pursued that forest, running hither and thither, looking in all directions, (thinking to himself,) "where could there be refuge?"
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. He pursued that forest, running hither and thither, looking in all directions, (thinking to himself,) "where could there be refuge?"

sanskrit: स तेषां छिद्रमन्विच्छन्प्रद्रुतो भयपीडितः ।न च निर्याति वै दूरं न च तैर्विप्रयुज्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: sa teṣāṁ chidramanvicchanpradruto bhayapīḍitaḥ ,na ca niryāti vai dūraṁ na ca tairviprayujyate.
english: Seeking an escape from them, he fled, afflicted by fear. Yet, he could neither get far away nor could he separate himself from them.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Seeking an escape from them, he fled, afflicted by fear. Yet, he could neither get far away nor could he separate himself from them.

sanskrit: अथापश्यद्वनं घोरं समन्ताद्वागुरावृतम् ।बाहुभ्यां संपरिष्वक्तं स्त्रिया परमघोरया ॥८॥
transliteration: athāpaśyadvanaṁ ghoraṁ samantādvāgurāvṛtam ,bāhubhyāṁ saṁpariṣvaktaṁ striyā paramaghorayā.
english: Then he saw a dreadful forest, encircled on all sides by nets, and embraced by the two arms of an exceedingly terrifying woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. Then he saw a dreadful forest, encircled on all sides by nets, and embraced by the two arms of an exceedingly terrifying woman.

sanskrit: पञ्चशीर्षधरैर्नागैः शैलैरिव समुन्नतैः ।नभःस्पृशैर्महावृक्षैः परिक्षिप्तं महावनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: pañcaśīrṣadharairnāgaiḥ śailairiva samunnataiḥ ,nabhaḥspṛśairmahāvṛkṣaiḥ parikṣiptaṁ mahāvanam.
english: This great forest was encompassed by five-headed serpents, by mountains, as it were, towering high, and by immense trees that touched the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. This great forest was encompassed by five-headed serpents, by mountains, as it were, towering high, and by immense trees that touched the sky.

sanskrit: वनमध्ये च तत्राभूदुदपानः समावृतः ।वल्लीभिस्तृणछन्नाभिर्गूढाभिरभिसंवृतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vanamadhye ca tatrābhūdudapānaḥ samāvṛtaḥ ,vallībhistṛṇachannābhirgūḍhābhirabhisaṁvṛtaḥ.
english: In the middle of the forest, there was a well, completely covered and hidden by creepers overgrown with grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. In the middle of the forest, there was a well, completely covered and hidden by creepers overgrown with grass.

sanskrit: पपात स द्विजस्तत्र निगूढे सलिलाशये ।विलग्नश्चाभवत्तस्मिँल्लतासंतानसंकटे ॥११॥
transliteration: papāta sa dvijastatra nigūḍhe salilāśaye ,vilagnaścābhavattasmiँllatāsaṁtānasaṁkaṭe.
english: That brahmin (dvija) fell into that hidden well (salilāśaya) and became entangled in the dense, constricted mass of creepers there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. That brahmin (dvija) fell into that hidden well (salilāśaya) and became entangled in the dense, constricted mass of creepers there.

sanskrit: पनसस्य यथा जातं वृन्तबद्धं महाफलम् ।स तथा लम्बते तत्र ऊर्ध्वपादो ह्यधःशिराः ॥१२॥
transliteration: panasasya yathā jātaṁ vṛntabaddhaṁ mahāphalam ,sa tathā lambate tatra ūrdhvapādo hyadhaḥśirāḥ.
english: Just as a large fruit grown on a jackfruit tree (panasa), bound by its stalk, hangs, so too he hung there, with his feet upwards and his head downwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. Just as a large fruit grown on a jackfruit tree (panasa), bound by its stalk, hangs, so too he hung there, with his feet upwards and his head downwards.

sanskrit: अथ तत्रापि चान्योऽस्य भूयो जात उपद्रवः ।कूपवीनाहवेलायामपश्यत महागजम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: atha tatrāpi cānyo'sya bhūyo jāta upadravaḥ ,kūpavīnāhavelāyāmapaśyata mahāgajam.
english: Then, another calamity arose for him there. At the mouth of the well, he saw a great elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. Then, another calamity arose for him there. At the mouth of the well, he saw a great elephant.

sanskrit: षड्वक्त्रं कृष्णशबलं द्विषट्कपदचारिणम् ।क्रमेण परिसर्पन्तं वल्लीवृक्षसमावृतम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: ṣaḍvaktraṁ kṛṣṇaśabalaṁ dviṣaṭkapadacāriṇam ,krameṇa parisarpantaṁ vallīvṛkṣasamāvṛtam.
english: I perceive the [wheel of time] as having six faces, dark and variegated, moving on twelve feet, gradually creeping around, and enveloped by creepers and trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. I perceive the [wheel of time] as having six faces, dark and variegated, moving on twelve feet, gradually creeping around, and enveloped by creepers and trees.

sanskrit: तस्य चापि प्रशाखासु वृक्षशाखावलम्बिनः ।नानारूपा मधुकरा घोररूपा भयावहाः ।आसते मधु संभृत्य पूर्वमेव निकेतजाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasya cāpi praśākhāsu vṛkṣaśākhāvalambinaḥ ,nānārūpā madhukarā ghorarūpā bhayāvahāḥ ,āsate madhu saṁbhṛtya pūrvameva niketajāḥ.
english: And on its sub-branches, hanging from the tree's branches, bees of various forms, dreadful-looking and frightening, having gathered honey, dwell there, having been born in the hive itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. And on its sub-branches, hanging from the tree's branches, bees of various forms, dreadful-looking and frightening, having gathered honey, dwell there, having been born in the hive itself.

sanskrit: भूयो भूयः समीहन्ते मधूनि भरतर्षभ ।स्वादनीयानि भूतानां न यैर्बालोऽपि तृप्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhūyo bhūyaḥ samīhante madhūni bharatarṣabha ,svādanīyāni bhūtānāṁ na yairbālo'pi tṛpyate.
english: O best of Bharatas, they repeatedly desire these palatable honeys for beings, yet even a child is not satisfied by them.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. O best of Bharatas, they repeatedly desire these palatable honeys for beings, yet even a child is not satisfied by them.

sanskrit: तेषां मधूनां बहुधा धारा प्रस्रवते सदा ।तां लम्बमानः स पुमान्धारां पिबति सर्वदा ।न चास्य तृष्णा विरता पिबमानस्य संकटे ॥१७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ madhūnāṁ bahudhā dhārā prasravate sadā ,tāṁ lambamānaḥ sa pumāndhārāṁ pibati sarvadā ,na cāsya tṛṣṇā viratā pibamānasya saṁkaṭe.
english: A stream of those honeys constantly flows forth in many ways. That man, hanging, always drinks that stream. Yet, his thirst (tṛṣṇā), even while drinking amidst distress, never ceases.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. A stream of those honeys constantly flows forth in many ways. That man, hanging, always drinks that stream. Yet, his thirst (tṛṣṇā), even while drinking amidst distress, never ceases.

sanskrit: अभीप्सति च तां नित्यमतृप्तः स पुनः पुनः ।न चास्य जीविते राजन्निर्वेदः समजायत ॥१८॥
transliteration: abhīpsati ca tāṁ nityamatṛptaḥ sa punaḥ punaḥ ,na cāsya jīvite rājannirvedaḥ samajāyata.
english: And that unsatisfied man constantly desires her again and again. Yet, O King, no disillusionment (nirveda) ever arose regarding his life.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. And that unsatisfied man constantly desires her again and again. Yet, O King, no disillusionment (nirveda) ever arose regarding his life.

sanskrit: तत्रैव च मनुष्यस्य जीविताशा प्रतिष्ठिता ।कृष्णाः श्वेताश्च तं वृक्षं कुट्टयन्ति स्म मूषकाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tatraiva ca manuṣyasya jīvitāśā pratiṣṭhitā ,kṛṣṇāḥ śvetāśca taṁ vṛkṣaṁ kuṭṭayanti sma mūṣakāḥ.
english: And precisely there, the hope for life (jīvitāśā) of man is firmly established. Black and white mice were gnawing at that tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. And precisely there, the hope for life (jīvitāśā) of man is firmly established. Black and white mice were gnawing at that tree.

sanskrit: व्यालैश्च वनदुर्गान्ते स्त्रिया च परमोग्रया ।कूपाधस्ताच्च नागेन वीनाहे कुञ्जरेण च ॥२०॥
transliteration: vyālaiśca vanadurgānte striyā ca paramograyā ,kūpādhastācca nāgena vīnāhe kuñjareṇa ca.
english: And by wild beasts at the edge of the difficult-to-traverse forest, and by an exceedingly fierce woman. And from below the well, by a great serpent (nāga), and by an elephant (kuñjara) at the entanglement.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. And by wild beasts at the edge of the difficult-to-traverse forest, and by an exceedingly fierce woman. And from below the well, by a great serpent (nāga), and by an elephant (kuñjara) at the entanglement.

sanskrit: वृक्षप्रपाताच्च भयं मूषकेभ्यश्च पञ्चमम् ।मधुलोभान्मधुकरैः षष्ठमाहुर्महद्भयम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vṛkṣaprapātācca bhayaṁ mūṣakebhyaśca pañcamam ,madhulobhānmadhukaraiḥ ṣaṣṭhamāhurmahadbhayam.
english: And fear arises from the fall of the tree, and the fifth (danger) is from the mice. The sixth, a great danger, they say, comes from the desire for honey (madhulobha), caused by the bees.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. And fear arises from the fall of the tree, and the fifth (danger) is from the mice. The sixth, a great danger, they say, comes from the desire for honey (madhulobha), caused by the bees.

sanskrit: एवं स वसते तत्र क्षिप्तः संसारसागरे ।न चैव जीविताशायां निर्वेदमुपगच्छति ॥२२॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa vasate tatra kṣiptaḥ saṁsārasāgare ,na caiva jīvitāśāyāṁ nirvedamupagacchati.
english: Thus, thrown into the ocean of worldly existence (saṃsāra), he dwells there, yet he does not develop dispassion (nirveda) towards the hope of life.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. Thus, thrown into the ocean of worldly existence (saṃsāra), he dwells there, yet he does not develop dispassion (nirveda) towards the hope of life.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अहो खलु महद्दुःखं कृच्छ्रवासं वसत्यसौ ।कथं तस्य रतिस्तत्र तुष्टिर्वा वदतां वर ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,aho khalu mahadduḥkhaṁ kṛcchravāsaṁ vasatyasau ,kathaṁ tasya ratistatra tuṣṭirvā vadatāṁ vara.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: Alas, what great misery! He lives in such a difficult state. O best of speakers, how can there be any joy (rati) or contentment (tuṣṭi) for him in that situation?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: Alas, what great misery! He lives in such a difficult state. O best of speakers, how can there be any joy (rati) or contentment (tuṣṭi) for him in that situation?

sanskrit: स देशः क्व नु यत्रासौ वसते धर्मसंकटे ।कथं वा स विमुच्येत नरस्तस्मान्महाभयात् ॥२॥
transliteration: sa deśaḥ kva nu yatrāsau vasate dharmasaṁkaṭe ,kathaṁ vā sa vimucyeta narastasmānmahābhayāt.
english: Where, indeed, is that place where he dwells, caught in a crisis concerning his natural law (dharma)? Or how might that person be liberated (vimucyeta) from such great fear?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. Where, indeed, is that place where he dwells, caught in a crisis concerning his natural law (dharma)? Or how might that person be liberated (vimucyeta) from such great fear?

sanskrit: एतन्मे सर्वमाचक्ष्व साधु चेष्टामहे तथा ।कृपा मे महती जाता तस्याभ्युद्धरणेन हि ॥३॥
transliteration: etanme sarvamācakṣva sādhu ceṣṭāmahe tathā ,kṛpā me mahatī jātā tasyābhyuddharaṇena hi.
english: Please tell me all this properly, and we shall endeavor accordingly. Indeed, great compassion (kṛpā) has arisen in me for his deliverance.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. Please tell me all this properly, and we shall endeavor accordingly. Indeed, great compassion (kṛpā) has arisen in me for his deliverance.

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।उपमानमिदं राजन्मोक्षविद्भिरुदाहृतम् ।सुगतिं विन्दते येन परलोकेषु मानवः ॥४॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,upamānamidaṁ rājanmokṣavidbhirudāhṛtam ,sugatiṁ vindate yena paralokeṣu mānavaḥ.
english: Vidura said: O King, this analogy has been declared by those who understand liberation (mokṣa), by which a human being attains a favorable state in the other worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. Vidura said: O King, this analogy has been declared by those who understand liberation (mokṣa), by which a human being attains a favorable state in the other worlds.

sanskrit: यत्तदुच्यति कान्तारं महत्संसार एव सः ।वनं दुर्गं हि यत्त्वेतत्संसारगहनं हि तत् ॥५॥
transliteration: yattaducyati kāntāraṁ mahatsaṁsāra eva saḥ ,vanaṁ durgaṁ hi yattvetatsaṁsāragahanaṁ hi tat.
english: That which is spoken of as a wilderness, that is indeed the great cycle of existence (saṃsāra). And that which is a difficult forest, that is indeed the dense thicket of existence (saṃsāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. That which is spoken of as a wilderness, that is indeed the great cycle of existence (saṃsāra). And that which is a difficult forest, that is indeed the dense thicket of existence (saṃsāra).

sanskrit: ये च ते कथिता व्याला व्याधयस्ते प्रकीर्तिताः ।या सा नारी बृहत्काया अधितिष्ठति तत्र वै ।तामाहुस्तु जरां प्राज्ञा वर्णरूपविनाशिनीम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ye ca te kathitā vyālā vyādhayaste prakīrtitāḥ ,yā sā nārī bṛhatkāyā adhitiṣṭhati tatra vai ,tāmāhustu jarāṁ prājñā varṇarūpavināśinīm.
english: And those serpents which were mentioned, they are declared to be diseases. As for that gigantic woman who dwells there, the wise call her old age, which destroys both complexion and form.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. And those serpents which were mentioned, they are declared to be diseases. As for that gigantic woman who dwells there, the wise call her old age, which destroys both complexion and form.

sanskrit: यस्तत्र कूपो नृपते स तु देहः शरीरिणाम् ।यस्तत्र वसतेऽधस्तान्महाहिः काल एव सः ।अन्तकः सर्वभूतानां देहिनां सर्वहार्यसौ ॥७॥
transliteration: yastatra kūpo nṛpate sa tu dehaḥ śarīriṇām ,yastatra vasate'dhastānmahāhiḥ kāla eva saḥ ,antakaḥ sarvabhūtānāṁ dehināṁ sarvahāryasau.
english: O King, that well which is there, that is indeed the body of embodied beings. And that great serpent who dwells below there, that is indeed Time (kāla). He is the ender of all beings, and the universal destroyer of all embodied ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. O King, that well which is there, that is indeed the body of embodied beings. And that great serpent who dwells below there, that is indeed Time (kāla). He is the ender of all beings, and the universal destroyer of all embodied ones.

sanskrit: कूपमध्ये च या जाता वल्ली यत्र स मानवः ।प्रताने लम्बते सा तु जीविताशा शरीरिणाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: kūpamadhye ca yā jātā vallī yatra sa mānavaḥ ,pratāne lambate sā tu jīvitāśā śarīriṇām.
english: The creeper that has grown in the middle of the well, to whose tendrils that man clings, that, indeed, is the hope of life for all embodied beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. The creeper that has grown in the middle of the well, to whose tendrils that man clings, that, indeed, is the hope of life for all embodied beings.

sanskrit: स यस्तु कूपवीनाहे तं वृक्षं परिसर्पति ।षड्वक्त्रः कुञ्जरो राजन्स तु संवत्सरः स्मृतः ।मुखानि ऋतवो मासाः पादा द्वादश कीर्तिताः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa yastu kūpavīnāhe taṁ vṛkṣaṁ parisarpati ,ṣaḍvaktraḥ kuñjaro rājansa tu saṁvatsaraḥ smṛtaḥ ,mukhāni ṛtavo māsāḥ pādā dvādaśa kīrtitāḥ.
english: O King, that six-faced elephant that moves around the tree on the rim of the well - that is indeed called the year. Its faces are said to be the seasons, and its twelve feet are declared to be the months.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. O King, that six-faced elephant that moves around the tree on the rim of the well - that is indeed called the year. Its faces are said to be the seasons, and its twelve feet are declared to be the months.

sanskrit: ये तु वृक्षं निकृन्तन्ति मूषकाः सततोत्थिताः ।रात्र्यहानि तु तान्याहुर्भूतानां परिचिन्तकाः ।ये ते मधुकरास्तत्र कामास्ते परिकीर्तिताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ye tu vṛkṣaṁ nikṛntanti mūṣakāḥ satatotthitāḥ ,rātryahāni tu tānyāhurbhūtānāṁ paricintakāḥ ,ye te madhukarāstatra kāmāste parikīrtitāḥ.
english: Those mice, constantly active, which gnaw at the tree, are indeed called nights and days by those who ponder the nature of beings. And those honey-gatherers (madhukara) there are declared to be desires (kāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. Those mice, constantly active, which gnaw at the tree, are indeed called nights and days by those who ponder the nature of beings. And those honey-gatherers (madhukara) there are declared to be desires (kāma).

sanskrit: यास्तु ता बहुशो धाराः स्रवन्ति मधुनिस्रवम् ।तांस्तु कामरसान्विद्याद्यत्र मज्जन्ति मानवाः ॥११॥
transliteration: yāstu tā bahuśo dhārāḥ sravanti madhunisravam ,tāṁstu kāmarasānvidyādyatra majjanti mānavāḥ.
english: And those many streams that flow with an abundance of honey - one should know them to be the pleasures of desire (kāmarasa), into which human beings plunge.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. And those many streams that flow with an abundance of honey - one should know them to be the pleasures of desire (kāmarasa), into which human beings plunge.

sanskrit: एवं संसारचक्रस्य परिवृत्तिं स्म ये विदुः ।ते वै संसारचक्रस्य पाशांश्छिन्दन्ति वै बुधाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁsāracakrasya parivṛttiṁ sma ye viduḥ ,te vai saṁsāracakrasya pāśāṁśchindanti vai budhāḥ.
english: Thus, those who truly understand the revolution of the cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra) are indeed the wise ones who cut the bonds of this cycle of saṃsāra.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. Thus, those who truly understand the revolution of the cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra) are indeed the wise ones who cut the bonds of this cycle of saṃsāra.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अहोऽभिहितमाख्यानं भवता तत्त्वदर्शिना ।भूय एव तु मे हर्षः श्रोतुं वागमृतं तव ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,aho'bhihitamākhyānaṁ bhavatā tattvadarśinā ,bhūya eva tu me harṣaḥ śrotuṁ vāgamṛtaṁ tava.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Oh, what a profound narrative you, a seer of truth, have presented! My joy is even greater to hear more of your nectar-like speech.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Oh, what a profound narrative you, a seer of truth, have presented! My joy is even greater to hear more of your nectar-like speech.'

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।शृणु भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि मार्गस्यैतस्य विस्तरम् ।यच्छ्रुत्वा विप्रमुच्यन्ते संसारेभ्यो विचक्षणाः ॥२॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,śṛṇu bhūyaḥ pravakṣyāmi mārgasyaitasya vistaram ,yacchrutvā vipramucyante saṁsārebhyo vicakṣaṇāḥ.
english: Vidura said: 'Listen again! I will describe the full extent of this path, by hearing which, the discerning ones are liberated from the cycles of transmigration (saṃsāra).'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. Vidura said: 'Listen again! I will describe the full extent of this path, by hearing which, the discerning ones are liberated from the cycles of transmigration (saṃsāra).'

sanskrit: यथा तु पुरुषो राजन्दीर्घमध्वानमास्थितः ।क्वचित्क्वचिच्छ्रमात्स्थाता कुरुते वासमेव वा ॥३॥
transliteration: yathā tu puruṣo rājandīrghamadhvānamāsthitaḥ ,kvacitkvacicchramātsthātā kurute vāsameva vā.
english: Just as a person (puruṣa), O King, having embarked upon a long journey, occasionally, due to weariness, pauses to rest or makes a stop.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. Just as a person (puruṣa), O King, having embarked upon a long journey, occasionally, due to weariness, pauses to rest or makes a stop.

sanskrit: एवं संसारपर्याये गर्भवासेषु भारत ।कुर्वन्ति दुर्बुधा वासं मुच्यन्ते तत्र पण्डिताः ॥४॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁsāraparyāye garbhavāseṣu bhārata ,kurvanti durbudhā vāsaṁ mucyante tatra paṇḍitāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, in this cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra) and dwelling in wombs, the foolish remain entangled, while the wise are liberated from it.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. O Bhārata, in this cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra) and dwelling in wombs, the foolish remain entangled, while the wise are liberated from it.

sanskrit: तस्मादध्वानमेवैतमाहुः शास्त्रविदो जनाः ।यत्तु संसारगहनं वनमाहुर्मनीषिणः ॥५॥
transliteration: tasmādadhvānamevaitamāhuḥ śāstravido janāḥ ,yattu saṁsāragahanaṁ vanamāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ.
english: Therefore, people familiar with the scriptures (śāstra) refer to this (cycle of existence) as a path. However, the wise (manīṣiṇah) call this profound worldly existence (saṃsāra) a dense forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. Therefore, people familiar with the scriptures (śāstra) refer to this (cycle of existence) as a path. However, the wise (manīṣiṇah) call this profound worldly existence (saṃsāra) a dense forest.

sanskrit: सोऽयं लोकसमावर्तो मर्त्यानां भरतर्षभ ।चराणां स्थावराणां च गृध्येत्तत्र न पण्डितः ॥६॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ lokasamāvarto martyānāṁ bharatarṣabha ,carāṇāṁ sthāvarāṇāṁ ca gṛdhyettatra na paṇḍitaḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, this is the cycle of worlds (lokasamāvarta) for mortals, for both moving and stationary beings. A wise person (paṇḍita) should not covet anything in it.
