search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. O best of Bharatas, this is the cycle of worlds (lokasamāvarta) for mortals, for both moving and stationary beings. A wise person (paṇḍita) should not covet anything in it.

sanskrit: शारीरा मानसाश्चैव मर्त्यानां ये तु व्याधयः ।प्रत्यक्षाश्च परोक्षाश्च ते व्यालाः कथिता बुधैः ॥७॥
transliteration: śārīrā mānasāścaiva martyānāṁ ye tu vyādhayaḥ ,pratyakṣāśca parokṣāśca te vyālāḥ kathitā budhaiḥ.
english: The diseases of mortals, both physical and mental, whether visible or invisible, are indeed called venomous serpents by the wise (budha).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. The diseases of mortals, both physical and mental, whether visible or invisible, are indeed called venomous serpents by the wise (budha).

sanskrit: क्लिश्यमानाश्च तैर्नित्यं हन्यमानाश्च भारत ।स्वकर्मभिर्महाव्यालैर्नोद्विजन्त्यल्पबुद्धयः ॥८॥
transliteration: kliśyamānāśca tairnityaṁ hanyamānāśca bhārata ,svakarmabhirmahāvyālairnodvijantyalpabuddhayaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, those of limited understanding are not disturbed, even though they are constantly tormented and struck down by their own actions (karma), which are like great serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. O Bhārata, those of limited understanding are not disturbed, even though they are constantly tormented and struck down by their own actions (karma), which are like great serpents.

sanskrit: अथापि तैर्विमुच्येत व्याधिभिः पुरुषो नृप ।आवृणोत्येव तं पश्चाज्जरा रूपविनाशिनी ॥९॥
transliteration: athāpi tairvimucyeta vyādhibhiḥ puruṣo nṛpa ,āvṛṇotyeva taṁ paścājjarā rūpavināśinī.
english: O King, even if a person (puruṣa) might be freed from those diseases, old age, which destroys beauty, certainly overwhelms him afterwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. O King, even if a person (puruṣa) might be freed from those diseases, old age, which destroys beauty, certainly overwhelms him afterwards.

sanskrit: शब्दरूपरसस्पर्शैर्गन्धैश्च विविधैरपि ।मज्जमानं महापङ्के निरालम्बे समन्ततः ॥१०॥
transliteration: śabdarūparasasparśairgandhaiśca vividhairapi ,majjamānaṁ mahāpaṅke nirālambe samantataḥ.
english: Sinking on all sides into a vast, supportless mire, (one is caught) by various sounds, forms, tastes, touches, and smells.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. Sinking on all sides into a vast, supportless mire, (one is caught) by various sounds, forms, tastes, touches, and smells.

sanskrit: संवत्सरर्तवो मासाः पक्षाहोरात्रसंधयः ।क्रमेणास्य प्रलुम्पन्ति रूपमायुस्तथैव च ॥११॥
transliteration: saṁvatsarartavo māsāḥ pakṣāhorātrasaṁdhayaḥ ,krameṇāsya pralumpanti rūpamāyustathaiva ca.
english: Years, seasons, months, fortnights, and the junctures of days and nights, in sequence, steal away his beauty and, similarly, his lifespan.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. Years, seasons, months, fortnights, and the junctures of days and nights, in sequence, steal away his beauty and, similarly, his lifespan.

sanskrit: एते कालस्य निधयो नैताञ्जानन्ति दुर्बुधाः ।अत्राभिलिखितान्याहुः सर्वभूतानि कर्मणा ॥१२॥
transliteration: ete kālasya nidhayo naitāñjānanti durbudhāḥ ,atrābhilikhitānyāhuḥ sarvabhūtāni karmaṇā.
english: These are the storehouses of time. Those with poor intellect do not understand them. It is said that all beings are recorded herein by their actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. These are the storehouses of time. Those with poor intellect do not understand them. It is said that all beings are recorded herein by their actions (karma).

sanskrit: रथं शरीरं भूतानां सत्त्वमाहुस्तु सारथिम् ।इन्द्रियाणि हयानाहुः कर्म बुद्धिश्च रश्मयः ॥१३॥
transliteration: rathaṁ śarīraṁ bhūtānāṁ sattvamāhustu sārathim ,indriyāṇi hayānāhuḥ karma buddhiśca raśmayaḥ.
english: They declare the body to be the chariot of beings, and the mind (sattva) indeed as the charioteer. They say the senses are the horses, and action (karma) and intellect (buddhi) are the reins.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. They declare the body to be the chariot of beings, and the mind (sattva) indeed as the charioteer. They say the senses are the horses, and action (karma) and intellect (buddhi) are the reins.

sanskrit: तेषां हयानां यो वेगं धावतामनुधावति ।स तु संसारचक्रेऽस्मिंश्चक्रवत्परिवर्तते ॥१४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ hayānāṁ yo vegaṁ dhāvatāmanudhāvati ,sa tu saṁsāracakre'smiṁścakravatparivartate.
english: Whoever pursues the speed of those running horses (senses), he indeed revolves like a wheel in this cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. Whoever pursues the speed of those running horses (senses), he indeed revolves like a wheel in this cycle of transmigration (saṃsāra).

sanskrit: यस्तान्यमयते बुद्ध्या स यन्ता न निवर्तते ।याम्यमाहू रथं ह्येनं मुह्यन्ते येन दुर्बुधाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yastānyamayate buddhyā sa yantā na nivartate ,yāmyamāhū rathaṁ hyenaṁ muhyante yena durbudhāḥ.
english: He who controls those (senses) with his intellect (buddhi), he is the true controller (yantā) and does not return (to the cycle of transmigration). Indeed, they declare this chariot (body) to be controllable, yet it is by this (chariot) that the foolish are deluded.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. He who controls those (senses) with his intellect (buddhi), he is the true controller (yantā) and does not return (to the cycle of transmigration). Indeed, they declare this chariot (body) to be controllable, yet it is by this (chariot) that the foolish are deluded.

sanskrit: स चैतत्प्राप्नुते राजन्यत्त्वं प्राप्तो नराधिप ।राज्यनाशं सुहृन्नाशं सुतनाशं च भारत ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa caitatprāpnute rājanyattvaṁ prāpto narādhipa ,rājyanāśaṁ suhṛnnāśaṁ sutanāśaṁ ca bhārata.
english: O King, O lord of men, and O Bhārata, he experiences exactly what you have experienced: the ruin of his kingdom, the loss of his friends, and the destruction of his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. O King, O lord of men, and O Bhārata, he experiences exactly what you have experienced: the ruin of his kingdom, the loss of his friends, and the destruction of his sons.

sanskrit: अनुतर्षुलमेवैतद्दुःखं भवति भारत ।साधुः परमदुःखानां दुःखभैषज्यमाचरेत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: anutarṣulamevaitadduḥkhaṁ bhavati bhārata ,sādhuḥ paramaduḥkhānāṁ duḥkhabhaiṣajyamācaret.
english: O Bhārata, this suffering indeed becomes a source of anguish. A wise person should apply the remedy for even the most extreme sorrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. O Bhārata, this suffering indeed becomes a source of anguish. A wise person should apply the remedy for even the most extreme sorrows.

sanskrit: न विक्रमो न चाप्यर्थो न मित्रं न सुहृज्जनः ।तथोन्मोचयते दुःखाद्यथात्मा स्थिरसंयमः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na vikramo na cāpyartho na mitraṁ na suhṛjjanaḥ ,tathonmocayate duḥkhādyathātmā sthirasaṁyamaḥ.
english: Neither valor, nor wealth, nor a friend, nor even well-wishing people, can liberate one from suffering (duḥkha) in the way that a self (ātman) with firm self-control can.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. Neither valor, nor wealth, nor a friend, nor even well-wishing people, can liberate one from suffering (duḥkha) in the way that a self (ātman) with firm self-control can.

sanskrit: तस्मान्मैत्रं समास्थाय शीलमापद्य भारत ।दमस्त्यागोऽप्रमादश्च ते त्रयो ब्रह्मणो हयाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasmānmaitraṁ samāsthāya śīlamāpadya bhārata ,damastyāgo'pramādaśca te trayo brahmaṇo hayāḥ.
english: Therefore, O Bhārata, having adopted friendliness and embraced good character (śīla), these three - self-restraint, renunciation, and vigilance - are considered the horses of Brahman (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. Therefore, O Bhārata, having adopted friendliness and embraced good character (śīla), these three - self-restraint, renunciation, and vigilance - are considered the horses of Brahman (brahman).

sanskrit: शीलरश्मिसमायुक्ते स्थितो यो मानसे रथे ।त्यक्त्वा मृत्युभयं राजन्ब्रह्मलोकं स गच्छति ॥२०॥
transliteration: śīlaraśmisamāyukte sthito yo mānase rathe ,tyaktvā mṛtyubhayaṁ rājanbrahmalokaṁ sa gacchati.
english: O King, he who, situated in the chariot of the mind and endowed with the rays of virtue (śīla), abandons the fear of death, goes to the world of Brahman (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 7 Verse 20. O King, he who, situated in the chariot of the mind and endowed with the rays of virtue (śīla), abandons the fear of death, goes to the world of Brahman (brahman).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।विदुरस्य तु तद्वाक्यं निशम्य कुरुसत्तमः ।पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्तः पपात भुवि मूर्छितः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vidurasya tu tadvākyaṁ niśamya kurusattamaḥ ,putraśokābhisaṁtaptaḥ papāta bhuvi mūrchitaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: But the foremost of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), having heard those words of Vidura, fell to the ground unconscious, deeply tormented by grief for his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: But the foremost of the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), having heard those words of Vidura, fell to the ground unconscious, deeply tormented by grief for his sons.

sanskrit: तं तथा पतितं भूमौ निःसंज्ञं प्रेक्ष्य बान्धवाः ।कृष्णद्वैपायनश्चैव क्षत्ता च विदुरस्तथा ॥२॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā patitaṁ bhūmau niḥsaṁjñaṁ prekṣya bāndhavāḥ ,kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaścaiva kṣattā ca vidurastathā.
english: Having seen him thus fallen unconscious on the ground, his relatives, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and also Vidura, the Kṣattā (minister).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. Having seen him thus fallen unconscious on the ground, his relatives, Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), and also Vidura, the Kṣattā (minister).

sanskrit: संजयः सुहृदश्चान्ये द्वाःस्था ये चास्य संमताः ।जलेन सुखशीतेन तालवृन्तैश्च भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjayaḥ suhṛdaścānye dvāḥsthā ye cāsya saṁmatāḥ ,jalena sukhaśītena tālavṛntaiśca bhārata.
english: Saṃjaya, other friends, and those doorkeepers who were respected by him, (all revived him) with pleasantly cool water and palm-leaf fans, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. Saṃjaya, other friends, and those doorkeepers who were respected by him, (all revived him) with pleasantly cool water and palm-leaf fans, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: पस्पृशुश्च करैर्गात्रं वीजमानाश्च यत्नतः ।अन्वासन्सुचिरं कालं धृतराष्ट्रं तथागतम् ॥४॥
transliteration: paspṛśuśca karairgātraṁ vījamānāśca yatnataḥ ,anvāsansuciraṁ kālaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ tathāgatam.
english: And they touched his body with their hands, fanning him diligently. For a long time, they remained around Dhritarashtra, who was in that state (of unconsciousness).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. And they touched his body with their hands, fanning him diligently. For a long time, they remained around Dhritarashtra, who was in that state (of unconsciousness).

sanskrit: अथ दीर्घस्य कालस्य लब्धसंज्ञो महीपतिः ।विललाप चिरं कालं पुत्राधिभिरभिप्लुतः ॥५॥
transliteration: atha dīrghasya kālasya labdhasaṁjño mahīpatiḥ ,vilalāpa ciraṁ kālaṁ putrādhibhirabhiplutaḥ.
english: Then, after a long time, the lord of the earth, having regained consciousness, lamented for a long time, deluged by the distress caused by his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. Then, after a long time, the lord of the earth, having regained consciousness, lamented for a long time, deluged by the distress caused by his sons.

sanskrit: धिगस्तु खलु मानुष्यं मानुष्ये च परिग्रहम् ।यतोमूलानि दुःखानि संभवन्ति मुहुर्मुहुः ॥६॥
transliteration: dhigastu khalu mānuṣyaṁ mānuṣye ca parigraham ,yatomūlāni duḥkhāni saṁbhavanti muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Shame on human existence, and on attachment to possessions in human life! From which sorrows repeatedly originate.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. Shame on human existence, and on attachment to possessions in human life! From which sorrows repeatedly originate.

sanskrit: पुत्रनाशेऽर्थनाशे च ज्ञातिसंबन्धिनामपि ।प्राप्यते सुमहद्दुःखं विषाग्निप्रतिमं विभो ॥७॥
transliteration: putranāśe'rthanāśe ca jñātisaṁbandhināmapi ,prāpyate sumahadduḥkhaṁ viṣāgnipratimaṁ vibho.
english: In the loss of sons, the loss of wealth, and even of kinsmen and relatives, a very great sorrow is experienced, which is like a fire of poison, O Lord!
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. In the loss of sons, the loss of wealth, and even of kinsmen and relatives, a very great sorrow is experienced, which is like a fire of poison, O Lord!

sanskrit: येन दह्यन्ति गात्राणि येन प्रज्ञा विनश्यति ।येनाभिभूतः पुरुषो मरणं बहु मन्यते ॥८॥
transliteration: yena dahyanti gātrāṇi yena prajñā vinaśyati ,yenābhibhūtaḥ puruṣo maraṇaṁ bahu manyate.
english: That by which the limbs are scorched, by which the intellect is destroyed, and by which a person (puruṣa), being overwhelmed, greatly desires death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. That by which the limbs are scorched, by which the intellect is destroyed, and by which a person (puruṣa), being overwhelmed, greatly desires death.

sanskrit: तदिदं व्यसनं प्राप्तं मया भाग्यविपर्ययात् ।तच्चैवाहं करिष्यामि अद्यैव द्विजसत्तम ॥९॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ vyasanaṁ prāptaṁ mayā bhāgyaviparyayāt ,taccaivāhaṁ kariṣyāmi adyaiva dvijasattama.
english: This very calamity has befallen me due to a reversal of fortune. And indeed, O best among the twice-born (dvija), I shall accomplish that very thing today.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. This very calamity has befallen me due to a reversal of fortune. And indeed, O best among the twice-born (dvija), I shall accomplish that very thing today.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा तु महात्मानं पितरं ब्रह्मवित्तमम् ।धृतराष्ट्रोऽभवन्मूढः शोकं च परमं गतः ।अभूच्च तूष्णीं राजासौ ध्यायमानो महीपते ॥१०॥
transliteration: ityuktvā tu mahātmānaṁ pitaraṁ brahmavittamam ,dhṛtarāṣṭro'bhavanmūḍhaḥ śokaṁ ca paramaṁ gataḥ ,abhūcca tūṣṇīṁ rājāsau dhyāyamāno mahīpate.
english: Having spoken thus to his great-souled father, the foremost knower of Brahman (brahman), Dhṛtarāṣṭra became bewildered and was overcome by immense sorrow (śoka). That king then fell silent, lost in contemplation, O ruler of the earth (mahīpate).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. Having spoken thus to his great-souled father, the foremost knower of Brahman (brahman), Dhṛtarāṣṭra became bewildered and was overcome by immense sorrow (śoka). That king then fell silent, lost in contemplation, O ruler of the earth (mahīpate).

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा कृष्णद्वैपायनः प्रभुः ।पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्तं पुत्रं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥११॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaḥ prabhuḥ ,putraśokābhisaṁtaptaṁ putraṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Having heard his words, the revered Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), the lord, spoke to his son, who was deeply afflicted by the sorrow for his children (śoka).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. Having heard his words, the revered Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), the lord, spoke to his son, who was deeply afflicted by the sorrow for his children (śoka).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र महाबाहो यत्त्वां वक्ष्यामि तच्छृणु ।श्रुतवानसि मेधावी धर्मार्थकुशलस्तथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra mahābāho yattvāṁ vakṣyāmi tacchṛṇu ,śrutavānasi medhāvī dharmārthakuśalastathā.
english: O mighty-armed Dhritarashtra, listen to what I am about to tell you. You are learned, intelligent, and skilled in matters of natural law (dharma) and material prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. O mighty-armed Dhritarashtra, listen to what I am about to tell you. You are learned, intelligent, and skilled in matters of natural law (dharma) and material prosperity.

sanskrit: न तेऽस्त्यविदितं किंचिद्वेदितव्यं परंतप ।अनित्यतां हि मर्त्यानां विजानासि न संशयः ॥१३॥
transliteration: na te'styaviditaṁ kiṁcidveditavyaṁ paraṁtapa ,anityatāṁ hi martyānāṁ vijānāsi na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O tormentor of foes, nothing that ought to be known remains unknown to you. Indeed, you truly understand the impermanence of mortals; there is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. O tormentor of foes, nothing that ought to be known remains unknown to you. Indeed, you truly understand the impermanence of mortals; there is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: अध्रुवे जीवलोके च स्थाने वाशाश्वते सति ।जीविते मरणान्ते च कस्माच्छोचसि भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: adhruve jīvaloke ca sthāne vāśāśvate sati ,jīvite maraṇānte ca kasmācchocasi bhārata.
english: O scion of Bharata, in this transient world of living beings, and in a realm that is not eternal, and in a life that culminates in death, why do you lament?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. O scion of Bharata, in this transient world of living beings, and in a realm that is not eternal, and in a life that culminates in death, why do you lament?

sanskrit: प्रत्यक्षं तव राजेन्द्र वैरस्यास्य समुद्भवः ।पुत्रं ते कारणं कृत्वा कालयोगेन कारितः ॥१५॥
transliteration: pratyakṣaṁ tava rājendra vairasyāsya samudbhavaḥ ,putraṁ te kāraṇaṁ kṛtvā kālayogena kāritaḥ.
english: O king of kings, the origin of this hostility is evident to you. It was brought about by the conjunction of time (kāla), with your son being made the primary reason.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. O king of kings, the origin of this hostility is evident to you. It was brought about by the conjunction of time (kāla), with your son being made the primary reason.

sanskrit: अवश्यं भवितव्ये च कुरूणां वैशसे नृप ।कस्माच्छोचसि ताञ्शूरान्गतान्परमिकां गतिम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: avaśyaṁ bhavitavye ca kurūṇāṁ vaiśase nṛpa ,kasmācchocasi tāñśūrāngatānparamikāṁ gatim.
english: O King, when the destruction of the Kurus was inevitable, why do you grieve for those brave warriors who have attained the ultimate state (gati)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. O King, when the destruction of the Kurus was inevitable, why do you grieve for those brave warriors who have attained the ultimate state (gati)?

sanskrit: जानता च महाबाहो विदुरेण महात्मना ।यतितं सर्वयत्नेन शमं प्रति जनेश्वर ॥१७॥
transliteration: jānatā ca mahābāho vidureṇa mahātmanā ,yatitaṁ sarvayatnena śamaṁ prati janeśvara.
english: O mighty-armed one, O lord of men, the great-souled (mahātman) Vidura, who knew (the future), indeed strove with every effort for peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. O mighty-armed one, O lord of men, the great-souled (mahātman) Vidura, who knew (the future), indeed strove with every effort for peace.

sanskrit: न च दैवकृतो मार्गः शक्यो भूतेन केनचित् ।घटतापि चिरं कालं नियन्तुमिति मे मतिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na ca daivakṛto mārgaḥ śakyo bhūtena kenacit ,ghaṭatāpi ciraṁ kālaṁ niyantumiti me matiḥ.
english: And the course (karma) ordained by divine destiny cannot be controlled by any being, even if one strives for a long time. This is my conviction.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. And the course (karma) ordained by divine destiny cannot be controlled by any being, even if one strives for a long time. This is my conviction.

sanskrit: देवतानां हि यत्कार्यं मया प्रत्यक्षतः श्रुतम् ।तत्तेऽहं संप्रवक्ष्यामि कथं स्थैर्यं भवेत्तव ॥१९॥
transliteration: devatānāṁ hi yatkāryaṁ mayā pratyakṣataḥ śrutam ,tatte'haṁ saṁpravakṣyāmi kathaṁ sthairyaṁ bhavettava.
english: Indeed, I will fully relate to you that work of the gods which I have directly heard, explaining how you can achieve steadfastness.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. Indeed, I will fully relate to you that work of the gods which I have directly heard, explaining how you can achieve steadfastness.

sanskrit: पुराहं त्वरितो यातः सभामैन्द्रीं जितक्लमः ।अपश्यं तत्र च तदा समवेतान्दिवौकसः ।नारदप्रमुखांश्चापि सर्वान्देवऋषींस्तथा ॥२०॥
transliteration: purāhaṁ tvarito yātaḥ sabhāmaindrīṁ jitaklamaḥ ,apaśyaṁ tatra ca tadā samavetāndivaukasaḥ ,nāradapramukhāṁścāpi sarvāndevaṛṣīṁstathā.
english: Formerly, having overcome fatigue, I swiftly went to Indra's assembly. There, at that time, I saw the assembled dwellers of heaven, and also all the divine sages headed by Narada.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. Formerly, having overcome fatigue, I swiftly went to Indra's assembly. There, at that time, I saw the assembled dwellers of heaven, and also all the divine sages headed by Narada.

sanskrit: तत्र चापि मया दृष्टा पृथिवी पृथिवीपते ।कार्यार्थमुपसंप्राप्ता देवतानां समीपतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tatra cāpi mayā dṛṣṭā pṛthivī pṛthivīpate ,kāryārthamupasaṁprāptā devatānāṁ samīpataḥ.
english: And there, O lord of the Earth, I also saw the Earth (Pṛthivī), who had arrived near the gods for a particular purpose.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. And there, O lord of the Earth, I also saw the Earth (Pṛthivī), who had arrived near the gods for a particular purpose.

sanskrit: उपगम्य तदा धात्री देवानाह समागतान् ।यत्कार्यं मम युष्माभिर्ब्रह्मणः सदने तदा ।प्रतिज्ञातं महाभागास्तच्छीघ्रं संविधीयताम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: upagamya tadā dhātrī devānāha samāgatān ,yatkāryaṁ mama yuṣmābhirbrahmaṇaḥ sadane tadā ,pratijñātaṁ mahābhāgāstacchīghraṁ saṁvidhīyatām.
english: Having approached them then, the Earth (Dhātrī) addressed the assembled gods: 'O noble ones, that task which you had promised me in Brahmā's (brahman) abode then, let it be accomplished quickly!'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. Having approached them then, the Earth (Dhātrī) addressed the assembled gods: 'O noble ones, that task which you had promised me in Brahmā's (brahman) abode then, let it be accomplished quickly!'

sanskrit: तस्यास्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा विष्णुर्लोकनमस्कृतः ।उवाच प्रहसन्वाक्यं पृथिवीं देवसंसदि ॥२३॥
transliteration: tasyāstadvacanaṁ śrutvā viṣṇurlokanamaskṛtaḥ ,uvāca prahasanvākyaṁ pṛthivīṁ devasaṁsadi.
english: Having heard her words, Vishnu, who is revered by the worlds, spoke to the Earth with a smile in the assembly of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. Having heard her words, Vishnu, who is revered by the worlds, spoke to the Earth with a smile in the assembly of the gods.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राणां यस्तु ज्येष्ठः शतस्य वै ।दुर्योधन इति ख्यातः स ते कार्यं करिष्यति ।तं च प्राप्य महीपालं कृतकृत्या भविष्यसि ॥२४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putrāṇāṁ yastu jyeṣṭhaḥ śatasya vai ,duryodhana iti khyātaḥ sa te kāryaṁ kariṣyati ,taṁ ca prāpya mahīpālaṁ kṛtakṛtyā bhaviṣyasi.
english: The one among Dhritarashtra's sons who is indeed the eldest of the hundred, known as Duryodhana, he will accomplish your purpose. And by obtaining that king, you will become one whose purpose is accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. The one among Dhritarashtra's sons who is indeed the eldest of the hundred, known as Duryodhana, he will accomplish your purpose. And by obtaining that king, you will become one whose purpose is accomplished.

sanskrit: तस्यार्थे पृथिवीपालाः कुरुक्षेत्रे समागताः ।अन्योन्यं घातयिष्यन्ति दृढैः शस्त्रैः प्रहारिणः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasyārthe pṛthivīpālāḥ kurukṣetre samāgatāḥ ,anyonyaṁ ghātayiṣyanti dṛḍhaiḥ śastraiḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: For her sake, kings (protectors of the earth) will assemble on Kurukshetra. Striking with strong weapons, they will slay each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. For her sake, kings (protectors of the earth) will assemble on Kurukshetra. Striking with strong weapons, they will slay each other.

sanskrit: ततस्ते भविता देवि भारस्य युधि नाशनम् ।गच्छ शीघ्रं स्वकं स्थानं लोकान्धारय शोभने ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataste bhavitā devi bhārasya yudhi nāśanam ,gaccha śīghraṁ svakaṁ sthānaṁ lokāndhāraya śobhane.
english: Then, O goddess, there will be the destruction of your burden in battle. Go quickly to your own place, O beautiful one, and sustain the worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. Then, O goddess, there will be the destruction of your burden in battle. Go quickly to your own place, O beautiful one, and sustain the worlds.

sanskrit: स एष ते सुतो राजँल्लोकसंहारकारणात् ।कलेरंशः समुत्पन्नो गान्धार्या जठरे नृप ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa eṣa te suto rājaँllokasaṁhārakāraṇāt ,kaleraṁśaḥ samutpanno gāndhāryā jaṭhare nṛpa.
english: O king, this son of yours, born from Gandhari's womb, is a portion of Kali, arisen for the destruction of the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. O king, this son of yours, born from Gandhari's womb, is a portion of Kali, arisen for the destruction of the world.

sanskrit: अमर्षी चपलश्चापि क्रोधनो दुष्प्रसाधनः ।दैवयोगात्समुत्पन्ना भ्रातरश्चास्य तादृशाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: amarṣī capalaścāpi krodhano duṣprasādhanaḥ ,daivayogātsamutpannā bhrātaraścāsya tādṛśāḥ.
english: He was intolerant, impulsive, and also prone to anger and difficult to pacify. By the force of destiny, his brothers were also of a similar nature.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. He was intolerant, impulsive, and also prone to anger and difficult to pacify. By the force of destiny, his brothers were also of a similar nature.

sanskrit: शकुनिर्मातुलश्चैव कर्णश्च परमः सखा ।समुत्पन्ना विनाशार्थं पृथिव्यां सहिता नृपाः ।एतमर्थं महाबाहो नारदो वेद तत्त्वतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: śakunirmātulaścaiva karṇaśca paramaḥ sakhā ,samutpannā vināśārthaṁ pṛthivyāṁ sahitā nṛpāḥ ,etamarthaṁ mahābāho nārado veda tattvataḥ.
english: Indeed, Shakuni, his maternal uncle, and Karna, his foremost friend, along with other kings, have appeared on earth specifically for destruction. O mighty-armed one, Narada knows this truth (tattva) in its essence.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. Indeed, Shakuni, his maternal uncle, and Karna, his foremost friend, along with other kings, have appeared on earth specifically for destruction. O mighty-armed one, Narada knows this truth (tattva) in its essence.

sanskrit: आत्मापराधात्पुत्रास्ते विनष्टाः पृथिवीपते ।मा ताञ्शोचस्व राजेन्द्र न हि शोकेऽस्ति कारणम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: ātmāparādhātputrāste vinaṣṭāḥ pṛthivīpate ,mā tāñśocasva rājendra na hi śoke'sti kāraṇam.
english: O lord of the earth, your sons perished due to their own self (ātman) transgressions. O king of kings, do not grieve for them, for there is indeed no cause for sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. O lord of the earth, your sons perished due to their own self (ātman) transgressions. O king of kings, do not grieve for them, for there is indeed no cause for sorrow.

sanskrit: न हि ते पाण्डवाः स्वल्पमपराध्यन्ति भारत ।पुत्रास्तव दुरात्मानो यैरियं घातिता मही ॥३१॥
transliteration: na hi te pāṇḍavāḥ svalpamaparādhyanti bhārata ,putrāstava durātmāno yairiyaṁ ghātitā mahī.
english: O descendant of Bharata, those Pāṇḍavas did not transgress even slightly. It was your wicked-minded sons by whom this entire earth was caused to be devastated.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. O descendant of Bharata, those Pāṇḍavas did not transgress even slightly. It was your wicked-minded sons by whom this entire earth was caused to be devastated.

sanskrit: नारदेन च भद्रं ते पूर्वमेव न संशयः ।युधिष्ठिरस्य समितौ राजसूये निवेदितम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: nāradena ca bhadraṁ te pūrvameva na saṁśayaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasya samitau rājasūye niveditam.
english: And indeed, it was already reported by Narada, to your good fortune, without a doubt, in Yudhishthira's assembly during the Rājasūya (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. And indeed, it was already reported by Narada, to your good fortune, without a doubt, in Yudhishthira's assembly during the Rājasūya (yajña).

sanskrit: पाण्डवाः कौरवाश्चैव समासाद्य परस्परम् ।न भविष्यन्ति कौन्तेय यत्ते कृत्यं तदाचर ॥३३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāḥ kauravāścaiva samāsādya parasparam ,na bhaviṣyanti kaunteya yatte kṛtyaṁ tadācara.
english: O Kaunteya (son of Kuntī), the Pāṇḍavas and the Kauravas, having encountered each other, will not survive. Therefore, whatever action is your duty, perform that.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. O Kaunteya (son of Kuntī), the Pāṇḍavas and the Kauravas, having encountered each other, will not survive. Therefore, whatever action is your duty, perform that.

sanskrit: नारदस्य वचः श्रुत्वा तदाशोचन्त पाण्डवाः ।एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं देवगुह्यं सनातनम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: nāradasya vacaḥ śrutvā tadāśocanta pāṇḍavāḥ ,etatte sarvamākhyātaṁ devaguhyaṁ sanātanam.
english: Having heard the words of Narada, the Pāṇḍavas then lamented. All of this, the eternal (sanātana) divine secret, has now been fully revealed to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 34. Having heard the words of Narada, the Pāṇḍavas then lamented. All of this, the eternal (sanātana) divine secret, has now been fully revealed to you.

sanskrit: कथं ते शोकनाशः स्यात्प्राणेषु च दया प्रभो ।स्नेहश्च पाण्डुपुत्रेषु ज्ञात्वा दैवकृतं विधिम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ te śokanāśaḥ syātprāṇeṣu ca dayā prabho ,snehaśca pāṇḍuputreṣu jñātvā daivakṛtaṁ vidhim.
english: O Lord, how can your grief (śoka) be destroyed, and how can there be compassion towards all living beings and affection towards the Pāṇḍavas, when you have understood this divinely ordained course of events (vidhi)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 35. O Lord, how can your grief (śoka) be destroyed, and how can there be compassion towards all living beings and affection towards the Pāṇḍavas, when you have understood this divinely ordained course of events (vidhi)?

sanskrit: एष चार्थो महाबाहो पूर्वमेव मया श्रुतः ।कथितो धर्मराजस्य राजसूये क्रतूत्तमे ॥३६॥
transliteration: eṣa cārtho mahābāho pūrvameva mayā śrutaḥ ,kathito dharmarājasya rājasūye kratūttame.
english: This matter, O mighty-armed one, was indeed heard by me previously. It was narrated by the king of righteousness (dharma) during the supreme Rājasūya sacrifice.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 36. This matter, O mighty-armed one, was indeed heard by me previously. It was narrated by the king of righteousness (dharma) during the supreme Rājasūya sacrifice.

sanskrit: यतितं धर्मपुत्रेण मया गुह्ये निवेदिते ।अविग्रहे कौरवाणां दैवं तु बलवत्तरम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: yatitaṁ dharmaputreṇa mayā guhye nivedite ,avigrahe kauravāṇāṁ daivaṁ tu balavattaram.
english: Though I, along with the son of righteousness (dharma), made an effort when the secret counsel was conveyed, divine will (daiva) is nevertheless more powerful concerning the prevention of conflict among the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 37. Though I, along with the son of righteousness (dharma), made an effort when the secret counsel was conveyed, divine will (daiva) is nevertheless more powerful concerning the prevention of conflict among the Kauravas.

sanskrit: अनतिक्रमणीयो हि विधी राजन्कथंचन ।कृतान्तस्य हि भूतेन स्थावरेण त्रसेन च ॥३८॥
transliteration: anatikramaṇīyo hi vidhī rājankathaṁcana ,kṛtāntasya hi bhūtena sthāvareṇa trasena ca.
english: Indeed, O king, the decree (vidhi) of destiny (kṛtānta) is in no way to be transgressed, whether by a stationary or a moving creature.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 38. Indeed, O king, the decree (vidhi) of destiny (kṛtānta) is in no way to be transgressed, whether by a stationary or a moving creature.

sanskrit: भवान्धर्मपरो यत्र बुद्धिश्रेष्ठश्च भारत ।मुह्यते प्राणिनां ज्ञात्वा गतिं चागतिमेव च ॥३९॥
transliteration: bhavāndharmaparo yatra buddhiśreṣṭhaśca bhārata ,muhyate prāṇināṁ jñātvā gatiṁ cāgatimeva ca.
english: Even you, O Bhārata, who are devoted to righteousness (dharma) and are supreme in intellect, become bewildered, having understood the destiny and the ultimate state of all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 39. Even you, O Bhārata, who are devoted to righteousness (dharma) and are supreme in intellect, become bewildered, having understood the destiny and the ultimate state of all beings.

sanskrit: त्वां तु शोकेन संतप्तं मुह्यमानं मुहुर्मुहुः ।ज्ञात्वा युधिष्ठिरो राजा प्राणानपि परित्यजेत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: tvāṁ tu śokena saṁtaptaṁ muhyamānaṁ muhurmuhuḥ ,jñātvā yudhiṣṭhiro rājā prāṇānapi parityajet.
english: Indeed, if King Yudhishthira were to realize that you are tormented by sorrow and repeatedly bewildered, he would even give up his own life.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 40. Indeed, if King Yudhishthira were to realize that you are tormented by sorrow and repeatedly bewildered, he would even give up his own life.

sanskrit: कृपालुर्नित्यशो वीरस्तिर्यग्योनिगतेष्वपि ।स कथं त्वयि राजेन्द्र कृपां वै न करिष्यति ॥४१॥
transliteration: kṛpālurnityaśo vīrastiryagyonigateṣvapi ,sa kathaṁ tvayi rājendra kṛpāṁ vai na kariṣyati.
english: He, who is always a compassionate hero, even towards those born in lower species, how could he possibly not show compassion towards you, O King of kings?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 41. He, who is always a compassionate hero, even towards those born in lower species, how could he possibly not show compassion towards you, O King of kings?

sanskrit: मम चैव नियोगेन विधेश्चाप्यनिवर्तनात् ।पाण्डवानां च कारुण्यात्प्राणान्धारय भारत ॥४२॥
transliteration: mama caiva niyogena vidheścāpyanivartanāt ,pāṇḍavānāṁ ca kāruṇyātprāṇāndhāraya bhārata.
english: O Bharata, preserve your life because of my command, the irreversibility of destiny, and the compassion of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 42. O Bharata, preserve your life because of my command, the irreversibility of destiny, and the compassion of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: एवं ते वर्तमानस्य लोके कीर्तिर्भविष्यति ।धर्मश्च सुमहांस्तात तप्तं स्याच्च तपश्चिरात् ॥४३॥
transliteration: evaṁ te vartamānasya loke kīrtirbhaviṣyati ,dharmaśca sumahāṁstāta taptaṁ syācca tapaścirāt.
english: Thus, for you, living in this manner, there will be fame in the world, O dear one. Your adherence to natural law (dharma) will be very great, and it will be as if austerity (tapas) has been performed by you for a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 43. Thus, for you, living in this manner, there will be fame in the world, O dear one. Your adherence to natural law (dharma) will be very great, and it will be as if austerity (tapas) has been performed by you for a long time.

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकसमुत्पन्नं हुताशं ज्वलितं यथा ।प्रज्ञाम्भसा महाराज निर्वापय सदा सदा ॥४४॥
transliteration: putraśokasamutpannaṁ hutāśaṁ jvalitaṁ yathā ,prajñāmbhasā mahārāja nirvāpaya sadā sadā.
english: O great king, always extinguish this burning fire, which has arisen from the grief for your sons, with the water of wisdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 44. O great king, always extinguish this burning fire, which has arisen from the grief for your sons, with the water of wisdom.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचनं व्यासस्यामिततेजसः ।मुहूर्तं समनुध्याय धृतराष्ट्रोऽभ्यभाषत ॥४५॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu vacanaṁ vyāsasyāmitatejasaḥ ,muhūrtaṁ samanudhyāya dhṛtarāṣṭro'bhyabhāṣata.
english: Having heard these words from Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable splendor, Dhṛtarāṣṭra reflected deeply for a moment and then spoke in reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 45. Having heard these words from Vyāsa, who possessed immeasurable splendor, Dhṛtarāṣṭra reflected deeply for a moment and then spoke in reply.

sanskrit: महता शोकजालेन प्रणुन्नोऽस्मि द्विजोत्तम ।नात्मानमवबुध्यामि मुह्यमानो मुहुर्मुहुः ॥४६॥
transliteration: mahatā śokajālena praṇunno'smi dvijottama ,nātmānamavabudhyāmi muhyamāno muhurmuhuḥ.
english: O best of brahmins (dvijottama), I am overwhelmed by a vast net of grief, and being repeatedly bewildered, I cannot comprehend myself (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 46. O best of brahmins (dvijottama), I am overwhelmed by a vast net of grief, and being repeatedly bewildered, I cannot comprehend myself (ātman).

sanskrit: इदं तु वचनं श्रुत्वा तव दैवनियोगजम् ।धारयिष्याम्यहं प्राणान्यतिष्ये च नशोचितुम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu vacanaṁ śrutvā tava daivaniyogajam ,dhārayiṣyāmyahaṁ prāṇānyatiṣye ca naśocitum.
english: However, having heard these words of yours, which arise from divine injunction, I shall sustain my life (prāṇa) and strive not to grieve.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 47. However, having heard these words of yours, which arise from divine injunction, I shall sustain my life (prāṇa) and strive not to grieve.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचनं व्यासः सत्यवतीसुतः ।धृतराष्ट्रस्य राजेन्द्र तत्रैवान्तरधीयत ॥४८॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu vacanaṁ vyāsaḥ satyavatīsutaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭrasya rājendra tatraivāntaradhīyata.
english: O king (Janamejaya), having heard this statement of Dhritarashtra, Vyasa, the son of Satyavati, disappeared right then and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 8 Verse 48. O king (Janamejaya), having heard this statement of Dhritarashtra, Vyasa, the son of Satyavati, disappeared right then and there.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।गते भगवति व्यासे धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ।किमचेष्टत विप्रर्षे तन्मे व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,gate bhagavati vyāse dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ ,kimaceṣṭata viprarṣe tanme vyākhyātumarhasi.
english: Janamejaya said: 'O Brahmin sage (viprarṣi), when the venerable Vyasa had departed, what did King Dhritarashtra do? Please explain that to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: 'O Brahmin sage (viprarṣi), when the venerable Vyasa had departed, what did King Dhritarashtra do? Please explain that to me.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा नरश्रेष्ठ चिरं ध्यात्वा त्वचेतनः ।संजयं योजयेत्युक्त्वा विदुरं प्रत्यभाषत ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā naraśreṣṭha ciraṁ dhyātvā tvacetanaḥ ,saṁjayaṁ yojayetyuktvā viduraṁ pratyabhāṣata.
english: Vaisampayana said: 'O best of men (naraśreṣṭha), having heard this and pondered for a long time, he (Dhritarashtra) became stunned. Then, saying "Summon Sanjaya!", he spoke to Vidura.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. Vaisampayana said: 'O best of men (naraśreṣṭha), having heard this and pondered for a long time, he (Dhritarashtra) became stunned. Then, saying "Summon Sanjaya!", he spoke to Vidura.'

sanskrit: क्षिप्रमानय गान्धारीं सर्वाश्च भरतस्त्रियः ।वधूं कुन्तीमुपादाय याश्चान्यास्तत्र योषितः ॥३॥
transliteration: kṣipramānaya gāndhārīṁ sarvāśca bharatastriyaḥ ,vadhūṁ kuntīmupādāya yāścānyāstatra yoṣitaḥ.
english: Quickly bring Gandhari, and all the women of the Bharata lineage (bharatastriyaḥ), along with my daughter-in-law Kunti, and any other women who are present there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. Quickly bring Gandhari, and all the women of the Bharata lineage (bharatastriyaḥ), along with my daughter-in-law Kunti, and any other women who are present there.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा स धर्मात्मा विदुरं धर्मवित्तमम् ।शोकविप्रहतज्ञानो यानमेवान्वपद्यत ॥४॥
transliteration: evamuktvā sa dharmātmā viduraṁ dharmavittamam ,śokaviprahatajñāno yānamevānvapadyata.
english: Having spoken thus to Vidura, the most excellent knower of natural law (dharma), he, the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) one, whose understanding had been overcome by grief, then boarded the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. Having spoken thus to Vidura, the most excellent knower of natural law (dharma), he, the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) one, whose understanding had been overcome by grief, then boarded the chariot.

sanskrit: गान्धारी चैव शोकार्ता भर्तुर्वचनचोदिता ।सह कुन्त्या यतो राजा सह स्त्रीभिरुपाद्रवत् ॥५॥
transliteration: gāndhārī caiva śokārtā bharturvacanacoditā ,saha kuntyā yato rājā saha strībhirupādravat.
english: And Gandhari, afflicted by grief and prompted by her husband's command, accompanied Kunti and the other women, and hurried to where the king was.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. And Gandhari, afflicted by grief and prompted by her husband's command, accompanied Kunti and the other women, and hurried to where the king was.

sanskrit: ताः समासाद्य राजानं भृशं शोकसमन्विताः ।आमन्त्र्यान्योन्यमीयुः स्म भृशमुच्चुक्रुशुस्ततः ॥६॥
transliteration: tāḥ samāsādya rājānaṁ bhṛśaṁ śokasamanvitāḥ ,āmantryānyonyamīyuḥ sma bhṛśamuccukruśustataḥ.
english: Having reached the king, they were intensely overcome by grief. After addressing one another, they then wailed loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. Having reached the king, they were intensely overcome by grief. After addressing one another, they then wailed loudly.

sanskrit: ताः समाश्वासयत्क्षत्ता ताभ्यश्चार्ततरः स्वयम् ।अश्रुकण्ठीः समारोप्य ततोऽसौ निर्ययौ पुरात् ॥७॥
transliteration: tāḥ samāśvāsayatkṣattā tābhyaścārtataraḥ svayam ,aśrukaṇṭhīḥ samāropya tato'sau niryayau purāt.
english: The charioteer (Vidura) consoled them, though he himself was more distressed than they were. After helping those with tearful voices to board (the vehicle), he then departed from the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. The charioteer (Vidura) consoled them, though he himself was more distressed than they were. After helping those with tearful voices to board (the vehicle), he then departed from the city.

sanskrit: ततः प्रणादः संजज्ञे सर्वेषु कुरुवेश्मसु ।आकुमारं पुरं सर्वमभवच्छोककर्शितम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ praṇādaḥ saṁjajñe sarveṣu kuruveśmasu ,ākumāraṁ puraṁ sarvamabhavacchokakarśitam.
english: Then, a great wailing arose in all the Kuru palaces. The entire city, including its children, became emaciated and distressed by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. Then, a great wailing arose in all the Kuru palaces. The entire city, including its children, became emaciated and distressed by grief.

sanskrit: अदृष्टपूर्वा या नार्यः पुरा देवगणैरपि ।पृथग्जनेन दृश्यन्त तास्तदा निहतेश्वराः ॥९॥
transliteration: adṛṣṭapūrvā yā nāryaḥ purā devagaṇairapi ,pṛthagjanena dṛśyanta tāstadā nihateśvarāḥ.
english: Those women, who previously had not been seen even by groups of gods, were then seen by common people, their husbands having been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. Those women, who previously had not been seen even by groups of gods, were then seen by common people, their husbands having been slain.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्य केशान्सुशुभान्भूषणान्यवमुच्य च ।एकवस्त्रधरा नार्यः परिपेतुरनाथवत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: prakīrya keśānsuśubhānbhūṣaṇānyavamucya ca ,ekavastradharā nāryaḥ paripeturanāthavat.
english: Scattering their very beautiful hair and casting off their ornaments, the women, wearing only a single garment, ran about like those without a protector or husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. Scattering their very beautiful hair and casting off their ornaments, the women, wearing only a single garment, ran about like those without a protector or husband.

sanskrit: श्वेतपर्वतरूपेभ्यो गृहेभ्यस्तास्त्वपाक्रमन् ।गुहाभ्य इव शैलानां पृषत्यो हतयूथपाः ॥११॥
transliteration: śvetaparvatarūpebhyo gṛhebhyastāstvapākraman ,guhābhya iva śailānāṁ pṛṣatyo hatayūthapāḥ.
english: They, whose herd leaders (husbands) had been slain, fled from their houses, which resembled white mountains, just as female deer (does) flee from the caves of mountains when their herd leaders are killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. They, whose herd leaders (husbands) had been slain, fled from their houses, which resembled white mountains, just as female deer (does) flee from the caves of mountains when their herd leaders are killed.

sanskrit: तान्युदीर्णानि नारीणां तदा वृन्दान्यनेकशः ।शोकार्तान्यद्रवन्राजन्किशोरीणामिवाङ्गने ॥१२॥
transliteration: tānyudīrṇāni nārīṇāṁ tadā vṛndānyanekaśaḥ ,śokārtānyadravanrājankiśorīṇāmivāṅgane.
english: O King, at that time, those numerous groups of women, agitated and overcome with grief, ran about like young girls in a courtyard.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. O King, at that time, those numerous groups of women, agitated and overcome with grief, ran about like young girls in a courtyard.

sanskrit: प्रगृह्य बाहून्क्रोशन्त्यः पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्पितॄनपि ।दर्शयन्तीव ता ह स्म युगान्ते लोकसंक्षयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: pragṛhya bāhūnkrośantyaḥ putrānbhrātṝnpitṝnapi ,darśayantīva tā ha sma yugānte lokasaṁkṣayam.
english: Crying out, clutching their arms, and calling out to their sons, brothers, and fathers, those women, indeed, seemed to display the world's destruction at the end of an age (yugānta).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. Crying out, clutching their arms, and calling out to their sons, brothers, and fathers, those women, indeed, seemed to display the world's destruction at the end of an age (yugānta).

sanskrit: विलपन्त्यो रुदन्त्यश्च धावमानास्ततस्ततः ।शोकेनाभ्याहतज्ञानाः कर्तव्यं न प्रजज्ञिरे ॥१४॥
transliteration: vilapantyo rudantyaśca dhāvamānāstatastataḥ ,śokenābhyāhatajñānāḥ kartavyaṁ na prajajñire.
english: Wailing, crying, and running hither and thither, their minds bewildered by grief, they did not know what their duty (kartavya) was.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. Wailing, crying, and running hither and thither, their minds bewildered by grief, they did not know what their duty (kartavya) was.

sanskrit: व्रीडां जग्मुः पुरा याः स्म सखीनामपि योषितः ।ता एकवस्त्रा निर्लज्जाः श्वश्रूणां पुरतोऽभवन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: vrīḍāṁ jagmuḥ purā yāḥ sma sakhīnāmapi yoṣitaḥ ,tā ekavastrā nirlajjāḥ śvaśrūṇāṁ purato'bhavan.
english: Those very women who, in the past, used to feel shame even before their female friends, now stood shameless and clad in a single garment in front of their mothers-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. Those very women who, in the past, used to feel shame even before their female friends, now stood shameless and clad in a single garment in front of their mothers-in-law.

sanskrit: परस्परं सुसूक्ष्मेषु शोकेष्वाश्वासयन्स्म याः ।ताः शोकविह्वला राजन्नुपैक्षन्त परस्परम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: parasparaṁ susūkṣmeṣu śokeṣvāśvāsayansma yāḥ ,tāḥ śokavihvalā rājannupaikṣanta parasparam.
english: O King, those women who used to comfort each other even in very subtle sorrows - they, overwhelmed by grief, neglected each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. O King, those women who used to comfort each other even in very subtle sorrows - they, overwhelmed by grief, neglected each other.

sanskrit: ताभिः परिवृतो राजा रुदतीभिः सहस्रशः ।निर्ययौ नगराद्दीनस्तूर्णमायोधनं प्रति ॥१७॥
transliteration: tābhiḥ parivṛto rājā rudatībhiḥ sahasraśaḥ ,niryayau nagarāddīnastūrṇamāyodhanaṁ prati.
english: The sorrowful king, surrounded by thousands of those weeping women, quickly departed from the city towards the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. The sorrowful king, surrounded by thousands of those weeping women, quickly departed from the city towards the battlefield.

sanskrit: शिल्पिनो वणिजो वैश्याः सर्वकर्मोपजीविनः ।ते पार्थिवं पुरस्कृत्य निर्ययुर्नगराद्बहिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: śilpino vaṇijo vaiśyāḥ sarvakarmopajīvinaḥ ,te pārthivaṁ puraskṛtya niryayurnagarādbahiḥ.
english: Artisans, merchants, Vaishyas, and those who subsist by all kinds of professions, they, making the king foremost, departed outside the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. Artisans, merchants, Vaishyas, and those who subsist by all kinds of professions, they, making the king foremost, departed outside the city.

sanskrit: तासां विक्रोशमानानामार्तानां कुरुसंक्षये ।प्रादुरासीन्महाञ्शब्दो व्यथयन्भुवनान्युत ॥१९॥
transliteration: tāsāṁ vikrośamānānāmārtānāṁ kurusaṁkṣaye ,prādurāsīnmahāñśabdo vyathayanbhuvanānyuta.
english: At the great destruction of the Kurus, a mighty sound arose from those distressed and crying women, indeed distressing the very worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. At the great destruction of the Kurus, a mighty sound arose from those distressed and crying women, indeed distressing the very worlds.

sanskrit: युगान्तकाले संप्राप्ते भूतानां दह्यतामिव ।अभावः स्यादयं प्राप्त इति भूतानि मेनिरे ॥२०॥
transliteration: yugāntakāle saṁprāpte bhūtānāṁ dahyatāmiva ,abhāvaḥ syādayaṁ prāpta iti bhūtāni menire.
english: When the time of the dissolution of the age (yuga) had arrived, and it seemed as though all beings were being consumed, the creatures believed, 'This destruction has surely come upon us.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. When the time of the dissolution of the age (yuga) had arrived, and it seemed as though all beings were being consumed, the creatures believed, 'This destruction has surely come upon us.'

sanskrit: भृशमुद्विग्नमनसस्ते पौराः कुरुसंक्षये ।प्राक्रोशन्त महाराज स्वनुरक्तास्तदा भृशम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: bhṛśamudvignamanasaste paurāḥ kurusaṁkṣaye ,prākrośanta mahārāja svanuraktāstadā bhṛśam.
english: O great king, those citizens, whose minds were exceedingly agitated by the destruction of the Kurus, and who were deeply loyal, then cried out loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. O great king, those citizens, whose minds were exceedingly agitated by the destruction of the Kurus, and who were deeply loyal, then cried out loudly.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।क्रोशमात्रं ततो गत्वा ददृशुस्तान्महारथान् ।शारद्वतं कृपं द्रौणिं कृतवर्माणमेव च ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,krośamātraṁ tato gatvā dadṛśustānmahārathān ,śāradvataṁ kṛpaṁ drauṇiṁ kṛtavarmāṇameva ca.
english: Vaiśampayana said: Then, having traveled just one krośa, they saw those great charioteers: Kṛpa (Śāradvata), Drauṇi, and also Kṛtavarmā.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Vaiśampayana said: Then, having traveled just one krośa, they saw those great charioteers: Kṛpa (Śāradvata), Drauṇi, and also Kṛtavarmā.

sanskrit: ते तु दृष्ट्वैव राजानं प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमीश्वरम् ।अश्रुकण्ठा विनिःश्वस्य रुदन्तमिदमब्रुवन् ॥२॥
transliteration: te tu dṛṣṭvaiva rājānaṁ prajñācakṣuṣamīśvaram ,aśrukaṇṭhā viniḥśvasya rudantamidamabruvan.
english: But they, having seen the king, the lord possessing the eye of wisdom (prajñācakṣus), sighed deeply. With throats choked with tears, they spoke this to him, who was himself weeping.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. But they, having seen the king, the lord possessing the eye of wisdom (prajñācakṣus), sighed deeply. With throats choked with tears, they spoke this to him, who was himself weeping.

sanskrit: पुत्रस्तव महाराज कृत्वा कर्म सुदुष्करम् ।गतः सानुचरो राजञ्शक्रलोकं महीपतिः ॥३॥
transliteration: putrastava mahārāja kṛtvā karma suduṣkaram ,gataḥ sānucaro rājañśakralokaṁ mahīpatiḥ.
english: O great king, your son, the ruler, having performed exceedingly difficult deeds (karma), has departed with his followers to the realm of Indra, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. O great king, your son, the ruler, having performed exceedingly difficult deeds (karma), has departed with his followers to the realm of Indra, O king.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनबलान्मुक्ता वयमेव त्रयो रथाः ।सर्वमन्यत्परिक्षीणं सैन्यं ते भरतर्षभ ॥४॥
transliteration: duryodhanabalānmuktā vayameva trayo rathāḥ ,sarvamanyatparikṣīṇaṁ sainyaṁ te bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bhāratas, only we three charioteers escaped from Duryodhana's army; all your other forces are completely destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. O best of Bhāratas, only we three charioteers escaped from Duryodhana's army; all your other forces are completely destroyed.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा राजानं कृपः शारद्वतस्तदा ।गान्धारीं पुत्रशोकार्तामिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥५॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā rājānaṁ kṛpaḥ śāradvatastadā ,gāndhārīṁ putraśokārtāmidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Having spoken thus to the king, Kṛpa, son of Śaradvan, then addressed Gāndhārī, who was afflicted by grief for her sons, with these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. Having spoken thus to the king, Kṛpa, son of Śaradvan, then addressed Gāndhārī, who was afflicted by grief for her sons, with these words.

sanskrit: अभीता युध्यमानास्ते घ्नन्तः शत्रुगणान्बहून् ।वीरकर्माणि कुर्वाणाः पुत्रास्ते निधनं गताः ॥६॥
transliteration: abhītā yudhyamānāste ghnantaḥ śatrugaṇānbahūn ,vīrakarmāṇi kurvāṇāḥ putrāste nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Your sons, fearless and fighting, killing many hordes of enemies, and performing heroic deeds, have met their death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. Your sons, fearless and fighting, killing many hordes of enemies, and performing heroic deeds, have met their death.

sanskrit: ध्रुवं संप्राप्य लोकांस्ते निर्मलाञ्शस्त्रनिर्जितान् ।भास्वरं देहमास्थाय विहरन्त्यमरा इव ॥७॥
transliteration: dhruvaṁ saṁprāpya lokāṁste nirmalāñśastranirjitān ,bhāsvaraṁ dehamāsthāya viharantyamarā iva.
english: They certainly attain those pure worlds, which are won by the sword, and, having assumed a radiant body, they enjoy themselves like immortals.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. They certainly attain those pure worlds, which are won by the sword, and, having assumed a radiant body, they enjoy themselves like immortals.

sanskrit: न हि कश्चिद्धि शूराणां युध्यमानः पराङ्मुखः ।शस्त्रेण निधनं प्राप्तो न च कश्चित्कृताञ्जलिः ॥८॥
transliteration: na hi kaściddhi śūrāṇāṁ yudhyamānaḥ parāṅmukhaḥ ,śastreṇa nidhanaṁ prāpto na ca kaścitkṛtāñjaliḥ.
english: Indeed, no hero fighting ever turns his back, and no one who meets death by a weapon is found with clasped hands (in supplication).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. Indeed, no hero fighting ever turns his back, and no one who meets death by a weapon is found with clasped hands (in supplication).

sanskrit: एतां तां क्षत्रियस्याहुः पुराणां परमां गतिम् ।शस्त्रेण निधनं संख्ये तान्न शोचितुमर्हसि ॥९॥
transliteration: etāṁ tāṁ kṣatriyasyāhuḥ purāṇāṁ paramāṁ gatim ,śastreṇa nidhanaṁ saṁkhye tānna śocitumarhasi.
english: This is what they declare to be the supreme, ancient destiny of a warrior (kṣatriya): death by a weapon in battle. Therefore, you ought not to grieve for them.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. This is what they declare to be the supreme, ancient destiny of a warrior (kṣatriya): death by a weapon in battle. Therefore, you ought not to grieve for them.

sanskrit: न चापि शत्रवस्तेषामृध्यन्ते राज्ञि पाण्डवाः ।शृणु यत्कृतमस्माभिरश्वत्थामपुरोगमैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: na cāpi śatravasteṣāmṛdhyante rājñi pāṇḍavāḥ ,śṛṇu yatkṛtamasmābhiraśvatthāmapurogamaiḥ.
english: O King, O Pāṇḍavas, their enemies also do not prosper. Listen to what has been accomplished by us, led by Aśvatthāman.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. O King, O Pāṇḍavas, their enemies also do not prosper. Listen to what has been accomplished by us, led by Aśvatthāman.

sanskrit: अधर्मेण हतं श्रुत्वा भीमसेनेन ते सुतम् ।सुप्तं शिबिरमाविश्य पाण्डूनां कदनं कृतम् ॥११॥
transliteration: adharmeṇa hataṁ śrutvā bhīmasenena te sutam ,suptaṁ śibiramāviśya pāṇḍūnāṁ kadanaṁ kṛtam.
english: Having heard that your son was killed unrighteously (adharmeṇa) by Bhimasena, I entered the sleeping camp and brought about the destruction of the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. Having heard that your son was killed unrighteously (adharmeṇa) by Bhimasena, I entered the sleeping camp and brought about the destruction of the Pandavas.

sanskrit: पाञ्चाला निहताः सर्वे धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमाः ।द्रुपदस्यात्मजाश्चैव द्रौपदेयाश्च पातिताः ॥१२॥
transliteration: pāñcālā nihatāḥ sarve dhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamāḥ ,drupadasyātmajāścaiva draupadeyāśca pātitāḥ.
english: All the Pañcālas, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, were slain; and indeed, the sons of Drupada and the sons of Draupadi (Draupadeyāḥ) were also killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. All the Pañcālas, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, were slain; and indeed, the sons of Drupada and the sons of Draupadi (Draupadeyāḥ) were also killed.

sanskrit: तथा विशसनं कृत्वा पुत्रशत्रुगणस्य ते ।प्राद्रवाम रणे स्थातुं न हि शक्यामहे त्रयः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tathā viśasanaṁ kṛtvā putraśatrugaṇasya te ,prādravāma raṇe sthātuṁ na hi śakyāmahe trayaḥ.
english: Thus, having slaughtered the host of your sons' enemies, we three fled, for indeed, we were not able to remain standing in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. Thus, having slaughtered the host of your sons' enemies, we three fled, for indeed, we were not able to remain standing in battle.

sanskrit: ते हि शूरा महेष्वासाः क्षिप्रमेष्यन्ति पाण्डवाः ।अमर्षवशमापन्ना वैरं प्रतिजिहीर्षवः ॥१४॥
transliteration: te hi śūrā maheṣvāsāḥ kṣiprameṣyanti pāṇḍavāḥ ,amarṣavaśamāpannā vairaṁ pratijihīrṣavaḥ.
english: Indeed, those brave and great archer Pandavas will quickly come, seized by indignation (amarṣa) and desirous of retaliation (vaira).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 14. Indeed, those brave and great archer Pandavas will quickly come, seized by indignation (amarṣa) and desirous of retaliation (vaira).

sanskrit: निहतानात्मजाञ्श्रुत्वा प्रमत्तान्पुरुषर्षभाः ।निनीषन्तः पदं शूराः क्षिप्रमेव यशस्विनि ॥१५॥
transliteration: nihatānātmajāñśrutvā pramattānpuruṣarṣabhāḥ ,ninīṣantaḥ padaṁ śūrāḥ kṣiprameva yaśasvini.
english: O glorious one (yaśasvini), we heroes (śūrāḥ), best among men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), having heard that the sons were slain while negligent, quickly wish to attain our objective.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 15. O glorious one (yaśasvini), we heroes (śūrāḥ), best among men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), having heard that the sons were slain while negligent, quickly wish to attain our objective.

sanskrit: पाण्डूनां किल्बिषं कृत्वा संस्थातुं नोत्सहामहे ।अनुजानीहि नो राज्ञि मा च शोके मनः कृथाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: pāṇḍūnāṁ kilbiṣaṁ kṛtvā saṁsthātuṁ notsahāmahe ,anujānīhi no rājñi mā ca śoke manaḥ kṛthāḥ.
english: Having committed an offense against the Pāṇḍavas, we are unable to remain. O Queen, permit us, and do not set your mind on grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 16. Having committed an offense against the Pāṇḍavas, we are unable to remain. O Queen, permit us, and do not set your mind on grief.

sanskrit: राजंस्त्वमनुजानीहि धैर्यमातिष्ठ चोत्तमम् ।निष्ठान्तं पश्य चापि त्वं क्षत्रधर्मं च केवलम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: rājaṁstvamanujānīhi dhairyamātiṣṭha cottamam ,niṣṭhāntaṁ paśya cāpi tvaṁ kṣatradharmaṁ ca kevalam.
english: O King, you permit [us] and adopt the best fortitude. And you also see the conclusion and the exclusive intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 17. O King, you permit [us] and adopt the best fortitude. And you also see the conclusion and the exclusive intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्त्वा राजानं कृत्वा चाभिप्रदक्षिणम् ।कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च द्रोणपुत्रश्च भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: ityevamuktvā rājānaṁ kṛtvā cābhipradakṣiṇam ,kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca droṇaputraśca bhārata.
english: Having thus spoken to the king and having performed respectful circumambulation, Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) [did so], O descendant of Bharata.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 18. Having thus spoken to the king and having performed respectful circumambulation, Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā) [did so], O descendant of Bharata.

sanskrit: अवेक्षमाणा राजानं धृतराष्ट्रं मनीषिणम् ।गङ्गामनु महात्मानस्तूर्णमश्वानचोदयन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: avekṣamāṇā rājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ manīṣiṇam ,gaṅgāmanu mahātmānastūrṇamaśvānacodayan.
english: The great-souled ones, observing the wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, quickly urged their horses along the Gaṅgā river.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 19. The great-souled ones, observing the wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, quickly urged their horses along the Gaṅgā river.

sanskrit: अपक्रम्य तु ते राजन्सर्व एव महारथाः ।आमन्त्र्यान्योन्यमुद्विग्नास्त्रिधा ते प्रययुस्ततः ॥२०॥
transliteration: apakramya tu te rājansarva eva mahārathāḥ ,āmantryānyonyamudvignāstridhā te prayayustataḥ.
english: O King, all those great warriors, agitated, departed and, after bidding each other farewell, then went their separate ways, splitting into three groups.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 20. O King, all those great warriors, agitated, departed and, after bidding each other farewell, then went their separate ways, splitting into three groups.

sanskrit: जगाम हास्तिनपुरं कृपः शारद्वतस्तदा ।स्वमेव राष्ट्रं हार्दिक्यो द्रौणिर्व्यासाश्रमं ययौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: jagāma hāstinapuraṁ kṛpaḥ śāradvatastadā ,svameva rāṣṭraṁ hārdikyo drauṇirvyāsāśramaṁ yayau.
english: Then Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat, went to Hastinapura. Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman) returned to his own kingdom, and Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) went to Vyāsa's hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 21. Then Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat, went to Hastinapura. Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman) returned to his own kingdom, and Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman) went to Vyāsa's hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: एवं ते प्रययुर्वीरा वीक्षमाणाः परस्परम् ।भयार्ताः पाण्डुपुत्राणामागस्कृत्वा महात्मनाम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: evaṁ te prayayurvīrā vīkṣamāṇāḥ parasparam ,bhayārtāḥ pāṇḍuputrāṇāmāgaskṛtvā mahātmanām.
english: Thus, those heroes departed, looking at each other, afflicted by fear because they had committed a great offense (āgas) against the noble sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 22. Thus, those heroes departed, looking at each other, afflicted by fear because they had committed a great offense (āgas) against the noble sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: समेत्य वीरा राजानं तदा त्वनुदिते रवौ ।विप्रजग्मुर्महाराज यथेच्छकमरिंदमाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: sametya vīrā rājānaṁ tadā tvanudite ravau ,viprajagmurmahārāja yathecchakamariṁdamāḥ.
english: O great king, O subduer of enemies, then, even before the sun had risen, those heroes, having met the king, dispersed as they wished.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 10 Verse 23. O great king, O subduer of enemies, then, even before the sun had risen, those heroes, having met the king, dispersed as they wished.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।हतेषु सर्वसैन्येषु धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।शुश्रुवे पितरं वृद्धं निर्यातं गजसाह्वयात् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,hateṣu sarvasainyeṣu dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śuśruve pitaraṁ vṛddhaṁ niryātaṁ gajasāhvayāt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When all the armies had been destroyed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king who upholds natural law (dharma), heard that his aged father had departed from Hastinapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When all the armies had been destroyed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king who upholds natural law (dharma), heard that his aged father had departed from Hastinapura.

sanskrit: सोऽभ्ययात्पुत्रशोकार्तः पुत्रशोकपरिप्लुतम् ।शोचमानो महाराज भ्रातृभिः सहितस्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: so'bhyayātputraśokārtaḥ putraśokapariplutam ,śocamāno mahārāja bhrātṛbhiḥ sahitastadā.
english: O great king, then, himself lamenting and distressed by the sorrow of his sons, he (Yudhiṣṭhira) approached his father, who was overwhelmed by the grief of his sons, accompanied by his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 2. O great king, then, himself lamenting and distressed by the sorrow of his sons, he (Yudhiṣṭhira) approached his father, who was overwhelmed by the grief of his sons, accompanied by his brothers.

sanskrit: अन्वीयमानो वीरेण दाशार्हेण महात्मना ।युयुधानेन च तथा तथैव च युयुत्सुना ॥३॥
transliteration: anvīyamāno vīreṇa dāśārheṇa mahātmanā ,yuyudhānena ca tathā tathaiva ca yuyutsunā.
english: He (Yudhiṣṭhira) was being followed by the heroic, great-souled Dāśārha, Yuyudhāna, and similarly by Yuyutsu.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 3. He (Yudhiṣṭhira) was being followed by the heroic, great-souled Dāśārha, Yuyudhāna, and similarly by Yuyutsu.

sanskrit: तमन्वगात्सुदुःखार्ता द्रौपदी शोककर्शिता ।सह पाञ्चालयोषिद्भिर्यास्तत्रासन्समागताः ॥४॥
transliteration: tamanvagātsuduḥkhārtā draupadī śokakarśitā ,saha pāñcālayoṣidbhiryāstatrāsansamāgatāḥ.
english: Greatly distressed and emaciated by grief, Draupadi followed him, accompanied by the women of Pañcāla who had gathered there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 4. Greatly distressed and emaciated by grief, Draupadi followed him, accompanied by the women of Pañcāla who had gathered there.

sanskrit: स गङ्गामनु वृन्दानि स्त्रीणां भरतसत्तम ।कुररीणामिवार्तानां क्रोशन्तीनां ददर्श ह ॥५॥
transliteration: sa gaṅgāmanu vṛndāni strīṇāṁ bharatasattama ,kurarīṇāmivārtānāṁ krośantīnāṁ dadarśa ha.
english: O best of the Bharatas, along the Ganga, he saw indeed flocks of women, distressed and crying out like curlews.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. O best of the Bharatas, along the Ganga, he saw indeed flocks of women, distressed and crying out like curlews.

sanskrit: ताभिः परिवृतो राजा रुदतीभिः सहस्रशः ।ऊर्ध्वबाहुभिरार्ताभिर्ब्रुवतीभिः प्रियाप्रिये ॥६॥
transliteration: tābhiḥ parivṛto rājā rudatībhiḥ sahasraśaḥ ,ūrdhvabāhubhirārtābhirbruvatībhiḥ priyāpriye.
english: The king, surrounded by thousands of those crying, distressed women, their arms uplifted, speaking both pleasant and unpleasant words.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. The king, surrounded by thousands of those crying, distressed women, their arms uplifted, speaking both pleasant and unpleasant words.

sanskrit: क्व नु धर्मज्ञता राज्ञः क्व नु साद्यानृशंसता ।यदावधीत्पितॄन्भ्रातॄन्गुरून्पुत्रान्सखीनपि ॥७॥
transliteration: kva nu dharmajñatā rājñaḥ kva nu sādyānṛśaṁsatā ,yadāvadhītpitṝnbhrātṝngurūnputrānsakhīnapi.
english: What has become of the king's understanding of natural law (dharma)? Where is that compassion today, since he killed fathers, brothers, preceptors (gurus), sons, and even friends?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. What has become of the king's understanding of natural law (dharma)? Where is that compassion today, since he killed fathers, brothers, preceptors (gurus), sons, and even friends?

sanskrit: घातयित्वा कथं द्रोणं भीष्मं चापि पितामहम् ।मनस्तेऽभून्महाबाहो हत्वा चापि जयद्रथम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ghātayitvā kathaṁ droṇaṁ bhīṣmaṁ cāpi pitāmaham ,manaste'bhūnmahābāho hatvā cāpi jayadratham.
english: O mighty-armed one, how could your mind be (unburdened) after having caused Drona and even grandfather Bhishma to be killed, and also after having killed Jayadratha?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. O mighty-armed one, how could your mind be (unburdened) after having caused Drona and even grandfather Bhishma to be killed, and also after having killed Jayadratha?

sanskrit: किं नु राज्येन ते कार्यं पितॄन्भ्रातॄनपश्यतः ।अभिमन्युं च दुर्धर्षं द्रौपदेयांश्च भारत ॥९॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu rājyena te kāryaṁ pitṝnbhrātṝnapaśyataḥ ,abhimanyuṁ ca durdharṣaṁ draupadeyāṁśca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, what use is a kingdom to you, who will not see your fathers (elders), brothers, the formidable Abhimanyu, and Draupadi's sons?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. O descendant of Bharata, what use is a kingdom to you, who will not see your fathers (elders), brothers, the formidable Abhimanyu, and Draupadi's sons?

sanskrit: अतीत्य ता महाबाहुः क्रोशन्तीः कुररीरिव ।ववन्दे पितरं ज्येष्ठं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥१०॥
transliteration: atītya tā mahābāhuḥ krośantīḥ kurarīriva ,vavande pitaraṁ jyeṣṭhaṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Having passed by those wailing women, who cried out like ospreys, the mighty-armed Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), bowed down to his elder father.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. Having passed by those wailing women, who cried out like ospreys, the mighty-armed Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma), bowed down to his elder father.

sanskrit: ततोऽभिवाद्य पितरं धर्मेणामित्रकर्शनाः ।न्यवेदयन्त नामानि पाण्डवास्तेऽपि सर्वशः ॥११॥
transliteration: tato'bhivādya pitaraṁ dharmeṇāmitrakarśanāḥ ,nyavedayanta nāmāni pāṇḍavāste'pi sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, having saluted their father according to custom (dharma), those Pandavas, the subduers of enemies, also announced their names, all of them in due order.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. Then, having saluted their father according to custom (dharma), those Pandavas, the subduers of enemies, also announced their names, all of them in due order.

sanskrit: तमात्मजान्तकरणं पिता पुत्रवधार्दितः ।अप्रीयमाणः शोकार्तः पाण्डवं परिषस्वजे ॥१२॥
transliteration: tamātmajāntakaraṇaṁ pitā putravadhārditaḥ ,aprīyamāṇaḥ śokārtaḥ pāṇḍavaṁ pariṣasvaje.
english: The father (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), tormented by the slaughter of his sons and distressed by grief, yet feeling no joy, embraced that Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), the slayer of his own sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. The father (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), tormented by the slaughter of his sons and distressed by grief, yet feeling no joy, embraced that Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), the slayer of his own sons.

sanskrit: धर्मराजं परिष्वज्य सान्त्वयित्वा च भारत ।दुष्टात्मा भीममन्वैच्छद्दिधक्षुरिव पावकः ॥१३॥
transliteration: dharmarājaṁ pariṣvajya sāntvayitvā ca bhārata ,duṣṭātmā bhīmamanvaicchaddidhakṣuriva pāvakaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, having embraced Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) and offered him solace, the wicked-minded one (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) then sought Bhīma, like a fire eager to consume.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 13. O Bhārata, having embraced Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) and offered him solace, the wicked-minded one (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) then sought Bhīma, like a fire eager to consume.

sanskrit: स कोपपावकस्तस्य शोकवायुसमीरितः ।भीमसेनमयं दावं दिधक्षुरिव दृश्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa kopapāvakastasya śokavāyusamīritaḥ ,bhīmasenamayaṁ dāvaṁ didhakṣuriva dṛśyate.
english: His (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) fire of wrath, fanned by the wind of grief, appears like one eager to consume Bhīmasena, who is a veritable forest fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 14. His (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) fire of wrath, fanned by the wind of grief, appears like one eager to consume Bhīmasena, who is a veritable forest fire.

sanskrit: तस्य संकल्पमाज्ञाय भीमं प्रत्यशुभं हरिः ।भीममाक्षिप्य पाणिभ्यां प्रददौ भीममायसम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasya saṁkalpamājñāya bhīmaṁ pratyaśubhaṁ hariḥ ,bhīmamākṣipya pāṇibhyāṁ pradadau bhīmamāyasam.
english: Understanding his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) malevolent intention towards Bhīma, Hari (Kṛṣṇa) swiftly pulled Bhīma aside with his (Kṛṣṇa's) own hands. Then he offered (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) an iron image of Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 15. Understanding his (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) malevolent intention towards Bhīma, Hari (Kṛṣṇa) swiftly pulled Bhīma aside with his (Kṛṣṇa's) own hands. Then he offered (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) an iron image of Bhīma.

sanskrit: प्रागेव तु महाबुद्धिर्बुद्ध्वा तस्येङ्गितं हरिः ।संविधानं महाप्राज्ञस्तत्र चक्रे जनार्दनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: prāgeva tu mahābuddhirbuddhvā tasyeṅgitaṁ hariḥ ,saṁvidhānaṁ mahāprājñastatra cakre janārdanaḥ.
english: Beforehand, the greatly intelligent Hari, Janārdana, being supremely wise and understanding his gesture, made an arrangement there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 16. Beforehand, the greatly intelligent Hari, Janārdana, being supremely wise and understanding his gesture, made an arrangement there.

sanskrit: तं तु गृह्यैव पाणिभ्यां भीमसेनमयस्मयम् ।बभञ्ज बलवान्राजा मन्यमानो वृकोदरम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: taṁ tu gṛhyaiva pāṇibhyāṁ bhīmasenamayasmayam ,babhañja balavānrājā manyamāno vṛkodaram.
english: But the powerful king, taking hold with both hands of that iron image of Bhīmasena, broke it, thinking it to be Vṛkodara himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 17. But the powerful king, taking hold with both hands of that iron image of Bhīmasena, broke it, thinking it to be Vṛkodara himself.

sanskrit: नागायुतबलप्राणः स राजा भीममायसम् ।भङ्क्त्वा विमथितोरस्कः सुस्राव रुधिरं मुखात् ॥१८॥
transliteration: nāgāyutabalaprāṇaḥ sa rājā bhīmamāyasam ,bhaṅktvā vimathitoraskaḥ susrāva rudhiraṁ mukhāt.
english: That king, whose vital force was equal to the strength of ten thousand elephants, having shattered the iron (image of) Bhīma, and with his chest churned up, profusely discharged blood from his mouth.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 18. That king, whose vital force was equal to the strength of ten thousand elephants, having shattered the iron (image of) Bhīma, and with his chest churned up, profusely discharged blood from his mouth.

sanskrit: ततः पपात मेदिन्यां तथैव रुधिरोक्षितः ।प्रपुष्पिताग्रशिखरः पारिजात इव द्रुमः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ papāta medinyāṁ tathaiva rudhirokṣitaḥ ,prapuṣpitāgraśikharaḥ pārijāta iva drumaḥ.
english: Then, drenched in blood, he fell to the earth, just like a Pārijāta tree with its topmost branches full of blossoms.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 19. Then, drenched in blood, he fell to the earth, just like a Pārijāta tree with its topmost branches full of blossoms.

sanskrit: पर्यगृह्णत तं विद्वान्सूतो गावल्गणिस्तदा ।मैवमित्यब्रवीच्चैनं शमयन्सान्त्वयन्निव ॥२०॥
transliteration: paryagṛhṇata taṁ vidvānsūto gāvalgaṇistadā ,maivamityabravīccainaṁ śamayansāntvayanniva.
english: Then, the wise charioteer (sūta), Sanjaya (Gāvalgaṇi), held him, and, as if pacifying and comforting him, said, 'Not so!'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 20. Then, the wise charioteer (sūta), Sanjaya (Gāvalgaṇi), held him, and, as if pacifying and comforting him, said, 'Not so!'

sanskrit: स तु कोपं समुत्सृज्य गतमन्युर्महामनाः ।हा हा भीमेति चुक्रोश भूयः शोकसमन्वितः ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa tu kopaṁ samutsṛjya gatamanyurmahāmanāḥ ,hā hā bhīmeti cukrośa bhūyaḥ śokasamanvitaḥ.
english: But he, having abandoned his anger and with his wrath gone, that great-souled one, cried out, 'Alas, alas, Bhīma!' again, overcome with sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 21. But he, having abandoned his anger and with his wrath gone, that great-souled one, cried out, 'Alas, alas, Bhīma!' again, overcome with sorrow.

sanskrit: तं विदित्वा गतक्रोधं भीमसेनवधार्दितम् ।वासुदेवो वरः पुंसामिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: taṁ viditvā gatakrodhaṁ bhīmasenavadhārditam ,vāsudevo varaḥ puṁsāmidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Having known him to be free from anger and tormented by the imagined slaying of Bhīmasena, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the best among men (puruṣa), spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 22. Having known him to be free from anger and tormented by the imagined slaying of Bhīmasena, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the best among men (puruṣa), spoke these words.

sanskrit: मा शुचो धृतराष्ट्र त्वं नैष भीमस्त्वया हतः ।आयसी प्रतिमा ह्येषा त्वया राजन्निपातिता ॥२३॥
transliteration: mā śuco dhṛtarāṣṭra tvaṁ naiṣa bhīmastvayā hataḥ ,āyasī pratimā hyeṣā tvayā rājannipātitā.
english: Do not grieve, Dhṛtarāṣṭra! This Bhīma was not killed by you. Indeed, O king, it was an iron statue that was struck down by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 23. Do not grieve, Dhṛtarāṣṭra! This Bhīma was not killed by you. Indeed, O king, it was an iron statue that was struck down by you.

sanskrit: त्वां क्रोधवशमापन्नं विदित्वा भरतर्षभ ।मयापकृष्टः कौन्तेयो मृत्योर्दंष्ट्रान्तरं गतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tvāṁ krodhavaśamāpannaṁ viditvā bharatarṣabha ,mayāpakṛṣṭaḥ kaunteyo mṛtyordaṁṣṭrāntaraṁ gataḥ.
english: O bull among the Bharatas, knowing that you had fallen into the grip of anger, I drew the son of Kunti away from the jaws of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 24. O bull among the Bharatas, knowing that you had fallen into the grip of anger, I drew the son of Kunti away from the jaws of death.

sanskrit: न हि ते राजशार्दूल बले तुल्योऽस्ति कश्चन ।कः सहेत महाबाहो बाह्वोर्निग्रहणं नरः ॥२५॥
transliteration: na hi te rājaśārdūla bale tulyo'sti kaścana ,kaḥ saheta mahābāho bāhvornigrahaṇaṁ naraḥ.
english: Indeed, O tiger among kings, no one is equal to your strength. O mighty-armed one, what man could endure the grip of your arms?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 25. Indeed, O tiger among kings, no one is equal to your strength. O mighty-armed one, what man could endure the grip of your arms?

sanskrit: यथान्तकमनुप्राप्य जीवन्कश्चिन्न मुच्यते ।एवं बाह्वन्तरं प्राप्य तव जीवेन्न कश्चन ॥२६॥
transliteration: yathāntakamanuprāpya jīvankaścinna mucyate ,evaṁ bāhvantaraṁ prāpya tava jīvenna kaścana.
english: Just as no one, having reached Death, is released alive; so too, no one, having reached the embrace of your arms, could live.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 26. Just as no one, having reached Death, is released alive; so too, no one, having reached the embrace of your arms, could live.

sanskrit: तस्मात्पुत्रेण या सा ते प्रतिमा कारितायसी ।भीमस्य सेयं कौरव्य तवैवोपहृता मया ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasmātputreṇa yā sā te pratimā kāritāyasī ,bhīmasya seyaṁ kauravya tavaivopahṛtā mayā.
english: Therefore, O Kaurava, that iron statue of Bhīma, which was commissioned by your son (Duryodhana), has now been presented to you by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 27. Therefore, O Kaurava, that iron statue of Bhīma, which was commissioned by your son (Duryodhana), has now been presented to you by me.

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकाभिसंतापाद्धर्मादपहृतं मनः ।तव राजेन्द्र तेन त्वं भीमसेनं जिघांससि ॥२८॥
transliteration: putraśokābhisaṁtāpāddharmādapahṛtaṁ manaḥ ,tava rājendra tena tvaṁ bhīmasenaṁ jighāṁsasi.
english: O King of kings, your mind has been stolen away from righteousness (dharma) by the intense agony of grieving for your son. Therefore, you desire to kill Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 28. O King of kings, your mind has been stolen away from righteousness (dharma) by the intense agony of grieving for your son. Therefore, you desire to kill Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: न च ते तत्क्षमं राजन्हन्यास्त्वं यद्वृकोदरम् ।न हि पुत्रा महाराज जीवेयुस्ते कथंचन ॥२९॥
transliteration: na ca te tatkṣamaṁ rājanhanyāstvaṁ yadvṛkodaram ,na hi putrā mahārāja jīveyuste kathaṁcana.
english: And that is not fitting for you, O King, that you would kill Vṛkodara (Bhīma). For truly, O Great King, your sons would certainly not live (if you did that).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 29. And that is not fitting for you, O King, that you would kill Vṛkodara (Bhīma). For truly, O Great King, your sons would certainly not live (if you did that).

sanskrit: तस्माद्यत्कृतमस्माभिर्मन्यमानैः क्षमं प्रति ।अनुमन्यस्व तत्सर्वं मा च शोके मनः कृथाः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tasmādyatkṛtamasmābhirmanyamānaiḥ kṣamaṁ prati ,anumanyasva tatsarvaṁ mā ca śoke manaḥ kṛthāḥ.
english: Therefore, whatever was done by us, considering it appropriate, you should approve all of that, and do not let your mind dwell in sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 11 Verse 30. Therefore, whatever was done by us, considering it appropriate, you should approve all of that, and do not let your mind dwell in sorrow.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तत एनमुपातिष्ठञ्शौचार्थं परिचारकाः ।कृतशौचं पुनश्चैनं प्रोवाच मधुसूदनः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tata enamupātiṣṭhañśaucārthaṁ paricārakāḥ ,kṛtaśaucaṁ punaścainaṁ provāca madhusūdanaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then his attendants approached him for the sake of purification. And after he had purified himself, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) spoke to him again.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then his attendants approached him for the sake of purification. And after he had purified himself, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) spoke to him again.
