sanskrit: राजन्नधीता वेदास्ते शास्त्राणि विविधानि च ।श्रुतानि च पुराणानि राजधर्माश्च केवलाः ॥२॥
transliteration: rājannadhītā vedāste śāstrāṇi vividhāni ca ,śrutāni ca purāṇāni rājadharmāśca kevalāḥ.
english: O King, you have studied the Vedas and various scriptures; you have also heard the Purāṇas and all the royal duties (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. O King, you have studied the Vedas and various scriptures; you have also heard the Purāṇas and all the royal duties (dharma).

sanskrit: एवं विद्वान्महाप्राज्ञ नाकार्षीर्वचनं तदा ।पाण्डवानधिकाञ्जानन्बले शौर्ये च कौरव ॥३॥
transliteration: evaṁ vidvānmahāprājña nākārṣīrvacanaṁ tadā ,pāṇḍavānadhikāñjānanbale śaurye ca kaurava.
english: Even though you are such a learned and greatly wise person, O scion of Kuru, you did not heed that advice at that time, even while knowing that the Pāṇḍavas were superior in strength and valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. Even though you are such a learned and greatly wise person, O scion of Kuru, you did not heed that advice at that time, even while knowing that the Pāṇḍavas were superior in strength and valor.

sanskrit: राजा हि यः स्थिरप्रज्ञः स्वयं दोषानवेक्षते ।देशकालविभागं च परं श्रेयः स विन्दति ॥४॥
transliteration: rājā hi yaḥ sthiraprajñaḥ svayaṁ doṣānavekṣate ,deśakālavibhāgaṁ ca paraṁ śreyaḥ sa vindati.
english: Indeed, the king who is firm in his wisdom and himself discerns faults and the appropriate considerations of time and place, he attains the highest well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. Indeed, the king who is firm in his wisdom and himself discerns faults and the appropriate considerations of time and place, he attains the highest well-being.

sanskrit: उच्यमानं च यः श्रेयो गृह्णीते नो हिताहिते ।आपदं समनुप्राप्य स शोचत्यनये स्थितः ॥५॥
transliteration: ucyamānaṁ ca yaḥ śreyo gṛhṇīte no hitāhite ,āpadaṁ samanuprāpya sa śocatyanaye sthitaḥ.
english: And he who does not accept the beneficial advice being offered, failing to discern what is advantageous and what is detrimental (hita-ahita), that person, having fallen into calamity, laments, remaining in a wrongful state.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. And he who does not accept the beneficial advice being offered, failing to discern what is advantageous and what is detrimental (hita-ahita), that person, having fallen into calamity, laments, remaining in a wrongful state.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्यवृत्तमात्मानं समवेक्षस्व भारत ।राजंस्त्वं ह्यविधेयात्मा दुर्योधनवशे स्थितः ॥६॥
transliteration: tato'nyavṛttamātmānaṁ samavekṣasva bhārata ,rājaṁstvaṁ hyavidheyātmā duryodhanavaśe sthitaḥ.
english: Therefore, O Bhārata, examine your self (ātman), which has adopted a different course of action. For you, O King, have an uncontrollable nature and are indeed under the sway of Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. Therefore, O Bhārata, examine your self (ātman), which has adopted a different course of action. For you, O King, have an uncontrollable nature and are indeed under the sway of Duryodhana.

sanskrit: आत्मापराधादायस्तस्तत्किं भीमं जिघांससि ।तस्मात्संयच्छ कोपं त्वं स्वमनुस्मृत्य दुष्कृतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: ātmāparādhādāyastastatkiṁ bhīmaṁ jighāṁsasi ,tasmātsaṁyaccha kopaṁ tvaṁ svamanusmṛtya duṣkṛtam.
english: Distressed by your own offense, why do you wish to kill Bhīma? Therefore, remembering your own misdeed, you should restrain your anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. Distressed by your own offense, why do you wish to kill Bhīma? Therefore, remembering your own misdeed, you should restrain your anger.

sanskrit: यस्तु तां स्पर्धया क्षुद्रः पाञ्चालीमानयत्सभाम् ।स हतो भीमसेनेन वैरं प्रतिचिकीर्षता ॥८॥
transliteration: yastu tāṁ spardhayā kṣudraḥ pāñcālīmānayatsabhām ,sa hato bhīmasenena vairaṁ praticikīrṣatā.
english: But that wretched person who, out of rivalry, brought Pāñcālī (Draupadī) into the assembly, has been killed by Bhīmasena, who was intent on seeking vengeance.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. But that wretched person who, out of rivalry, brought Pāñcālī (Draupadī) into the assembly, has been killed by Bhīmasena, who was intent on seeking vengeance.

sanskrit: आत्मनोऽतिक्रमं पश्य पुत्रस्य च दुरात्मनः ।यदनागसि पाण्डूनां परित्यागः परंतप ॥९॥
transliteration: ātmano'tikramaṁ paśya putrasya ca durātmanaḥ ,yadanāgasi pāṇḍūnāṁ parityāgaḥ paraṁtapa.
english: Observe your own transgression and that of your wicked son, O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), for it was the abandonment of the innocent Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. Observe your own transgression and that of your wicked son, O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), for it was the abandonment of the innocent Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स कृष्णेन सर्वं सत्यं जनाधिप ।उवाच देवकीपुत्रं धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa kṛṣṇena sarvaṁ satyaṁ janādhipa ,uvāca devakīputraṁ dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ.
english: O lord of men, King Dhritarashtra, having been addressed thus by Krishna, then spoke the complete truth to Devaki's son (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. O lord of men, King Dhritarashtra, having been addressed thus by Krishna, then spoke the complete truth to Devaki's son (Krishna).

sanskrit: एवमेतन्महाबाहो यथा वदसि माधव ।पुत्रस्नेहस्तु धर्मात्मन्धैर्यान्मां समचालयत् ॥११॥
transliteration: evametanmahābāho yathā vadasi mādhava ,putrasnehastu dharmātmandhairyānmāṁ samacālayat.
english: O mighty-armed Mādhava, what you say is indeed true. But, O virtuous one whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness, my affection for my sons caused me to deviate from my firmness.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. O mighty-armed Mādhava, what you say is indeed true. But, O virtuous one whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness, my affection for my sons caused me to deviate from my firmness.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या तु पुरुषव्याघ्रो बलवान्सत्यविक्रमः ।त्वद्गुप्तो नागमत्कृष्ण भीमो बाह्वन्तरं मम ॥१२॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā tu puruṣavyāghro balavānsatyavikramaḥ ,tvadgupto nāgamatkṛṣṇa bhīmo bāhvantaraṁ mama.
english: But fortunately, O Krishna, that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the powerful Bhīma, whose valor is true, protected by you, did not come into my embrace.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. But fortunately, O Krishna, that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), the powerful Bhīma, whose valor is true, protected by you, did not come into my embrace.

sanskrit: इदानीं त्वहमेकाग्रो गतमन्युर्गतज्वरः ।मध्यमं पाण्डवं वीरं स्प्रष्टुमिच्छामि केशव ॥१३॥
transliteration: idānīṁ tvahamekāgro gatamanyurgatajvaraḥ ,madhyamaṁ pāṇḍavaṁ vīraṁ spraṣṭumicchāmi keśava.
english: Now, however, O Keśava, I am single-minded, free from anger, and free from distress. I desire to embrace the heroic middle Pāṇḍava (Bhīma).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. Now, however, O Keśava, I am single-minded, free from anger, and free from distress. I desire to embrace the heroic middle Pāṇḍava (Bhīma).

sanskrit: हतेषु पार्थिवेन्द्रेषु पुत्रेषु निहतेषु च ।पाण्डुपुत्रेषु मे शर्म प्रीतिश्चाप्यवतिष्ठते ॥१४॥
transliteration: hateṣu pārthivendreṣu putreṣu nihateṣu ca ,pāṇḍuputreṣu me śarma prītiścāpyavatiṣṭhate.
english: Even though my own sons, the chief among kings, have been killed and slain, still, happiness and affection (prīti) for me remain with the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. Even though my own sons, the chief among kings, have been killed and slain, still, happiness and affection (prīti) for me remain with the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: ततः स भीमं च धनंजयं च माद्र्याश्च पुत्रौ पुरुषप्रवीरौ ।पस्पर्श गात्रैः प्ररुदन्सुगात्रानाश्वास्य कल्याणमुवाच चैनान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa bhīmaṁ ca dhanaṁjayaṁ ca; mādryāśca putrau puruṣapravīrau ,pasparśa gātraiḥ prarudansugātrā;nāśvāsya kalyāṇamuvāca cainān.
english: Then, profusely weeping, he (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) touched with his body Bhīma, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), and the two sons of Mādrī, those foremost among men, who had beautiful bodies. After consoling them, he spoke words of well-being to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 12 Verse 15. Then, profusely weeping, he (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) touched with his body Bhīma, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), and the two sons of Mādrī, those foremost among men, who had beautiful bodies. After consoling them, he spoke words of well-being to them.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।धृतराष्ट्राभ्यनुज्ञातास्ततस्ते कुरुपुंगवाः ।अभ्ययुर्भ्रातरः सर्वे गान्धारीं सहकेशवाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dhṛtarāṣṭrābhyanujñātāstataste kurupuṁgavāḥ ,abhyayurbhrātaraḥ sarve gāndhārīṁ sahakeśavāḥ.
english: Vaiśampaayana said: Then, all those brothers, the best among the Kurus, having been granted permission by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, approached Gāndhārī along with Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Vaiśampaayana said: Then, all those brothers, the best among the Kurus, having been granted permission by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, approached Gāndhārī along with Keśava (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: ततो ज्ञात्वा हतामित्रं धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।गान्धारी पुत्रशोकार्ता शप्तुमैच्छदनिन्दिता ॥२॥
transliteration: tato jñātvā hatāmitraṁ dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,gāndhārī putraśokārtā śaptumaicchadaninditā.
english: Then, Gāndhārī, who was blameless and greatly distressed by the grief for her sons, having realized that Yudhiṣṭhira was the king of (dharma) righteousness and his enemies had been slain, wished to utter a curse.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. Then, Gāndhārī, who was blameless and greatly distressed by the grief for her sons, having realized that Yudhiṣṭhira was the king of (dharma) righteousness and his enemies had been slain, wished to utter a curse.

sanskrit: तस्याः पापमभिप्रायं विदित्वा पाण्डवान्प्रति ।ऋषिः सत्यवतीपुत्रः प्रागेव समबुध्यत ॥३॥
transliteration: tasyāḥ pāpamabhiprāyaṁ viditvā pāṇḍavānprati ,ṛṣiḥ satyavatīputraḥ prāgeva samabudhyata.
english: Having understood her wicked intention concerning the Pāṇḍavas, the sage, son of Satyavatī, had already perceived it beforehand.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. Having understood her wicked intention concerning the Pāṇḍavas, the sage, son of Satyavatī, had already perceived it beforehand.

sanskrit: स गङ्गायामुपस्पृश्य पुण्यगन्धं पयः शुचि ।तं देशमुपसंपेदे परमर्षिर्मनोजवः ॥४॥
transliteration: sa gaṅgāyāmupaspṛśya puṇyagandhaṁ payaḥ śuci ,taṁ deśamupasaṁpede paramarṣirmanojavaḥ.
english: Having bathed in the pure, sacred-smelling water of the Gaṅgā, that great sage, swift as thought, approached that region.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. Having bathed in the pure, sacred-smelling water of the Gaṅgā, that great sage, swift as thought, approached that region.

sanskrit: दिव्येन चक्षुषा पश्यन्मनसानुद्धतेन च ।सर्वप्राणभृतां भावं स तत्र समबुध्यत ॥५॥
transliteration: divyena cakṣuṣā paśyanmanasānuddhatena ca ,sarvaprāṇabhṛtāṁ bhāvaṁ sa tatra samabudhyata.
english: Perceiving with his divine eye and with an undisturbed mind, he understood there the nature (bhāva) of all living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. Perceiving with his divine eye and with an undisturbed mind, he understood there the nature (bhāva) of all living beings.

sanskrit: स स्नुषामब्रवीत्काले कल्यवादी महातपाः ।शापकालमवाक्षिप्य शमकालमुदीरयन् ॥६॥
transliteration: sa snuṣāmabravītkāle kalyavādī mahātapāḥ ,śāpakālamavākṣipya śamakālamudīrayan.
english: He, the great ascetic (tapas), speaking auspiciously at the proper moment, addressed his daughter-in-law, setting aside the moment for a curse and proclaiming a moment for tranquility.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. He, the great ascetic (tapas), speaking auspiciously at the proper moment, addressed his daughter-in-law, setting aside the moment for a curse and proclaiming a moment for tranquility.

sanskrit: न कोपः पाण्डवे कार्यो गान्धारि शममाप्नुहि ।रजो निगृह्यतामेतच्छृणु चेदं वचो मम ॥७॥
transliteration: na kopaḥ pāṇḍave kāryo gāndhāri śamamāpnuhi ,rajo nigṛhyatāmetacchṛṇu cedaṁ vaco mama.
english: O Gāndhārī, do not harbor anger towards the Pāṇḍavas; attain peace. This agitation must be restrained. And listen to these words of mine.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. O Gāndhārī, do not harbor anger towards the Pāṇḍavas; attain peace. This agitation must be restrained. And listen to these words of mine.

sanskrit: उक्तास्यष्टादशाहानि पुत्रेण जयमिच्छता ।शिवमाशास्स्व मे मातर्युध्यमानस्य शत्रुभिः ॥८॥
transliteration: uktāsyaṣṭādaśāhāni putreṇa jayamicchatā ,śivamāśāssva me mātaryudhyamānasya śatrubhiḥ.
english: Your son, desiring victory, spoke to you for eighteen days, saying, 'O mother, bless me, who am fighting against enemies!'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. Your son, desiring victory, spoke to you for eighteen days, saying, 'O mother, bless me, who am fighting against enemies!'

sanskrit: सा तथा याच्यमाना त्वं काले काले जयैषिणा ।उक्तवत्यसि गान्धारि यतो धर्मस्ततो जयः ॥९॥
transliteration: sā tathā yācyamānā tvaṁ kāle kāle jayaiṣiṇā ,uktavatyasi gāndhāri yato dharmastato jayaḥ.
english: O Gāndhārī, you, thus implored repeatedly by the one (your son) desiring victory, had replied, 'Where there is natural law (dharma), there is victory.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. O Gāndhārī, you, thus implored repeatedly by the one (your son) desiring victory, had replied, 'Where there is natural law (dharma), there is victory.'

sanskrit: न चाप्यतीतां गान्धारि वाचं ते वितथामहम् ।स्मरामि भाषमाणायास्तथा प्रणिहिता ह्यसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: na cāpyatītāṁ gāndhāri vācaṁ te vitathāmaham ,smarāmi bhāṣamāṇāyāstathā praṇihitā hyasi.
english: And indeed, O Gāndhārī, I do not remember that past utterance of yours as false, for you spoke it with such profound attention and sincerity.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. And indeed, O Gāndhārī, I do not remember that past utterance of yours as false, for you spoke it with such profound attention and sincerity.

sanskrit: सा त्वं धर्मं परिस्मृत्य वाचा चोक्त्वा मनस्विनि ।कोपं संयच्छ गान्धारि मैवं भूः सत्यवादिनि ॥११॥
transliteration: sā tvaṁ dharmaṁ parismṛtya vācā coktvā manasvini ,kopaṁ saṁyaccha gāndhāri maivaṁ bhūḥ satyavādini.
english: Therefore, O resolute Gandhari, remembering your natural law (dharma) and having spoken with your words, restrain your anger. Do not be like this, O truth-speaker.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. Therefore, O resolute Gandhari, remembering your natural law (dharma) and having spoken with your words, restrain your anger. Do not be like this, O truth-speaker.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।भगवन्नाभ्यसूयामि नैतानिच्छामि नश्यतः ।पुत्रशोकेन तु बलान्मनो विह्वलतीव मे ॥१२॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,bhagavannābhyasūyāmi naitānicchāmi naśyataḥ ,putraśokena tu balānmano vihvalatīva me.
english: Gandhari said: "O venerable one, I do not blame you, nor do I wish for these [sons] to perish. But my mind, due to the grief for my sons, seems to waver forcibly."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. Gandhari said: "O venerable one, I do not blame you, nor do I wish for these [sons] to perish. But my mind, due to the grief for my sons, seems to waver forcibly."

sanskrit: यथैव कुन्त्या कौन्तेया रक्षितव्यास्तथा मया ।यथैव धृतराष्ट्रेण रक्षितव्यास्तथा मया ॥१३॥
transliteration: yathaiva kuntyā kaunteyā rakṣitavyāstathā mayā ,yathaiva dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa rakṣitavyāstathā mayā.
english: Just as Kunti's sons (Kaunteyas) should be protected by Kunti, similarly they should be protected by me. And just as they should be protected by Dhritarashtra, similarly they should be protected by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. Just as Kunti's sons (Kaunteyas) should be protected by Kunti, similarly they should be protected by me. And just as they should be protected by Dhritarashtra, similarly they should be protected by me.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनापराधेन शकुनेः सौबलस्य च ।कर्णदुःशासनाभ्यां च वृत्तोऽयं कुरुसंक्षयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: duryodhanāparādhena śakuneḥ saubalasya ca ,karṇaduḥśāsanābhyāṁ ca vṛtto'yaṁ kurusaṁkṣayaḥ.
english: This destruction of the Kurus has occurred due to Duryodhana's offense, and that of Shakuni (the son of Subala), and by Karna and Duhshasana.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. This destruction of the Kurus has occurred due to Duryodhana's offense, and that of Shakuni (the son of Subala), and by Karna and Duhshasana.

sanskrit: नापराध्यति बीभत्सुर्न च पार्थो वृकोदरः ।नकुलः सहदेवो वा नैव जातु युधिष्ठिरः ॥१५॥
transliteration: nāparādhyati bībhatsurna ca pārtho vṛkodaraḥ ,nakulaḥ sahadevo vā naiva jātu yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Neither Bībhatsu (Arjuna), nor Pārtha (Arjuna), nor Vṛkodara (Bhīma) commit any wrong. Nor do Nakula, Sahadeva, or even Yudhiṣṭhira ever err.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. Neither Bībhatsu (Arjuna), nor Pārtha (Arjuna), nor Vṛkodara (Bhīma) commit any wrong. Nor do Nakula, Sahadeva, or even Yudhiṣṭhira ever err.

sanskrit: युध्यमाना हि कौरव्याः कृन्तमानाः परस्परम् ।निहताः सहिताश्चान्यैस्तत्र नास्त्यप्रियं मम ॥१६॥
transliteration: yudhyamānā hi kauravyāḥ kṛntamānāḥ parasparam ,nihatāḥ sahitāścānyaistatra nāstyapriyaṁ mama.
english: Indeed, when the Kauravas were fighting and cutting down each other, and were slain along with others, I found nothing unpleasant in that.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. Indeed, when the Kauravas were fighting and cutting down each other, and were slain along with others, I found nothing unpleasant in that.

sanskrit: यत्तु कर्माकरोद्भीमो वासुदेवस्य पश्यतः ।दुर्योधनं समाहूय गदायुद्धे महामनाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yattu karmākarodbhīmo vāsudevasya paśyataḥ ,duryodhanaṁ samāhūya gadāyuddhe mahāmanāḥ.
english: But the deed that the great-souled Bhīma performed, having challenged Duryodhana to a mace-fight, while Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) was watching...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. But the deed that the great-souled Bhīma performed, having challenged Duryodhana to a mace-fight, while Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) was watching...

sanskrit: शिक्षयाभ्यधिकं ज्ञात्वा चरन्तं बहुधा रणे ।अधो नाभ्यां प्रहृतवांस्तन्मे कोपमवर्धयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: śikṣayābhyadhikaṁ jñātvā carantaṁ bahudhā raṇe ,adho nābhyāṁ prahṛtavāṁstanme kopamavardhayat.
english: (Bhīma), knowing Duryodhana to be superior in martial training (śikṣā) and moving diversely in battle, struck him below the navel. That (act) increased my anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. (Bhīma), knowing Duryodhana to be superior in martial training (śikṣā) and moving diversely in battle, struck him below the navel. That (act) increased my anger.

sanskrit: कथं नु धर्मं धर्मज्ञैः समुद्दिष्टं महात्मभिः ।त्यजेयुराहवे शूराः प्राणहेतोः कथंचन ॥१९॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu dharmaṁ dharmajñaiḥ samuddiṣṭaṁ mahātmabhiḥ ,tyajeyurāhave śūrāḥ prāṇahetoḥ kathaṁcana.
english: How indeed could brave warriors ever abandon the natural law (dharma), which has been clearly taught by great souls and those who know the natural law (dharma), for the sake of their lives in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. How indeed could brave warriors ever abandon the natural law (dharma), which has been clearly taught by great souls and those who know the natural law (dharma), for the sake of their lives in battle?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्या भीमसेनोऽथ भीतवत् ।गान्धारीं प्रत्युवाचेदं वचः सानुनयं तदा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tacchrutvā vacanaṁ tasyā bhīmaseno'tha bhītavat ,gāndhārīṁ pratyuvācedaṁ vacaḥ sānunayaṁ tadā.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, having heard her words, Bhīmasena, as if frightened, humbly replied to Gāndhārī with these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, having heard her words, Bhīmasena, as if frightened, humbly replied to Gāndhārī with these words.

sanskrit: अधर्मो यदि वा धर्मस्त्रासात्तत्र मया कृतः ।आत्मानं त्रातुकामेन तन्मे त्वं क्षन्तुमर्हसि ॥२॥
transliteration: adharmo yadi vā dharmastrāsāttatra mayā kṛtaḥ ,ātmānaṁ trātukāmena tanme tvaṁ kṣantumarhasi.
english: If an act of unrighteousness, or even a righteous act (dharma), was done by me there out of fear, motivated by the desire to save my own self (ātman), then you ought to forgive me for that.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. If an act of unrighteousness, or even a righteous act (dharma), was done by me there out of fear, motivated by the desire to save my own self (ātman), then you ought to forgive me for that.

sanskrit: न हि युद्धेन पुत्रस्ते धर्मेण स महाबलः ।शक्यः केनचिदुद्यन्तुमतो विषममाचरम् ॥३॥
transliteration: na hi yuddhena putraste dharmeṇa sa mahābalaḥ ,śakyaḥ kenacidudyantumato viṣamamācaram.
english: Indeed, your son, that very mighty one, could not be overcome by anyone through righteous means (dharma) or by direct battle; therefore, I performed an unfair act.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. Indeed, your son, that very mighty one, could not be overcome by anyone through righteous means (dharma) or by direct battle; therefore, I performed an unfair act.

sanskrit: सैन्यस्यैकोऽवशिष्टोऽयं गदायुद्धे च वीर्यवान् ।मां हत्वा न हरेद्राज्यमिति चैतत्कृतं मया ॥४॥
transliteration: sainyasyaiko'vaśiṣṭo'yaṁ gadāyuddhe ca vīryavān ,māṁ hatvā na haredrājyamiti caitatkṛtaṁ mayā.
english: This one, the only remaining member of the army, and mighty in mace-fighting, would not seize the kingdom after killing me; this (action) was indeed undertaken by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. This one, the only remaining member of the army, and mighty in mace-fighting, would not seize the kingdom after killing me; this (action) was indeed undertaken by me.

sanskrit: राजपुत्रीं च पाञ्चालीमेकवस्त्रां रजस्वलाम् ।भवत्या विदितं सर्वमुक्तवान्यत्सुतस्तव ॥५॥
transliteration: rājaputrīṁ ca pāñcālīmekavastrāṁ rajasvalām ,bhavatyā viditaṁ sarvamuktavānyatsutastava.
english: And the princess (rājaputrī) Draupadī (pāñcālī), clad in a single garment, during her menstrual period – all of this, what your son said, is known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. And the princess (rājaputrī) Draupadī (pāñcālī), clad in a single garment, during her menstrual period – all of this, what your son said, is known to you.

sanskrit: सुयोधनमसंगृह्य न शक्या भूः ससागरा ।केवला भोक्तुमस्माभिरतश्चैतत्कृतं मया ॥६॥
transliteration: suyodhanamasaṁgṛhya na śakyā bhūḥ sasāgarā ,kevalā bhoktumasmābhirataścaitatkṛtaṁ mayā.
english: Without securing Suyodhana (Duryodhana), this earth (bhū) with its oceans cannot be enjoyed by us alone. Therefore, this (action) was indeed undertaken by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. Without securing Suyodhana (Duryodhana), this earth (bhū) with its oceans cannot be enjoyed by us alone. Therefore, this (action) was indeed undertaken by me.

sanskrit: तच्चाप्यप्रियमस्माकं पुत्रस्ते समुपाचरत् ।द्रौपद्या यत्सभामध्ये सव्यमूरुमदर्शयत् ॥७॥
transliteration: taccāpyapriyamasmākaṁ putraste samupācarat ,draupadyā yatsabhāmadhye savyamūrumadarśayat.
english: And that (action), which was also disagreeable to us, your son performed: namely, that he revealed his left thigh to Draupadī in the assembly (sabhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. And that (action), which was also disagreeable to us, your son performed: namely, that he revealed his left thigh to Draupadī in the assembly (sabhā).

sanskrit: तत्रैव वध्यः सोऽस्माकं दुराचारोऽम्ब ते सुतः ।धर्मराजाज्ञया चैव स्थिताः स्म समये तदा ॥८॥
transliteration: tatraiva vadhyaḥ so'smākaṁ durācāro'mba te sutaḥ ,dharmarājājñayā caiva sthitāḥ sma samaye tadā.
english: O mother, your ill-behaved son was indeed deserving of death by us right then and there. We then remained committed to the command of the king of righteousness (dharma) at that appointed time.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. O mother, your ill-behaved son was indeed deserving of death by us right then and there. We then remained committed to the command of the king of righteousness (dharma) at that appointed time.

sanskrit: वैरमुद्धुक्षितं राज्ञि पुत्रेण तव तन्महत् ।क्लेशिताश्च वने नित्यं तत एतत्कृतं मया ॥९॥
transliteration: vairamuddhukṣitaṁ rājñi putreṇa tava tanmahat ,kleśitāśca vane nityaṁ tata etatkṛtaṁ mayā.
english: O queen, that great enmity was ignited by your son. And because we were constantly tormented in the forest, this action was done by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. O queen, that great enmity was ignited by your son. And because we were constantly tormented in the forest, this action was done by me.

sanskrit: वैरस्यास्य गतः पारं हत्वा दुर्योधनं रणे ।राज्यं युधिष्ठिरः प्राप्तो वयं च गतमन्यवः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vairasyāsya gataḥ pāraṁ hatvā duryodhanaṁ raṇe ,rājyaṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ prāpto vayaṁ ca gatamanyavaḥ.
english: Having killed Duryodhana in battle, I reached the culmination of this enmity. Yudhiṣṭhira obtained the kingdom, and we are now free from anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10. Having killed Duryodhana in battle, I reached the culmination of this enmity. Yudhiṣṭhira obtained the kingdom, and we are now free from anger.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।न तस्यैष वधस्तात यत्प्रशंससि मे सुतम् ।कृतवांश्चापि तत्सर्वं यदिदं भाषसे मयि ॥११॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,na tasyaiṣa vadhastāta yatpraśaṁsasi me sutam ,kṛtavāṁścāpi tatsarvaṁ yadidaṁ bhāṣase mayi.
english: Gāndhārī spoke: 'O dear one, this killing of his (my son) is not something to be commended, for you praise (your actions against) my son. However, he (Duryodhana) indeed committed all that which you relate to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. Gāndhārī spoke: 'O dear one, this killing of his (my son) is not something to be commended, for you praise (your actions against) my son. However, he (Duryodhana) indeed committed all that which you relate to me.'

sanskrit: हताश्वे नकुले यत्तद्वृषसेनेन भारत ।अपिबः शोणितं संख्ये दुःशासनशरीरजम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: hatāśve nakule yattadvṛṣasenena bhārata ,apibaḥ śoṇitaṁ saṁkhye duḥśāsanaśarīrajam.
english: O Bhārata, because of Vṛṣasena's act of killing Nakula's horses, you drank the blood from Duḥśāsana's body in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. O Bhārata, because of Vṛṣasena's act of killing Nakula's horses, you drank the blood from Duḥśāsana's body in battle.

sanskrit: सद्भिर्विगर्हितं घोरमनार्यजनसेवितम् ।क्रूरं कर्माकरोः कस्मात्तदयुक्तं वृकोदर ॥१३॥
transliteration: sadbhirvigarhitaṁ ghoramanāryajanasevitam ,krūraṁ karmākaroḥ kasmāttadayuktaṁ vṛkodara.
english: O Vṛkodara, why did you perform that cruel, dreadful, and inappropriate act (karma), which is condemned by virtuous people and practiced by the ignoble?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. O Vṛkodara, why did you perform that cruel, dreadful, and inappropriate act (karma), which is condemned by virtuous people and practiced by the ignoble?

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।अन्यस्यापि न पातव्यं रुधिरं किं पुनः स्वकम् ।यथैवात्मा तथा भ्राता विशेषो नास्ति कश्चन ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,anyasyāpi na pātavyaṁ rudhiraṁ kiṁ punaḥ svakam ,yathaivātmā tathā bhrātā viśeṣo nāsti kaścana.
english: Bhīmasena said: One should not drink the blood of another, let alone one's own kin's blood! For a brother is just like one's own self (ātman); there is absolutely no distinction.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. Bhīmasena said: One should not drink the blood of another, let alone one's own kin's blood! For a brother is just like one's own self (ātman); there is absolutely no distinction.

sanskrit: रुधिरं न व्यतिक्रामद्दन्तोष्ठं मेऽम्ब मा शुचः ।वैवस्वतस्तु तद्वेद हस्तौ मे रुधिरोक्षितौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: rudhiraṁ na vyatikrāmaddantoṣṭhaṁ me'mba mā śucaḥ ,vaivasvatastu tadveda hastau me rudhirokṣitau.
english: O Mother, do not grieve! The blood did not pass beyond my teeth and lips. Only Vaivasvata (Yama) knows that truth. But my hands are indeed covered with blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. O Mother, do not grieve! The blood did not pass beyond my teeth and lips. Only Vaivasvata (Yama) knows that truth. But my hands are indeed covered with blood.

sanskrit: हताश्वं नकुलं दृष्ट्वा वृषसेनेन संयुगे ।भ्रातॄणां संप्रहृष्टानां त्रासः संजनितो मया ॥१६॥
transliteration: hatāśvaṁ nakulaṁ dṛṣṭvā vṛṣasenena saṁyuge ,bhrātṝṇāṁ saṁprahṛṣṭānāṁ trāsaḥ saṁjanito mayā.
english: When I saw Nakula, whose horses had been slain by Vṛṣasena in battle, I instilled fear into the hearts of the (Kaurava) brothers, who had been greatly delighted.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. When I saw Nakula, whose horses had been slain by Vṛṣasena in battle, I instilled fear into the hearts of the (Kaurava) brothers, who had been greatly delighted.

sanskrit: केशपक्षपरामर्शे द्रौपद्या द्यूतकारिते ।क्रोधाद्यदब्रुवं चाहं तच्च मे हृदि वर्तते ॥१७॥
transliteration: keśapakṣaparāmarśe draupadyā dyūtakārite ,krodhādyadabruvaṁ cāhaṁ tacca me hṛdi vartate.
english: Whatever I said in anger when Draupadi's hair was seized during the gambling match, that (resolve) also remains in my heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 17. Whatever I said in anger when Draupadi's hair was seized during the gambling match, that (resolve) also remains in my heart.

sanskrit: क्षत्रधर्माच्च्युतो राज्ञि भवेयं शाश्वतीः समाः ।प्रतिज्ञां तामनिस्तीर्य ततस्तत्कृतवानहम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: kṣatradharmāccyuto rājñi bhaveyaṁ śāśvatīḥ samāḥ ,pratijñāṁ tāmanistīrya tatastatkṛtavānaham.
english: O Queen, I would be fallen from my warrior's code (kṣatradharma) for eternal years if I did not fulfill that vow. Therefore, I have indeed carried out that action.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 18. O Queen, I would be fallen from my warrior's code (kṣatradharma) for eternal years if I did not fulfill that vow. Therefore, I have indeed carried out that action.

sanskrit: न मामर्हसि गान्धारि दोषेण परिशङ्कितुम् ।अनिगृह्य पुरा पुत्रानस्मास्वनपकारिषु ॥१९॥
transliteration: na māmarhasi gāndhāri doṣeṇa pariśaṅkitum ,anigṛhya purā putrānasmāsvanapakāriṣu.
english: O Gandhari, you ought not to accuse me of fault, especially since you previously did not restrain your sons when we (the Pāṇḍavas) were doing them no harm.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 19. O Gandhari, you ought not to accuse me of fault, especially since you previously did not restrain your sons when we (the Pāṇḍavas) were doing them no harm.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।वृद्धस्यास्य शतं पुत्रान्निघ्नंस्त्वमपराजितः ।कस्मान्न शेषयः कंचिद्येनाल्पमपराधितम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,vṛddhasyāsya śataṁ putrānnighnaṁstvamaparājitaḥ ,kasmānna śeṣayaḥ kaṁcidyenālpamaparādhitam.
english: Gandhari said: "You, who are undefeated, having slain the hundred sons of this old man, why do you not spare anyone, even one by whom only a slight offense was committed?"
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 20. Gandhari said: "You, who are undefeated, having slain the hundred sons of this old man, why do you not spare anyone, even one by whom only a slight offense was committed?"

sanskrit: संतानमावयोस्तात वृद्धयोर्हृतराज्ययोः ।कथमन्धद्वयस्यास्य यष्टिरेका न वर्जिता ॥२१॥
transliteration: saṁtānamāvayostāta vṛddhayorhṛtarājyayoḥ ,kathamandhadvayasyāsya yaṣṭirekā na varjitā.
english: "O son, concerning the progeny of us two aged ones who have lost our kingdom, how is it that not even a single staff (meaning a son) was spared for this blind couple?"
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 21. "O son, concerning the progeny of us two aged ones who have lost our kingdom, how is it that not even a single staff (meaning a son) was spared for this blind couple?"

sanskrit: शेषे ह्यवस्थिते तात पुत्राणामन्तके त्वयि ।न मे दुःखं भवेदेतद्यदि त्वं धर्ममाचरः ॥२२॥
transliteration: śeṣe hyavasthite tāta putrāṇāmantake tvayi ,na me duḥkhaṁ bhavedetadyadi tvaṁ dharmamācaraḥ.
english: "O son, if even one (son) had indeed remained, despite you being the annihilator of my sons, this sorrow would not be mine, if you had only practiced your righteous conduct (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 14 Verse 22. "O son, if even one (son) had indeed remained, despite you being the annihilator of my sons, this sorrow would not be mine, if you had only practiced your righteous conduct (dharma)."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा तु गान्धारी युधिष्ठिरमपृच्छत ।क्व स राजेति सक्रोधा पुत्रपौत्रवधार्दिता ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā tu gāndhārī yudhiṣṭhiramapṛcchata ,kva sa rājeti sakrodhā putrapautravadhārditā.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Having spoken thus, Gandhari, enraged and tormented by the slaughter of her sons and grandsons, asked Yudhishthira, 'Where is that king?'"
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: "Having spoken thus, Gandhari, enraged and tormented by the slaughter of her sons and grandsons, asked Yudhishthira, 'Where is that king?'"

sanskrit: तामभ्यगच्छद्राजेन्द्रो वेपमानः कृताञ्जलिः ।युधिष्ठिर इदं चैनां मधुरं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: tāmabhyagacchadrājendro vepamānaḥ kṛtāñjaliḥ ,yudhiṣṭhira idaṁ caināṁ madhuraṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: The king (rājendraḥ) Yudhishthira, trembling and with folded hands, approached her. Yudhishthira then spoke these gentle words to her.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. The king (rājendraḥ) Yudhishthira, trembling and with folded hands, approached her. Yudhishthira then spoke these gentle words to her.

sanskrit: पुत्रहन्ता नृशंसोऽहं तव देवि युधिष्ठिरः ।शापार्हः पृथिवीनाशे हेतुभूतः शपस्व माम् ॥३॥
transliteration: putrahantā nṛśaṁso'haṁ tava devi yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śāpārhaḥ pṛthivīnāśe hetubhūtaḥ śapasva mām.
english: O goddess (devi), I am Yudhishthira, the cruel slayer of your sons. I am deserving of a curse, having been the cause of this earth's destruction. Therefore, curse me!
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. O goddess (devi), I am Yudhishthira, the cruel slayer of your sons. I am deserving of a curse, having been the cause of this earth's destruction. Therefore, curse me!

sanskrit: न हि मे जीवितेनार्थो न राज्येन धनेन वा ।तादृशान्सुहृदो हत्वा मूढस्यास्य सुहृद्द्रुहः ॥४॥
transliteration: na hi me jīvitenārtho na rājyena dhanena vā ,tādṛśānsuhṛdo hatvā mūḍhasyāsya suhṛddruhaḥ.
english: Indeed, for me, this foolish betrayer of friends, there is no purpose in life, nor in a kingdom, or in wealth, after having killed such dear friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. Indeed, for me, this foolish betrayer of friends, there is no purpose in life, nor in a kingdom, or in wealth, after having killed such dear friends.

sanskrit: तमेवंवादिनं भीतं संनिकर्षगतं तदा ।नोवाच किंचिद्गान्धारी निःश्वासपरमा भृशम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tamevaṁvādinaṁ bhītaṁ saṁnikarṣagataṁ tadā ,novāca kiṁcidgāndhārī niḥśvāsaparamā bhṛśam.
english: Then, Gandhari, deeply filled with sighs, said nothing to him, who was speaking in this manner, frightened, and standing so near.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. Then, Gandhari, deeply filled with sighs, said nothing to him, who was speaking in this manner, frightened, and standing so near.

sanskrit: तस्यावनतदेहस्य पादयोर्निपतिष्यतः ।युधिष्ठिरस्य नृपतेर्धर्मज्ञा धर्मदर्शिनी ।अङ्गुल्यग्राणि ददृशे देवी पट्टान्तरेण सा ॥६॥
transliteration: tasyāvanatadehasya pādayornipatiṣyataḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasya nṛpaterdharmajñā dharmadarśinī ,aṅgulyagrāṇi dadṛśe devī paṭṭāntareṇa sā.
english: As that King Yudhishthira, whose body was bowed, was about to fall at her feet, she, the queen (devī), who knew and perceived the natural law (dharma), saw his fingertips through her bandage.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. As that King Yudhishthira, whose body was bowed, was about to fall at her feet, she, the queen (devī), who knew and perceived the natural law (dharma), saw his fingertips through her bandage.

sanskrit: ततः स कुनखीभूतो दर्शनीयनखो नृपः ।तं दृष्ट्वा चार्जुनोऽगच्छद्वासुदेवस्य पृष्ठतः ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa kunakhībhūto darśanīyanakho nṛpaḥ ,taṁ dṛṣṭvā cārjuno'gacchadvāsudevasya pṛṣṭhataḥ.
english: Then that king (Yudhishthira), whose once beautiful nails had become disfigured, and seeing him (the king in that state), Arjuna went behind Vasudeva (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. Then that king (Yudhishthira), whose once beautiful nails had become disfigured, and seeing him (the king in that state), Arjuna went behind Vasudeva (Krishna).

sanskrit: एवं संचेष्टमानांस्तानितश्चेतश्च भारत ।गान्धारी विगतक्रोधा सान्त्वयामास मातृवत् ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁceṣṭamānāṁstānitaścetaśca bhārata ,gāndhārī vigatakrodhā sāntvayāmāsa mātṛvat.
english: O Bharata, Gandhari, her anger having departed, consoled them, who were struggling here and there, just like a mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. O Bharata, Gandhari, her anger having departed, consoled them, who were struggling here and there, just like a mother.

sanskrit: तया ते समनुज्ञाता मातरं वीरमातरम् ।अभ्यगच्छन्त सहिताः पृथां पृथुलवक्षसः ॥९॥
transliteration: tayā te samanujñātā mātaraṁ vīramātaram ,abhyagacchanta sahitāḥ pṛthāṁ pṛthulavakṣasaḥ.
english: Having been permitted by her (Gandhari), they, the broad-chested ones, together approached their mother, Pritha (Kunti), the mother of heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. Having been permitted by her (Gandhari), they, the broad-chested ones, together approached their mother, Pritha (Kunti), the mother of heroes.

sanskrit: चिरस्य दृष्ट्वा पुत्रान्सा पुत्राधिभिरभिप्लुता ।बाष्पमाहारयद्देवी वस्त्रेणावृत्य वै मुखम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: cirasya dṛṣṭvā putrānsā putrādhibhirabhiplutā ,bāṣpamāhārayaddevī vastreṇāvṛtya vai mukham.
english: Having seen her sons after a long time, she, the queen, overwhelmed by the distress concerning her sons, produced tears, indeed covering her face with her garment.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. Having seen her sons after a long time, she, the queen, overwhelmed by the distress concerning her sons, produced tears, indeed covering her face with her garment.

sanskrit: ततो बाष्पं समुत्सृज्य सह पुत्रैस्तथा पृथा ।अपश्यदेताञ्शस्त्रौघैर्बहुधा परिविक्षतान् ॥११॥
transliteration: tato bāṣpaṁ samutsṛjya saha putraistathā pṛthā ,apaśyadetāñśastraughairbahudhā parivikṣatān.
english: Then, after shedding tears with her sons, Pritha also saw them severely wounded in many places by weapon attacks.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. Then, after shedding tears with her sons, Pritha also saw them severely wounded in many places by weapon attacks.

sanskrit: सा तानेकैकशः पुत्रान्संस्पृशन्ती पुनः पुनः ।अन्वशोचन्त दुःखार्ता द्रौपदीं च हतात्मजाम् ।रुदतीमथ पाञ्चालीं ददर्श पतितां भुवि ॥१२॥
transliteration: sā tānekaikaśaḥ putrānsaṁspṛśantī punaḥ punaḥ ,anvaśocanta duḥkhārtā draupadīṁ ca hatātmajām ,rudatīmatha pāñcālīṁ dadarśa patitāṁ bhuvi.
english: She, afflicted by sorrow, repeatedly touching her sons one by one again and again, grieved for them and for Draupadi, whose children were slain. Then she saw Panchali, weeping and fallen on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. She, afflicted by sorrow, repeatedly touching her sons one by one again and again, grieved for them and for Draupadi, whose children were slain. Then she saw Panchali, weeping and fallen on the ground.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।आर्ये पौत्राः क्व ते सर्वे सौभद्रसहिता गताः ।न त्वां तेऽद्याभिगच्छन्ति चिरदृष्टां तपस्विनीम् ।किं नु राज्येन वै कार्यं विहीनायाः सुतैर्मम ॥१३॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,ārye pautrāḥ kva te sarve saubhadrasahitā gatāḥ ,na tvāṁ te'dyābhigacchanti ciradṛṣṭāṁ tapasvinīm ,kiṁ nu rājyena vai kāryaṁ vihīnāyāḥ sutairmama.
english: Draupadi said: "O venerable lady! Where have all your grandsons, including Saubhadra, gone? They do not approach you now, after having been seen for a long time, and you are a suffering woman. What indeed is the purpose of a kingdom for me, who am deprived of my sons?"
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. Draupadi said: "O venerable lady! Where have all your grandsons, including Saubhadra, gone? They do not approach you now, after having been seen for a long time, and you are a suffering woman. What indeed is the purpose of a kingdom for me, who am deprived of my sons?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तां समाश्वासयामास पृथा पृथुललोचना ।उत्थाप्य याज्ञसेनीं तु रुदतीं शोककर्शिताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tāṁ samāśvāsayāmāsa pṛthā pṛthulalocanā ,utthāpya yājñasenīṁ tu rudatīṁ śokakarśitām.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Wide-eyed Pṛthā, after raising up Yājñasenī, who was weeping and afflicted by sorrow (śoka), then comforted her.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. Vaiśampāyana said: Wide-eyed Pṛthā, after raising up Yājñasenī, who was weeping and afflicted by sorrow (śoka), then comforted her.

sanskrit: तयैव सहिता चापि पुत्रैरनुगता पृथा ।अभ्यगच्छत गान्धारीमार्तामार्ततरा स्वयम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tayaiva sahitā cāpi putrairanugatā pṛthā ,abhyagacchata gāndhārīmārtāmārtatarā svayam.
english: Pṛthā, accompanied by Draupadi and followed by her sons, herself went to meet the distressed Gāndhārī, (Pṛthā being) even more distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. Pṛthā, accompanied by Draupadi and followed by her sons, herself went to meet the distressed Gāndhārī, (Pṛthā being) even more distressed.

sanskrit: तामुवाचाथ गान्धारी सह वध्वा यशस्विनीम् ।मैवं पुत्रीति शोकार्ता पश्य मामपि दुःखिताम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tāmuvācātha gāndhārī saha vadhvā yaśasvinīm ,maivaṁ putrīti śokārtā paśya māmapi duḥkhitām.
english: Then Gāndhārī, accompanied by her glorious daughter-in-law, said to her (Draupadi): "My daughter, do not be so overcome by sorrow (śoka). Look at me; I am also suffering."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. Then Gāndhārī, accompanied by her glorious daughter-in-law, said to her (Draupadi): "My daughter, do not be so overcome by sorrow (śoka). Look at me; I am also suffering."

sanskrit: मन्ये लोकविनाशोऽयं कालपर्यायचोदितः ।अवश्यभावी संप्राप्तः स्वभावाल्लोमहर्षणः ॥१७॥
transliteration: manye lokavināśo'yaṁ kālaparyāyacoditaḥ ,avaśyabhāvī saṁprāptaḥ svabhāvāllomaharṣaṇaḥ.
english: I believe this destruction of the world, impelled by the turning of time (kāla), is inevitable and has come to pass, being hair-raising by its very nature.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 17. I believe this destruction of the world, impelled by the turning of time (kāla), is inevitable and has come to pass, being hair-raising by its very nature.

sanskrit: इदं तत्समनुप्राप्तं विदुरस्य वचो महत् ।असिद्धानुनये कृष्णे यदुवाच महामतिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: idaṁ tatsamanuprāptaṁ vidurasya vaco mahat ,asiddhānunaye kṛṣṇe yaduvāca mahāmatiḥ.
english: Now, that important statement of the great-minded Vidura, which he spoke to Kṛṣṇa when all persuasion failed, has come to pass.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 18. Now, that important statement of the great-minded Vidura, which he spoke to Kṛṣṇa when all persuasion failed, has come to pass.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्नपरिहार्येऽर्थे व्यतीते च विशेषतः ।मा शुचो न हि शोच्यास्ते संग्रामे निधनं गताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasminnaparihārye'rthe vyatīte ca viśeṣataḥ ,mā śuco na hi śocyāste saṁgrāme nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Do not grieve over that unavoidable matter, which has now especially come to pass. For indeed, those who attained death in battle are not to be lamented.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 19. Do not grieve over that unavoidable matter, which has now especially come to pass. For indeed, those who attained death in battle are not to be lamented.

sanskrit: यथैव त्वं तथैवाहं को वा माश्वासयिष्यति ।ममैव ह्यपराधेन कुलमग्र्यं विनाशितम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yathaiva tvaṁ tathaivāhaṁ ko vā māśvāsayiṣyati ,mamaiva hyaparādhena kulamagryaṁ vināśitam.
english: You are in the same state as I am; so who will console me? For indeed, it is solely due to my offense that this foremost family has been destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 15 Verse 20. You are in the same state as I am; so who will console me? For indeed, it is solely due to my offense that this foremost family has been destroyed.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा तु गान्धारी कुरूणामाविकर्तनम् ।अपश्यत्तत्र तिष्ठन्ती सर्वं दिव्येन चक्षुषा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā tu gāndhārī kurūṇāmāvikartanam ,apaśyattatra tiṣṭhantī sarvaṁ divyena cakṣuṣā.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken these words, Gāndhārī, standing at that spot, perceived the complete destruction of the Kurus with her divine sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken these words, Gāndhārī, standing at that spot, perceived the complete destruction of the Kurus with her divine sight.

sanskrit: पतिव्रता महाभागा समानव्रतचारिणी ।उग्रेण तपसा युक्ता सततं सत्यवादिनी ॥२॥
transliteration: pativratā mahābhāgā samānavratacāriṇī ,ugreṇa tapasā yuktā satataṁ satyavādinī.
english: She was a chaste (pativratā) and highly fortunate woman, observing the same vows [as her husband]. Endowed with intense asceticism (tapas), she was always truthful.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. She was a chaste (pativratā) and highly fortunate woman, observing the same vows [as her husband]. Endowed with intense asceticism (tapas), she was always truthful.

sanskrit: वरदानेन कृष्णस्य महर्षेः पुण्यकर्मणः ।दिव्यज्ञानबलोपेता विविधं पर्यदेवयत् ॥३॥
transliteration: varadānena kṛṣṇasya maharṣeḥ puṇyakarmaṇaḥ ,divyajñānabalopetā vividhaṁ paryadevayat.
english: Due to the boon (vara) granted by Krishna, the great sage (maharṣi) of meritorious deeds, she, endowed with divine knowledge and strength, lamented in many ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. Due to the boon (vara) granted by Krishna, the great sage (maharṣi) of meritorious deeds, she, endowed with divine knowledge and strength, lamented in many ways.

sanskrit: ददर्श सा बुद्धिमती दूरादपि यथान्तिके ।रणाजिरं नृवीराणामद्भुतं लोमहर्षणम् ॥४॥
transliteration: dadarśa sā buddhimatī dūrādapi yathāntike ,raṇājiraṁ nṛvīrāṇāmadbhutaṁ lomaharṣaṇam.
english: That intelligent woman saw, even from a distance as if it were near, the wondrous and hair-raising battlefield of the human heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. That intelligent woman saw, even from a distance as if it were near, the wondrous and hair-raising battlefield of the human heroes.

sanskrit: अस्थिकेशपरिस्तीर्णं शोणितौघपरिप्लुतम् ।शरीरैर्बहुसाहस्रैर्विनिकीर्णं समन्ततः ॥५॥
transliteration: asthikeśaparistīrṇaṁ śoṇitaughapariplutam ,śarīrairbahusāhasrairvinikīrṇaṁ samantataḥ.
english: It was strewn with bones and hair, deluged by torrents of blood, and scattered everywhere with thousands of bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. It was strewn with bones and hair, deluged by torrents of blood, and scattered everywhere with thousands of bodies.

sanskrit: गजाश्वरथयोधानामावृतं रुधिराविलैः ।शरीरैरशिरस्कैश्च विदेहैश्च शिरोगणैः ॥६॥
transliteration: gajāśvarathayodhānāmāvṛtaṁ rudhirāvilaiḥ ,śarīrairaśiraskaiśca videhaiśca śirogaṇaiḥ.
english: It was covered with the blood-stained bodies of elephants, horses, chariots, and warriors, including headless bodies and numerous severed heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. It was covered with the blood-stained bodies of elephants, horses, chariots, and warriors, including headless bodies and numerous severed heads.

sanskrit: गजाश्वनरवीराणां निःसत्त्वैरभिसंवृतम् ।सृगालबडकाकोलकङ्ककाकनिषेवितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: gajāśvanaravīrāṇāṁ niḥsattvairabhisaṁvṛtam ,sṛgālabaḍakākolakaṅkakākaniṣevitam.
english: It was completely covered with the lifeless bodies of elephants, horses, and human warriors, and frequented by jackals, ravens, vultures, and crows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. It was completely covered with the lifeless bodies of elephants, horses, and human warriors, and frequented by jackals, ravens, vultures, and crows.

sanskrit: रक्षसां पुरुषादानां मोदनं कुरराकुलम् ।अशिवाभिः शिवाभिश्च नादितं गृध्रसेवितम् ॥८॥
transliteration: rakṣasāṁ puruṣādānāṁ modanaṁ kurarākulam ,aśivābhiḥ śivābhiśca nāditaṁ gṛdhrasevitam.
english: It was a delight for the man-eating demons (rakṣasas), filled with noisy curlews, resounding with the cries of both inauspicious and other she-jackals, and frequented by vultures.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. It was a delight for the man-eating demons (rakṣasas), filled with noisy curlews, resounding with the cries of both inauspicious and other she-jackals, and frequented by vultures.

sanskrit: ततो व्यासाभ्यनुज्ञातो धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ।पाण्डुपुत्राश्च ते सर्वे युधिष्ठिरपुरोगमाः ॥९॥
transliteration: tato vyāsābhyanujñāto dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ ,pāṇḍuputrāśca te sarve yudhiṣṭhirapurogamāḥ.
english: Thereafter, King Dhritarashtra, having been permitted by Vyasa, and all those sons of Pandu with Yudhishthira at their forefront...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. Thereafter, King Dhritarashtra, having been permitted by Vyasa, and all those sons of Pandu with Yudhishthira at their forefront...

sanskrit: वासुदेवं पुरस्कृत्य हतबन्धुं च पार्थिवम् ।कुरुस्त्रियः समासाद्य जग्मुरायोधनं प्रति ॥१०॥
transliteration: vāsudevaṁ puraskṛtya hatabandhuṁ ca pārthivam ,kurustriyaḥ samāsādya jagmurāyodhanaṁ prati.
english: Placing Vāsudeva (Krishna) at the forefront, and accompanied by the king whose relatives had been slain, the Kuru women approached the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. Placing Vāsudeva (Krishna) at the forefront, and accompanied by the king whose relatives had been slain, the Kuru women approached the battlefield.

sanskrit: समासाद्य कुरुक्षेत्रं ताः स्त्रियो निहतेश्वराः ।अपश्यन्त हतांस्तत्र पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्पितॄन्पतीन् ॥११॥
transliteration: samāsādya kurukṣetraṁ tāḥ striyo nihateśvarāḥ ,apaśyanta hatāṁstatra putrānbhrātṝnpitṝnpatīn.
english: Upon reaching Kurukshetra, those women, whose lords had been slain, saw their sons, brothers, fathers, and husbands lying dead there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. Upon reaching Kurukshetra, those women, whose lords had been slain, saw their sons, brothers, fathers, and husbands lying dead there.

sanskrit: क्रव्यादैर्भक्ष्यमाणान्वै गोमायुबडवायसैः ।भूतैः पिशाचै रक्षोभिर्विविधैश्च निशाचरैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: kravyādairbhakṣyamāṇānvai gomāyubaḍavāyasaiḥ ,bhūtaiḥ piśācai rakṣobhirvividhaiśca niśācaraiḥ.
english: Indeed, they were being devoured by carrion-eating creatures, by jackals and vultures, as well as by various ghosts, goblins, rākṣasas, and night-stalkers.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. Indeed, they were being devoured by carrion-eating creatures, by jackals and vultures, as well as by various ghosts, goblins, rākṣasas, and night-stalkers.

sanskrit: रुद्राक्रीडनिभं दृष्ट्वा तदा विशसनं स्त्रियः ।महार्हेभ्योऽथ यानेभ्यो विक्रोशन्त्यो निपेतिरे ॥१३॥
transliteration: rudrākrīḍanibhaṁ dṛṣṭvā tadā viśasanaṁ striyaḥ ,mahārhebhyo'tha yānebhyo vikrośantyo nipetire.
english: When the women saw that slaughter, which resembled Rudra's playground, they then wailed loudly and fell down from their costly conveyances.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. When the women saw that slaughter, which resembled Rudra's playground, they then wailed loudly and fell down from their costly conveyances.

sanskrit: अदृष्टपूर्वं पश्यन्त्यो दुःखार्ता भरतस्त्रियः ।शरीरेष्वस्खलन्नन्या न्यपतंश्चापरा भुवि ॥१४॥
transliteration: adṛṣṭapūrvaṁ paśyantyo duḥkhārtā bharatastriyaḥ ,śarīreṣvaskhalannanyā nyapataṁścāparā bhuvi.
english: Beholding a sight never seen before, the Bharata women, afflicted with sorrow, some stumbled over the bodies, while others fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. Beholding a sight never seen before, the Bharata women, afflicted with sorrow, some stumbled over the bodies, while others fell to the ground.

sanskrit: श्रान्तानां चाप्यनाथानां नासीत्काचन चेतना ।पाञ्चालकुरुयोषाणां कृपणं तदभून्महत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: śrāntānāṁ cāpyanāthānāṁ nāsītkācana cetanā ,pāñcālakuruyoṣāṇāṁ kṛpaṇaṁ tadabhūnmahat.
english: For the weary and helpless Pañcāla and Kuru women, there was no consciousness or awareness. Their suffering was truly great and pitiable.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. For the weary and helpless Pañcāla and Kuru women, there was no consciousness or awareness. Their suffering was truly great and pitiable.

sanskrit: दुःखोपहतचित्ताभिः समन्तादनुनादितम् ।दृष्ट्वायोधनमत्युग्रं धर्मज्ञा सुबलात्मजा ॥१६॥
transliteration: duḥkhopahatacittābhiḥ samantādanunāditam ,dṛṣṭvāyodhanamatyugraṁ dharmajñā subalātmajā.
english: Gāndhārī (Subalātmajā), who understood (dharma), having seen that exceedingly dreadful battlefield, which was filled with the sounds of lamentation from women whose hearts were shattered by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. Gāndhārī (Subalātmajā), who understood (dharma), having seen that exceedingly dreadful battlefield, which was filled with the sounds of lamentation from women whose hearts were shattered by grief.

sanskrit: ततः सा पुण्डरीकाक्षमामन्त्र्य पुरुषोत्तमम् ।कुरूणां वैशसं दृष्ट्वा दुःखाद्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sā puṇḍarīkākṣamāmantrya puruṣottamam ,kurūṇāṁ vaiśasaṁ dṛṣṭvā duḥkhādvacanamabravīt.
english: Then she (Gāndhārī), having addressed the lotus-eyed supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama) (Kṛṣṇa), and having witnessed the destruction of the Kurus, spoke words born of her profound sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 17. Then she (Gāndhārī), having addressed the lotus-eyed supreme cosmic person (puruṣottama) (Kṛṣṇa), and having witnessed the destruction of the Kurus, spoke words born of her profound sorrow.

sanskrit: पश्यैताः पुण्डरीकाक्ष स्नुषा मे निहतेश्वराः ।प्रकीर्णकेशाः क्रोशन्तीः कुररीरिव माधव ॥१८॥
transliteration: paśyaitāḥ puṇḍarīkākṣa snuṣā me nihateśvarāḥ ,prakīrṇakeśāḥ krośantīḥ kurarīriva mādhava.
english: O Lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), O Mādhava, behold these my daughters-in-law, whose husbands have been slain, with dishevelled hair, crying out like ospreys.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 18. O Lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), O Mādhava, behold these my daughters-in-law, whose husbands have been slain, with dishevelled hair, crying out like ospreys.

sanskrit: अमूस्त्वभिसमागम्य स्मरन्त्यो भरतर्षभान् ।पृथगेवाभ्यधावन्त पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्पितॄन्पतीन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: amūstvabhisamāgamya smarantyo bharatarṣabhān ,pṛthagevābhyadhāvanta putrānbhrātṝnpitṝnpatīn.
english: But those women, having gathered together and remembering the best among the Bhāratas, indeed ran separately towards their sons, brothers, fathers, and husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 19. But those women, having gathered together and remembering the best among the Bhāratas, indeed ran separately towards their sons, brothers, fathers, and husbands.

sanskrit: वीरसूभिर्महाबाहो हतपुत्राभिरावृतम् ।क्वचिच्च वीरपत्नीभिर्हतवीराभिराकुलम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: vīrasūbhirmahābāho hataputrābhirāvṛtam ,kvacicca vīrapatnībhirhatavīrābhirākulam.
english: O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), (the battlefield is) covered by mothers of heroes whose sons have been slain, and in some places, it is filled with wives of heroes whose husbands have been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 20. O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), (the battlefield is) covered by mothers of heroes whose sons have been slain, and in some places, it is filled with wives of heroes whose husbands have been slain.

sanskrit: शोभितं पुरुषव्याघ्रैर्भीष्मकर्णाभिमन्युभिः ।द्रोणद्रुपदशल्यैश्च ज्वलद्भिरिव पावकैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: śobhitaṁ puruṣavyāghrairbhīṣmakarṇābhimanyubhiḥ ,droṇadrupadaśalyaiśca jvaladbhiriva pāvakaiḥ.
english: (The battlefield is) adorned by tiger-like men (puruṣavyāghra), like blazing fires, such as Bhīṣma, Karṇa, Abhimanyu, Droṇa, Drupada, and Śalya.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 21. (The battlefield is) adorned by tiger-like men (puruṣavyāghra), like blazing fires, such as Bhīṣma, Karṇa, Abhimanyu, Droṇa, Drupada, and Śalya.

sanskrit: काञ्चनैः कवचैर्निष्कैर्मणिभिश्च महात्मनाम् ।अङ्गदैर्हस्तकेयूरैः स्रग्भिश्च समलंकृतम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: kāñcanaiḥ kavacairniṣkairmaṇibhiśca mahātmanām ,aṅgadairhastakeyūraiḥ sragbhiśca samalaṁkṛtam.
english: It was well adorned with golden armors, necklaces, and jewels belonging to great-souled (ātman) beings, as well as with armlets, wrist-bracelets, and garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 22. It was well adorned with golden armors, necklaces, and jewels belonging to great-souled (ātman) beings, as well as with armlets, wrist-bracelets, and garlands.

sanskrit: वीरबाहुविसृष्टाभिः शक्तिभिः परिघैरपि ।खड्गैश्च विमलैस्तीक्ष्णैः सशरैश्च शरासनैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: vīrabāhuvisṛṣṭābhiḥ śaktibhiḥ parighairapi ,khaḍgaiśca vimalaistīkṣṇaiḥ saśaraiśca śarāsanaiḥ.
english: It was also adorned with spears (śakti) hurled by the powerful arms of warriors, with iron clubs, with spotless and sharp swords, and with bows accompanied by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 23. It was also adorned with spears (śakti) hurled by the powerful arms of warriors, with iron clubs, with spotless and sharp swords, and with bows accompanied by arrows.

sanskrit: क्रव्यादसंघैर्मुदितैस्तिष्ठद्भिः सहितैः क्वचित् ।क्वचिदाक्रीडमानैश्च शयानैरपरैः क्वचित् ॥२४॥
transliteration: kravyādasaṁghairmuditaistiṣṭhadbhiḥ sahitaiḥ kvacit ,kvacidākrīḍamānaiśca śayānairaparaiḥ kvacit.
english: And here and there, it was filled with joyful groups of scavengers, some standing together, others playing, and still others lying down.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 24. And here and there, it was filled with joyful groups of scavengers, some standing together, others playing, and still others lying down.

sanskrit: एतदेवंविधं वीर संपश्यायोधनं विभो ।पश्यमाना च दह्यामि शोकेनाहं जनार्दन ॥२५॥
transliteration: etadevaṁvidhaṁ vīra saṁpaśyāyodhanaṁ vibho ,paśyamānā ca dahyāmi śokenāhaṁ janārdana.
english: O hero (vīra), O all-pervading Lord (vibhu), behold this kind of battlefield! And seeing it, O Janardana, I am consumed by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 25. O hero (vīra), O all-pervading Lord (vibhu), behold this kind of battlefield! And seeing it, O Janardana, I am consumed by grief.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालानां कुरूणां च विनाशं मधुसूदन ।पञ्चानामिव भूतानां नाहं वधमचिन्तयम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: pāñcālānāṁ kurūṇāṁ ca vināśaṁ madhusūdana ,pañcānāmiva bhūtānāṁ nāhaṁ vadhamacintayam.
english: O Madhusūdana, I did not anticipate the destruction of the Pāñcālas and the Kurus, any more than one would expect the annihilation of the five fundamental elements (bhūta).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 26. O Madhusūdana, I did not anticipate the destruction of the Pāñcālas and the Kurus, any more than one would expect the annihilation of the five fundamental elements (bhūta).

sanskrit: तान्सुपर्णाश्च गृध्राश्च निष्कर्षन्त्यसृगुक्षितान् ।निगृह्य कवचेषूग्रा भक्षयन्ति सहस्रशः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tānsuparṇāśca gṛdhrāśca niṣkarṣantyasṛgukṣitān ,nigṛhya kavaceṣūgrā bhakṣayanti sahasraśaḥ.
english: Eagles and vultures drag those warriors, drenched in blood, from the battlefield. Fierce predators grasp them even in their armor and devour them by the thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 27. Eagles and vultures drag those warriors, drenched in blood, from the battlefield. Fierce predators grasp them even in their armor and devour them by the thousands.

sanskrit: जयद्रथस्य कर्णस्य तथैव द्रोणभीष्मयोः ।अभिमन्योर्विनाशं च कश्चिन्तयितुमर्हति ॥२८॥
transliteration: jayadrathasya karṇasya tathaiva droṇabhīṣmayoḥ ,abhimanyorvināśaṁ ca kaścintayitumarhati.
english: Who could possibly have imagined the destruction of Jayadratha, Karṇa, and likewise, of Droṇa and Bhīṣma, as well as Abhimanyu's ruin?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 28. Who could possibly have imagined the destruction of Jayadratha, Karṇa, and likewise, of Droṇa and Bhīṣma, as well as Abhimanyu's ruin?

sanskrit: अवध्यकल्पान्निहतान्दृष्ट्वाहं मधुसूदन ।गृध्रकङ्कबडश्येनश्वसृगालादनीकृतान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: avadhyakalpānnihatāndṛṣṭvāhaṁ madhusūdana ,gṛdhrakaṅkabaḍaśyenaśvasṛgālādanīkṛtān.
english: O Madhusūdana, when I saw those who were virtually invincible, slain and turned into food for vultures, kites, large hawks, dogs, and jackals...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 29. O Madhusūdana, when I saw those who were virtually invincible, slain and turned into food for vultures, kites, large hawks, dogs, and jackals...

sanskrit: अमर्षवशमापन्नान्दुर्योधनवशे स्थितान् ।पश्येमान्पुरुषव्याघ्रान्संशान्तान्पावकानिव ॥३०॥
transliteration: amarṣavaśamāpannānduryodhanavaśe sthitān ,paśyemānpuruṣavyāghrānsaṁśāntānpāvakāniva.
english: Behold these tiger-like men, who had fallen under the sway of indignation and remained under Duryodhana's control, now extinguished like fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 30. Behold these tiger-like men, who had fallen under the sway of indignation and remained under Duryodhana's control, now extinguished like fires.

sanskrit: शयनान्युचिताः सर्वे मृदूनि विमलानि च ।विपन्नास्तेऽद्य वसुधां विवृतामधिशेरते ॥३१॥
transliteration: śayanānyucitāḥ sarve mṛdūni vimalāni ca ,vipannāste'dya vasudhāṁ vivṛtāmadhiśerate.
english: All those who were accustomed to soft and pure beds, they have perished and today lie upon the bare earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 31. All those who were accustomed to soft and pure beds, they have perished and today lie upon the bare earth.

sanskrit: बन्दिभिः सततं काले स्तुवद्भिरभिनन्दिताः ।शिवानामशिवा घोराः शृण्वन्ति विविधा गिरः ॥३२॥
transliteration: bandibhiḥ satataṁ kāle stuvadbhirabhinanditāḥ ,śivānāmaśivā ghorāḥ śṛṇvanti vividhā giraḥ.
english: Those who were constantly acclaimed by praising bards at the appropriate time now hear various inauspicious and terrible sounds (of jackals).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 32. Those who were constantly acclaimed by praising bards at the appropriate time now hear various inauspicious and terrible sounds (of jackals).

sanskrit: ये पुरा शेरते वीराः शयनेषु यशस्विनः ।चन्दनागुरुदिग्धाङ्गास्तेऽद्य पांसुषु शेरते ॥३३॥
transliteration: ye purā śerate vīrāḥ śayaneṣu yaśasvinaḥ ,candanāgurudigdhāṅgāste'dya pāṁsuṣu śerate.
english: Those glorious heroes, whose bodies were formerly smeared with sandalwood and agaru and who lay on beds, today lie in the dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 33. Those glorious heroes, whose bodies were formerly smeared with sandalwood and agaru and who lay on beds, today lie in the dust.

sanskrit: तेषामाभरणान्येते गृध्रगोमायुवायसाः ।आक्षिपन्त्यशिवा घोरा विनदन्तः पुनः पुनः ॥३४॥
transliteration: teṣāmābharaṇānyete gṛdhragomāyuvāyasāḥ ,ākṣipantyaśivā ghorā vinadantaḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: These dreadful and terrifying vultures, jackals, and crows shriek repeatedly as they snatch away the ornaments from those fallen warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 34. These dreadful and terrifying vultures, jackals, and crows shriek repeatedly as they snatch away the ornaments from those fallen warriors.

sanskrit: चापानि विशिखान्पीतान्निस्त्रिंशान्विमला गदाः ।युद्धाभिमानिनः प्रीता जीवन्त इव बिभ्रति ॥३५॥
transliteration: cāpāni viśikhānpītānnistriṁśānvimalā gadāḥ ,yuddhābhimāninaḥ prītā jīvanta iva bibhrati.
english: The warriors, proud of their combat (yuddha) and still content, hold bows, gleaming arrows, spotless swords, and maces, as if they were still alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 35. The warriors, proud of their combat (yuddha) and still content, hold bows, gleaming arrows, spotless swords, and maces, as if they were still alive.

sanskrit: सुरूपवर्णा बहवः क्रव्यादैरवघट्टिताः ।ऋषभप्रतिरूपाक्षाः शेरते हरितस्रजः ॥३६॥
transliteration: surūpavarṇā bahavaḥ kravyādairavaghaṭṭitāḥ ,ṛṣabhapratirūpākṣāḥ śerate haritasrajaḥ.
english: Many (warriors), once of beautiful form and complexion, lie mangled by carnivores, their bull-like eyes fixed, still adorned with green garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 36. Many (warriors), once of beautiful form and complexion, lie mangled by carnivores, their bull-like eyes fixed, still adorned with green garlands.

sanskrit: अपरे पुनरालिङ्ग्य गदाः परिघबाहवः ।शेरतेऽभिमुखाः शूरा दयिता इव योषितः ॥३७॥
transliteration: apare punarāliṅgya gadāḥ parighabāhavaḥ ,śerate'bhimukhāḥ śūrā dayitā iva yoṣitaḥ.
english: Furthermore, other brave warriors, with arms like iron bars (parigha), lie embracing their maces, facing forward, just as beloved women would embrace their lovers.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 37. Furthermore, other brave warriors, with arms like iron bars (parigha), lie embracing their maces, facing forward, just as beloved women would embrace their lovers.

sanskrit: बिभ्रतः कवचान्यन्ये विमलान्यायुधानि च ।न धर्षयन्ति क्रव्यादा जीवन्तीति जनार्दन ॥३८॥
transliteration: bibhrataḥ kavacānyanye vimalānyāyudhāni ca ,na dharṣayanti kravyādā jīvantīti janārdana.
english: O Janardana, the flesh-eating animals (kravyāda) do not dare to attack those brave warriors, even after they have fallen, who bear pure armors and weapons, as if (iti) thinking they are still alive (jīvanti).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 38. O Janardana, the flesh-eating animals (kravyāda) do not dare to attack those brave warriors, even after they have fallen, who bear pure armors and weapons, as if (iti) thinking they are still alive (jīvanti).

sanskrit: क्रव्यादैः कृष्यमाणानामपरेषां महात्मनाम् ।शातकौम्भ्यः स्रजश्चित्रा विप्रकीर्णाः समन्ततः ॥३९॥
transliteration: kravyādaiḥ kṛṣyamāṇānāmapareṣāṁ mahātmanām ,śātakaumbhyaḥ srajaścitrā viprakīrṇāḥ samantataḥ.
english: Golden (śātakauṁbhya) and colorful (citra) garlands (sraj) lie scattered (viprakīrṇa) all around (samantataḥ) from other great-souled (mahātman) warriors who are being dragged away by the flesh-eating animals (kravyāda).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 39. Golden (śātakauṁbhya) and colorful (citra) garlands (sraj) lie scattered (viprakīrṇa) all around (samantataḥ) from other great-souled (mahātman) warriors who are being dragged away by the flesh-eating animals (kravyāda).

sanskrit: एते गोमायवो भीमा निहतानां यशस्विनाम् ।कण्ठान्तरगतान्हारानाक्षिपन्ति सहस्रशः ॥४०॥
transliteration: ete gomāyavo bhīmā nihatānāṁ yaśasvinām ,kaṇṭhāntaragatānhārānākṣipanti sahasraśaḥ.
english: These terrible (bhīma) jackals (gomāyava) snatch away (ākṣipanti) by the thousands (sahasraśaḥ) the necklaces (hāra) that adorn the necks of the slain (nihata) and glorious (yaśasvin) warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 40. These terrible (bhīma) jackals (gomāyava) snatch away (ākṣipanti) by the thousands (sahasraśaḥ) the necklaces (hāra) that adorn the necks of the slain (nihata) and glorious (yaśasvin) warriors.

sanskrit: सर्वेष्वपररात्रेषु याननन्दन्त बन्दिनः ।स्तुतिभिश्च परार्ध्याभिरुपचारैश्च शिक्षिताः ॥४१॥
transliteration: sarveṣvapararātreṣu yānanandanta bandinaḥ ,stutibhiśca parārdhyābhirupacāraiśca śikṣitāḥ.
english: In all the late nights (apararātreṣu), the bards (bandin), who were skilled (śikṣita) with praises (stuti) and most excellent (parārghya) services (upacāra), used to delight (anandanta) those (warriors).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 41. In all the late nights (apararātreṣu), the bards (bandin), who were skilled (śikṣita) with praises (stuti) and most excellent (parārghya) services (upacāra), used to delight (anandanta) those (warriors).

sanskrit: तानिमाः परिदेवन्ति दुःखार्ताः परमाङ्गनाः ।कृपणं वृष्णिशार्दूल दुःखशोकार्दिता भृशम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tānimāḥ paridevanti duḥkhārtāḥ paramāṅganāḥ ,kṛpaṇaṁ vṛṣṇiśārdūla duḥkhaśokārditā bhṛśam.
english: O best of the Vṛṣṇis, these noble women, deeply distressed by sorrow and grief, lament piteously over those [circumstances].
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 42. O best of the Vṛṣṇis, these noble women, deeply distressed by sorrow and grief, lament piteously over those [circumstances].

sanskrit: रक्तोत्पलवनानीव विभान्ति रुचिराणि वै ।मुखानि परमस्त्रीणां परिशुष्काणि केशव ॥४३॥
transliteration: raktotpalavanānīva vibhānti rucirāṇi vai ,mukhāni paramastrīṇāṁ pariśuṣkāṇi keśava.
english: O Keśava, the beautiful faces of the noble women, though now completely withered, certainly shine like groves of red lotuses.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 43. O Keśava, the beautiful faces of the noble women, though now completely withered, certainly shine like groves of red lotuses.

sanskrit: रुदितोपरता ह्येता ध्यायन्त्यः संपरिप्लुताः ।कुरुस्त्रियोऽभिगच्छन्ति तेन तेनैव दुःखिताः ॥४४॥
transliteration: ruditoparatā hyetā dhyāyantyaḥ saṁpariplutāḥ ,kurustriyo'bhigacchanti tena tenaiva duḥkhitāḥ.
english: Indeed, these Kuru women, having ceased their weeping, yet completely overwhelmed and distressed, wander about, meditating on that very [suffering].
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 44. Indeed, these Kuru women, having ceased their weeping, yet completely overwhelmed and distressed, wander about, meditating on that very [suffering].

sanskrit: एतान्यादित्यवर्णानि तपनीयनिभानि च ।रोषरोदनताम्राणि वक्त्राणि कुरुयोषिताम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: etānyādityavarṇāni tapanīyanibhāni ca ,roṣarodanatāmrāṇi vaktrāṇi kuruyoṣitām.
english: And these faces of the Kuru women, which were sun-colored and brilliant like molten gold, are now coppery red from anger and weeping.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 45. And these faces of the Kuru women, which were sun-colored and brilliant like molten gold, are now coppery red from anger and weeping.

sanskrit: आसामपरिपूर्णार्थं निशम्य परिदेवितम् ।इतरेतरसंक्रन्दान्न विजानन्ति योषितः ॥४६॥
transliteration: āsāmaparipūrṇārthaṁ niśamya paridevitam ,itaretarasaṁkrandānna vijānanti yoṣitaḥ.
english: After hearing their own incomplete lamentations, the women, amid their mutual cries, are unable to comprehend (what has happened).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 46. After hearing their own incomplete lamentations, the women, amid their mutual cries, are unable to comprehend (what has happened).

sanskrit: एता दीर्घमिवोच्छ्वस्य विक्रुश्य च विलप्य च ।विस्पन्दमाना दुःखेन वीरा जहति जीवितम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: etā dīrghamivocchvasya vikruśya ca vilapya ca ,vispandamānā duḥkhena vīrā jahati jīvitam.
english: These women, sighing deeply as it were, and having cried out and wailed, abandon their lives, trembling with sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 47. These women, sighing deeply as it were, and having cried out and wailed, abandon their lives, trembling with sorrow.

sanskrit: बह्व्यो दृष्ट्वा शरीराणि क्रोशन्ति विलपन्ति च ।पाणिभिश्चापरा घ्नन्ति शिरांसि मृदुपाणयः ॥४८॥
transliteration: bahvyo dṛṣṭvā śarīrāṇi krośanti vilapanti ca ,pāṇibhiścāparā ghnanti śirāṁsi mṛdupāṇayaḥ.
english: Many women, having seen the bodies, cry out and lament. And others, with their soft hands, strike their heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 48. Many women, having seen the bodies, cry out and lament. And others, with their soft hands, strike their heads.

sanskrit: शिरोभिः पतितैर्हस्तैः सर्वाङ्गैर्यूथशः कृतैः ।इतरेतरसंपृक्तैराकीर्णा भाति मेदिनी ॥४९॥
transliteration: śirobhiḥ patitairhastaiḥ sarvāṅgairyūthaśaḥ kṛtaiḥ ,itaretarasaṁpṛktairākīrṇā bhāti medinī.
english: The earth appears strewn with fallen heads, hands, and all limbs, which are piled in heaps and mingled with one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 49. The earth appears strewn with fallen heads, hands, and all limbs, which are piled in heaps and mingled with one another.

sanskrit: विशिरस्कानथो कायान्दृष्ट्वा घोराभिनन्दिनः ।मुह्यन्त्यनुचिता नार्यो विदेहानि शिरांसि च ॥५०॥
transliteration: viśiraskānatho kāyāndṛṣṭvā ghorābhinandinaḥ ,muhyantyanucitā nāryo videhāni śirāṁsi ca.
english: Upon seeing headless bodies and detached heads - objects that those delighting in horror would embrace - the unaccustomed women faint.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 50. Upon seeing headless bodies and detached heads - objects that those delighting in horror would embrace - the unaccustomed women faint.

sanskrit: शिरः कायेन संधाय प्रेक्षमाणा विचेतसः ।अपश्यन्त्यो परं तत्र नेदमस्येति दुःखिताः ॥५१॥
transliteration: śiraḥ kāyena saṁdhāya prekṣamāṇā vicetasaḥ ,apaśyantyo paraṁ tatra nedamasyeti duḥkhitāḥ.
english: Distraught, looking on and trying to rejoin a head with its body, these women, unable to find the correct match there, are distressed, thinking, "This head (or body) does not belong to this one."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 51. Distraught, looking on and trying to rejoin a head with its body, these women, unable to find the correct match there, are distressed, thinking, "This head (or body) does not belong to this one."

sanskrit: बाहूरुचरणानन्यान्विशिखोन्मथितान्पृथक् ।संदधत्योऽसुखाविष्टा मूर्छन्त्येताः पुनः पुनः ॥५२॥
transliteration: bāhūrucaraṇānanyānviśikhonmathitānpṛthak ,saṁdadhatyo'sukhāviṣṭā mūrchantyetāḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: These women, overcome with distress, repeatedly faint as they try to rejoin other arms, thighs, and feet that lie separately, shattered by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 52. These women, overcome with distress, repeatedly faint as they try to rejoin other arms, thighs, and feet that lie separately, shattered by arrows.

sanskrit: उत्कृत्तशिरसश्चान्यान्विजग्धान्मृगपक्षिभिः ।दृष्ट्वा काश्चिन्न जानन्ति भर्तॄन्भरतयोषितः ॥५३॥
transliteration: utkṛttaśirasaścānyānvijagdhānmṛgapakṣibhiḥ ,dṛṣṭvā kāścinna jānanti bhartṝnbharatayoṣitaḥ.
english: And seeing others whose heads have been severed and bodies devoured by beasts and birds, some of these Bharata women cannot recognize their husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 53. And seeing others whose heads have been severed and bodies devoured by beasts and birds, some of these Bharata women cannot recognize their husbands.

sanskrit: पाणिभिश्चापरा घ्नन्ति शिरांसि मधुसूदन ।प्रेक्ष्य भ्रातॄन्पितॄन्पुत्रान्पतींश्च निहतान्परैः ॥५४॥
transliteration: pāṇibhiścāparā ghnanti śirāṁsi madhusūdana ,prekṣya bhrātṝnpitṝnputrānpatīṁśca nihatānparaiḥ.
english: O Madhusūdana, others will strike heads with their hands after seeing their brothers, fathers, sons, and husbands slain by enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 54. O Madhusūdana, others will strike heads with their hands after seeing their brothers, fathers, sons, and husbands slain by enemies.

sanskrit: बाहुभिश्च सखड्गैश्च शिरोभिश्च सकुण्डलैः ।अगम्यकल्पा पृथिवी मांसशोणितकर्दमा ॥५५॥
transliteration: bāhubhiśca sakhaḍgaiśca śirobhiśca sakuṇḍalaiḥ ,agamyakalpā pṛthivī māṁsaśoṇitakardamā.
english: The earth becomes almost impassable, its mud formed of flesh and blood, strewn with arms, swords, and heads adorned with earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 55. The earth becomes almost impassable, its mud formed of flesh and blood, strewn with arms, swords, and heads adorned with earrings.

sanskrit: न दुःखेषूचिताः पूर्वं दुःखं गाहन्त्यनिन्दिताः ।भ्रातृभिः पितृभिः पुत्रैरुपकीर्णां वसुंधराम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: na duḥkheṣūcitāḥ pūrvaṁ duḥkhaṁ gāhantyaninditāḥ ,bhrātṛbhiḥ pitṛbhiḥ putrairupakīrṇāṁ vasuṁdharām.
english: The blameless women, who were not accustomed to suffering before, now plunge into great sorrow, beholding the earth strewn with (the bodies of) their brothers, fathers, and sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 56. The blameless women, who were not accustomed to suffering before, now plunge into great sorrow, beholding the earth strewn with (the bodies of) their brothers, fathers, and sons.

sanskrit: यूथानीव किशोरीणां सुकेशीनां जनार्दन ।स्नुषाणां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पश्य वृन्दान्यनेकशः ॥५७॥
transliteration: yūthānīva kiśorīṇāṁ sukeśīnāṁ janārdana ,snuṣāṇāṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya paśya vṛndānyanekaśaḥ.
english: O Janārdana, behold the many groups of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's daughters-in-law, who are like herds of young girls with beautiful hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 57. O Janārdana, behold the many groups of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's daughters-in-law, who are like herds of young girls with beautiful hair.

sanskrit: अतो दुःखतरं किं नु केशव प्रतिभाति मे ।यदिमाः कुर्वते सर्वा रूपमुच्चावचं स्त्रियः ॥५८॥
transliteration: ato duḥkhataraṁ kiṁ nu keśava pratibhāti me ,yadimāḥ kurvate sarvā rūpamuccāvacaṁ striyaḥ.
english: O Keśava, what indeed could appear more sorrowful to me than the fact that all these women assume varied forms?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 58. O Keśava, what indeed could appear more sorrowful to me than the fact that all these women assume varied forms?

sanskrit: नूनमाचरितं पापं मया पूर्वेषु जन्मसु ।या पश्यामि हतान्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्भ्रातॄंश्च केशव ।एवमार्ता विलपती ददर्श निहतं सुतम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: nūnamācaritaṁ pāpaṁ mayā pūrveṣu janmasu ,yā paśyāmi hatānputrānpautrānbhrātṝṁśca keśava ,evamārtā vilapatī dadarśa nihataṁ sutam.
english: Surely, a great sin (pāpam) was committed by me in previous lives (janmasu), O Keśava, for I see my sons, grandsons, and brothers slain. Thus distressed and lamenting, she saw her slain son.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 16 Verse 59. Surely, a great sin (pāpam) was committed by me in previous lives (janmasu), O Keśava, for I see my sons, grandsons, and brothers slain. Thus distressed and lamenting, she saw her slain son.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो दुर्योधनं दृष्ट्वा गान्धारी शोककर्शिता ।सहसा न्यपतद्भूमौ छिन्नेव कदली वने ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato duryodhanaṁ dṛṣṭvā gāndhārī śokakarśitā ,sahasā nyapatadbhūmau chinneva kadalī vane.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Gāndhārī, weakened by grief, upon seeing Duryodhana, suddenly collapsed to the ground, like a plantain tree felled in a forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Gāndhārī, weakened by grief, upon seeing Duryodhana, suddenly collapsed to the ground, like a plantain tree felled in a forest.

sanskrit: सा तु लब्ध्वा पुनः संज्ञां विक्रुश्य च पुनः पुनः ।दुर्योधनमभिप्रेक्ष्य शयानं रुधिरोक्षितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: sā tu labdhvā punaḥ saṁjñāṁ vikruśya ca punaḥ punaḥ ,duryodhanamabhiprekṣya śayānaṁ rudhirokṣitam.
english: But she, having regained consciousness, and having cried out again and again, looked closely at Duryodhana, who was lying there drenched in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. But she, having regained consciousness, and having cried out again and again, looked closely at Duryodhana, who was lying there drenched in blood.

sanskrit: परिष्वज्य च गान्धारी कृपणं पर्यदेवयत् ।हा हा पुत्रेति शोकार्ता विललापाकुलेन्द्रिया ॥३॥
transliteration: pariṣvajya ca gāndhārī kṛpaṇaṁ paryadevayat ,hā hā putreti śokārtā vilalāpākulendriyā.
