english: And embracing (her fallen sons), Gandhari lamented piteously. Overwhelmed by grief (śokārtā), and with her senses bewildered, she wailed, "Alas, alas, my son!"
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. And embracing (her fallen sons), Gandhari lamented piteously. Overwhelmed by grief (śokārtā), and with her senses bewildered, she wailed, "Alas, alas, my son!"

sanskrit: सुगूढजत्रु विपुलं हारनिष्कनिषेवितम् ।वारिणा नेत्रजेनोरः सिञ्चन्ती शोकतापिता ।समीपस्थं हृषीकेशमिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥४॥
transliteration: sugūḍhajatru vipulaṁ hāraniṣkaniṣevitam ,vāriṇā netrajenoraḥ siñcantī śokatāpitā ,samīpasthaṁ hṛṣīkeśamidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: With her broad chest, whose collarbones were well-hidden and which was accustomed to being adorned with necklaces and golden coins (hāranīṣkanisevitam), now drenched by the tears (netrajena vāriṇā) flowing from her eyes, and tormented by grief (śokatāpitā), she addressed these words to Krishna (hṛṣīkeśa), who stood nearby.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. With her broad chest, whose collarbones were well-hidden and which was accustomed to being adorned with necklaces and golden coins (hāranīṣkanisevitam), now drenched by the tears (netrajena vāriṇā) flowing from her eyes, and tormented by grief (śokatāpitā), she addressed these words to Krishna (hṛṣīkeśa), who stood nearby.

sanskrit: उपस्थितेऽस्मिन्संग्रामे ज्ञातीनां संक्षये विभो ।मामयं प्राह वार्ष्णेय प्राञ्जलिर्नृपसत्तमः ।अस्मिञ्ज्ञातिसमुद्धर्षे जयमम्बा ब्रवीतु मे ॥५॥
transliteration: upasthite'sminsaṁgrāme jñātīnāṁ saṁkṣaye vibho ,māmayaṁ prāha vārṣṇeya prāñjalirnṛpasattamaḥ ,asmiñjñātisamuddharṣe jayamambā bravītu me.
english: O Lord (vibho)! When this battle (saṅgrāme) involving the annihilation (saṃkṣaye) of our kinsmen (jñātīnām) was imminent, this supreme king (nṛpasattamaḥ) (Duryodhana), with folded hands (prāñjaliḥ), said to me, "O Mother, may you declare victory (jayam) for me in this great struggle (jñātisamuddharṣe) among kinsmen."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. O Lord (vibho)! When this battle (saṅgrāme) involving the annihilation (saṃkṣaye) of our kinsmen (jñātīnām) was imminent, this supreme king (nṛpasattamaḥ) (Duryodhana), with folded hands (prāñjaliḥ), said to me, "O Mother, may you declare victory (jayam) for me in this great struggle (jñātisamuddharṣe) among kinsmen."

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते जानती सर्वमहं स्वं व्यसनागमम् ।अब्रुवं पुरुषव्याघ्र यतो धर्मस्ततो जयः ॥६॥
transliteration: ityukte jānatī sarvamahaṁ svaṁ vyasanāgamam ,abruvaṁ puruṣavyāghra yato dharmastato jayaḥ.
english: When this was said (ityukte), I, knowing fully well my own impending calamity (vyasanāgamam), spoke (abruvam), "O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra) (Krishna)! Where there is natural law (dharma), there is victory (jaya)."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. When this was said (ityukte), I, knowing fully well my own impending calamity (vyasanāgamam), spoke (abruvam), "O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra) (Krishna)! Where there is natural law (dharma), there is victory (jaya)."

sanskrit: यथा न युध्यमानस्त्वं संप्रमुह्यसि पुत्रक ।ध्रुवं शस्त्रजिताँल्लोकान्प्राप्तास्यमरवद्विभो ॥७॥
transliteration: yathā na yudhyamānastvaṁ saṁpramuhyasi putraka ,dhruvaṁ śastrajitāँllokānprāptāsyamaravadvibho.
english: So that you, my son, do not become utterly bewildered while fighting. Indeed, O mighty one, you will attain worlds conquered by weapons, living like the immortals.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. So that you, my son, do not become utterly bewildered while fighting. Indeed, O mighty one, you will attain worlds conquered by weapons, living like the immortals.

sanskrit: इत्येवमब्रुवं पूर्वं नैनं शोचामि वै प्रभो ।धृतराष्ट्रं तु शोचामि कृपणं हतबान्धवम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ityevamabruvaṁ pūrvaṁ nainaṁ śocāmi vai prabho ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ tu śocāmi kṛpaṇaṁ hatabāndhavam.
english: Thus I spoke before. Indeed, O lord, I do not grieve for him, but I do grieve for Dhritarashtra, the wretched man whose kinsmen have been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. Thus I spoke before. Indeed, O lord, I do not grieve for him, but I do grieve for Dhritarashtra, the wretched man whose kinsmen have been slain.

sanskrit: अमर्षणं युधां श्रेष्ठं कृतास्त्रं युद्धदुर्मदम् ।शयानं वीरशयने पश्य माधव मे सुतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: amarṣaṇaṁ yudhāṁ śreṣṭhaṁ kṛtāstraṁ yuddhadurmadam ,śayānaṁ vīraśayane paśya mādhava me sutam.
english: Behold, O Madhava, my son - impatient of insult, the best among warriors, skilled in arms, formidable in battle - now lying on a hero's bed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. Behold, O Madhava, my son - impatient of insult, the best among warriors, skilled in arms, formidable in battle - now lying on a hero's bed.

sanskrit: योऽयं मूर्धावसिक्तानामग्रे याति परंतपः ।सोऽयं पांसुषु शेतेऽद्य पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yo'yaṁ mūrdhāvasiktānāmagre yāti paraṁtapaḥ ,so'yaṁ pāṁsuṣu śete'dya paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: This very tormentor of foes, who used to walk at the forefront of consecrated kings, now today lies in the dust. Behold the relentless change (paryaya) brought about by time.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. This very tormentor of foes, who used to walk at the forefront of consecrated kings, now today lies in the dust. Behold the relentless change (paryaya) brought about by time.

sanskrit: ध्रुवं दुर्योधनो वीरो गतिं नसुलभां गतः ।तथा ह्यभिमुखः शेते शयने वीरसेविते ॥११॥
transliteration: dhruvaṁ duryodhano vīro gatiṁ nasulabhāṁ gataḥ ,tathā hyabhimukhaḥ śete śayane vīrasevite.
english: Surely, the heroic Duryodhana has attained a difficult-to-achieve state. Indeed, he lies facing forward on a bed honored by warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. Surely, the heroic Duryodhana has attained a difficult-to-achieve state. Indeed, he lies facing forward on a bed honored by warriors.

sanskrit: यं पुरा पर्युपासीना रमयन्ति महीक्षितः ।महीतलस्थं निहतं गृध्रास्तं पर्युपासते ॥१२॥
transliteration: yaṁ purā paryupāsīnā ramayanti mahīkṣitaḥ ,mahītalasthaṁ nihataṁ gṛdhrāstaṁ paryupāsate.
english: He whom kings formerly sat around and delighted, that same person, now lying slain on the ground, vultures attend.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. He whom kings formerly sat around and delighted, that same person, now lying slain on the ground, vultures attend.

sanskrit: यं पुरा व्यजनैरग्र्यैरुपवीजन्ति योषितः ।तमद्य पक्षव्यजनैरुपवीजन्ति पक्षिणः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yaṁ purā vyajanairagryairupavījanti yoṣitaḥ ,tamadya pakṣavyajanairupavījanti pakṣiṇaḥ.
english: He whom women formerly fanned with excellent fans, him today birds fan with their wings acting as fans.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. He whom women formerly fanned with excellent fans, him today birds fan with their wings acting as fans.

sanskrit: एष शेते महाबाहुर्बलवान्सत्यविक्रमः ।सिंहेनेव द्विपः संख्ये भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥१४॥
transliteration: eṣa śete mahābāhurbalavānsatyavikramaḥ ,siṁheneva dvipaḥ saṁkhye bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: This mighty-armed, powerful, truly valorous one lies here, felled in battle by Bhimasena, just as an elephant is struck down by a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. This mighty-armed, powerful, truly valorous one lies here, felled in battle by Bhimasena, just as an elephant is struck down by a lion.

sanskrit: पश्य दुर्योधनं कृष्ण शयानं रुधिरोक्षितम् ।निहतं भीमसेनेन गदामुद्यम्य भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: paśya duryodhanaṁ kṛṣṇa śayānaṁ rudhirokṣitam ,nihataṁ bhīmasenena gadāmudyamya bhārata.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, O descendant of Bharata, behold Duryodhana, lying drenched in blood, slain by Bhīmasena who had raised his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. O Kṛṣṇa, O descendant of Bharata, behold Duryodhana, lying drenched in blood, slain by Bhīmasena who had raised his mace.

sanskrit: अक्षौहिणीर्महाबाहुर्दश चैकां च केशव ।अनयद्यः पुरा संख्ये सोऽनयान्निधनं गतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇīrmahābāhurdaśa caikāṁ ca keśava ,anayadyaḥ purā saṁkhye so'nayānnidhanaṁ gataḥ.
english: O Keśava, that mighty-armed man, who previously led eleven (ten and one) akṣauhiṇīs into battle, he has now met his death due to misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. O Keśava, that mighty-armed man, who previously led eleven (ten and one) akṣauhiṇīs into battle, he has now met his death due to misfortune.

sanskrit: एष दुर्योधनः शेते महेष्वासो महारथः ।शार्दूल इव सिंहेन भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥१७॥
transliteration: eṣa duryodhanaḥ śete maheṣvāso mahārathaḥ ,śārdūla iva siṁhena bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: This Duryodhana, a great archer and a great warrior, lies here, struck down by Bhīmasena, just as a tiger is felled by a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. This Duryodhana, a great archer and a great warrior, lies here, struck down by Bhīmasena, just as a tiger is felled by a lion.

sanskrit: विदुरं ह्यवमन्यैष पितरं चैव मन्दभाक् ।बालो वृद्धावमानेन मन्दो मृत्युवशं गतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: viduraṁ hyavamanyaiṣa pitaraṁ caiva mandabhāk ,bālo vṛddhāvamānena mando mṛtyuvaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: Indeed, this ill-fated, foolish person, having disregarded Vidura and even his own father, has fallen under the sway of death due to his disrespect for elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. Indeed, this ill-fated, foolish person, having disregarded Vidura and even his own father, has fallen under the sway of death due to his disrespect for elders.

sanskrit: निःसपत्ना मही यस्य त्रयोदश समाः स्थिता ।स शेते निहतो भूमौ पुत्रो मे पृथिवीपतिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: niḥsapatnā mahī yasya trayodaśa samāḥ sthitā ,sa śete nihato bhūmau putro me pṛthivīpatiḥ.
english: My son, the lord of the earth (pṛthivīpati), for whom the entire land remained undisputed for thirteen years, now lies slain on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. My son, the lord of the earth (pṛthivīpati), for whom the entire land remained undisputed for thirteen years, now lies slain on the ground.

sanskrit: अपश्यं कृष्ण पृथिवीं धार्तराष्ट्रानुशासनात् ।पूर्णां हस्तिगवाश्वस्य वार्ष्णेय न तु तच्चिरम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: apaśyaṁ kṛṣṇa pṛthivīṁ dhārtarāṣṭrānuśāsanāt ,pūrṇāṁ hastigavāśvasya vārṣṇeya na tu tacciram.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, O Vārṣṇeya, I saw the earth, teeming with elephants, cows, and horses under the command of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons; but that did not last long.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. O Kṛṣṇa, O Vārṣṇeya, I saw the earth, teeming with elephants, cows, and horses under the command of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons; but that did not last long.

sanskrit: तामेवाद्य महाबाहो पश्याम्यन्यानुशासनात् ।हीनां हस्तिगवाश्वेन किं नु जीवामि माधव ॥२१॥
transliteration: tāmevādya mahābāho paśyāmyanyānuśāsanāt ,hīnāṁ hastigavāśvena kiṁ nu jīvāmi mādhava.
english: O mighty-armed (Mahābāho), O Mādhava, today I see that very same earth, now bereft of elephants, cows, and horses, under the command of others. How can I possibly continue to live?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. O mighty-armed (Mahābāho), O Mādhava, today I see that very same earth, now bereft of elephants, cows, and horses, under the command of others. How can I possibly continue to live?

sanskrit: इदं कृच्छ्रतरं पश्य पुत्रस्यापि वधान्मम ।यदिमाः पर्युपासन्ते हताञ्शूरान्रणे स्त्रियः ॥२२॥
transliteration: idaṁ kṛcchrataraṁ paśya putrasyāpi vadhānmama ,yadimāḥ paryupāsante hatāñśūrānraṇe striyaḥ.
english: Behold this, which is even more agonizing than the death of my son: these women are attending to the fallen heroes on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. Behold this, which is even more agonizing than the death of my son: these women are attending to the fallen heroes on the battlefield.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्णकेशां सुश्रोणीं दुर्योधनभुजाङ्कगाम् ।रुक्मवेदीनिभां पश्य कृष्ण लक्ष्मणमातरम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: prakīrṇakeśāṁ suśroṇīṁ duryodhanabhujāṅkagām ,rukmavedīnibhāṁ paśya kṛṣṇa lakṣmaṇamātaram.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, behold Lakṣmaṇa's mother (Bhanumati), with her hair disheveled and beautiful hips, now lying on Duryodhana's arm, she who once resembled a golden altar.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. O Kṛṣṇa, behold Lakṣmaṇa's mother (Bhanumati), with her hair disheveled and beautiful hips, now lying on Duryodhana's arm, she who once resembled a golden altar.

sanskrit: नूनमेषा पुरा बाला जीवमाने महाभुजे ।भुजावाश्रित्य रमते सुभुजस्य मनस्विनी ॥२४॥
transliteration: nūnameṣā purā bālā jīvamāne mahābhuje ,bhujāvāśritya ramate subhujasya manasvinī.
english: Surely, this spirited young woman (Bhanumati) formerly delighted, relying on the arms of the great-armed, strong-armed (Duryodhana) while he was alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 24. Surely, this spirited young woman (Bhanumati) formerly delighted, relying on the arms of the great-armed, strong-armed (Duryodhana) while he was alive.

sanskrit: कथं तु शतधा नेदं हृदयं मम दीर्यते ।पश्यन्त्या निहतं पुत्रं पुत्रेण सहितं रणे ॥२५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ tu śatadhā nedaṁ hṛdayaṁ mama dīryate ,paśyantyā nihataṁ putraṁ putreṇa sahitaṁ raṇe.
english: But why does not my heart burst into a hundred pieces, as I (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) see my son (Duryodhana) slain in battle, along with his own son (Lakṣmaṇa)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 25. But why does not my heart burst into a hundred pieces, as I (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) see my son (Duryodhana) slain in battle, along with his own son (Lakṣmaṇa)?

sanskrit: पुत्रं रुधिरसंसिक्तमुपजिघ्रत्यनिन्दिता ।दुर्योधनं तु वामोरूः पाणिना परिमार्जति ॥२६॥
transliteration: putraṁ rudhirasaṁsiktamupajighratyaninditā ,duryodhanaṁ tu vāmorūḥ pāṇinā parimārjati.
english: The blameless woman (Bhanumati) sniffs her son (Lakṣmaṇa), who is drenched in blood. And with her hand, the woman with beautiful thighs caresses Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 26. The blameless woman (Bhanumati) sniffs her son (Lakṣmaṇa), who is drenched in blood. And with her hand, the woman with beautiful thighs caresses Duryodhana.

sanskrit: किं नु शोचति भर्तारं पुत्रं चैषा मनस्विनी ।तथा ह्यवस्थिता भाति पुत्रं चाप्यभिवीक्ष्य सा ॥२७॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu śocati bhartāraṁ putraṁ caiṣā manasvinī ,tathā hyavasthitā bhāti putraṁ cāpyabhivīkṣya sā.
english: Why, indeed, does this high-minded woman grieve for her husband and son? For she appears in such a state, having just looked upon her son.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 27. Why, indeed, does this high-minded woman grieve for her husband and son? For she appears in such a state, having just looked upon her son.

sanskrit: स्वशिरः पञ्चशाखाभ्यामभिहत्यायतेक्षणा ।पतत्युरसि वीरस्य कुरुराजस्य माधव ॥२८॥
transliteration: svaśiraḥ pañcaśākhābhyāmabhihatyāyatekṣaṇā ,patatyurasi vīrasya kururājasya mādhava.
english: O Madhava, with wide eyes, having struck her own head with her two hands, she falls onto the chest of the hero, the king of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 28. O Madhava, with wide eyes, having struck her own head with her two hands, she falls onto the chest of the hero, the king of the Kurus.

sanskrit: पुण्डरीकनिभा भाति पुण्डरीकान्तरप्रभा ।मुखं विमृज्य पुत्रस्य भर्तुश्चैव तपस्विनी ॥२९॥
transliteration: puṇḍarīkanibhā bhāti puṇḍarīkāntaraprabhā ,mukhaṁ vimṛjya putrasya bhartuścaiva tapasvinī.
english: The pitiable woman, whose appearance is lotus-like and whose brilliance is like the inner part of a lotus, wipes the faces of her son and her husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 29. The pitiable woman, whose appearance is lotus-like and whose brilliance is like the inner part of a lotus, wipes the faces of her son and her husband.

sanskrit: यदि चाप्यागमाः सन्ति यदि वा श्रुतयस्तथा ।ध्रुवं लोकानवाप्तोऽयं नृपो बाहुबलार्जितान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: yadi cāpyāgamāḥ santi yadi vā śrutayastathā ,dhruvaṁ lokānavāpto'yaṁ nṛpo bāhubalārjitān.
english: If there are indeed traditions (āgamas), or if there are sacred texts (śrutis) that state it to be so, then this king has certainly attained worlds earned by the strength of his arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 17 Verse 30. If there are indeed traditions (āgamas), or if there are sacred texts (śrutis) that state it to be so, then this king has certainly attained worlds earned by the strength of his arms.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।पश्य माधव पुत्रान्मे शतसंख्याञ्जितक्लमान् ।गदया भीमसेनेन भूयिष्ठं निहतान्रणे ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,paśya mādhava putrānme śatasaṁkhyāñjitaklamān ,gadayā bhīmasenena bhūyiṣṭhaṁ nihatānraṇe.
english: Gandhari said: "O Madhava, behold my hundred sons, who had conquered weariness, mostly slain in battle by Bhimasena with his mace."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. Gandhari said: "O Madhava, behold my hundred sons, who had conquered weariness, mostly slain in battle by Bhimasena with his mace."

sanskrit: इदं दुःखतरं मेऽद्य यदिमा मुक्तमूर्धजाः ।हतपुत्रा रणे बालाः परिधावन्ति मे स्नुषाः ॥२॥
transliteration: idaṁ duḥkhataraṁ me'dya yadimā muktamūrdhajāḥ ,hataputrā raṇe bālāḥ paridhāvanti me snuṣāḥ.
english: This is even more sorrowful for me today, that these young daughters-in-law of mine, whose sons have been killed in battle, wander about with their hair disheveled.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. This is even more sorrowful for me today, that these young daughters-in-law of mine, whose sons have been killed in battle, wander about with their hair disheveled.

sanskrit: प्रासादतलचारिण्यश्चरणैर्भूषणान्वितैः ।आपन्ना यत्स्पृशन्तीमा रुधिरार्द्रां वसुंधराम् ॥३॥
transliteration: prāsādatalacāriṇyaścaraṇairbhūṣaṇānvitaiḥ ,āpannā yatspṛśantīmā rudhirārdrāṁ vasuṁdharām.
english: It is distressing that these women, who once walked on palace terraces with their feet adorned with ornaments, now in their distress, touch this blood-soaked earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. It is distressing that these women, who once walked on palace terraces with their feet adorned with ornaments, now in their distress, touch this blood-soaked earth.

sanskrit: गृध्रानुत्सारयन्त्यश्च गोमायून्वायसांस्तथा ।शोकेनार्ता विघूर्णन्त्यो मत्ता इव चरन्त्युत ॥४॥
transliteration: gṛdhrānutsārayantyaśca gomāyūnvāyasāṁstathā ,śokenārtā vighūrṇantyo mattā iva carantyuta.
english: And driving away vultures, jackals, and crows, these women, afflicted by grief, stagger about as if maddened.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. And driving away vultures, jackals, and crows, these women, afflicted by grief, stagger about as if maddened.

sanskrit: एषान्या त्वनवद्याङ्गी करसंमितमध्यमा ।घोरं तद्वैशसं दृष्ट्वा निपतत्यतिदुःखिता ॥५॥
transliteration: eṣānyā tvanavadyāṅgī karasaṁmitamadhyamā ,ghoraṁ tadvaiśasaṁ dṛṣṭvā nipatatyatiduḥkhitā.
english: This other woman, whose limbs are faultless and whose waist is like a fist, falls down exceedingly distressed after seeing that dreadful slaughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. This other woman, whose limbs are faultless and whose waist is like a fist, falls down exceedingly distressed after seeing that dreadful slaughter.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा मे पार्थिवसुतामेतां लक्ष्मणमातरम् ।राजपुत्रीं महाबाहो मनो न व्युपशाम्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā me pārthivasutāmetāṁ lakṣmaṇamātaram ,rājaputrīṁ mahābāho mano na vyupaśāmyati.
english: O mighty-armed one, after seeing this king's daughter, Lakṣmaṇa's mother, this princess, my mind does not become calm.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. O mighty-armed one, after seeing this king's daughter, Lakṣmaṇa's mother, this princess, my mind does not become calm.

sanskrit: भ्रातॄंश्चान्याः पतींश्चान्याः पुत्रांश्च निहतान्भुवि ।दृष्ट्वा परिपतन्त्येताः प्रगृह्य सुभुजा भुजान् ॥७॥
transliteration: bhrātṝṁścānyāḥ patīṁścānyāḥ putrāṁśca nihatānbhuvi ,dṛṣṭvā paripatantyetāḥ pragṛhya subhujā bhujān.
english: These women with beautiful arms, having seen their brothers, and others their husbands, and still others their sons slain on the earth, fall down seizing their own arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. These women with beautiful arms, having seen their brothers, and others their husbands, and still others their sons slain on the earth, fall down seizing their own arms.

sanskrit: मध्यमानां तु नारीणां वृद्धानां चापराजित ।आक्रन्दं हतबन्धूनां दारुणे वैशसे शृणु ॥८॥
transliteration: madhyamānāṁ tu nārīṇāṁ vṛddhānāṁ cāparājita ,ākrandaṁ hatabandhūnāṁ dāruṇe vaiśase śṛṇu.
english: O unconquered one, listen to the lamentation of the middle-aged women and the old women, whose relatives have been slain in the dreadful slaughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. O unconquered one, listen to the lamentation of the middle-aged women and the old women, whose relatives have been slain in the dreadful slaughter.

sanskrit: रथनीडानि देहांश्च हतानां गजवाजिनाम् ।आश्रिताः श्रममोहार्ताः स्थिताः पश्य महाबल ॥९॥
transliteration: rathanīḍāni dehāṁśca hatānāṁ gajavājinām ,āśritāḥ śramamohārtāḥ sthitāḥ paśya mahābala.
english: O mighty one, behold those who are distressed by fatigue and delusion, standing amidst the shattered chariots and the bodies of slain elephants and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. O mighty one, behold those who are distressed by fatigue and delusion, standing amidst the shattered chariots and the bodies of slain elephants and horses.

sanskrit: अन्या चापहृतं कायाच्चारुकुण्डलमुन्नसम् ।स्वस्य बन्धोः शिरः कृष्ण गृहीत्वा पश्य तिष्ठति ॥१०॥
transliteration: anyā cāpahṛtaṁ kāyāccārukuṇḍalamunnasam ,svasya bandhoḥ śiraḥ kṛṣṇa gṛhītvā paśya tiṣṭhati.
english: And another woman, O Kṛṣṇa, behold! She stands, having taken the prominent head of her own relative, a head that was adorned with a beautiful earring and had been removed from the body.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. And another woman, O Kṛṣṇa, behold! She stands, having taken the prominent head of her own relative, a head that was adorned with a beautiful earring and had been removed from the body.

sanskrit: पूर्वजातिकृतं पापं मन्ये नाल्पमिवानघ ।एताभिरनवद्याभिर्मया चैवाल्पमेधया ॥११॥
transliteration: pūrvajātikṛtaṁ pāpaṁ manye nālpamivānagha ,etābhiranavadyābhirmayā caivālpamedhayā.
english: O sinless one, I believe that the sin (pāpam) committed in a previous birth (pūrvajātikṛtam) is not at all insignificant, having been caused by these blameless women and indeed by me, who am of little understanding.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. O sinless one, I believe that the sin (pāpam) committed in a previous birth (pūrvajātikṛtam) is not at all insignificant, having been caused by these blameless women and indeed by me, who am of little understanding.

sanskrit: तदिदं धर्मराजेन यातितं नो जनार्दन ।न हि नाशोऽस्ति वार्ष्णेय कर्मणोः शुभपापयोः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ dharmarājena yātitaṁ no janārdana ,na hi nāśo'sti vārṣṇeya karmaṇoḥ śubhapāpayoḥ.
english: O Janārdana, this (suffering) has been inflicted upon us by the King of Justice (Dharmarāja). For, O Vārṣṇeya, there is certainly no destruction (nāśaḥ) of (karma) actions (karmaṇoḥ), whether good or bad (śubhapāpayoḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. O Janārdana, this (suffering) has been inflicted upon us by the King of Justice (Dharmarāja). For, O Vārṣṇeya, there is certainly no destruction (nāśaḥ) of (karma) actions (karmaṇoḥ), whether good or bad (śubhapāpayoḥ).

sanskrit: प्रत्यग्रवयसः पश्य दर्शनीयकुचोदराः ।कुलेषु जाता ह्रीमत्यः कृष्णपक्षाक्षिमूर्धजाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: pratyagravayasaḥ paśya darśanīyakucodarāḥ ,kuleṣu jātā hrīmatyaḥ kṛṣṇapakṣākṣimūrdhajāḥ.
english: Behold these women, who are in the prime of their youth, with captivating breasts and bellies, born into noble families, bashful, and possessing dark-lashed eyes and black hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 13. Behold these women, who are in the prime of their youth, with captivating breasts and bellies, born into noble families, bashful, and possessing dark-lashed eyes and black hair.

sanskrit: हंसगद्गदभाषिण्यो दुःखशोकप्रमोहिताः ।सारस्य इव वाशन्त्यः पतिताः पश्य माधव ॥१४॥
transliteration: haṁsagadgadabhāṣiṇyo duḥkhaśokapramohitāḥ ,sārasya iva vāśantyaḥ patitāḥ paśya mādhava.
english: O Madhava, behold them, speaking with faltering voices like swans, overwhelmed by sorrow and grief, fallen, and wailing like cranes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 14. O Madhava, behold them, speaking with faltering voices like swans, overwhelmed by sorrow and grief, fallen, and wailing like cranes.

sanskrit: फुल्लपद्मप्रकाशानि पुण्डरीकाक्ष योषिताम् ।अनवद्यानि वक्त्राणि तपत्यसुखरश्मिवान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: phullapadmaprakāśāni puṇḍarīkākṣa yoṣitām ,anavadyāni vaktrāṇi tapatyasukharaśmivān.
english: O lotus-eyed one (Puṇḍarīkākṣa), the sun, with its unpleasant rays, is scorching the flawless faces of these women, which are as radiant as blossoming lotuses.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 15. O lotus-eyed one (Puṇḍarīkākṣa), the sun, with its unpleasant rays, is scorching the flawless faces of these women, which are as radiant as blossoming lotuses.

sanskrit: ईर्षूणां मम पुत्राणां वासुदेवावरोधनम् ।मत्तमातङ्गदर्पाणां पश्यन्त्यद्य पृथग्जनाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: īrṣūṇāṁ mama putrāṇāṁ vāsudevāvarodhanam ,mattamātaṅgadarpāṇāṁ paśyantyadya pṛthagjanāḥ.
english: Today, ordinary people are witnessing the abduction of Vāsudeva's women, as well as the women of my sons, who are envious and possess the pride of rutting elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 16. Today, ordinary people are witnessing the abduction of Vāsudeva's women, as well as the women of my sons, who are envious and possess the pride of rutting elephants.

sanskrit: शतचन्द्राणि चर्माणि ध्वजांश्चादित्यसंनिभान् ।रौक्माणि चैव वर्माणि निष्कानपि च काञ्चनान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: śatacandrāṇi carmāṇi dhvajāṁścādityasaṁnibhān ,raukmāṇi caiva varmāṇi niṣkānapi ca kāñcanān.
english: And shields bearing a hundred moon-like ornaments, and banners resplendent like the sun, and also golden armors, and golden necklaces.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 17. And shields bearing a hundred moon-like ornaments, and banners resplendent like the sun, and also golden armors, and golden necklaces.

sanskrit: शीर्षत्राणानि चैतानि पुत्राणां मे महीतले ।पश्य दीप्तानि गोविन्द पावकान्सुहुतानिव ॥१८॥
transliteration: śīrṣatrāṇāni caitāni putrāṇāṁ me mahītale ,paśya dīptāni govinda pāvakānsuhutāniva.
english: O Govinda, behold these helmets of my sons lying on the ground, gleaming like well-offered sacrificial fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 18. O Govinda, behold these helmets of my sons lying on the ground, gleaming like well-offered sacrificial fires.

sanskrit: एष दुःशासनः शेते शूरेणामित्रघातिना ।पीतशोणितसर्वाङ्गो भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: eṣa duḥśāsanaḥ śete śūreṇāmitraghātinā ,pītaśoṇitasarvāṅgo bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: This Duḥśāsana lies there, his entire body having had its blood drunk, brought down by the brave Bhīmasena, the slayer of foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 19. This Duḥśāsana lies there, his entire body having had its blood drunk, brought down by the brave Bhīmasena, the slayer of foes.

sanskrit: गदया वीरघातिन्या पश्य माधव मे सुतम् ।द्यूतक्लेशाननुस्मृत्य द्रौपद्या चोदितेन च ॥२०॥
transliteration: gadayā vīraghātinyā paśya mādhava me sutam ,dyūtakleśānanusmṛtya draupadyā coditena ca.
english: O Mādhava, behold my son [struck down] by the mace, the slayer of heroes; [by the one who was] urged by Draupadī and who remembered the suffering caused by the dice game.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 20. O Mādhava, behold my son [struck down] by the mace, the slayer of heroes; [by the one who was] urged by Draupadī and who remembered the suffering caused by the dice game.

sanskrit: उक्ता ह्यनेन पाञ्चाली सभायां द्यूतनिर्जिता ।प्रियं चिकीर्षता भ्रातुः कर्णस्य च जनार्दन ॥२१॥
transliteration: uktā hyanena pāñcālī sabhāyāṁ dyūtanirjitā ,priyaṁ cikīrṣatā bhrātuḥ karṇasya ca janārdana.
english: O Janārdana, indeed, Draupadi (pāñcālī), having been conquered in the gambling match in the assembly, was addressed by him who desired to please his brother and Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 21. O Janārdana, indeed, Draupadi (pāñcālī), having been conquered in the gambling match in the assembly, was addressed by him who desired to please his brother and Karṇa.

sanskrit: सहैव सहदेवेन नकुलेनार्जुनेन च ।दासभार्यासि पाञ्चालि क्षिप्रं प्रविश नो गृहान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: sahaiva sahadevena nakulenārjunena ca ,dāsabhāryāsi pāñcāli kṣipraṁ praviśa no gṛhān.
english: Along with Sahadeva, Nakula, and Arjuna - O Pāñcālī, you are a slave's wife; quickly enter our houses!
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 22. Along with Sahadeva, Nakula, and Arjuna - O Pāñcālī, you are a slave's wife; quickly enter our houses!

sanskrit: ततोऽहमब्रुवं कृष्ण तदा दुर्योधनं नृपम् ।मृत्युपाशपरिक्षिप्तं शकुनिं पुत्र वर्जय ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato'hamabruvaṁ kṛṣṇa tadā duryodhanaṁ nṛpam ,mṛtyupāśaparikṣiptaṁ śakuniṁ putra varjaya.
english: Then, O Kṛṣṇa, I said to King Duryodhana at that time, 'O son, abandon Śakuni, who is encircled by the noose of death.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 23. Then, O Kṛṣṇa, I said to King Duryodhana at that time, 'O son, abandon Śakuni, who is encircled by the noose of death.'

sanskrit: निबोधैनं सुदुर्बुद्धिं मातुलं कलहप्रियम् ।क्षिप्रमेनं परित्यज्य पुत्र शाम्यस्व पाण्डवैः ॥२४॥
transliteration: nibodhainaṁ sudurbuddhiṁ mātulaṁ kalahapriyam ,kṣipramenaṁ parityajya putra śāmyasva pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: Understand this exceedingly evil-minded uncle (mātula), who is fond of quarrel. O son, quickly abandoning him, make peace with the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 24. Understand this exceedingly evil-minded uncle (mātula), who is fond of quarrel. O son, quickly abandoning him, make peace with the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: न बुध्यसे त्वं दुर्बुद्धे भीमसेनममर्षणम् ।वाङ्नाराचैस्तुदंस्तीक्ष्णैरुल्काभिरिव कुञ्जरम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: na budhyase tvaṁ durbuddhe bhīmasenamamarṣaṇam ,vāṅnārācaistudaṁstīkṣṇairulkābhiriva kuñjaram.
english: O foolish one, you do not understand Bhīmasena, who is fiercely intolerant, as you pierce him with sharp word-arrows, just as one would pierce an elephant with flaming torches.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 25. O foolish one, you do not understand Bhīmasena, who is fiercely intolerant, as you pierce him with sharp word-arrows, just as one would pierce an elephant with flaming torches.

sanskrit: तानेष रभसः क्रूरो वाक्शल्यानवधारयन् ।उत्ससर्ज विषं तेषु सर्पो गोवृषभेष्विव ॥२६॥
transliteration: tāneṣa rabhasaḥ krūro vākśalyānavadhārayan ,utsasarja viṣaṁ teṣu sarpo govṛṣabheṣviva.
english: This impetuous and cruel person, not restraining his sharp verbal attacks, spewed forth venom upon them, just as a serpent among cows and bulls.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 26. This impetuous and cruel person, not restraining his sharp verbal attacks, spewed forth venom upon them, just as a serpent among cows and bulls.

sanskrit: एष दुःशासनः शेते विक्षिप्य विपुलौ भुजौ ।निहतो भीमसेनेन सिंहेनेव महर्षभः ॥२७॥
transliteration: eṣa duḥśāsanaḥ śete vikṣipya vipulau bhujau ,nihato bhīmasenena siṁheneva maharṣabhaḥ.
english: Here lies Duḥśāsana, his two mighty arms cast aside, slain by Bhīmasena, just as a great bull is killed by a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 27. Here lies Duḥśāsana, his two mighty arms cast aside, slain by Bhīmasena, just as a great bull is killed by a lion.

sanskrit: अत्यर्थमकरोद्रौद्रं भीमसेनोऽत्यमर्षणः ।दुःशासनस्य यत्क्रुद्धोऽपिबच्छोणितमाहवे ॥२८॥
transliteration: atyarthamakarodraudraṁ bhīmaseno'tyamarṣaṇaḥ ,duḥśāsanasya yatkruddho'pibacchoṇitamāhave.
english: The extremely intolerant Bhīmasena performed an exceedingly dreadful act: enraged, he drank Duḥśāsana's blood in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 18 Verse 28. The extremely intolerant Bhīmasena performed an exceedingly dreadful act: enraged, he drank Duḥśāsana's blood in battle.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।एष माधव पुत्रो मे विकर्णः प्राज्ञसंमतः ।भूमौ विनिहतः शेते भीमेन शतधा कृतः ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,eṣa mādhava putro me vikarṇaḥ prājñasaṁmataḥ ,bhūmau vinihataḥ śete bhīmena śatadhā kṛtaḥ.
english: Gāndhārī said: O Mādhava, this is my son Vikarṇa, esteemed by the wise. He lies on the ground, struck down and torn into a hundred pieces by Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 1. Gāndhārī said: O Mādhava, this is my son Vikarṇa, esteemed by the wise. He lies on the ground, struck down and torn into a hundred pieces by Bhīma.

sanskrit: गजमध्यगतः शेते विकर्णो मधुसूदन ।नीलमेघपरिक्षिप्तः शरदीव दिवाकरः ॥२॥
transliteration: gajamadhyagataḥ śete vikarṇo madhusūdana ,nīlameghaparikṣiptaḥ śaradīva divākaraḥ.
english: O Madhusūdana, Vikarṇa lies fallen amidst the elephants, like the sun enveloped by dark clouds in autumn.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 2. O Madhusūdana, Vikarṇa lies fallen amidst the elephants, like the sun enveloped by dark clouds in autumn.

sanskrit: अस्य चापग्रहेणैष पाणिः कृतकिणो महान् ।कथंचिच्छिद्यते गृध्रैरत्तुकामैस्तलत्रवान् ॥३॥
transliteration: asya cāpagraheṇaiṣa pāṇiḥ kṛtakiṇo mahān ,kathaṁcicchidyate gṛdhrairattukāmaistalatravān.
english: This great hand of his, scarred from holding the bow and protected by a gauntlet, is somehow being torn apart by vultures eager to feed.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 3. This great hand of his, scarred from holding the bow and protected by a gauntlet, is somehow being torn apart by vultures eager to feed.

sanskrit: अस्य भार्यामिषप्रेप्सून्गृध्रानेतांस्तपस्विनी ।वारयत्यनिशं बाला न च शक्नोति माधव ॥४॥
transliteration: asya bhāryāmiṣaprepsūngṛdhrānetāṁstapasvinī ,vārayatyaniśaṁ bālā na ca śaknoti mādhava.
english: O Mādhava, his young wife, suffering greatly, constantly tries to ward off these vultures who are eager for his flesh, but she is unable to.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 4. O Mādhava, his young wife, suffering greatly, constantly tries to ward off these vultures who are eager for his flesh, but she is unable to.

sanskrit: युवा वृन्दारकः शूरो विकर्णः पुरुषर्षभ ।सुखोचितः सुखार्हश्च शेते पांसुषु माधव ॥५॥
transliteration: yuvā vṛndārakaḥ śūro vikarṇaḥ puruṣarṣabha ,sukhocitaḥ sukhārhaśca śete pāṁsuṣu mādhava.
english: O Madhava, Vikarna, this young, excellent, and brave man - indeed, a chief among men - who was accustomed to comfort and deserving of it, now lies in the dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 5. O Madhava, Vikarna, this young, excellent, and brave man - indeed, a chief among men - who was accustomed to comfort and deserving of it, now lies in the dust.

sanskrit: कर्णिनालीकनाराचैर्भिन्नमर्माणमाहवे ।अद्यापि न जहात्येनं लक्ष्मीर्भरतसत्तमम् ॥६॥
transliteration: karṇinālīkanārācairbhinnamarmāṇamāhave ,adyāpi na jahātyenaṁ lakṣmīrbharatasattamam.
english: Even now, glory (lakṣmī) does not abandon this best among Bharatas, whose vital parts were pierced in battle by barbed, tubular, and iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 6. Even now, glory (lakṣmī) does not abandon this best among Bharatas, whose vital parts were pierced in battle by barbed, tubular, and iron arrows.

sanskrit: एष संग्रामशूरेण प्रतिज्ञां पालयिष्यता ।दुर्मुखोऽभिमुखः शेते हतोऽरिगणहा रणे ॥७॥
transliteration: eṣa saṁgrāmaśūreṇa pratijñāṁ pālayiṣyatā ,durmukho'bhimukhaḥ śete hato'rigaṇahā raṇe.
english: This Durmukha, the slayer of enemy multitudes, lies slain in battle, facing forward, (killed) by the valiant warrior who was fulfilling his vow.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 7. This Durmukha, the slayer of enemy multitudes, lies slain in battle, facing forward, (killed) by the valiant warrior who was fulfilling his vow.

sanskrit: तस्यैतद्वदनं कृष्ण श्वापदैरर्धभक्षितम् ।विभात्यभ्यधिकं तात सप्तम्यामिव चन्द्रमाः ॥८॥
transliteration: tasyaitadvadanaṁ kṛṣṇa śvāpadairardhabhakṣitam ,vibhātyabhyadhikaṁ tāta saptamyāmiva candramāḥ.
english: O Krishna, dear father, his face, half-eaten by wild animals, shines even more radiantly, like the moon on the seventh night (of the lunar cycle).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 8. O Krishna, dear father, his face, half-eaten by wild animals, shines even more radiantly, like the moon on the seventh night (of the lunar cycle).

sanskrit: शूरस्य हि रणे कृष्ण यस्याननमथेदृशम् ।स कथं निहतोऽमित्रैः पांसून्ग्रसति मे सुतः ॥९॥
transliteration: śūrasya hi raṇe kṛṣṇa yasyānanamathedṛśam ,sa kathaṁ nihato'mitraiḥ pāṁsūngrasati me sutaḥ.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, how is it that my son, who was indeed a hero in battle and whose face was always so valiant, now lies killed by enemies, eating dust?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 9. O Kṛṣṇa, how is it that my son, who was indeed a hero in battle and whose face was always so valiant, now lies killed by enemies, eating dust?

sanskrit: यस्याहवमुखे सौम्य स्थाता नैवोपपद्यते ।स कथं दुर्मुखोऽमित्रैर्हतो विबुधलोकजित् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yasyāhavamukhe saumya sthātā naivopapadyate ,sa kathaṁ durmukho'mitrairhato vibudhalokajit.
english: O gentle one, how is it that the conqueror of the celestial realms, before whom no foe could stand in the face of battle, now lies killed by enemies with a cheerless face?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 10. O gentle one, how is it that the conqueror of the celestial realms, before whom no foe could stand in the face of battle, now lies killed by enemies with a cheerless face?

sanskrit: चित्रसेनं हतं भूमौ शयानं मधुसूदन ।धार्तराष्ट्रमिमं पश्य प्रतिमानं दनुष्मताम् ॥११॥
transliteration: citrasenaṁ hataṁ bhūmau śayānaṁ madhusūdana ,dhārtarāṣṭramimaṁ paśya pratimānaṁ danuṣmatām.
english: O Madhusūdana, behold this Citrasena, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra and the foremost among archers, slain and lying on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 11. O Madhusūdana, behold this Citrasena, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra and the foremost among archers, slain and lying on the ground.

sanskrit: तं चित्रमाल्याभरणं युवत्यः शोककर्शिताः ।क्रव्यादसंघैः सहिता रुदन्त्यः पर्युपासते ॥१२॥
transliteration: taṁ citramālyābharaṇaṁ yuvatyaḥ śokakarśitāḥ ,kravyādasaṁghaiḥ sahitā rudantyaḥ paryupāsate.
english: Young women, emaciated by grief, weeping, and accompanied by hordes of flesh-eating creatures, surround him who was once adorned with colorful garlands and ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 12. Young women, emaciated by grief, weeping, and accompanied by hordes of flesh-eating creatures, surround him who was once adorned with colorful garlands and ornaments.

sanskrit: स्त्रीणां रुदितनिर्घोषः श्वापदानां च गर्जितम् ।चित्ररूपमिदं कृष्ण विचित्रं प्रतिभाति मे ॥१३॥
transliteration: strīṇāṁ ruditanirghoṣaḥ śvāpadānāṁ ca garjitam ,citrarūpamidaṁ kṛṣṇa vicitraṁ pratibhāti me.
english: O Krishna, this scene, with the lamentation of women and the roaring of wild beasts, appears to me both wondrous in its form and exceedingly strange.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 13. O Krishna, this scene, with the lamentation of women and the roaring of wild beasts, appears to me both wondrous in its form and exceedingly strange.

sanskrit: युवा वृन्दारको नित्यं प्रवरस्त्रीनिषेवितः ।विविंशतिरसौ शेते ध्वस्तः पांसुषु माधव ॥१४॥
transliteration: yuvā vṛndārako nityaṁ pravarastrīniṣevitaḥ ,viviṁśatirasau śete dhvastaḥ pāṁsuṣu mādhava.
english: O Madhava, that Vivimshati, who was always young, excellent, and attended by noble ladies, now lies fallen in the dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 14. O Madhava, that Vivimshati, who was always young, excellent, and attended by noble ladies, now lies fallen in the dust.

sanskrit: शरसंकृत्तवर्माणं वीरं विशसने हतम् ।परिवार्यासते गृध्राः परिविंशा विविंशतिम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: śarasaṁkṛttavarmāṇaṁ vīraṁ viśasane hatam ,parivāryāsate gṛdhrāḥ pariviṁśā viviṁśatim.
english: Having completely surrounded the hero Vivimshati, who was struck down in the slaughter with his armor cut through by arrows, vultures sit around him, encircling him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 15. Having completely surrounded the hero Vivimshati, who was struck down in the slaughter with his armor cut through by arrows, vultures sit around him, encircling him from all sides.

sanskrit: प्रविश्य समरे वीरः पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ।आविश्य शयने शेते पुनः सत्पुरुषोचितम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: praviśya samare vīraḥ pāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm ,āviśya śayane śete punaḥ satpuruṣocitam.
english: The hero, having penetrated the Pandava army in battle, now lies on the ground, yet still in a manner befitting a noble man.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 16. The hero, having penetrated the Pandava army in battle, now lies on the ground, yet still in a manner befitting a noble man.

sanskrit: स्मितोपपन्नं सुनसं सुभ्रु ताराधिपोपमम् ।अतीव शुभ्रं वदनं पश्य कृष्ण विविंशतेः ॥१७॥
transliteration: smitopapannaṁ sunasaṁ subhru tārādhipopamam ,atīva śubhraṁ vadanaṁ paśya kṛṣṇa viviṁśateḥ.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, behold the exceedingly radiant face of Viviṃśati, which is adorned with a smile, has a beautiful nose and lovely eyebrows, and shines like the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 17. O Kṛṣṇa, behold the exceedingly radiant face of Viviṃśati, which is adorned with a smile, has a beautiful nose and lovely eyebrows, and shines like the moon.

sanskrit: यं स्म तं पर्युपासन्ते वसुं वासवयोषितः ।क्रीडन्तमिव गन्धर्वं देवकन्याः सहस्रशः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yaṁ sma taṁ paryupāsante vasuṁ vāsavayoṣitaḥ ,krīḍantamiva gandharvaṁ devakanyāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: He whom the wives of the Vasus always worship, and whom thousands of celestial maidens attend upon like a Gandharva (celestial musician) at play.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 18. He whom the wives of the Vasus always worship, and whom thousands of celestial maidens attend upon like a Gandharva (celestial musician) at play.

sanskrit: हन्तारं वीरसेनानां शूरं समितिशोभनम् ।निबर्हणममित्राणां दुःसहं विषहेत कः ॥१९॥
transliteration: hantāraṁ vīrasenānāṁ śūraṁ samitiśobhanam ,nibarhaṇamamitrāṇāṁ duḥsahaṁ viṣaheta kaḥ.
english: Who could endure the formidable Duḥsaha, the slayer of heroic armies, the hero who shines gloriously in assemblies, and the annihilator of foes?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 19. Who could endure the formidable Duḥsaha, the slayer of heroic armies, the hero who shines gloriously in assemblies, and the annihilator of foes?

sanskrit: दुःसहस्यैतदाभाति शरीरं संवृतं शरैः ।गिरिरात्मरुहैः फुल्लैः कर्णिकारैरिवावृतः ॥२०॥
transliteration: duḥsahasyaitadābhāti śarīraṁ saṁvṛtaṁ śaraiḥ ,girirātmaruhaiḥ phullaiḥ karṇikārairivāvṛtaḥ.
english: This body of Duḥsaha appears covered with arrows, just as a mountain is covered with its own blooming karṇikāra flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 20. This body of Duḥsaha appears covered with arrows, just as a mountain is covered with its own blooming karṇikāra flowers.

sanskrit: शातकौम्भ्या स्रजा भाति कवचेन च भास्वता ।अग्निनेव गिरिः श्वेतो गतासुरपि दुःसहः ॥२१॥
transliteration: śātakaumbhyā srajā bhāti kavacena ca bhāsvatā ,agnineva giriḥ śveto gatāsurapi duḥsahaḥ.
english: He shines with a golden garland and a brilliant armor. Even though his life has departed, he remains formidable, like a white mountain aflame.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 19 Verse 21. He shines with a golden garland and a brilliant armor. Even though his life has departed, he remains formidable, like a white mountain aflame.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।अध्यर्धगुणमाहुर्यं बले शौर्ये च माधव ।पित्रा त्वया च दाशार्ह दृप्तं सिंहमिवोत्कटम् ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,adhyardhaguṇamāhuryaṁ bale śaurye ca mādhava ,pitrā tvayā ca dāśārha dṛptaṁ siṁhamivotkaṭam.
english: Gandhari said: 'O Madhava, O Dasharha, they say that he was one and a half times superior in strength and valor. He was fierce and formidable like a proud lion, through his father and through you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 1. Gandhari said: 'O Madhava, O Dasharha, they say that he was one and a half times superior in strength and valor. He was fierce and formidable like a proud lion, through his father and through you.'

sanskrit: यो बिभेद चमूमेको मम पुत्रस्य दुर्भिदाम् ।स भूत्वा मृत्युरन्येषां स्वयं मृत्युवशं गतः ॥२॥
transliteration: yo bibheda camūmeko mama putrasya durbhidām ,sa bhūtvā mṛtyuranyeṣāṁ svayaṁ mṛtyuvaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: He who single-handedly pierced my son's impregnable army, himself, having become the death of others, has now fallen under the sway of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 2. He who single-handedly pierced my son's impregnable army, himself, having become the death of others, has now fallen under the sway of death.

sanskrit: तस्योपलक्षये कृष्ण कार्ष्णेरमिततेजसः ।अभिमन्योर्हतस्यापि प्रभा नैवोपशाम्यति ॥३॥
transliteration: tasyopalakṣaye kṛṣṇa kārṣṇeramitatejasaḥ ,abhimanyorhatasyāpi prabhā naivopaśāmyati.
english: O Krishna, I observe that the splendor of that Abhimanyu, the son of Kṛṣṇa (kārṣṇi) of immeasurable brilliance, does not diminish, even though he is slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 3. O Krishna, I observe that the splendor of that Abhimanyu, the son of Kṛṣṇa (kārṣṇi) of immeasurable brilliance, does not diminish, even though he is slain.

sanskrit: एषा विराटदुहिता स्नुषा गाण्डीवधन्वनः ।आर्ता बाला पतिं वीरं शोच्या शोचत्यनिन्दिता ॥४॥
transliteration: eṣā virāṭaduhitā snuṣā gāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ ,ārtā bālā patiṁ vīraṁ śocyā śocatyaninditā.
english: This blameless young woman, the daughter of Virāṭa and daughter-in-law of Arjuna, the wielder of Gāṇḍīva, is distressed and pitiable as she laments her heroic husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 4. This blameless young woman, the daughter of Virāṭa and daughter-in-law of Arjuna, the wielder of Gāṇḍīva, is distressed and pitiable as she laments her heroic husband.

sanskrit: तमेषा हि समासाद्य भार्या भर्तारमन्तिके ।विराटदुहिता कृष्ण पाणिना परिमार्जति ॥५॥
transliteration: tameṣā hi samāsādya bhāryā bhartāramantike ,virāṭaduhitā kṛṣṇa pāṇinā parimārjati.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, this daughter of Virāṭa, his wife, having indeed approached her husband lying close by, gently strokes him with her hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 5. O Kṛṣṇa, this daughter of Virāṭa, his wife, having indeed approached her husband lying close by, gently strokes him with her hand.

sanskrit: तस्य वक्त्रमुपाघ्राय सौभद्रस्य यशस्विनी ।विबुद्धकमलाकारं कम्बुवृत्तशिरोधरम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tasya vaktramupāghrāya saubhadrasya yaśasvinī ,vibuddhakamalākāraṁ kambuvṛttaśirodharam.
english: The glorious Uttarā, having kissed the face of Saubhadra (Abhimanyu) - a face resembling a fully bloomed lotus and a neck rounded like a conch.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 6. The glorious Uttarā, having kissed the face of Saubhadra (Abhimanyu) - a face resembling a fully bloomed lotus and a neck rounded like a conch.

sanskrit: काम्यरूपवती चैषा परिष्वजति भामिनी ।लज्जमाना पुरेवैनं माध्वीकमदमूर्छिता ॥७॥
transliteration: kāmyarūpavatī caiṣā pariṣvajati bhāminī ,lajjamānā purevainaṁ mādhvīkamadamūrchitā.
english: And this beautiful woman, endowed with a charming form, embraces him. She is as if swooning from the intoxication of honey-wine, embracing him shyly, just as she used to before.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 7. And this beautiful woman, endowed with a charming form, embraces him. She is as if swooning from the intoxication of honey-wine, embracing him shyly, just as she used to before.

sanskrit: तस्य क्षतजसंदिग्धं जातरूपपरिष्कृतम् ।विमुच्य कवचं कृष्ण शरीरमभिवीक्षते ॥८॥
transliteration: tasya kṣatajasaṁdigdhaṁ jātarūpapariṣkṛtam ,vimucya kavacaṁ kṛṣṇa śarīramabhivīkṣate.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, having removed his armor, which was stained with blood and adorned with gold, she looks at the body.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 8. O Kṛṣṇa, having removed his armor, which was stained with blood and adorned with gold, she looks at the body.

sanskrit: अवेक्षमाणा तं बाला कृष्ण त्वामभिभाषते ।अयं ते पुण्डरीकाक्ष सदृशाक्षो निपातितः ॥९॥
transliteration: avekṣamāṇā taṁ bālā kṛṣṇa tvāmabhibhāṣate ,ayaṁ te puṇḍarīkākṣa sadṛśākṣo nipātitaḥ.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, O lotus-eyed one, looking at him, the young woman addresses you: "This one, whose eyes resemble yours, has been struck down."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 9. O Kṛṣṇa, O lotus-eyed one, looking at him, the young woman addresses you: "This one, whose eyes resemble yours, has been struck down."

sanskrit: बले वीर्ये च सदृशस्तेजसा चैव तेऽनघ ।रूपेण च तवात्यर्थं शेते भुवि निपातितः ॥१०॥
transliteration: bale vīrye ca sadṛśastejasā caiva te'nagha ,rūpeṇa ca tavātyarthaṁ śete bhuvi nipātitaḥ.
english: O sinless one (anagha), he was similar to you in strength and valor, and indeed in splendor. And having been struck down, he lies greatly resembling your form on the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 10. O sinless one (anagha), he was similar to you in strength and valor, and indeed in splendor. And having been struck down, he lies greatly resembling your form on the earth.

sanskrit: अत्यन्तसुकुमारस्य राङ्कवाजिनशायिनः ।कच्चिदद्य शरीरं ते भूमौ न परितप्यते ॥११॥
transliteration: atyantasukumārasya rāṅkavājinaśāyinaḥ ,kaccidadya śarīraṁ te bhūmau na paritapyate.
english: I hope that today his body, of one so exceedingly delicate who used to sleep on blankets of Rānku deer skin, does not suffer on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 11. I hope that today his body, of one so exceedingly delicate who used to sleep on blankets of Rānku deer skin, does not suffer on the ground.

sanskrit: मातङ्गभुजवर्ष्माणौ ज्याक्षेपकठिनत्वचौ ।काञ्चनाङ्गदिनौ शेषे निक्षिप्य विपुलौ भुजौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: mātaṅgabhujavarṣmāṇau jyākṣepakaṭhinatvacau ,kāñcanāṅgadinau śeṣe nikṣipya vipulau bhujau.
english: Having laid down his broad, mighty arms, which resembled elephant trunks in their strength, whose skin was hardened by the repeated drawing of the bowstring, and which were adorned with golden armlets, he rested.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 12. Having laid down his broad, mighty arms, which resembled elephant trunks in their strength, whose skin was hardened by the repeated drawing of the bowstring, and which were adorned with golden armlets, he rested.

sanskrit: व्यायम्य बहुधा नूनं सुखसुप्तः श्रमादिव ।एवं विलपतीमार्तां न हि मामभिभाषसे ॥१३॥
transliteration: vyāyamya bahudhā nūnaṁ sukhasuptaḥ śramādiva ,evaṁ vilapatīmārtāṁ na hi māmabhibhāṣase.
english: Surely, having exerted yourself greatly, you are sleeping soundly as if from exhaustion. Yet, though I am thus distressed and lamenting, you certainly do not speak to me!
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 13. Surely, having exerted yourself greatly, you are sleeping soundly as if from exhaustion. Yet, though I am thus distressed and lamenting, you certainly do not speak to me!

sanskrit: आर्यामार्य सुभद्रां त्वमिमांश्च त्रिदशोपमान् ।पितॄन्मां चैव दुःखार्तां विहाय क्व गमिष्यसि ॥१४॥
transliteration: āryāmārya subhadrāṁ tvamimāṁśca tridaśopamān ,pitṝnmāṁ caiva duḥkhārtāṁ vihāya kva gamiṣyasi.
english: O noble one, where will you go, abandoning this noble lady (āryā) Subhadra, and these fathers who are like gods, and even me, who am deeply sorrowful?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 14. O noble one, where will you go, abandoning this noble lady (āryā) Subhadra, and these fathers who are like gods, and even me, who am deeply sorrowful?

sanskrit: तस्य शोणितसंदिग्धान्केशानुन्नाम्य पाणिना ।उत्सङ्गे वक्त्रमाधाय जीवन्तमिव पृच्छति ।स्वस्रीयं वासुदेवस्य पुत्रं गाण्डीवधन्वनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasya śoṇitasaṁdigdhānkeśānunnāmya pāṇinā ,utsaṅge vaktramādhāya jīvantamiva pṛcchati ,svasrīyaṁ vāsudevasya putraṁ gāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ.
english: Raising his blood-stained hair with her hand, and placing his face on her lap, she (Uttara) asks him as if he were alive – her nephew (svasrījya) through Vasudeva (Krishna), the son of the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 15. Raising his blood-stained hair with her hand, and placing his face on her lap, she (Uttara) asks him as if he were alive – her nephew (svasrījya) through Vasudeva (Krishna), the son of the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna).

sanskrit: कथं त्वां रणमध्यस्थं जघ्नुरेते महारथाः ।धिगस्तु क्रूरकर्तॄंस्तान्कृपकर्णजयद्रथान् ॥१६॥
transliteration: kathaṁ tvāṁ raṇamadhyasthaṁ jaghnurete mahārathāḥ ,dhigastu krūrakartṝṁstānkṛpakarṇajayadrathān.
english: How could these great warriors have slain you, who stood in the midst of battle? Shame upon those cruel perpetrators - Kṛpa, Karṇa, and Jayadratha!
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 16. How could these great warriors have slain you, who stood in the midst of battle? Shame upon those cruel perpetrators - Kṛpa, Karṇa, and Jayadratha!

sanskrit: द्रोणद्रौणायनी चोभौ यैरसि व्यसनीकृतः ।रथर्षभाणां सर्वेषां कथमासीत्तदा मनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: droṇadrauṇāyanī cobhau yairasi vyasanīkṛtaḥ ,ratharṣabhāṇāṁ sarveṣāṁ kathamāsīttadā manaḥ.
english: By whom, including both Droṇa and Aśvatthāman (Drauṇāyani), you were brought to ruin; how then was the mind (manas) of all those chief charioteers at that time?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 17. By whom, including both Droṇa and Aśvatthāman (Drauṇāyani), you were brought to ruin; how then was the mind (manas) of all those chief charioteers at that time?

sanskrit: बालं त्वां परिवार्यैकं मम दुःखाय जघ्नुषाम् ।कथं नु पाण्डवानां च पाञ्चालानां च पश्यताम् ।त्वं वीर निधनं प्राप्तो नाथवान्सन्ननाथवत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: bālaṁ tvāṁ parivāryaikaṁ mama duḥkhāya jaghnuṣām ,kathaṁ nu pāṇḍavānāṁ ca pāñcālānāṁ ca paśyatām ,tvaṁ vīra nidhanaṁ prāpto nāthavānsannanāthavat.
english: O hero (vīra), how indeed could you, a single child, having been surrounded for my sorrow by those who killed you, have met your death (nidhanam) like one without a protector (anāthavat) while the Pāṇḍavas and Pāñcālas were watching, though you had protectors (nāthavān)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 18. O hero (vīra), how indeed could you, a single child, having been surrounded for my sorrow by those who killed you, have met your death (nidhanam) like one without a protector (anāthavat) while the Pāṇḍavas and Pāñcālas were watching, though you had protectors (nāthavān)?

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा बहुभिराक्रन्दे निहतं त्वामनाथवत् ।वीरः पुरुषशार्दूलः कथं जीवति पाण्डवः ॥१९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā bahubhirākrande nihataṁ tvāmanāthavat ,vīraḥ puruṣaśārdūlaḥ kathaṁ jīvati pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Having seen you, the hero and tiger among men (puruṣaśārdūlaḥ), slain helplessly (anāthavat) by many amidst the great uproar (āklande), how does this Pāṇḍava (i.e., Yudhiṣṭhira himself) still live?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 19. Having seen you, the hero and tiger among men (puruṣaśārdūlaḥ), slain helplessly (anāthavat) by many amidst the great uproar (āklande), how does this Pāṇḍava (i.e., Yudhiṣṭhira himself) still live?

sanskrit: न राज्यलाभो विपुलः शत्रूणां वा पराभवः ।प्रीतिं दास्यति पार्थानां त्वामृते पुष्करेक्षण ॥२०॥
transliteration: na rājyalābho vipulaḥ śatrūṇāṁ vā parābhavaḥ ,prītiṁ dāsyati pārthānāṁ tvāmṛte puṣkarekṣaṇa.
english: O lotus-eyed one, without you, neither the vast gain of a kingdom nor the defeat of our enemies will bring joy to the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 20. O lotus-eyed one, without you, neither the vast gain of a kingdom nor the defeat of our enemies will bring joy to the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तव शस्त्रजिताँल्लोकान्धर्मेण च दमेन च ।क्षिप्रमन्वागमिष्यामि तत्र मां प्रतिपालय ॥२१॥
transliteration: tava śastrajitāँllokāndharmeṇa ca damena ca ,kṣipramanvāgamiṣyāmi tatra māṁ pratipālaya.
english: I will quickly follow you to those worlds which you have conquered through your weapons, and through natural law (dharma) and self-control; await me there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 21. I will quickly follow you to those worlds which you have conquered through your weapons, and through natural law (dharma) and self-control; await me there.

sanskrit: दुर्मरं पुनरप्राप्ते काले भवति केनचित् ।यदहं त्वां रणे दृष्ट्वा हतं जीवामि दुर्भगा ॥२२॥
transliteration: durmaraṁ punaraprāpte kāle bhavati kenacit ,yadahaṁ tvāṁ raṇe dṛṣṭvā hataṁ jīvāmi durbhagā.
english: It is difficult for anyone to die before their appointed time has arrived. But alas, O wretched one, that I live after seeing you slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 22. It is difficult for anyone to die before their appointed time has arrived. But alas, O wretched one, that I live after seeing you slain in battle.

sanskrit: कामिदानीं नरव्याघ्र श्लक्ष्णया स्मितया गिरा ।पितृलोके समेत्यान्यां मामिवामन्त्रयिष्यसि ॥२३॥
transliteration: kāmidānīṁ naravyāghra ślakṣṇayā smitayā girā ,pitṛloke sametyānyāṁ māmivāmantrayiṣyasi.
english: O tiger among men, having arrived in the world of ancestors (pitṛloka), whom will you now address with gentle, smiling words, just as you addressed me?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 23. O tiger among men, having arrived in the world of ancestors (pitṛloka), whom will you now address with gentle, smiling words, just as you addressed me?

sanskrit: नूनमप्सरसां स्वर्गे मनांसि प्रमथिष्यसि ।परमेण च रूपेण गिरा च स्मितपूर्वया ॥२४॥
transliteration: nūnamapsarasāṁ svarge manāṁsi pramathiṣyasi ,parameṇa ca rūpeṇa girā ca smitapūrvayā.
english: Certainly, you will agitate the minds of the Apsaras (celestial nymphs) in heaven with your supreme beauty and your speech, which is preceded by a smile.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 24. Certainly, you will agitate the minds of the Apsaras (celestial nymphs) in heaven with your supreme beauty and your speech, which is preceded by a smile.

sanskrit: प्राप्य पुण्यकृताँल्लोकानप्सरोभिः समेयिवान् ।सौभद्र विहरन्काले स्मरेथाः सुकृतानि मे ॥२५॥
transliteration: prāpya puṇyakṛtāँllokānapsarobhiḥ sameyivān ,saubhadra viharankāle smarethāḥ sukṛtāni me.
english: O son of Subhadrā (Saubhadra), having reached the worlds of the meritorious and having joined the Apsaras (celestial nymphs), you should remember my good deeds while enjoying yourself there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 25. O son of Subhadrā (Saubhadra), having reached the worlds of the meritorious and having joined the Apsaras (celestial nymphs), you should remember my good deeds while enjoying yourself there.

sanskrit: एतावानिह संवासो विहितस्ते मया सह ।षण्मासान्सप्तमे मासि त्वं वीर निधनं गतः ॥२६॥
transliteration: etāvāniha saṁvāso vihitaste mayā saha ,ṣaṇmāsānsaptame māsi tvaṁ vīra nidhanaṁ gataḥ.
english: This is the extent of your dwelling here with me, ordained for you; after six months, in the seventh month, O hero, you will meet your death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 26. This is the extent of your dwelling here with me, ordained for you; after six months, in the seventh month, O hero, you will meet your death.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवचनामेतामपकर्षन्ति दुःखिताम् ।उत्तरां मोघसंकल्पां मत्स्यराजकुलस्त्रियः ॥२७॥
transliteration: ityuktavacanāmetāmapakarṣanti duḥkhitām ,uttarāṁ moghasaṁkalpāṁ matsyarājakulastriyaḥ.
english: The women of the Matsya king's family thus drag away this distressed Uttara, whose aspirations were in vain, after she had spoken these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 27. The women of the Matsya king's family thus drag away this distressed Uttara, whose aspirations were in vain, after she had spoken these words.

sanskrit: उत्तरामपकृष्यैनामार्तामार्ततराः स्वयम् ।विराटं निहतं दृष्ट्वा क्रोशन्ति विलपन्ति च ॥२८॥
transliteration: uttarāmapakṛṣyaināmārtāmārtatarāḥ svayam ,virāṭaṁ nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā krośanti vilapanti ca.
english: Seeing Virata killed, they themselves, even more distressed, pulled away the distressed Uttara, and cried out and lamented.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 28. Seeing Virata killed, they themselves, even more distressed, pulled away the distressed Uttara, and cried out and lamented.

sanskrit: द्रोणास्त्रशरसंकृत्तं शयानं रुधिरोक्षितम् ।विराटं वितुदन्त्येते गृध्रगोमायुवायसाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: droṇāstraśarasaṁkṛttaṁ śayānaṁ rudhirokṣitam ,virāṭaṁ vitudantyete gṛdhragomāyuvāyasāḥ.
english: These vultures, jackals, and crows peck at Virata, who lies drenched in blood, cut down by Drona's weapon-arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 29. These vultures, jackals, and crows peck at Virata, who lies drenched in blood, cut down by Drona's weapon-arrows.

sanskrit: वितुद्यमानं विहगैर्विराटमसितेक्षणाः ।न शक्नुवन्ति विवशा निवर्तयितुमातुराः ॥३०॥
transliteration: vitudyamānaṁ vihagairvirāṭamasitekṣaṇāḥ ,na śaknuvanti vivaśā nivartayitumāturāḥ.
english: Helpless and distressed, the dark-eyed women are unable to repel the birds that are pecking at Virata.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 30. Helpless and distressed, the dark-eyed women are unable to repel the birds that are pecking at Virata.

sanskrit: आसामातपतप्तानामायासेन च योषिताम् ।श्रमेण च विवर्णानां रूपाणां विगतं वपुः ॥३१॥
transliteration: āsāmātapataptānāmāyāsena ca yoṣitām ,śrameṇa ca vivarṇānāṁ rūpāṇāṁ vigataṁ vapuḥ.
english: The bodily beauty of these women, scorched by the sun and discolored by exertion and fatigue, has vanished.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 31. The bodily beauty of these women, scorched by the sun and discolored by exertion and fatigue, has vanished.

sanskrit: उत्तरं चाभिमन्युं च काम्बोजं च सुदक्षिणम् ।शिशूनेतान्हतान्पश्य लक्ष्मणं च सुदर्शनम् ।आयोधनशिरोमध्ये शयानं पश्य माधव ॥३२॥
transliteration: uttaraṁ cābhimanyuṁ ca kāmbojaṁ ca sudakṣiṇam ,śiśūnetānhatānpaśya lakṣmaṇaṁ ca sudarśanam ,āyodhanaśiromadhye śayānaṁ paśya mādhava.
english: O Madhava, behold these slain children: Uttara, Abhimanyu, Kambhoja, and Sudakshina. And see Lakshmana and Sudarshana lying in the very heart of the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 20 Verse 32. O Madhava, behold these slain children: Uttara, Abhimanyu, Kambhoja, and Sudakshina. And see Lakshmana and Sudarshana lying in the very heart of the battlefield.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।एष वैकर्तनः शेते महेष्वासो महारथः ।ज्वलितानलवत्संख्ये संशान्तः पार्थतेजसा ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,eṣa vaikartanaḥ śete maheṣvāso mahārathaḥ ,jvalitānalavatsaṁkhye saṁśāntaḥ pārthatejasā.
english: Gandhari said: 'This Karna (Vaikartana), a great archer and mighty warrior, who was like a blazing fire in battle, now lies extinguished by Arjuna's (Partha) valor.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 1. Gandhari said: 'This Karna (Vaikartana), a great archer and mighty warrior, who was like a blazing fire in battle, now lies extinguished by Arjuna's (Partha) valor.'

sanskrit: पश्य वैकर्तनं कर्णं निहत्यातिरथान्बहून् ।शोणितौघपरीताङ्गं शयानं पतितं भुवि ॥२॥
transliteration: paśya vaikartanaṁ karṇaṁ nihatyātirathānbahūn ,śoṇitaughaparītāṅgaṁ śayānaṁ patitaṁ bhuvi.
english: Behold Karna (Vaikartana), who, after striking down many supreme warriors, now lies fallen on the earth, his body completely covered in streams of blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 2. Behold Karna (Vaikartana), who, after striking down many supreme warriors, now lies fallen on the earth, his body completely covered in streams of blood.

sanskrit: अमर्षी दीर्घरोषश्च महेष्वासो महारथः ।रणे विनिहतः शेते शूरो गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥३॥
transliteration: amarṣī dīrgharoṣaśca maheṣvāso mahārathaḥ ,raṇe vinihataḥ śete śūro gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: That impatient, long-resentful one, the great archer, the mighty warrior - that hero - now lies slain in battle by Arjuna (the wielder of the Gandiva bow).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 3. That impatient, long-resentful one, the great archer, the mighty warrior - that hero - now lies slain in battle by Arjuna (the wielder of the Gandiva bow).

sanskrit: यं स्म पाण्डवसंत्रासान्मम पुत्रा महारथाः ।प्रायुध्यन्त पुरस्कृत्य मातङ्गा इव यूथपम् ॥४॥
transliteration: yaṁ sma pāṇḍavasaṁtrāsānmama putrā mahārathāḥ ,prāyudhyanta puraskṛtya mātaṅgā iva yūthapam.
english: My great warrior sons, out of fear of the Pandavas, fought, placing him at their front, just like elephants follow their herd leader.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 4. My great warrior sons, out of fear of the Pandavas, fought, placing him at their front, just like elephants follow their herd leader.

sanskrit: शार्दूलमिव सिंहेन समरे सव्यसाचिना ।मातङ्गमिव मत्तेन मातङ्गेन निपातितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: śārdūlamiva siṁhena samare savyasācinā ,mātaṅgamiva mattena mātaṅgena nipātitam.
english: He was struck down in battle by Savyasācin (Arjuna), just as a tiger is felled by a lion, or an elephant by a mad elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 5. He was struck down in battle by Savyasācin (Arjuna), just as a tiger is felled by a lion, or an elephant by a mad elephant.

sanskrit: समेताः पुरुषव्याघ्र निहतं शूरमाहवे ।प्रकीर्णमूर्धजाः पत्न्यो रुदत्यः पर्युपासते ॥६॥
transliteration: sametāḥ puruṣavyāghra nihataṁ śūramāhave ,prakīrṇamūrdhajāḥ patnyo rudatyaḥ paryupāsate.
english: O tiger among men! His wives, with disheveled hair and weeping, are attending upon the hero slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 6. O tiger among men! His wives, with disheveled hair and weeping, are attending upon the hero slain in battle.

sanskrit: उद्विग्नः सततं यस्माद्धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।त्रयोदश समा निद्रां चिन्तयन्नाध्यगच्छत ॥७॥
transliteration: udvignaḥ satataṁ yasmāddharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,trayodaśa samā nidrāṁ cintayannādhyagacchata.
english: Because of whom, Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of Righteousness (dharma), was constantly agitated; for thirteen years, reflecting, he could not obtain sleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 7. Because of whom, Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of Righteousness (dharma), was constantly agitated; for thirteen years, reflecting, he could not obtain sleep.

sanskrit: अनाधृष्यः परैर्युद्धे शत्रुभिर्मघवानिव ।युगान्ताग्निरिवार्चिष्मान्हिमवानिव च स्थिरः ॥८॥
transliteration: anādhṛṣyaḥ parairyuddhe śatrubhirmaghavāniva ,yugāntāgnirivārciṣmānhimavāniva ca sthiraḥ.
english: He is unassailable by foes in battle, like Indra (maghavān). He is radiant like the fire at the end of an eon, and as firm as the Himalayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 8. He is unassailable by foes in battle, like Indra (maghavān). He is radiant like the fire at the end of an eon, and as firm as the Himalayas.

sanskrit: स भूत्वा शरणं वीरो धार्तराष्ट्रस्य माधव ।भूमौ विनिहतः शेते वातरुग्ण इव द्रुमः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa bhūtvā śaraṇaṁ vīro dhārtarāṣṭrasya mādhava ,bhūmau vinihataḥ śete vātarugṇa iva drumaḥ.
english: O Mādhava (mādhava), that hero, who had become the refuge of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, now lies slain on the ground like a tree shattered by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 9. O Mādhava (mādhava), that hero, who had become the refuge of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, now lies slain on the ground like a tree shattered by the wind.

sanskrit: पश्य कर्णस्य पत्नीं त्वं वृषसेनस्य मातरम् ।लालप्यमानाः करुणं रुदतीं पतितां भुवि ॥१०॥
transliteration: paśya karṇasya patnīṁ tvaṁ vṛṣasenasya mātaram ,lālapyamānāḥ karuṇaṁ rudatīṁ patitāṁ bhuvi.
english: Behold, you, Karṇa's wife, the mother of Vṛṣasena, who lies fallen on the ground, weeping and lamenting piteously.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 10. Behold, you, Karṇa's wife, the mother of Vṛṣasena, who lies fallen on the ground, weeping and lamenting piteously.

sanskrit: आचार्यशापोऽनुगतो ध्रुवं त्वां यदग्रसच्चक्रमियं धरा ते ।ततः शरेणापहृतं शिरस्ते धनंजयेनाहवे शत्रुमध्ये ॥११॥
transliteration: ācāryaśāpo'nugato dhruvaṁ tvāṁ; yadagrasaccakramiyaṁ dharā te ,tataḥ śareṇāpahṛtaṁ śiraste; dhanaṁjayenāhave śatrumadhye.
english: Surely, the guru's (ācārya) curse (śāpa) has befallen you, because this earth swallowed your chariot wheel. Thereafter, your head was severed by Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) in battle, amidst your enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 11. Surely, the guru's (ācārya) curse (śāpa) has befallen you, because this earth swallowed your chariot wheel. Thereafter, your head was severed by Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) in battle, amidst your enemies.

sanskrit: अहो धिगेषा पतिता विसंज्ञा समीक्ष्य जाम्बूनदबद्धनिष्कम् ।कर्णं महाबाहुमदीनसत्त्वं सुषेणमाता रुदती भृशार्ता ॥१२॥
transliteration: aho dhigeṣā patitā visaṁjñā; samīkṣya jāmbūnadabaddhaniṣkam ,karṇaṁ mahābāhumadīnasattvaṁ; suṣeṇamātā rudatī bhṛśārtā.
english: Ah, alas! How disgraceful! Susheṇa's mother, utterly distraught and weeping intensely, has fallen unconscious upon seeing Karṇa - the mighty-armed hero of indomitable spirit - wearing his necklace of refined gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 12. Ah, alas! How disgraceful! Susheṇa's mother, utterly distraught and weeping intensely, has fallen unconscious upon seeing Karṇa - the mighty-armed hero of indomitable spirit - wearing his necklace of refined gold.

sanskrit: अल्पावशेषो हि कृतो महात्मा शरीरभक्षैः परिभक्षयद्भिः ।द्रष्टुं न संप्रीतिकरः शशीव कृष्णश्य पक्षस्य चतुर्दशाहे ॥१३॥
transliteration: alpāvaśeṣo hi kṛto mahātmā; śarīrabhakṣaiḥ paribhakṣayadbhiḥ ,draṣṭuṁ na saṁprītikaraḥ śaśīva; kṛṣṇaśya pakṣasya caturdaśāhe.
english: Indeed, the great soul (ātman) (Karṇa) has been left with very little, devoured by those (predators) that consumed his body parts. He is not pleasant to behold, just like the moon on the fourteenth day of the dark fortnight.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 13. Indeed, the great soul (ātman) (Karṇa) has been left with very little, devoured by those (predators) that consumed his body parts. He is not pleasant to behold, just like the moon on the fourteenth day of the dark fortnight.

sanskrit: सावर्तमाना पतिता पृथिव्यामुत्थाय दीना पुनरेव चैषा ।कर्णस्य वक्त्रं परिजिघ्रमाणा रोरूयते पुत्रवधाभितप्ता ॥१४॥
transliteration: sāvartamānā patitā pṛthivyā;mutthāya dīnā punareva caiṣā ,karṇasya vaktraṁ parijighramāṇā; rorūyate putravadhābhitaptā.
english: She, rolling on the ground, fell, and then this distressed woman rose again. Smelling Karṇa's face, she wails loudly, consumed by grief over her son's death.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 21 Verse 14. She, rolling on the ground, fell, and then this distressed woman rose again. Smelling Karṇa's face, she wails loudly, consumed by grief over her son's death.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।आवन्त्यं भीमसेनेन भक्षयन्ति निपातितम् ।गृध्रगोमायवः शूरं बहुबन्धुमबन्धुवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,āvantyaṁ bhīmasenena bhakṣayanti nipātitam ,gṛdhragomāyavaḥ śūraṁ bahubandhumabandhuvat.
english: Gāndhārī said: 'Vultures and jackals are devouring the heroic king of Avanti, who was struck down by Bhīmasena. Though he had many relatives, they consume him as if he were utterly without kin.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 1. Gāndhārī said: 'Vultures and jackals are devouring the heroic king of Avanti, who was struck down by Bhīmasena. Though he had many relatives, they consume him as if he were utterly without kin.'

sanskrit: तं पश्य कदनं कृत्वा शत्रूणां मधुसूदन ।शयानं वीरशयने रुधिरेण समुक्षितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: taṁ paśya kadanaṁ kṛtvā śatrūṇāṁ madhusūdana ,śayānaṁ vīraśayane rudhireṇa samukṣitam.
english: O Madhusudana, behold him, who, having caused destruction to his enemies, is now lying on the hero's bed (the battlefield), drenched in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 2. O Madhusudana, behold him, who, having caused destruction to his enemies, is now lying on the hero's bed (the battlefield), drenched in blood.

sanskrit: तं सृगालाश्च कङ्काश्च क्रव्यादाश्च पृथग्विधाः ।तेन तेन विकर्षन्ति पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ sṛgālāśca kaṅkāśca kravyādāśca pṛthagvidhāḥ ,tena tena vikarṣanti paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: Jackals, vultures, and various other carnivorous creatures are dragging him this way and that. Behold this alteration of time's course!
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 3. Jackals, vultures, and various other carnivorous creatures are dragging him this way and that. Behold this alteration of time's course!

sanskrit: शयानं वीरशयने वीरमाक्रन्दसारिणम् ।आवन्त्यमभितो नार्यो रुदत्यः पर्युपासते ॥४॥
transliteration: śayānaṁ vīraśayane vīramākrandasāriṇam ,āvantyamabhito nāryo rudatyaḥ paryupāsate.
english: Weeping women surround and lament the hero of Avanti, who is lying on the hero's bed (the battlefield) and uttering cries.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 4. Weeping women surround and lament the hero of Avanti, who is lying on the hero's bed (the battlefield) and uttering cries.

sanskrit: प्रातिपीयं महेष्वासं हतं भल्लेन बाह्लिकम् ।प्रसुप्तमिव शार्दूलं पश्य कृष्ण मनस्विनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: prātipīyaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ hataṁ bhallena bāhlikam ,prasuptamiva śārdūlaṁ paśya kṛṣṇa manasvinam.
english: O Krishna, behold Bahlika, the high-minded great archer from Prātipīya, slain by an arrow, now lying like a deeply sleeping tiger.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 5. O Krishna, behold Bahlika, the high-minded great archer from Prātipīya, slain by an arrow, now lying like a deeply sleeping tiger.

sanskrit: अतीव मुखवर्णोऽस्य निहतस्यापि शोभते ।सोमस्येवाभिपूर्णस्य पौर्णमास्यां समुद्यतः ॥६॥
transliteration: atīva mukhavarṇo'sya nihatasyāpi śobhate ,somasyevābhipūrṇasya paurṇamāsyāṁ samudyataḥ.
english: Even though he is slain, his face's complexion shines exceedingly, like the full moon that has fully risen on the full moon night.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 6. Even though he is slain, his face's complexion shines exceedingly, like the full moon that has fully risen on the full moon night.

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकाभितप्तेन प्रतिज्ञां परिरक्षता ।पाकशासनिना संख्ये वार्द्धक्षत्रिर्निपातितः ॥७॥
transliteration: putraśokābhitaptena pratijñāṁ parirakṣatā ,pākaśāsaninā saṁkhye vārddhakṣatrirnipātitaḥ.
english: Tormented by grief for his son and fulfilling his vow, the son of Indra (Arjuna) struck down Vārdhakṣatri (Jayadratha) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 7. Tormented by grief for his son and fulfilling his vow, the son of Indra (Arjuna) struck down Vārdhakṣatri (Jayadratha) in battle.

sanskrit: एकादश चमूर्जित्वा रक्ष्यमाणं महात्मना ।सत्यं चिकीर्षता पश्य हतमेनं जयद्रथम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ekādaśa camūrjitvā rakṣyamāṇaṁ mahātmanā ,satyaṁ cikīrṣatā paśya hatamenaṁ jayadratham.
english: Behold this slain Jayadratha, whom a great soul (mahātman) was protecting! (This was achieved by one) who, having conquered eleven armies, desired to fulfill his vow.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 8. Behold this slain Jayadratha, whom a great soul (mahātman) was protecting! (This was achieved by one) who, having conquered eleven armies, desired to fulfill his vow.

sanskrit: सिन्धुसौवीरभर्तारं दर्पपूर्णं मनस्विनम् ।भक्षयन्ति शिवा गृध्रा जनार्दन जयद्रथम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sindhusauvīrabhartāraṁ darpapūrṇaṁ manasvinam ,bhakṣayanti śivā gṛdhrā janārdana jayadratham.
english: O Janārdana! Jackals and vultures are devouring Jayadratha, the arrogant, proud ruler of Sindhu and Sauvīra.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 9. O Janārdana! Jackals and vultures are devouring Jayadratha, the arrogant, proud ruler of Sindhu and Sauvīra.

sanskrit: संरक्ष्यमाणं भार्याभिरनुरक्ताभिरच्युत ।भषन्तो व्यपकर्षन्ति गहनं निम्नमन्तिकात् ॥१०॥
transliteration: saṁrakṣyamāṇaṁ bhāryābhiranuraktābhiracyuta ,bhaṣanto vyapakarṣanti gahanaṁ nimnamantikāt.
english: O Acyuta, even when someone is being protected by devoted wives, those who bark (like dogs) drag him away from close by into a deep, low place.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 10. O Acyuta, even when someone is being protected by devoted wives, those who bark (like dogs) drag him away from close by into a deep, low place.

sanskrit: तमेताः पर्युपासन्ते रक्षमाणा महाभुजम् ।सिन्धुसौवीरगान्धारकाम्बोजयवनस्त्रियः ॥११॥
transliteration: tametāḥ paryupāsante rakṣamāṇā mahābhujam ,sindhusauvīragāndhārakāmbojayavanastriyaḥ.
english: These women from Sindhu, Sauvira, Gandhara, Kamboja, and Yavana attend upon that great-armed man, protecting him.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 11. These women from Sindhu, Sauvira, Gandhara, Kamboja, and Yavana attend upon that great-armed man, protecting him.

sanskrit: यदा कृष्णामुपादाय प्राद्रवत्केकयैः सह ।तदैव वध्यः पाण्डूनां जनार्दन जयद्रथः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yadā kṛṣṇāmupādāya prādravatkekayaiḥ saha ,tadaiva vadhyaḥ pāṇḍūnāṁ janārdana jayadrathaḥ.
english: O Janardana (Krishna), when Jayadratha fled with the Kekayas after seizing Krishna (Draupadi), then he was certainly deserving of being killed by the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 12. O Janardana (Krishna), when Jayadratha fled with the Kekayas after seizing Krishna (Draupadi), then he was certainly deserving of being killed by the Pandavas.

sanskrit: दुःशलां मानयद्भिस्तु यदा मुक्तो जयद्रथः ।कथमद्य न तां कृष्ण मानयन्ति स्म ते पुनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: duḥśalāṁ mānayadbhistu yadā mukto jayadrathaḥ ,kathamadya na tāṁ kṛṣṇa mānayanti sma te punaḥ.
english: But when Jayadratha was released by those who honored Duḥśalā, O Krishna, why do they not honor her (Draupadi) again today?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 13. But when Jayadratha was released by those who honored Duḥśalā, O Krishna, why do they not honor her (Draupadi) again today?

sanskrit: सैषा मम सुता बाला विलपन्ती सुदुःखिता ।प्रमापयति चात्मानमाक्रोशति च पाण्डवान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: saiṣā mama sutā bālā vilapantī suduḥkhitā ,pramāpayati cātmānamākrośati ca pāṇḍavān.
english: This young daughter of mine, deeply distressed and weeping, is attempting to end her own life (ātman) and is cursing the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 14. This young daughter of mine, deeply distressed and weeping, is attempting to end her own life (ātman) and is cursing the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: किं नु दुःखतरं कृष्ण परं मम भविष्यति ।यत्सुता विधवा बाला स्नुषाश्च निहतेश्वराः ॥१५॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu duḥkhataraṁ kṛṣṇa paraṁ mama bhaviṣyati ,yatsutā vidhavā bālā snuṣāśca nihateśvarāḥ.
english: O Krishna, what greater sorrow could there be for me than this: my young daughter (sutā bālā) is a widow, and my daughters-in-law (snuṣāḥ) have had their husbands killed (nihateśvarāḥ)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 15. O Krishna, what greater sorrow could there be for me than this: my young daughter (sutā bālā) is a widow, and my daughters-in-law (snuṣāḥ) have had their husbands killed (nihateśvarāḥ)?

sanskrit: अहो धिग्दुःशलां पश्य वीतशोकभयामिव ।शिरो भर्तुरनासाद्य धावमानामितस्ततः ॥१६॥
transliteration: aho dhigduḥśalāṁ paśya vītaśokabhayāmiva ,śiro bharturanāsādya dhāvamānāmitastataḥ.
