english: Alas! Shame on Duḥśalā! See Duḥśalā, who appears as if free from sorrow and fear (vītaśokabhayā), running here and there without having found her husband's head (śiraḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 16. Alas! Shame on Duḥśalā! See Duḥśalā, who appears as if free from sorrow and fear (vītaśokabhayā), running here and there without having found her husband's head (śiraḥ).

sanskrit: वारयामास यः सर्वान्पाण्डवान्पुत्रगृद्धिनः ।स हत्वा विपुलाः सेनाः स्वयं मृत्युवशं गतः ॥१७॥
transliteration: vārayāmāsa yaḥ sarvānpāṇḍavānputragṛddhinaḥ ,sa hatvā vipulāḥ senāḥ svayaṁ mṛtyuvaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: He who had restrained all the Pāṇḍavas, despite their eagerness for sons, having slain vast armies, himself met his death (mṛtyuvaśam gataḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 17. He who had restrained all the Pāṇḍavas, despite their eagerness for sons, having slain vast armies, himself met his death (mṛtyuvaśam gataḥ).

sanskrit: तं मत्तमिव मातङ्गं वीरं परमदुर्जयम् ।परिवार्य रुदन्त्येताः स्त्रियश्चन्द्रोपमाननाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: taṁ mattamiva mātaṅgaṁ vīraṁ paramadurjayam ,parivārya rudantyetāḥ striyaścandropamānanāḥ.
english: These women, whose faces are like the moon, are weeping as they surround that hero, who was exceedingly difficult to conquer and now lies like an intoxicated elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 22 Verse 18. These women, whose faces are like the moon, are weeping as they surround that hero, who was exceedingly difficult to conquer and now lies like an intoxicated elephant.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।एष शल्यो हतः शेते साक्षान्नकुलमातुलः ।धर्मज्ञेन सता तात धर्मराजेन संयुगे ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,eṣa śalyo hataḥ śete sākṣānnakulamātulaḥ ,dharmajñena satā tāta dharmarājena saṁyuge.
english: Gandhari spoke: "Dear child (tāta), this Salya, Nakula's own maternal uncle, lies slain in battle by the righteous Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira), the knower of natural law (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 1. Gandhari spoke: "Dear child (tāta), this Salya, Nakula's own maternal uncle, lies slain in battle by the righteous Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira), the knower of natural law (dharma)."

sanskrit: यस्त्वया स्पर्धते नित्यं सर्वत्र पुरुषर्षभ ।स एष निहतः शेते मद्रराजो महारथः ॥२॥
transliteration: yastvayā spardhate nityaṁ sarvatra puruṣarṣabha ,sa eṣa nihataḥ śete madrarājo mahārathaḥ.
english: O bull among men (puruṣarṣabha), that very king of Madra, the great charioteer, who constantly competed with you everywhere, now lies slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 2. O bull among men (puruṣarṣabha), that very king of Madra, the great charioteer, who constantly competed with you everywhere, now lies slain.

sanskrit: येन संगृह्णता तात रथमाधिरथेर्युधि ।जयार्थं पाण्डुपुत्राणां तथा तेजोवधः कृतः ॥३॥
transliteration: yena saṁgṛhṇatā tāta rathamādhiratheryudhi ,jayārthaṁ pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ tathā tejovadhaḥ kṛtaḥ.
english: O dear child (tāta), it was by him (Salya), while skillfully controlling Adhiratha's son (Karna)'s chariot in battle for the victory of the Pandu's sons, that the destruction of (Karna's) glory was thus accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 3. O dear child (tāta), it was by him (Salya), while skillfully controlling Adhiratha's son (Karna)'s chariot in battle for the victory of the Pandu's sons, that the destruction of (Karna's) glory was thus accomplished.

sanskrit: अहो धिक्पश्य शल्यस्य पूर्णचन्द्रसुदर्शनम् ।मुखं पद्मपलाशाक्षं वडैरादष्टमव्रणम् ॥४॥
transliteration: aho dhikpaśya śalyasya pūrṇacandrasudarśanam ,mukhaṁ padmapalāśākṣaṁ vaḍairādaṣṭamavraṇam.
english: Oh, alas! Behold Shalya's face, which was as beautiful as a full moon and had eyes like lotus petals, now scarred with wounds from horse bites.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 4. Oh, alas! Behold Shalya's face, which was as beautiful as a full moon and had eyes like lotus petals, now scarred with wounds from horse bites.

sanskrit: एषा चामीकराभस्य तप्तकाञ्चनसप्रभा ।आस्याद्विनिःसृता जिह्वा भक्ष्यते कृष्ण पक्षिभिः ॥५॥
transliteration: eṣā cāmīkarābhasya taptakāñcanasaprabhā ,āsyādviniḥsṛtā jihvā bhakṣyate kṛṣṇa pakṣibhiḥ.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, this tongue, which once shone like refined gold with the brilliance of molten gold, having emerged from his mouth, is now being devoured by birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 5. O Kṛṣṇa, this tongue, which once shone like refined gold with the brilliance of molten gold, having emerged from his mouth, is now being devoured by birds.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरेण निहतं शल्यं समितिशोभनम् ।रुदन्त्यः पर्युपासन्ते मद्रराजकुलस्त्रियः ॥६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhireṇa nihataṁ śalyaṁ samitiśobhanam ,rudantyaḥ paryupāsante madrarājakulastriyaḥ.
english: The women of the royal Madra family, weeping, are gathered around Shalya, who was splendid in assembly and was slain by Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 6. The women of the royal Madra family, weeping, are gathered around Shalya, who was splendid in assembly and was slain by Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: एताः सुसूक्ष्मवसना मद्रराजं नरर्षभम् ।क्रोशन्त्यभिसमासाद्य क्षत्रियाः क्षत्रियर्षभम् ॥७॥
transliteration: etāḥ susūkṣmavasanā madrarājaṁ nararṣabham ,krośantyabhisamāsādya kṣatriyāḥ kṣatriyarṣabham.
english: These Kṣatriya women, adorned in very fine garments, having approached the Madra king, that bull among men and best among warriors, are wailing.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 7. These Kṣatriya women, adorned in very fine garments, having approached the Madra king, that bull among men and best among warriors, are wailing.

sanskrit: शल्यं निपतितं नार्यः परिवार्याभितः स्थिताः ।वाशिता गृष्टयः पङ्के परिमग्नमिवर्षभम् ॥८॥
transliteration: śalyaṁ nipatitaṁ nāryaḥ parivāryābhitaḥ sthitāḥ ,vāśitā gṛṣṭayaḥ paṅke parimagnamivarṣabham.
english: The women stood surrounding the fallen Shalya, and wailed like heifers bellowing around a bull completely submerged in mud.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 8. The women stood surrounding the fallen Shalya, and wailed like heifers bellowing around a bull completely submerged in mud.

sanskrit: शल्यं शरणदं शूरं पश्यैनं रथसत्तमम् ।शयानं वीरशयने शरैर्विशकलीकृतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: śalyaṁ śaraṇadaṁ śūraṁ paśyainaṁ rathasattamam ,śayānaṁ vīraśayane śarairviśakalīkṛtam.
english: Behold him, Shalya, the protector, the brave hero, the best of charioteers (rathasattama), lying on the hero's bed (vīraśayana), completely torn to pieces by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 9. Behold him, Shalya, the protector, the brave hero, the best of charioteers (rathasattama), lying on the hero's bed (vīraśayana), completely torn to pieces by arrows.

sanskrit: एष शैलालयो राजा भगदत्तः प्रतापवान् ।गजाङ्कुशधरः श्रेष्ठः शेते भुवि निपातितः ॥१०॥
transliteration: eṣa śailālayo rājā bhagadattaḥ pratāpavān ,gajāṅkuśadharaḥ śreṣṭhaḥ śete bhuvi nipātitaḥ.
english: This is King Bhagadatta, the powerful and foremost mountain-dweller and wielder of the elephant-goad, who now lies struck down on the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 10. This is King Bhagadatta, the powerful and foremost mountain-dweller and wielder of the elephant-goad, who now lies struck down on the earth.

sanskrit: यस्य रुक्ममयी माला शिरस्येषा विराजते ।श्वापदैर्भक्ष्यमाणस्य शोभयन्तीव मूर्धजान् ॥११॥
transliteration: yasya rukmamayī mālā śirasyeṣā virājate ,śvāpadairbhakṣyamāṇasya śobhayantīva mūrdhajān.
english: This golden garland (rukma-mayī mālā), which shines upon his head, now appears to adorn the hair of him who is being devoured by beasts of prey.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 11. This golden garland (rukma-mayī mālā), which shines upon his head, now appears to adorn the hair of him who is being devoured by beasts of prey.

sanskrit: एतेन किल पार्थस्य युद्धमासीत्सुदारुणम् ।लोमहर्षणमत्युग्रं शक्रस्य बलिना यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: etena kila pārthasya yuddhamāsītsudāruṇam ,lomaharṣaṇamatyugraṁ śakrasya balinā yathā.
english: Indeed, because of this, Arjuna (pārtha) experienced a very dreadful, hair-raising, and exceedingly fierce battle, just like the one between Indra and Bali.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 12. Indeed, because of this, Arjuna (pārtha) experienced a very dreadful, hair-raising, and exceedingly fierce battle, just like the one between Indra and Bali.

sanskrit: योधयित्वा महाबाहुरेष पार्थं धनंजयम् ।संशयं गमयित्वा च कुन्तीपुत्रेण पातितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yodhayitvā mahābāhureṣa pārthaṁ dhanaṁjayam ,saṁśayaṁ gamayitvā ca kuntīputreṇa pātitaḥ.
english: This mighty-armed one (Bhishma), after making Arjuna (pārtha), also known as Dhanañjaya, fight, and having brought him to a state of doubt, was then struck down by the son of Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 13. This mighty-armed one (Bhishma), after making Arjuna (pārtha), also known as Dhanañjaya, fight, and having brought him to a state of doubt, was then struck down by the son of Kunti.

sanskrit: यस्य नास्ति समो लोके शौर्ये वीर्ये च कश्चन ।स एष निहतः शेते भीष्मो भीष्मकृदाहवे ॥१४॥
transliteration: yasya nāsti samo loke śaurye vīrye ca kaścana ,sa eṣa nihataḥ śete bhīṣmo bhīṣmakṛdāhave.
english: He who has no equal in the world in valor and prowess, that very Bhishma, who caused terror (bhīṣmakṛt) in battle, now lies slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 14. He who has no equal in the world in valor and prowess, that very Bhishma, who caused terror (bhīṣmakṛt) in battle, now lies slain.

sanskrit: पश्य शांतनवं कृष्ण शयानं सूर्यवर्चसम् ।युगान्त इव कालेन पातितं सूर्यमम्बरात् ॥१५॥
transliteration: paśya śāṁtanavaṁ kṛṣṇa śayānaṁ sūryavarcasam ,yugānta iva kālena pātitaṁ sūryamambarāt.
english: O Krishna, behold the son of Shantanu (śāntanava) lying there, radiant like the sun, struck down by time, just as the sun itself falls from the sky at the end of an eon.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 15. O Krishna, behold the son of Shantanu (śāntanava) lying there, radiant like the sun, struck down by time, just as the sun itself falls from the sky at the end of an eon.

sanskrit: एष तप्त्वा रणे शत्रूञ्शस्त्रतापेन वीर्यवान् ।नरसूर्योऽस्तमभ्येति सूर्योऽस्तमिव केशव ॥१६॥
transliteration: eṣa taptvā raṇe śatrūñśastratāpena vīryavān ,narasūryo'stamabhyeti sūryo'stamiva keśava.
english: O Keśava, this valorous man, a sun among men, having scorched his enemies in battle with the intense heat of his weapons, now goes to his setting, just like the sun sets.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 16. O Keśava, this valorous man, a sun among men, having scorched his enemies in battle with the intense heat of his weapons, now goes to his setting, just like the sun sets.

sanskrit: शरतल्पगतं वीरं धर्मे देवापिना समम् ।शयानं वीरशयने पश्य शूरनिषेविते ॥१७॥
transliteration: śaratalpagataṁ vīraṁ dharme devāpinā samam ,śayānaṁ vīraśayane paśya śūraniṣevite.
english: Behold the hero, lying on a bed of arrows (śara-talpa), equal to Devāpi in righteousness (dharma), resting on the hero's bed (vīra-śayana) frequented by brave warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 17. Behold the hero, lying on a bed of arrows (śara-talpa), equal to Devāpi in righteousness (dharma), resting on the hero's bed (vīra-śayana) frequented by brave warriors.

sanskrit: कर्णिनालीकनाराचैरास्तीर्य शयनोत्तमम् ।आविश्य शेते भगवान्स्कन्दः शरवणं यथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: karṇinālīkanārācairāstīrya śayanottamam ,āviśya śete bhagavānskandaḥ śaravaṇaṁ yathā.
english: Having spread an excellent bed with broad-headed arrows (karṇi), shaft-like arrows (nālīka), and iron arrows (nārāca), the revered one (bhagavān) lies down, just as Skanda entered the grove of reeds (śara-vaṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 18. Having spread an excellent bed with broad-headed arrows (karṇi), shaft-like arrows (nālīka), and iron arrows (nārāca), the revered one (bhagavān) lies down, just as Skanda entered the grove of reeds (śara-vaṇa).

sanskrit: अतूलपूर्णं गाङ्गेयस्त्रिभिर्बाणैः समन्वितम् ।उपधायोपधानाग्र्यं दत्तं गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥१९॥
transliteration: atūlapūrṇaṁ gāṅgeyastribhirbāṇaiḥ samanvitam ,upadhāyopadhānāgryaṁ dattaṁ gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: For Gaṅgeya (Bhishma), the wielder of Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna), having placed the excellent pillow  -  which was not filled with cotton but was provided with three arrows  -  gave it as a headrest.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 19. For Gaṅgeya (Bhishma), the wielder of Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna), having placed the excellent pillow  -  which was not filled with cotton but was provided with three arrows  -  gave it as a headrest.

sanskrit: पालयानः पितुः शास्त्रमूर्ध्वरेता महायशाः ।एष शांतनवः शेते माधवाप्रतिमो युधि ॥२०॥
transliteration: pālayānaḥ pituḥ śāstramūrdhvaretā mahāyaśāḥ ,eṣa śāṁtanavaḥ śete mādhavāpratimo yudhi.
english: This son of Śantanu, Bhīṣma, who upheld his father's vow (śāstra) and was a celibate (ūrdhvaretā) of great renown, now lies on the battlefield, a warrior whose prowess was unequaled even by Madhava (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 20. This son of Śantanu, Bhīṣma, who upheld his father's vow (śāstra) and was a celibate (ūrdhvaretā) of great renown, now lies on the battlefield, a warrior whose prowess was unequaled even by Madhava (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: धर्मात्मा तात धर्मज्ञः पारंपर्येण निर्णये ।अमर्त्य इव मर्त्यः सन्नेष प्राणानधारयत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: dharmātmā tāta dharmajñaḥ pāraṁparyeṇa nirṇaye ,amartya iva martyaḥ sanneṣa prāṇānadhārayat.
english: O father, this righteous soul (dharmātmā), who knows (natural) law (dharma) and made decisions based on tradition, maintained his vital breaths (prāṇa) as if he were immortal, despite being a mortal.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 21. O father, this righteous soul (dharmātmā), who knows (natural) law (dharma) and made decisions based on tradition, maintained his vital breaths (prāṇa) as if he were immortal, despite being a mortal.

sanskrit: नास्ति युद्धे कृती कश्चिन्न विद्वान्न पराक्रमी ।यत्र शांतनवो भीष्मः शेतेऽद्य निहतः परैः ॥२२॥
transliteration: nāsti yuddhe kṛtī kaścinna vidvānna parākramī ,yatra śāṁtanavo bhīṣmaḥ śete'dya nihataḥ paraiḥ.
english: There is no one (left) in battle who is truly accomplished, learned, or valorous, (seeing that) where Bhīṣma, the son of Śantanu, now lies struck down by his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 22. There is no one (left) in battle who is truly accomplished, learned, or valorous, (seeing that) where Bhīṣma, the son of Śantanu, now lies struck down by his enemies.

sanskrit: स्वयमेतेन शूरेण पृच्छ्यमानेन पाण्डवैः ।धर्मज्ञेनाहवे मृत्युराख्यातः सत्यवादिना ॥२३॥
transliteration: svayametena śūreṇa pṛcchyamānena pāṇḍavaiḥ ,dharmajñenāhave mṛtyurākhyātaḥ satyavādinā.
english: This hero, a knower of (natural) law (dharma) and a truth-speaker (satyavādin), when questioned by the Pāṇḍavas, himself revealed the manner of his death in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 23. This hero, a knower of (natural) law (dharma) and a truth-speaker (satyavādin), when questioned by the Pāṇḍavas, himself revealed the manner of his death in battle.

sanskrit: प्रनष्टः कुरुवंशश्च पुनर्येन समुद्धृतः ।स गतः कुरुभिः सार्धं महाबुद्धिः पराभवम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: pranaṣṭaḥ kuruvaṁśaśca punaryena samuddhṛtaḥ ,sa gataḥ kurubhiḥ sārdhaṁ mahābuddhiḥ parābhavam.
english: He, the immensely wise one, by whom the Kuru dynasty was again uplifted after it had been lost, has now met with defeat along with the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 24. He, the immensely wise one, by whom the Kuru dynasty was again uplifted after it had been lost, has now met with defeat along with the Kurus.

sanskrit: धर्मेषु कुरवः कं नु परिप्रक्ष्यन्ति माधव ।गते देवव्रते स्वर्गं देवकल्पे नरर्षभे ॥२५॥
transliteration: dharmeṣu kuravaḥ kaṁ nu pariprakṣyanti mādhava ,gate devavrate svargaṁ devakalpe nararṣabhe.
english: O Madhava, now that Devavrata (Bhishma), that god-like bull among men, has departed to heaven, whom indeed will the Kurus consult on matters of natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 25. O Madhava, now that Devavrata (Bhishma), that god-like bull among men, has departed to heaven, whom indeed will the Kurus consult on matters of natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्य विनेतारमाचार्यं सात्यकेस्तथा ।तं पश्य पतितं द्रोणं कुरूणां गुरुसत्तमम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: arjunasya vinetāramācāryaṁ sātyakestathā ,taṁ paśya patitaṁ droṇaṁ kurūṇāṁ gurusattamam.
english: Behold Drona, the preceptor and instructor of Arjuna, and similarly of Satyaki, who has now fallen; he was the foremost among the teachers of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 26. Behold Drona, the preceptor and instructor of Arjuna, and similarly of Satyaki, who has now fallen; he was the foremost among the teachers of the Kurus.

sanskrit: अस्त्रं चतुर्विधं वेद यथैव त्रिदशेश्वरः ।भार्गवो वा महावीर्यस्तथा द्रोणोऽपि माधव ॥२७॥
transliteration: astraṁ caturvidhaṁ veda yathaiva tridaśeśvaraḥ ,bhārgavo vā mahāvīryastathā droṇo'pi mādhava.
english: O Madhava, Drona too knows the four kinds of weapons, just as the lord of the gods or the immensely powerful Bhargava (Parashurama) does.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 27. O Madhava, Drona too knows the four kinds of weapons, just as the lord of the gods or the immensely powerful Bhargava (Parashurama) does.

sanskrit: यस्य प्रसादाद्बीभत्सुः पाण्डवः कर्म दुष्करम् ।चकार स हतः शेते नैनमस्त्राण्यपालयन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: yasya prasādādbībhatsuḥ pāṇḍavaḥ karma duṣkaram ,cakāra sa hataḥ śete nainamastrāṇyapālayan.
english: He by whose favor Arjuna (Bibhatsu), the Pandava, performed arduous feats (karma) - that very Drona now lies slain. His weapons could not protect him.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 28. He by whose favor Arjuna (Bibhatsu), the Pandava, performed arduous feats (karma) - that very Drona now lies slain. His weapons could not protect him.

sanskrit: यं पुरोधाय कुरव आह्वयन्ति स्म पाण्डवान् ।सोऽयं शस्त्रभृतां श्रेष्ठो द्रोणः शस्त्रैः पृथक्कृतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: yaṁ purodhāya kurava āhvayanti sma pāṇḍavān ,so'yaṁ śastrabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭho droṇaḥ śastraiḥ pṛthakkṛtaḥ.
english: He whom the Kurus placed at their forefront when they challenged the Pandavas - this very Drona, the best among weapon-wielders, has been destroyed by weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 29. He whom the Kurus placed at their forefront when they challenged the Pandavas - this very Drona, the best among weapon-wielders, has been destroyed by weapons.

sanskrit: यस्य निर्दहतः सेनां गतिरग्नेरिवाभवत् ।स भूमौ निहतः शेते शान्तार्चिरिव पावकः ॥३०॥
transliteration: yasya nirdahataḥ senāṁ gatiragnerivābhavat ,sa bhūmau nihataḥ śete śāntārciriva pāvakaḥ.
english: He whose advance, consuming the enemy army, was like that of fire, now lies slain on the ground, like a fire whose flames have been extinguished.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 30. He whose advance, consuming the enemy army, was like that of fire, now lies slain on the ground, like a fire whose flames have been extinguished.

sanskrit: धनुर्मुष्टिरशीर्णश्च हस्तावापश्च माधव ।द्रोणस्य निहतस्यापि दृश्यते जीवतो यथा ॥३१॥
transliteration: dhanurmuṣṭiraśīrṇaśca hastāvāpaśca mādhava ,droṇasya nihatasyāpi dṛśyate jīvato yathā.
english: O Madhava, Drona's bow-grip (dhanurmuṣṭi) and arm-guard (hastāvāpa) appear unbroken and intact, even though he is slain, as if he were still alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 31. O Madhava, Drona's bow-grip (dhanurmuṣṭi) and arm-guard (hastāvāpa) appear unbroken and intact, even though he is slain, as if he were still alive.

sanskrit: वेदा यस्माच्च चत्वारः सर्वास्त्राणि च केशव ।अनपेतानि वै शूराद्यथैवादौ प्रजापतेः ॥३२॥
transliteration: vedā yasmācca catvāraḥ sarvāstrāṇi ca keśava ,anapetāni vai śūrādyathaivādau prajāpateḥ.
english: O Keśava, from whom (Droṇa) the four Vedas and all weapons were inherent, just as they were inherent in Prajāpati in the very beginning.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 32. O Keśava, from whom (Droṇa) the four Vedas and all weapons were inherent, just as they were inherent in Prajāpati in the very beginning.

sanskrit: वन्दनार्हाविमौ तस्य बन्दिभिर्वन्दितौ शुभौ ।गोमायवो विकर्षन्ति पादौ शिष्यशतार्चितौ ॥३३॥
transliteration: vandanārhāvimau tasya bandibhirvanditau śubhau ,gomāyavo vikarṣanti pādau śiṣyaśatārcitau.
english: His (Droṇa's) two auspicious feet, worthy of veneration, praised by bards and worshipped by hundreds of disciples, are now being dragged by jackals.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 33. His (Droṇa's) two auspicious feet, worthy of veneration, praised by bards and worshipped by hundreds of disciples, are now being dragged by jackals.

sanskrit: द्रोणं द्रुपदपुत्रेण निहतं मधुसूदन ।कृपी कृपणमन्वास्ते दुःखोपहतचेतना ॥३४॥
transliteration: droṇaṁ drupadaputreṇa nihataṁ madhusūdana ,kṛpī kṛpaṇamanvāste duḥkhopahatacetanā.
english: O Madhusūdana, Kṛpī, whose consciousness is overwhelmed by sorrow, miserably attends to Droṇa, who was killed by the son of Drupada.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 34. O Madhusūdana, Kṛpī, whose consciousness is overwhelmed by sorrow, miserably attends to Droṇa, who was killed by the son of Drupada.

sanskrit: तां पश्य रुदतीमार्तां मुक्तकेशीमधोमुखीम् ।हतं पतिमुपासन्तीं द्रोणं शस्त्रभृतां वरम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tāṁ paśya rudatīmārtāṁ muktakeśīmadhomukhīm ,hataṁ patimupāsantīṁ droṇaṁ śastrabhṛtāṁ varam.
english: Behold her, weeping, distressed, with disheveled hair and downcast face, attending to her slain husband, Droṇa, who was the best among those who bear weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 35. Behold her, weeping, distressed, with disheveled hair and downcast face, attending to her slain husband, Droṇa, who was the best among those who bear weapons.

sanskrit: बाणैर्भिन्नतनुत्राणं धृष्टद्युम्नेन केशव ।उपास्ते वै मृधे द्रोणं जटिला ब्रह्मचारिणी ॥३६॥
transliteration: bāṇairbhinnatanutrāṇaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnena keśava ,upāste vai mṛdhe droṇaṁ jaṭilā brahmacāriṇī.
english: O Keśava, a female ascetic (brahmacāriṇī) with matted hair indeed sits by Droṇa in battle, whose armor was shattered by Dhṛṣṭadyumna's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 36. O Keśava, a female ascetic (brahmacāriṇī) with matted hair indeed sits by Droṇa in battle, whose armor was shattered by Dhṛṣṭadyumna's arrows.

sanskrit: प्रेतकृत्ये च यतते कृपी कृपणमातुरा ।हतस्य समरे भर्तुः सुकुमारी यशस्विनी ॥३७॥
transliteration: pretakṛtye ca yatate kṛpī kṛpaṇamāturā ,hatasya samare bhartuḥ sukumārī yaśasvinī.
english: And Kṛpī, delicate and glorious, greatly distressed, endeavors for the funeral rites of her husband, who was slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 37. And Kṛpī, delicate and glorious, greatly distressed, endeavors for the funeral rites of her husband, who was slain in battle.

sanskrit: अग्नीनाहृत्य विधिवच्चितां प्रज्वाल्य सर्वशः ।द्रोणमाधाय गायन्ति त्रीणि सामानि सामगाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: agnīnāhṛtya vidhivaccitāṁ prajvālya sarvaśaḥ ,droṇamādhāya gāyanti trīṇi sāmāni sāmagāḥ.
english: Having ritually collected the fires and entirely kindled the pyre, and having placed Droṇa upon it, the chanters of the Sāman (Sāma Veda priests) sing three Sāman chants.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 38. Having ritually collected the fires and entirely kindled the pyre, and having placed Droṇa upon it, the chanters of the Sāman (Sāma Veda priests) sing three Sāman chants.

sanskrit: किरन्ति च चितामेते जटिला ब्रह्मचारिणः ।धनुर्भिः शक्तिभिश्चैव रथनीडैश्च माधव ॥३९॥
transliteration: kiranti ca citāmete jaṭilā brahmacāriṇaḥ ,dhanurbhiḥ śaktibhiścaiva rathanīḍaiśca mādhava.
english: And O Mādhava, these male ascetics (brahmacāriṇaḥ) with matted hair strew the pyre with bows, spears (śakti), and even with chariot parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 39. And O Mādhava, these male ascetics (brahmacāriṇaḥ) with matted hair strew the pyre with bows, spears (śakti), and even with chariot parts.

sanskrit: शस्त्रैश्च विविधैरन्यैर्धक्ष्यन्ते भूरितेजसम् ।त एते द्रोणमाधाय शंसन्ति च रुदन्ति च ॥४०॥
transliteration: śastraiśca vividhairanyairdhakṣyante bhūritejasam ,ta ete droṇamādhāya śaṁsanti ca rudanti ca.
english: And with various other weapons, they will consume the greatly glorious one (Droṇa). These very ones, having focused on Droṇa, praise him and weep.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 40. And with various other weapons, they will consume the greatly glorious one (Droṇa). These very ones, having focused on Droṇa, praise him and weep.

sanskrit: सामभिस्त्रिभिरन्तःस्थैरनुशंसन्ति चापरे ।अग्नावग्निमिवाधाय द्रोणं हुत्वा हुताशने ॥४१॥
transliteration: sāmabhistribhirantaḥsthairanuśaṁsanti cāpare ,agnāvagnimivādhāya droṇaṁ hutvā hutāśane.
english: And others chant (Sāman verses) to him, three of which are recited inwardly. Having offered Droṇa into the sacrificial fire, as if placing fire upon fire, (they proceed).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 41. And others chant (Sāman verses) to him, three of which are recited inwardly. Having offered Droṇa into the sacrificial fire, as if placing fire upon fire, (they proceed).

sanskrit: गच्छन्त्यभिमुखा गङ्गां द्रोणशिष्या द्विजातयः ।अपसव्यां चितिं कृत्वा पुरस्कृत्य कृपीं तदा ॥४२॥
transliteration: gacchantyabhimukhā gaṅgāṁ droṇaśiṣyā dvijātayaḥ ,apasavyāṁ citiṁ kṛtvā puraskṛtya kṛpīṁ tadā.
english: Then, Droṇa's disciples, the twice-born (dvijātayaḥ), proceed facing towards the Ganges, having performed a counter-clockwise circumambulation of the funeral pyre and having placed Kṛpī in front (of the procession).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 23 Verse 42. Then, Droṇa's disciples, the twice-born (dvijātayaḥ), proceed facing towards the Ganges, having performed a counter-clockwise circumambulation of the funeral pyre and having placed Kṛpī in front (of the procession).

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।सोमदत्तसुतं पश्य युयुधानेन पातितम् ।वितुद्यमानं विहगैर्बहुभिर्माधवान्तिके ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,somadattasutaṁ paśya yuyudhānena pātitam ,vitudyamānaṁ vihagairbahubhirmādhavāntike.
english: Gandhārī spoke: 'Behold Somadatta's son, struck down by Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), and now being pecked at by many birds near Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa).'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 1. Gandhārī spoke: 'Behold Somadatta's son, struck down by Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), and now being pecked at by many birds near Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa).'

sanskrit: पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्तः सोमदत्तो जनार्दन ।युयुधानं महेष्वासं गर्हयन्निव दृश्यते ॥२॥
transliteration: putraśokābhisaṁtaptaḥ somadatto janārdana ,yuyudhānaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ garhayanniva dṛśyate.
english: O Janardana, Somadatta, deeply distressed by the grief for his son, appears as if reproaching the great archer Yuyudhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 2. O Janardana, Somadatta, deeply distressed by the grief for his son, appears as if reproaching the great archer Yuyudhana.

sanskrit: असौ तु भूरिश्रवसो माता शोकपरिप्लुता ।आश्वासयति भर्तारं सोमदत्तमनिन्दिता ॥३॥
transliteration: asau tu bhūriśravaso mātā śokapariplutā ,āśvāsayati bhartāraṁ somadattamaninditā.
english: But that blameless mother of Bhuriśravas, who is overwhelmed with sorrow, consoles her husband Somadatta.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 3. But that blameless mother of Bhuriśravas, who is overwhelmed with sorrow, consoles her husband Somadatta.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या नेदं महाराज दारुणं भरतक्षयम् ।कुरुसंक्रन्दनं घोरं युगान्तमनुपश्यसि ॥४॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā nedaṁ mahārāja dāruṇaṁ bharatakṣayam ,kurusaṁkrandanaṁ ghoraṁ yugāntamanupaśyasi.
english: O great king, fortunately you do not have to witness this terrible destruction of the Bharatas, this fearful lamentation of the Kurus, which resembles the end of an age.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 4. O great king, fortunately you do not have to witness this terrible destruction of the Bharatas, this fearful lamentation of the Kurus, which resembles the end of an age.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या यूपध्वजं वीरं पुत्रं भूरिसहस्रदम् ।अनेकक्रतुयज्वानं निहतं नाद्य पश्यसि ॥५॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā yūpadhvajaṁ vīraṁ putraṁ bhūrisahasradam ,anekakratuyajvānaṁ nihataṁ nādya paśyasi.
english: Fortunately, you do not see today your son, the hero, whose banner was the post for Vedic rituals, the giver of many thousands, who had performed many Vedic rituals (yajña), now slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 5. Fortunately, you do not see today your son, the hero, whose banner was the post for Vedic rituals, the giver of many thousands, who had performed many Vedic rituals (yajña), now slain.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या स्नुषाणामाक्रन्दे घोरं विलपितं बहु ।न शृणोषि महाराज सारसीनामिवार्णवे ॥६॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā snuṣāṇāmākrande ghoraṁ vilapitaṁ bahu ,na śṛṇoṣi mahārāja sārasīnāmivārṇave.
english: It is fortunate, O great king, that you do not hear the loud, terrible lamentation of your daughters-in-law, which is like the cries of female cranes in the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 6. It is fortunate, O great king, that you do not hear the loud, terrible lamentation of your daughters-in-law, which is like the cries of female cranes in the water.

sanskrit: एकवस्त्रानुसंवीताः प्रकीर्णासितमूर्धजाः ।स्नुषास्ते परिधावन्ति हतापत्या हतेश्वराः ॥७॥
transliteration: ekavastrānusaṁvītāḥ prakīrṇāsitamūrdhajāḥ ,snuṣāste paridhāvanti hatāpatyā hateśvarāḥ.
english: Your daughters-in-law, wearing single garments, with their black hair dishevelled, run about, having lost their children and their husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 7. Your daughters-in-law, wearing single garments, with their black hair dishevelled, run about, having lost their children and their husbands.

sanskrit: श्वापदैर्भक्ष्यमाणं त्वमहो दिष्ट्या न पश्यसि ।छिन्नबाहुं नरव्याघ्रमर्जुनेन निपातितम् ॥८॥
transliteration: śvāpadairbhakṣyamāṇaṁ tvamaho diṣṭyā na paśyasi ,chinnabāhuṁ naravyāghramarjunena nipātitam.
english: Alas! It is fortunate that you do not see the mighty warrior (naravyāghra), with severed arms, struck down by Arjuna, being devoured by wild animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 8. Alas! It is fortunate that you do not see the mighty warrior (naravyāghra), with severed arms, struck down by Arjuna, being devoured by wild animals.

sanskrit: शलं विनिहतं संख्ये भूरिश्रवसमेव च ।स्नुषाश्च विधवाः सर्वा दिष्ट्या नाद्येह पश्यसि ॥९॥
transliteration: śalaṁ vinihataṁ saṁkhye bhūriśravasameva ca ,snuṣāśca vidhavāḥ sarvā diṣṭyā nādyeha paśyasi.
english: And today, it is fortunate that you do not see here all your daughters-in-law as widows, nor Śala and Bhūriśravas, slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 9. And today, it is fortunate that you do not see here all your daughters-in-law as widows, nor Śala and Bhūriśravas, slain in battle.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या तत्काञ्चनं छत्रं यूपकेतोर्महात्मनः ।विनिकीर्णं रथोपस्थे सौमदत्तेर्न पश्यसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā tatkāñcanaṁ chatraṁ yūpaketormahātmanaḥ ,vinikīrṇaṁ rathopasthe saumadatterna paśyasi.
english: Fortunately, do you not see that golden parasol of the great-souled Saumadatti (Bhūriśravas), also known as Yūpaketu, lying scattered on the floor of his chariot?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 10. Fortunately, do you not see that golden parasol of the great-souled Saumadatti (Bhūriśravas), also known as Yūpaketu, lying scattered on the floor of his chariot?

sanskrit: अमूस्तु भूरिश्रवसो भार्याः सात्यकिना हतम् ।परिवार्यानुशोचन्ति भर्तारमसितेक्षणाः ॥११॥
transliteration: amūstu bhūriśravaso bhāryāḥ sātyakinā hatam ,parivāryānuśocanti bhartāramasitekṣaṇāḥ.
english: But these dark-eyed wives of Bhūriśravas are lamenting, having surrounded their husband who was slain by Sātyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 11. But these dark-eyed wives of Bhūriśravas are lamenting, having surrounded their husband who was slain by Sātyaki.

sanskrit: एता विलप्य बहुलं भर्तृशोकेन कर्शिताः ।पतन्त्यभिमुखा भूमौ कृपणं बत केशव ॥१२॥
transliteration: etā vilapya bahulaṁ bhartṛśokena karśitāḥ ,patantyabhimukhā bhūmau kṛpaṇaṁ bata keśava.
english: O Keśava, alas! These women, emaciated by the grief for their husband, having lamented greatly, fall miserably to the ground, facing (him).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 12. O Keśava, alas! These women, emaciated by the grief for their husband, having lamented greatly, fall miserably to the ground, facing (him).

sanskrit: बीभत्सुरतिबीभत्सं कर्मेदमकरोत्कथम् ।प्रमत्तस्य यदच्छैत्सीद्बाहुं शूरस्य यज्वनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bībhatsuratibībhatsaṁ karmedamakarotkatham ,pramattasya yadacchaitsīdbāhuṁ śūrasya yajvanaḥ.
english: How could Bibhatsu (Arjuna) have performed this extremely heinous deed, namely, that he cut off the arm of that brave performer of Vedic rituals (yajvan) who was heedless?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 13. How could Bibhatsu (Arjuna) have performed this extremely heinous deed, namely, that he cut off the arm of that brave performer of Vedic rituals (yajvan) who was heedless?

sanskrit: ततः पापतरं कर्म कृतवानपि सात्यकिः ।यस्मात्प्रायोपविष्टस्य प्राहार्षीत्संशितात्मनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ pāpataraṁ karma kṛtavānapi sātyakiḥ ,yasmātprāyopaviṣṭasya prāhārṣītsaṁśitātmanaḥ.
english: Even Satyaki committed an even greater sin, for he struck down a man who was observing a fast unto death and whose soul was disciplined.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 14. Even Satyaki committed an even greater sin, for he struck down a man who was observing a fast unto death and whose soul was disciplined.

sanskrit: एको द्वाभ्यां हतः शेषे त्वमधर्मेण धार्मिकः ।इति यूपध्वजस्यैताः स्त्रियः क्रोशन्ति माधव ॥१५॥
transliteration: eko dvābhyāṁ hataḥ śeṣe tvamadharmeṇa dhārmikaḥ ,iti yūpadhvajasyaitāḥ striyaḥ krośanti mādhava.
english: "One man was killed by two, and in this remaining situation, you, O righteous one, have allowed this through unrighteousness (adharma)!" Thus, O Madhava, these women of Yūpadhvaja lament.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 15. "One man was killed by two, and in this remaining situation, you, O righteous one, have allowed this through unrighteousness (adharma)!" Thus, O Madhava, these women of Yūpadhvaja lament.

sanskrit: भार्या यूपध्वजस्यैषा करसंमितमध्यमा ।कृत्वोत्सङ्गे भुजं भर्तुः कृपणं पर्यदेवयत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhāryā yūpadhvajasyaiṣā karasaṁmitamadhyamā ,kṛtvotsaṅge bhujaṁ bhartuḥ kṛpaṇaṁ paryadevayat.
english: The slender-waisted wife of Yūpadhvaja, taking her husband's arm onto her lap, lamented piteously.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 16. The slender-waisted wife of Yūpadhvaja, taking her husband's arm onto her lap, lamented piteously.

sanskrit: अयं स रशनोत्कर्षी पीनस्तनविमर्दनः ।नाभ्यूरुजघनस्पर्शी नीवीविस्रंसनः करः ॥१७॥
transliteration: ayaṁ sa raśanotkarṣī pīnastanavimardanaḥ ,nābhyūrujaghanasparśī nīvīvisraṁsanaḥ karaḥ.
english: "This is that very hand which used to pull at my girdle, press against my full breasts, touch my navel, thighs, and hips, and loosen the knot of my lower garment."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 17. "This is that very hand which used to pull at my girdle, press against my full breasts, touch my navel, thighs, and hips, and loosen the knot of my lower garment."

sanskrit: वासुदेवस्य सांनिध्ये पार्थेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।युध्यतः समरेऽन्येन प्रमत्तस्य निपातितः ॥१८॥
transliteration: vāsudevasya sāṁnidhye pārthenākliṣṭakarmaṇā ,yudhyataḥ samare'nyena pramattasya nipātitaḥ.
english: In the presence of Vāsudeva, Pārtha, whose actions are unfailing, struck down an opponent in battle who was fighting another and was thus distracted.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 18. In the presence of Vāsudeva, Pārtha, whose actions are unfailing, struck down an opponent in battle who was fighting another and was thus distracted.

sanskrit: किं नु वक्ष्यसि संसत्सु कथासु च जनार्दन ।अर्जुनस्य महत्कर्म स्वयं वा स किरीटवान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu vakṣyasi saṁsatsu kathāsu ca janārdana ,arjunasya mahatkarma svayaṁ vā sa kirīṭavān.
english: O Janārdana, what indeed will you say in assemblies and stories about Arjuna's great deed, or what will that crowned one (Arjuna) himself say?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 19. O Janārdana, what indeed will you say in assemblies and stories about Arjuna's great deed, or what will that crowned one (Arjuna) himself say?

sanskrit: इत्येवं गर्हयित्वैषा तूष्णीमास्ते वराङ्गना ।तामेतामनुशोचन्ति सपत्न्यः स्वामिव स्नुषाम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ garhayitvaiṣā tūṣṇīmāste varāṅganā ,tāmetāmanuśocanti sapatnyaḥ svāmiva snuṣām.
english: Having reproached in this manner, this excellent woman (Draupadi) remains silent. Her co-wives grieve for her as if she were their own daughter-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 20. Having reproached in this manner, this excellent woman (Draupadi) remains silent. Her co-wives grieve for her as if she were their own daughter-in-law.

sanskrit: गान्धारराजः शकुनिर्बलवान्सत्यविक्रमः ।निहतः सहदेवेन भागिनेयेन मातुलः ॥२१॥
transliteration: gāndhārarājaḥ śakunirbalavānsatyavikramaḥ ,nihataḥ sahadevena bhāgineyena mātulaḥ.
english: King Shakuni of Gandhāra, powerful and of true valor, was struck down by Sahadeva; the maternal uncle (Shakuni was killed) by the nephew (Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 21. King Shakuni of Gandhāra, powerful and of true valor, was struck down by Sahadeva; the maternal uncle (Shakuni was killed) by the nephew (Sahadeva).

sanskrit: यः पुरा हेमदण्डाभ्यां व्यजनाभ्यां स्म वीज्यते ।स एष पक्षिभिः पक्षैः शयान उपवीज्यते ॥२२॥
transliteration: yaḥ purā hemadaṇḍābhyāṁ vyajanābhyāṁ sma vījyate ,sa eṣa pakṣibhiḥ pakṣaiḥ śayāna upavījyate.
english: He who was formerly fanned by two fans with golden staffs is now, while lying down, being fanned by birds with their wings.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 22. He who was formerly fanned by two fans with golden staffs is now, while lying down, being fanned by birds with their wings.

sanskrit: यः स्म रूपाणि कुरुते शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।तस्य मायाविनो माया दग्धाः पाण्डवतेजसा ॥२३॥
transliteration: yaḥ sma rūpāṇi kurute śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ ,tasya māyāvino māyā dagdhāḥ pāṇḍavatejasā.
english: The illusions (māyā) of that magician (māyāvin), who used to create forms by hundreds and thousands, have been burned by the power of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 23. The illusions (māyā) of that magician (māyāvin), who used to create forms by hundreds and thousands, have been burned by the power of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: मायया निकृतिप्रज्ञो जितवान्यो युधिष्ठिरम् ।सभायां विपुलं राज्यं स पुनर्जीवितं जितः ॥२४॥
transliteration: māyayā nikṛtiprajño jitavānyo yudhiṣṭhiram ,sabhāyāṁ vipulaṁ rājyaṁ sa punarjīvitaṁ jitaḥ.
english: He who, with his cunning intellect, vanquished Yudhiṣṭhira through illusion (māyā) and won a vast kingdom in the assembly, that very person is now, in turn, conquered and deprived of his own life.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 24. He who, with his cunning intellect, vanquished Yudhiṣṭhira through illusion (māyā) and won a vast kingdom in the assembly, that very person is now, in turn, conquered and deprived of his own life.

sanskrit: शकुन्ताः शकुनिं कृष्ण समन्तात्पर्युपासते ।कितवं मम पुत्राणां विनाशायोपशिक्षितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: śakuntāḥ śakuniṁ kṛṣṇa samantātparyupāsate ,kitavaṁ mama putrāṇāṁ vināśāyopaśikṣitam.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, birds are surrounding that gambler Śakuni from all sides, he who was instructed for the destruction of my sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 25. O Kṛṣṇa, birds are surrounding that gambler Śakuni from all sides, he who was instructed for the destruction of my sons.

sanskrit: एतेनैतन्महद्वैरं प्रसक्तं पाण्डवैः सह ।वधाय मम पुत्राणामात्मनः सगणस्य च ॥२६॥
transliteration: etenaitanmahadvairaṁ prasaktaṁ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha ,vadhāya mama putrāṇāmātmanaḥ sagaṇasya ca.
english: Because of this, a great enmity has arisen with the Pāṇḍavas, leading to the destruction of my sons, myself (ātman), and my followers.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 26. Because of this, a great enmity has arisen with the Pāṇḍavas, leading to the destruction of my sons, myself (ātman), and my followers.

sanskrit: यथैव मम पुत्राणां लोकाः शस्त्रजिताः प्रभो ।एवमस्यापि दुर्बुद्धेर्लोकाः शस्त्रेण वै जिताः ॥२७॥
transliteration: yathaiva mama putrāṇāṁ lokāḥ śastrajitāḥ prabho ,evamasyāpi durbuddherlokāḥ śastreṇa vai jitāḥ.
english: O Lord, just as the realms of my sons have been won through battle, so too have the realms of this evil-minded one indeed been conquered by force of arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 27. O Lord, just as the realms of my sons have been won through battle, so too have the realms of this evil-minded one indeed been conquered by force of arms.

sanskrit: कथं च नायं तत्रापि पुत्रान्मे भ्रातृभिः सह ।विरोधयेदृजुप्रज्ञाननृजुर्मधुसूदन ॥२८॥
transliteration: kathaṁ ca nāyaṁ tatrāpi putrānme bhrātṛbhiḥ saha ,virodhayedṛjuprajñānanṛjurmadhusūdana.
english: O Madhusūdana, O you of straightforward wisdom, how will this crooked one (Duryodhana) not cause conflict between my sons and their brothers even in that situation?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 24 Verse 28. O Madhusūdana, O you of straightforward wisdom, how will this crooked one (Duryodhana) not cause conflict between my sons and their brothers even in that situation?

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।काम्बोजं पश्य दुर्धर्षं काम्बोजास्तरणोचितम् ।शयानमृषभस्कन्धं हतं पांसुशु माधव ॥१॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,kāmbojaṁ paśya durdharṣaṁ kāmbojāstaraṇocitam ,śayānamṛṣabhaskandhaṁ hataṁ pāṁsuśu mādhava.
english: Gāndhārī said: O Mādhava, behold the irresistible Kāmboja king, accustomed to Kāmboja coverings, with bull-like shoulders, lying slain in the dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 1. Gāndhārī said: O Mādhava, behold the irresistible Kāmboja king, accustomed to Kāmboja coverings, with bull-like shoulders, lying slain in the dust.

sanskrit: यस्य क्षतजसंदिग्धौ बाहू चन्दनरूषितौ ।अवेक्ष्य कृपणं भार्या विलपत्यतिदुःखिता ॥२॥
transliteration: yasya kṣatajasaṁdigdhau bāhū candanarūṣitau ,avekṣya kṛpaṇaṁ bhāryā vilapatyatiduḥkhitā.
english: His extremely distressed wife, seeing his two arms - which were once adorned with sandalwood but now appear stained with blood - laments piteously.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 2. His extremely distressed wife, seeing his two arms - which were once adorned with sandalwood but now appear stained with blood - laments piteously.

sanskrit: इमौ तौ परिघप्रख्यौ बाहू शुभतलाङ्गुली ।ययोर्विवरमापन्नां न रतिर्मां पुराजहत् ॥३॥
transliteration: imau tau parighaprakhyau bāhū śubhatalāṅgulī ,yayorvivaramāpannāṁ na ratirmāṁ purājahat.
english: These are those very arms, strong as iron bars and possessing beautiful palms and fingers. Formerly, when I rested within their embrace, pleasure (rati) never abandoned me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 3. These are those very arms, strong as iron bars and possessing beautiful palms and fingers. Formerly, when I rested within their embrace, pleasure (rati) never abandoned me.

sanskrit: कां गतिं नु गमिष्यामि त्वया हीना जनेश्वर ।दूरबन्धुरनाथेव अतीव मधुरस्वरा ॥४॥
transliteration: kāṁ gatiṁ nu gamiṣyāmi tvayā hīnā janeśvara ,dūrabandhuranātheva atīva madhurasvarā.
english: O lord of people, deprived of you, what destiny indeed shall I attain? I shall become like an orphan, with distant relatives and no protector, despite my exceedingly sweet voice.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 4. O lord of people, deprived of you, what destiny indeed shall I attain? I shall become like an orphan, with distant relatives and no protector, despite my exceedingly sweet voice.

sanskrit: आतपे क्लाम्यमानानां विविधानामिव स्रजाम् ।क्लान्तानामपि नारीणां न श्रीर्जहति वै तनुम् ॥५॥
transliteration: ātape klāmyamānānāṁ vividhānāmiva srajām ,klāntānāmapi nārīṇāṁ na śrīrjahati vai tanum.
english: Just as the freshness of various garlands wilting in the sun does not abandon their form, similarly, even for exhausted women, beauty (śrī) indeed does not abandon their body.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 5. Just as the freshness of various garlands wilting in the sun does not abandon their form, similarly, even for exhausted women, beauty (śrī) indeed does not abandon their body.

sanskrit: शयानमभितः शूरं कालिङ्गं मधुसूदन ।पश्य दीप्ताङ्गदयुगप्रतिबद्धमहाभुजम् ॥६॥
transliteration: śayānamabhitaḥ śūraṁ kāliṅgaṁ madhusūdana ,paśya dīptāṅgadayugapratibaddhamahābhujam.
english: O Madhusūdana, behold the brave Kalinga king lying nearby, whose mighty arms are adorned with a pair of shining armlets.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 6. O Madhusūdana, behold the brave Kalinga king lying nearby, whose mighty arms are adorned with a pair of shining armlets.

sanskrit: मागधानामधिपतिं जयत्सेनं जनार्दन ।परिवार्य प्ररुदिता मागध्यः पश्य योषितः ॥७॥
transliteration: māgadhānāmadhipatiṁ jayatsenaṁ janārdana ,parivārya praruditā māgadhyaḥ paśya yoṣitaḥ.
english: O Janārdana, behold the women of Magadha, who are weeping aloud, having surrounded Jayatsena, the ruler of the Magadhas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 7. O Janārdana, behold the women of Magadha, who are weeping aloud, having surrounded Jayatsena, the ruler of the Magadhas.

sanskrit: आसामायतनेत्राणां सुस्वराणां जनार्दन ।मनःश्रुतिहरो नादो मनो मोहयतीव मे ॥८॥
transliteration: āsāmāyatanetrāṇāṁ susvarāṇāṁ janārdana ,manaḥśrutiharo nādo mano mohayatīva me.
english: O Janārdana, the sound (or wailing) of these long-eyed, sweet-voiced women, which steals away the mind and hearing, seems to bewilder my mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 8. O Janārdana, the sound (or wailing) of these long-eyed, sweet-voiced women, which steals away the mind and hearing, seems to bewilder my mind.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्णसर्वाभरणा रुदन्त्यः शोककर्शिताः ।स्वास्तीर्णशयनोपेता मागध्यः शेरते भुवि ॥९॥
transliteration: prakīrṇasarvābharaṇā rudantyaḥ śokakarśitāḥ ,svāstīrṇaśayanopetā māgadhyaḥ śerate bhuvi.
english: The Magadha women, with all their ornaments scattered, weeping and emaciated by grief, lie on the ground upon their well-spread beds.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 9. The Magadha women, with all their ornaments scattered, weeping and emaciated by grief, lie on the ground upon their well-spread beds.

sanskrit: कोसलानामधिपतिं राजपुत्रं बृहद्बलम् ।भर्तारं परिवार्यैताः पृथक्प्ररुदिताः स्त्रियः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kosalānāmadhipatiṁ rājaputraṁ bṛhadbalam ,bhartāraṁ parivāryaitāḥ pṛthakpraruditāḥ striyaḥ.
english: These women, surrounding their husband, the prince Bṛhadbala, who was the lord of the Kośalas, cried separately.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 10. These women, surrounding their husband, the prince Bṛhadbala, who was the lord of the Kośalas, cried separately.

sanskrit: अस्य गात्रगतान्बाणान्कार्ष्णिबाहुबलार्पितान् ।उद्धरन्त्यसुखाविष्टा मूर्छमानाः पुनः पुनः ॥११॥
transliteration: asya gātragatānbāṇānkārṣṇibāhubalārpitān ,uddharantyasukhāviṣṭā mūrchamānāḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Distressed and repeatedly fainting, they are pulling out the arrows that are embedded in his body, which were lodged there by the strength of Kṛṣṇa's son's arm.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 11. Distressed and repeatedly fainting, they are pulling out the arrows that are embedded in his body, which were lodged there by the strength of Kṛṣṇa's son's arm.

sanskrit: आसां सर्वानवद्यानामातपेन परिश्रमात् ।प्रम्लाननलिनाभानि भान्ति वक्त्राणि माधव ॥१२॥
transliteration: āsāṁ sarvānavadyānāmātapena pariśramāt ,pramlānanalinābhāni bhānti vaktrāṇi mādhava.
english: O Mādhava, the faces of all these faultless women, which resemble withered lotuses, glow due to their exhaustion from the sun's heat.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 12. O Mādhava, the faces of all these faultless women, which resemble withered lotuses, glow due to their exhaustion from the sun's heat.

sanskrit: द्रोणेन निहताः शूराः शेरते रुचिराङ्गदाः ।द्रोणेनाभिमुखाः सर्वे भ्रातरः पञ्च केकयाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: droṇena nihatāḥ śūrāḥ śerate rucirāṅgadāḥ ,droṇenābhimukhāḥ sarve bhrātaraḥ pañca kekayāḥ.
english: The heroic warriors, slain by Droṇa, who wore shining armlets, lie (dead). All five Kekaya brothers, who confronted Droṇa, also lie (dead).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 13. The heroic warriors, slain by Droṇa, who wore shining armlets, lie (dead). All five Kekaya brothers, who confronted Droṇa, also lie (dead).

sanskrit: तप्तकाञ्चनवर्माणस्ताम्रध्वजरथस्रजः ।भासयन्ति महीं भासा ज्वलिता इव पावकाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: taptakāñcanavarmāṇastāmradhvajarathasrajaḥ ,bhāsayanti mahīṁ bhāsā jvalitā iva pāvakāḥ.
english: With armors of molten gold and copper-colored banners, chariots, and garlands, they illuminate the earth with their brilliance, just like blazing fires.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 14. With armors of molten gold and copper-colored banners, chariots, and garlands, they illuminate the earth with their brilliance, just like blazing fires.

sanskrit: द्रोणेन द्रुपदं संख्ये पश्य माधव पातितम् ।महाद्विपमिवारण्ये सिंहेन महता हतम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: droṇena drupadaṁ saṁkhye paśya mādhava pātitam ,mahādvipamivāraṇye siṁhena mahatā hatam.
english: O Mādhava, behold Drupada struck down in battle by Droṇa, like a great elephant slain in the forest by a mighty lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 15. O Mādhava, behold Drupada struck down in battle by Droṇa, like a great elephant slain in the forest by a mighty lion.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालराज्ञो विपुलं पुण्डरीकाक्ष पाण्डुरम् ।आतपत्रं समाभाति शरदीव दिवाकरः ॥१६॥
transliteration: pāñcālarājño vipulaṁ puṇḍarīkākṣa pāṇḍuram ,ātapatraṁ samābhāti śaradīva divākaraḥ.
english: O lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), the vast, white sunshade of the Pañcāla king shines brightly, like the sun in autumn.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 16. O lotus-eyed one (puṇḍarīkākṣa), the vast, white sunshade of the Pañcāla king shines brightly, like the sun in autumn.

sanskrit: एतास्तु द्रुपदं वृद्धं स्नुषा भार्याश्च दुःखिताः ।दग्ध्वा गच्छन्ति पाञ्चाल्यं राजानमपसव्यतः ॥१७॥
transliteration: etāstu drupadaṁ vṛddhaṁ snuṣā bhāryāśca duḥkhitāḥ ,dagdhvā gacchanti pāñcālyaṁ rājānamapasavyataḥ.
english: Indeed, these distressed daughters-in-law and wives, having cremated the aged Drupada, proceed to King Pāñcālya, performing a counter-clockwise circumambulation.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 17. Indeed, these distressed daughters-in-law and wives, having cremated the aged Drupada, proceed to King Pāñcālya, performing a counter-clockwise circumambulation.

sanskrit: धृष्टकेतुं महेष्वासं चेदिपुंगवमङ्गनाः ।द्रोणेन निहतं शूरं हरन्ति हृतचेतसः ॥१८॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭaketuṁ maheṣvāsaṁ cedipuṁgavamaṅganāḥ ,droṇena nihataṁ śūraṁ haranti hṛtacetasaḥ.
english: The women, their minds distraught, carry away the hero Dhṛṣṭaketu, the great archer and foremost among the Cedis, who was slain by Droṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 18. The women, their minds distraught, carry away the hero Dhṛṣṭaketu, the great archer and foremost among the Cedis, who was slain by Droṇa.

sanskrit: द्रोणास्त्रमभिहत्यैष विमर्दे मधुसूदन ।महेष्वासो हतः शेते नद्या हृत इव द्रुमः ॥१९॥
transliteration: droṇāstramabhihatyaiṣa vimarde madhusūdana ,maheṣvāso hataḥ śete nadyā hṛta iva drumaḥ.
english: O Madhusūdana, this great archer, having been struck down by Droṇa's weapon in the conflict, lies killed, just like a tree swept away by a river.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 19. O Madhusūdana, this great archer, having been struck down by Droṇa's weapon in the conflict, lies killed, just like a tree swept away by a river.

sanskrit: एष चेदिपतिः शूरो धृष्टकेतुर्महारथः ।शेते विनिहतः संख्ये हत्वा शत्रून्सहस्रशः ॥२०॥
transliteration: eṣa cedipatiḥ śūro dhṛṣṭaketurmahārathaḥ ,śete vinihataḥ saṁkhye hatvā śatrūnsahasraśaḥ.
english: This hero Dhṛṣṭaketu, the lord of the Cedis and a great charioteer, lies completely slain in battle, having himself killed thousands of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 20. This hero Dhṛṣṭaketu, the lord of the Cedis and a great charioteer, lies completely slain in battle, having himself killed thousands of enemies.

sanskrit: वितुद्यमानं विहगैस्तं भार्याः प्रत्युपस्थिताः ।चेदिराजं हृषीकेश हतं सबलबान्धवम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vitudyamānaṁ vihagaistaṁ bhāryāḥ pratyupasthitāḥ ,cedirājaṁ hṛṣīkeśa hataṁ sabalabāndhavam.
english: O Hṛṣīkeśa, his wives have come to stand before him, the King of Cedis, who lies slain along with his forces and kinsmen, and is being torn at by birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 21. O Hṛṣīkeśa, his wives have come to stand before him, the King of Cedis, who lies slain along with his forces and kinsmen, and is being torn at by birds.

sanskrit: दाशार्हीपुत्रजं वीरं शयानं सत्यविक्रमम् ।आरोप्याङ्के रुदन्त्येताश्चेदिराजवराङ्गनाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: dāśārhīputrajaṁ vīraṁ śayānaṁ satyavikramam ,āropyāṅke rudantyetāścedirājavarāṅganāḥ.
english: These excellent wives of the Chedi king, weeping, placed upon their laps that truly valorous hero, the son of the Dāśārha woman (Subhadra), as he lay there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 22. These excellent wives of the Chedi king, weeping, placed upon their laps that truly valorous hero, the son of the Dāśārha woman (Subhadra), as he lay there.

sanskrit: अस्य पुत्रं हृषीकेश सुवक्त्रं चारुकुण्डलम् ।द्रोणेन समरे पश्य निकृत्तं बहुधा शरैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: asya putraṁ hṛṣīkeśa suvaktraṁ cārukuṇḍalam ,droṇena samare paśya nikṛttaṁ bahudhā śaraiḥ.
english: O Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna), behold this son, handsome-faced, with beautiful earrings, cut down in battle by Droṇa in many ways with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 23. O Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna), behold this son, handsome-faced, with beautiful earrings, cut down in battle by Droṇa in many ways with arrows.

sanskrit: पितरं नूनमाजिस्थं युध्यमानं परैः सह ।नाजहात्पृष्ठतो वीरमद्यापि मधुसूदन ॥२४॥
transliteration: pitaraṁ nūnamājisthaṁ yudhyamānaṁ paraiḥ saha ,nājahātpṛṣṭhato vīramadyāpi madhusūdana.
english: O Madhusūdana (Krishna), surely this hero (Abhimanyu) did not abandon his father who was standing in battle, fighting with enemies, not even from behind, not even now.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 24. O Madhusūdana (Krishna), surely this hero (Abhimanyu) did not abandon his father who was standing in battle, fighting with enemies, not even from behind, not even now.

sanskrit: एवं ममापि पुत्रस्य पुत्रः पितरमन्वगात् ।दुर्योधनं महाबाहो लक्ष्मणः परवीरहा ॥२५॥
transliteration: evaṁ mamāpi putrasya putraḥ pitaramanvagāt ,duryodhanaṁ mahābāho lakṣmaṇaḥ paravīrahā.
english: O mighty-armed one, just as my son's son followed his father, so too Lakṣmaṇa, the slayer of enemy heroes, followed his father Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 25. O mighty-armed one, just as my son's son followed his father, so too Lakṣmaṇa, the slayer of enemy heroes, followed his father Duryodhana.

sanskrit: विन्दानुविन्दावावन्त्यौ पतितौ पश्य माधव ।हिमान्ते पुष्पितौ शालौ मरुता गलिताविव ॥२६॥
transliteration: vindānuvindāvāvantyau patitau paśya mādhava ,himānte puṣpitau śālau marutā galitāviva.
english: O Mādhava, behold Vindā and Anuvindā, the two princes of Avanti, fallen, like two blooming śāla trees withered by the wind at the end of winter.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 26. O Mādhava, behold Vindā and Anuvindā, the two princes of Avanti, fallen, like two blooming śāla trees withered by the wind at the end of winter.

sanskrit: काञ्चनाङ्गदवर्माणौ बाणखड्गधनुर्धरौ ।ऋषभप्रतिरूपाक्षौ शयानौ विमलस्रजौ ॥२७॥
transliteration: kāñcanāṅgadavarmāṇau bāṇakhaḍgadhanurdharau ,ṛṣabhapratirūpākṣau śayānau vimalasrajau.
english: They are lying down, adorned with golden armlets and armor, holding arrows, swords, and bows, with eyes resembling those of bulls, and wearing pure garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 27. They are lying down, adorned with golden armlets and armor, holding arrows, swords, and bows, with eyes resembling those of bulls, and wearing pure garlands.

sanskrit: अवध्याः पाण्डवाः कृष्ण सर्व एव त्वया सह ।ये मुक्ता द्रोणभीष्माभ्यां कर्णाद्वैकर्तनात्कृपात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: avadhyāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ kṛṣṇa sarva eva tvayā saha ,ye muktā droṇabhīṣmābhyāṁ karṇādvaikartanātkṛpāt.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, all the Pāṇḍavas, along with you, are invincible (literally, not to be killed). They are the ones who escaped from Droṇa and Bhīṣma, from Karṇa, from Vaikartana (Karṇa), and from Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 28. O Kṛṣṇa, all the Pāṇḍavas, along with you, are invincible (literally, not to be killed). They are the ones who escaped from Droṇa and Bhīṣma, from Karṇa, from Vaikartana (Karṇa), and from Kṛpa.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनाद्द्रोणसुतात्सैन्धवाच्च महारथात् ।सोमदत्ताद्विकर्णाच्च शूराच्च कृतवर्मणः ।ये हन्युः शस्त्रवेगेन देवानपि नरर्षभाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: duryodhanāddroṇasutātsaindhavācca mahārathāt ,somadattādvikarṇācca śūrācca kṛtavarmaṇaḥ ,ye hanyuḥ śastravegena devānapi nararṣabhāḥ.
english: And from Duryodhana, from Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), and from Saindhava (Jayadratha), the great charioteers; from Somadatta and Vikarṇa; and from the brave Kṛtavarman. These best of men (the Pāṇḍavas) could even kill the gods with the force of their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 29. And from Duryodhana, from Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), and from Saindhava (Jayadratha), the great charioteers; from Somadatta and Vikarṇa; and from the brave Kṛtavarman. These best of men (the Pāṇḍavas) could even kill the gods with the force of their weapons.

sanskrit: त इमे निहताः संख्ये पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ।नातिभारोऽस्ति दैवस्य ध्रुवं माधव कश्चन ।यदिमे निहताः शूराः क्षत्रियैः क्षत्रियर्षभाः ॥३०॥
transliteration: ta ime nihatāḥ saṁkhye paśya kālasya paryayam ,nātibhāro'sti daivasya dhruvaṁ mādhava kaścana ,yadime nihatāḥ śūrāḥ kṣatriyaiḥ kṣatriyarṣabhāḥ.
english: O Mādhava, behold the turn of time! These very ones have been slain in battle. There is certainly no undue burden upon divine destiny. If these heroic, preeminent kṣatriyas are indeed slain by other kṣatriyas...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 30. O Mādhava, behold the turn of time! These very ones have been slain in battle. There is certainly no undue burden upon divine destiny. If these heroic, preeminent kṣatriyas are indeed slain by other kṣatriyas...

sanskrit: तदैव निहताः कृष्ण मम पुत्रास्तरस्विनः ।यदैवाकृतकामस्त्वमुपप्लव्यं गतः पुनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tadaiva nihatāḥ kṛṣṇa mama putrāstarasvinaḥ ,yadaivākṛtakāmastvamupaplavyaṁ gataḥ punaḥ.
english: O Krishna, my powerful sons were indeed slain at that very moment when you, with your desires unfulfilled, returned to Upaplavyam.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 31. O Krishna, my powerful sons were indeed slain at that very moment when you, with your desires unfulfilled, returned to Upaplavyam.

sanskrit: शंतनोश्चैव पुत्रेण प्राज्ञेन विदुरेण च ।तदैवोक्तास्मि मा स्नेहं कुरुष्वात्मसुतेष्विति ॥३२॥
transliteration: śaṁtanoścaiva putreṇa prājñena vidureṇa ca ,tadaivoktāsmi mā snehaṁ kuruṣvātmasuteṣviti.
english: And at that very time, I was told by the wise son of Śantanu, Vidura, 'Do not show affection for your own sons.'
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 32. And at that very time, I was told by the wise son of Śantanu, Vidura, 'Do not show affection for your own sons.'

sanskrit: तयोर्न दर्शनं तात मिथ्या भवितुमर्हति ।अचिरेणैव मे पुत्रा भस्मीभूता जनार्दन ॥३३॥
transliteration: tayorna darśanaṁ tāta mithyā bhavitumarhati ,acireṇaiva me putrā bhasmībhūtā janārdana.
english: O dear one, the foresight of those two (Bhishma and Vidura) cannot be false. O Janārdana, my sons will very soon be reduced to ashes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 33. O dear one, the foresight of those two (Bhishma and Vidura) cannot be false. O Janārdana, my sons will very soon be reduced to ashes.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा न्यपतद्भूमौ गान्धारी शोककर्शिता ।दुःखोपहतविज्ञाना धैर्यमुत्सृज्य भारत ॥३४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā nyapatadbhūmau gāndhārī śokakarśitā ,duḥkhopahatavijñānā dhairyamutsṛjya bhārata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Gāndhārī, tormented by grief and with her understanding overwhelmed by sorrow, abandoned all courage and fell to the ground, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 34. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Gāndhārī, tormented by grief and with her understanding overwhelmed by sorrow, abandoned all courage and fell to the ground, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: ततः कोपपरीताङ्गी पुत्रशोकपरिप्लुता ।जगाम शौरिं दोषेण गान्धारी व्यथितेन्द्रिया ॥३५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kopaparītāṅgī putraśokapariplutā ,jagāma śauriṁ doṣeṇa gāndhārī vyathitendriyā.
english: Then, Gāndhārī, whose limbs were overcome by anger, submerged in sorrow for her sons, and with her senses greatly disturbed, approached Śauri (Kṛṣṇa) with an accusation.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 35. Then, Gāndhārī, whose limbs were overcome by anger, submerged in sorrow for her sons, and with her senses greatly disturbed, approached Śauri (Kṛṣṇa) with an accusation.

sanskrit: गान्धार्युवाच ।पाण्डवा धार्तराष्ट्राश्च द्रुग्धाः कृष्ण परस्परम् ।उपेक्षिता विनश्यन्तस्त्वया कस्माज्जनार्दन ॥३६॥
transliteration: gāndhāryuvāca ,pāṇḍavā dhārtarāṣṭrāśca drugdhāḥ kṛṣṇa parasparam ,upekṣitā vinaśyantastvayā kasmājjanārdana.
english: Gāndhārī said: O Kṛṣṇa, O Janārdana, why were the Pāṇḍavas and the Dhārtarāṣṭras, who wronged each other and were perishing, neglected by you?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 36. Gāndhārī said: O Kṛṣṇa, O Janārdana, why were the Pāṇḍavas and the Dhārtarāṣṭras, who wronged each other and were perishing, neglected by you?

sanskrit: शक्तेन बहुभृत्येन विपुले तिष्ठता बले ।उभयत्र समर्थेन श्रुतवाक्येन चैव ह ॥३७॥
transliteration: śaktena bahubhṛtyena vipule tiṣṭhatā bale ,ubhayatra samarthena śrutavākyena caiva ha.
english: (You, O Janārdana), who are capable, who have many followers, who stand with immense power, who are competent for both parties, and whose words were indeed heard (by both sides).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 37. (You, O Janārdana), who are capable, who have many followers, who stand with immense power, who are competent for both parties, and whose words were indeed heard (by both sides).

sanskrit: इच्छतोपेक्षितो नाशः कुरूणां मधुसूदन ।यस्मात्त्वया महाबाहो फलं तस्मादवाप्नुहि ॥३८॥
transliteration: icchatopekṣito nāśaḥ kurūṇāṁ madhusūdana ,yasmāttvayā mahābāho phalaṁ tasmādavāpnuhi.
english: O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), though you desired (to prevent it), the destruction of the Kurus was neglected by you. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, you shall obtain the consequence from that.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 38. O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), though you desired (to prevent it), the destruction of the Kurus was neglected by you. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, you shall obtain the consequence from that.

sanskrit: पतिशुश्रूषया यन्मे तपः किंचिदुपार्जितम् ।तेन त्वां दुरवापात्मञ्शप्स्ये चक्रगदाधर ॥३९॥
transliteration: patiśuśrūṣayā yanme tapaḥ kiṁcidupārjitam ,tena tvāṁ duravāpātmañśapsye cakragadādhara.
english: Whatever spiritual austerity (tapas) I have accumulated through devoted service to my husband, by that very power, O you whose true self (ātman) is difficult to grasp (duravāpātman), O bearer of the discus and mace (cakragadādhara), I will curse you.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 39. Whatever spiritual austerity (tapas) I have accumulated through devoted service to my husband, by that very power, O you whose true self (ātman) is difficult to grasp (duravāpātman), O bearer of the discus and mace (cakragadādhara), I will curse you.

sanskrit: यस्मात्परस्परं घ्नन्तो ज्ञातयः कुरुपाण्डवाः ।उपेक्षितास्ते गोविन्द तस्माज्ज्ञातीन्वधिष्यसि ॥४०॥
transliteration: yasmātparasparaṁ ghnanto jñātayaḥ kurupāṇḍavāḥ ,upekṣitāste govinda tasmājjñātīnvadhiṣyasi.
english: O Govinda (govinda), because you neglected your Kuru and Pandava kinsmen who were engaged in mutual killing, therefore you shall kill your (own) kinsmen.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 40. O Govinda (govinda), because you neglected your Kuru and Pandava kinsmen who were engaged in mutual killing, therefore you shall kill your (own) kinsmen.

sanskrit: त्वमप्युपस्थिते वर्षे षट्त्रिंशे मधुसूदन ।हतज्ञातिर्हतामात्यो हतपुत्रो वनेचरः ।कुत्सितेनाभ्युपायेन निधनं समवाप्स्यसि ॥४१॥
transliteration: tvamapyupasthite varṣe ṣaṭtriṁśe madhusūdana ,hatajñātirhatāmātyo hataputro vanecaraḥ ,kutsitenābhyupāyena nidhanaṁ samavāpsyasi.
english: O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), when the thirty-sixth year arrives, you yourself, with your relatives dead, your ministers dead, and your sons dead, wandering in the forest, shall meet your end through a despicable means.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 41. O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), when the thirty-sixth year arrives, you yourself, with your relatives dead, your ministers dead, and your sons dead, wandering in the forest, shall meet your end through a despicable means.

sanskrit: तवाप्येवं हतसुता निहतज्ञातिबान्धवाः ।स्त्रियः परिपतिष्यन्ति यथैता भरतस्त्रियः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tavāpyevaṁ hatasutā nihatajñātibāndhavāḥ ,striyaḥ paripatiṣyanti yathaitā bharatastriyaḥ.
english: In this very way, your women, whose sons have been slain and whose kinsmen and relatives have been destroyed, will also fall into distress, just like these women of the Bhārata lineage.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 42. In this very way, your women, whose sons have been slain and whose kinsmen and relatives have been destroyed, will also fall into distress, just like these women of the Bhārata lineage.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं घोरं वासुदेवो महामनाः ।उवाच देवीं गान्धारीमीषदभ्युत्स्मयन्निव ॥४३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tacchrutvā vacanaṁ ghoraṁ vāsudevo mahāmanāḥ ,uvāca devīṁ gāndhārīmīṣadabhyutsmayanniva.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that terrible speech, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the great-souled one, spoke to Queen Gāndhārī, appearing as if with a slight smile.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 43. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that terrible speech, Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the great-souled one, spoke to Queen Gāndhārī, appearing as if with a slight smile.

sanskrit: संहर्ता वृष्णिचक्रस्य नान्यो मद्विद्यते शुभे ।जानेऽहमेतदप्येवं चीर्णं चरसि क्षत्रिये ॥४४॥
transliteration: saṁhartā vṛṣṇicakrasya nānyo madvidyate śubhe ,jāne'hametadapyevaṁ cīrṇaṁ carasi kṣatriye.
english: O auspicious one, there is no one other than me who will be the destroyer of the Vṛṣṇi clan. O Kṣatriya woman, I know that you have indeed performed (fulfilled) this curse in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 44. O auspicious one, there is no one other than me who will be the destroyer of the Vṛṣṇi clan. O Kṣatriya woman, I know that you have indeed performed (fulfilled) this curse in this manner.

sanskrit: अवध्यास्ते नरैरन्यैरपि वा देवदानवैः ।परस्परकृतं नाशमतः प्राप्स्यन्ति यादवाः ॥४५॥
transliteration: avadhyāste narairanyairapi vā devadānavaiḥ ,parasparakṛtaṁ nāśamataḥ prāpsyanti yādavāḥ.
english: They (the Yadavas) are not to be slain by other men, nor even by gods or Dānavas (demons). Therefore, the Yadavas will experience destruction brought about by themselves, through mutual conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 45. They (the Yadavas) are not to be slain by other men, nor even by gods or Dānavas (demons). Therefore, the Yadavas will experience destruction brought about by themselves, through mutual conflict.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवति दाशार्हे पाण्डवास्त्रस्तचेतसः ।बभूवुर्भृशसंविग्ना निराशाश्चापि जीविते ॥४६॥
transliteration: ityuktavati dāśārhe pāṇḍavāstrastacetasaḥ ,babhūvurbhṛśasaṁvignā nirāśāścāpi jīvite.
english: As soon as Krishna, the descendant of Daśārha, had spoken thus, the Pāṇḍavas, their minds struck with terror, became exceedingly agitated and lost all hope for their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 25 Verse 46. As soon as Krishna, the descendant of Daśārha, had spoken thus, the Pāṇḍavas, their minds struck with terror, became exceedingly agitated and lost all hope for their lives.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ गान्धारि मा च शोके मनः कृथाः ।तवैव ह्यपराधेन कुरवो निधनं गताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha gāndhāri mā ca śoke manaḥ kṛthāḥ ,tavaiva hyaparādhena kuravo nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Vāsudeva (Krishna) said: "Arise, arise, Gāndhārī! Do not dwell on sorrow. For indeed, it is solely due to your own transgression that the Kurus met their demise."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 1. Vāsudeva (Krishna) said: "Arise, arise, Gāndhārī! Do not dwell on sorrow. For indeed, it is solely due to your own transgression that the Kurus met their demise."

sanskrit: या त्वं पुत्रं दुरात्मानमीर्षुमत्यन्तमानिनम् ।दुर्योधनं पुरस्कृत्य दुष्कृतं साधु मन्यसे ॥२॥
transliteration: yā tvaṁ putraṁ durātmānamīrṣumatyantamāninam ,duryodhanaṁ puraskṛtya duṣkṛtaṁ sādhu manyase.
english: You, who championed your evil-minded, envious, and exceedingly arrogant son Duryodhana, considering his wrongdoing to be virtuous...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 2. You, who championed your evil-minded, envious, and exceedingly arrogant son Duryodhana, considering his wrongdoing to be virtuous...

sanskrit: निष्ठुरं वैरपरुषं वृद्धानां शासनातिगम् ।कथमात्मकृतं दोषं मय्याधातुमिहेच्छसि ॥३॥
transliteration: niṣṭhuraṁ vairaparuṣaṁ vṛddhānāṁ śāsanātigam ,kathamātmakṛtaṁ doṣaṁ mayyādhātumihecchasi.
english: How can you wish to attribute to me this fault, which was self-inflicted, harsh, ruthless due to hostility, and went against the commands of the elders?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 3. How can you wish to attribute to me this fault, which was self-inflicted, harsh, ruthless due to hostility, and went against the commands of the elders?

sanskrit: मृतं वा यदि वा नष्टं योऽतीतमनुशोचति ।दुःखेन लभते दुःखं द्वावनर्थौ प्रपद्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: mṛtaṁ vā yadi vā naṣṭaṁ yo'tītamanuśocati ,duḥkhena labhate duḥkhaṁ dvāvanarthau prapadyate.
english: Whoever grieves over something dead, lost, or belonging to the past obtains sorrow through that very sorrow, and thus encounters two misfortunes.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 4. Whoever grieves over something dead, lost, or belonging to the past obtains sorrow through that very sorrow, and thus encounters two misfortunes.

sanskrit: तपोर्थीयं ब्राह्मणी धत्त गर्भं गौर्वोढारं धावितारं तुरंगी ।शूद्रा दासं पशुपालं तु वैश्या वधार्थीयं त्वद्विधा राजपुत्री ॥५॥
transliteration: taporthīyaṁ brāhmaṇī dhatta garbhaṁ; gaurvoḍhāraṁ dhāvitāraṁ turaṁgī ,śūdrā dāsaṁ paśupālaṁ tu vaiśyā; vadhārthīyaṁ tvadvidhā rājaputrī.
english: A brahmin woman bears a child for the purpose of spiritual discipline (tapas); a cow bears one who will carry burdens; a mare bears a swift runner; a śūdra woman bears a servant; and a vaiśya woman bears a cattle-herder. But a princess like you bears one destined for destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 5. A brahmin woman bears a child for the purpose of spiritual discipline (tapas); a cow bears one who will carry burdens; a mare bears a swift runner; a śūdra woman bears a servant; and a vaiśya woman bears a cattle-herder. But a princess like you bears one destined for destruction.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तच्छ्रुत्वा वासुदेवस्य पुनरुक्तं वचोऽप्रियम् ।तूष्णीं बभूव गान्धारी शोकव्याकुललोचना ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tacchrutvā vāsudevasya punaruktaṁ vaco'priyam ,tūṣṇīṁ babhūva gāndhārī śokavyākulalocanā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that unpleasant, repeated speech of Vāsudeva (Krishna), Gāndhārī, whose eyes were agitated by sorrow, became silent.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 6. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that unpleasant, repeated speech of Vāsudeva (Krishna), Gāndhārī, whose eyes were agitated by sorrow, became silent.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रस्तु राजर्षिर्निगृह्याबुद्धिजं तमः ।पर्यपृच्छत धर्मात्मा धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥७॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrastu rājarṣirnigṛhyābuddhijaṁ tamaḥ ,paryapṛcchata dharmātmā dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: But Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the royal sage (rājarṣi), having suppressed the darkness (tamas) born of his lack of understanding, and being righteous-souled (dharmātmā), questioned Dharmarāja Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 7. But Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the royal sage (rājarṣi), having suppressed the darkness (tamas) born of his lack of understanding, and being righteous-souled (dharmātmā), questioned Dharmarāja Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: जीवतां परिमाणज्ञः सैन्यानामसि पाण्डव ।हतानां यदि जानीषे परिमाणं वदस्व मे ॥८॥
transliteration: jīvatāṁ parimāṇajñaḥ sainyānāmasi pāṇḍava ,hatānāṁ yadi jānīṣe parimāṇaṁ vadasva me.
english: O Pāṇḍava, you are the knower of the count of the living armies. If you know the number of those who were slain, tell it to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 8. O Pāṇḍava, you are the knower of the count of the living armies. If you know the number of those who were slain, tell it to me.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।दशायुतानामयुतं सहस्राणि च विंशतिः ।कोट्यः षष्टिश्च षट्चैव येऽस्मिन्राजमृधे हताः ॥९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,daśāyutānāmayutaṁ sahasrāṇi ca viṁśatiḥ ,koṭyaḥ ṣaṣṭiśca ṣaṭcaiva ye'sminrājamṛdhe hatāḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: In this royal battle (rājamṛdhe), sixty-six crores (koṭis) and one lakh thirty thousand soldiers were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 9. Yudhiṣṭhira said: In this royal battle (rājamṛdhe), sixty-six crores (koṭis) and one lakh thirty thousand soldiers were slain.

sanskrit: अलक्ष्याणां तु वीराणां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश ।दश चान्यानि राजेन्द्र शतं षष्टिश्च पञ्च च ॥१०॥
transliteration: alakṣyāṇāṁ tu vīrāṇāṁ sahasrāṇi caturdaśa ,daśa cānyāni rājendra śataṁ ṣaṣṭiśca pañca ca.
english: But, O King of kings (Rājendra), there were also fourteen thousand unidentifiable heroes, and ten thousand others, as well as one hundred sixty-five (more).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 10. But, O King of kings (Rājendra), there were also fourteen thousand unidentifiable heroes, and ten thousand others, as well as one hundred sixty-five (more).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।युधिष्ठिर गतिं कां ते गताः पुरुषसत्तमाः ।आचक्ष्व मे महाबाहो सर्वज्ञो ह्यसि मे मतः ॥११॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhira gatiṁ kāṁ te gatāḥ puruṣasattamāḥ ,ācakṣva me mahābāho sarvajño hyasi me mataḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: O Yudhiṣṭhira, what state or destination have those best of men (puruṣa) attained? Tell me, O mighty-armed one, for you are indeed regarded as all-knowing by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 11. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: O Yudhiṣṭhira, what state or destination have those best of men (puruṣa) attained? Tell me, O mighty-armed one, for you are indeed regarded as all-knowing by me.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।यैर्हुतानि शरीराणि हृष्टैः परमसंयुगे ।देवराजसमाँल्लोकान्गतास्ते सत्यविक्रमाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,yairhutāni śarīrāṇi hṛṣṭaiḥ paramasaṁyuge ,devarājasamāँllokāngatāste satyavikramāḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: Those truly valorous warriors who joyfully sacrificed their bodies in the supreme battle have gone to worlds equal to those of Indra, the king of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 12. Yudhishthira said: Those truly valorous warriors who joyfully sacrificed their bodies in the supreme battle have gone to worlds equal to those of Indra, the king of the gods.

sanskrit: ये त्वहृष्टेन मनसा मर्तव्यमिति भारत ।युध्यमाना हताः संख्ये ते गन्धर्वैः समागताः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ye tvahṛṣṭena manasā martavyamiti bhārata ,yudhyamānā hatāḥ saṁkhye te gandharvaiḥ samāgatāḥ.
english: But O Bharata, those who, fighting in battle, were killed with an unhappy mind, thinking "one must die", they have gone to dwell among the Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 13. But O Bharata, those who, fighting in battle, were killed with an unhappy mind, thinking "one must die", they have gone to dwell among the Gandharvas.

sanskrit: ये तु संग्रामभूमिष्ठा याचमानाः पराङ्मुखाः ।शस्त्रेण निधनं प्राप्ता गतास्ते गुह्यकान्प्रति ॥१४॥
transliteration: ye tu saṁgrāmabhūmiṣṭhā yācamānāḥ parāṅmukhāḥ ,śastreṇa nidhanaṁ prāptā gatāste guhyakānprati.
english: But those who, standing on the battlefield, were retreating and begging for quarter, and thus met their death by weapons, they have gone to the Guhyakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 14. But those who, standing on the battlefield, were retreating and begging for quarter, and thus met their death by weapons, they have gone to the Guhyakas.

sanskrit: पीड्यमानाः परैर्ये तु हीयमाना निरायुधाः ।ह्रीनिषेधा महात्मानः परानभिमुखा रणे ॥१५॥
transliteration: pīḍyamānāḥ parairye tu hīyamānā nirāyudhāḥ ,hrīniṣedhā mahātmānaḥ parānabhimukhā raṇe.
english: But those great-souled individuals who, being tormented by enemies, losing ground, and unarmed, were prevented by their sense of shame (hrī) from retreating, and thus remained facing their foes in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 15. But those great-souled individuals who, being tormented by enemies, losing ground, and unarmed, were prevented by their sense of shame (hrī) from retreating, and thus remained facing their foes in battle.

sanskrit: छिद्यमानाः शितैः शस्त्रैः क्षत्रधर्मपरायणाः ।गतास्ते ब्रह्मसदनं हता वीराः सुवर्चसः ॥१६॥
transliteration: chidyamānāḥ śitaiḥ śastraiḥ kṣatradharmaparāyaṇāḥ ,gatāste brahmasadanaṁ hatā vīrāḥ suvarcasaḥ.
english: The glorious heroes, dedicated to the intrinsic nature (dharma) of warriors, even though they were being cut down by sharp weapons, attained the abode of Brahman (brahman) when they were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 16. The glorious heroes, dedicated to the intrinsic nature (dharma) of warriors, even though they were being cut down by sharp weapons, attained the abode of Brahman (brahman) when they were slain.

sanskrit: ये तत्र निहता राजन्नन्तरायोधनं प्रति ।यथा कथंचित्ते राजन्संप्राप्ता उत्तरान्कुरून् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ye tatra nihatā rājannantarāyodhanaṁ prati ,yathā kathaṁcitte rājansaṁprāptā uttarānkurūn.
english: O King, those who were killed there in the battle, they, somehow or other, reached the Northern Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 17. O King, those who were killed there in the battle, they, somehow or other, reached the Northern Kurus.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।केन ज्ञानबलेनैवं पुत्र पश्यसि सिद्धवत् ।तन्मे वद महाबाहो श्रोतव्यं यदि वै मया ॥१८॥
