transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,kena jñānabalenaivaṁ putra paśyasi siddhavat ,tanme vada mahābāho śrotavyaṁ yadi vai mayā.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: O son, O mighty-armed one, by what power of knowledge do you perceive things in this manner, as if you were a perfected sage? Tell that to me, if indeed I am meant to hear it.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 18. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: O son, O mighty-armed one, by what power of knowledge do you perceive things in this manner, as if you were a perfected sage? Tell that to me, if indeed I am meant to hear it.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।निदेशाद्भवतः पूर्वं वने विचरता मया ।तीर्थयात्राप्रसङ्गेन संप्राप्तोऽयमनुग्रहः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,nideśādbhavataḥ pūrvaṁ vane vicaratā mayā ,tīrthayātrāprasaṅgena saṁprāpto'yamanugrahaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: This favor (anugraha) was previously obtained by me, while I was wandering in the forest by your command, on the occasion of a pilgrimage.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 19. Yudhiṣṭhira said: This favor (anugraha) was previously obtained by me, while I was wandering in the forest by your command, on the occasion of a pilgrimage.

sanskrit: देवर्षिर्लोमशो दृष्टस्ततः प्राप्तोऽस्म्यनुस्मृतिम् ।दिव्यं चक्षुरपि प्राप्तं ज्ञानयोगेन वै पुरा ॥२०॥
transliteration: devarṣirlomaśo dṛṣṭastataḥ prāpto'smyanusmṛtim ,divyaṁ cakṣurapi prāptaṁ jñānayogena vai purā.
english: I saw the divine sage Lomasha, and from that, I obtained recollection (anusmṛti). Indeed, in the past, I also gained a divine eye through the yoga of knowledge (jñānayoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 20. I saw the divine sage Lomasha, and from that, I obtained recollection (anusmṛti). Indeed, in the past, I also gained a divine eye through the yoga of knowledge (jñānayoga).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।येऽत्रानाथा जनस्यास्य सनाथा ये च भारत ।कच्चित्तेषां शरीराणि धक्ष्यन्ति विधिपूर्वकम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,ye'trānāthā janasyāsya sanāthā ye ca bhārata ,kaccitteṣāṁ śarīrāṇi dhakṣyanti vidhipūrvakam.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Bhārata, among these people, both those who are without protection and those who have protection - will their bodies indeed be cremated according to the prescribed rites?"
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 21. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Bhārata, among these people, both those who are without protection and those who have protection - will their bodies indeed be cremated according to the prescribed rites?"

sanskrit: न येषां सन्ति कर्तारो न च येऽत्राहिताग्नयः ।वयं च कस्य कुर्यामो बहुत्वात्तात कर्मणः ॥२२॥
transliteration: na yeṣāṁ santi kartāro na ca ye'trāhitāgnayaḥ ,vayaṁ ca kasya kuryāmo bahutvāttāta karmaṇaḥ.
english: For those who have no one to perform their rites, and for those who here have not maintained the sacred fires, for whom should we perform the funeral ceremonies, O dear one, given the multitude of such duties?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 22. For those who have no one to perform their rites, and for those who here have not maintained the sacred fires, for whom should we perform the funeral ceremonies, O dear one, given the multitude of such duties?

sanskrit: यान्सुपर्णाश्च गृध्राश्च विकर्षन्ति ततस्ततः ।तेषां तु कर्मणा लोका भविष्यन्ति युधिष्ठिर ॥२३॥
transliteration: yānsuparṇāśca gṛdhrāśca vikarṣanti tatastataḥ ,teṣāṁ tu karmaṇā lokā bhaviṣyanti yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhishthira, for those whose bodies eagles and vultures drag here and there, what realms will be theirs by virtue of their own actions (karma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 23. O Yudhishthira, for those whose bodies eagles and vultures drag here and there, what realms will be theirs by virtue of their own actions (karma)?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तो महाप्राज्ञः कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।आदिदेश सुधर्माणं धौम्यं सूतं च संजयम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamukto mahāprājñaḥ kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,ādideśa sudharmāṇaṁ dhaumyaṁ sūtaṁ ca saṁjayam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having been thus addressed, the very wise Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Kuntī, gave instructions to Sudharman, Dhaumya, and the charioteer Saṃjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 24. Vaiśampāyana said: Having been thus addressed, the very wise Yudhiṣṭhira, son of Kuntī, gave instructions to Sudharman, Dhaumya, and the charioteer Saṃjaya.

sanskrit: विदुरं च महाबुद्धिं युयुत्सुं चैव कौरवम् ।इन्द्रसेनमुखांश्चैव भृत्यान्सूतांश्च सर्वशः ॥२५॥
transliteration: viduraṁ ca mahābuddhiṁ yuyutsuṁ caiva kauravam ,indrasenamukhāṁścaiva bhṛtyānsūtāṁśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: (He ordered) also Vidura, the highly intelligent one, and Yuyutsu, the Kaurava; and furthermore, all the servants and charioteers headed by Indrasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 25. (He ordered) also Vidura, the highly intelligent one, and Yuyutsu, the Kaurava; and furthermore, all the servants and charioteers headed by Indrasena.

sanskrit: भवन्तः कारयन्त्वेषां प्रेतकार्याणि सर्वशः ।यथा चानाथवत्किंचिच्छरीरं न विनश्यति ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhavantaḥ kārayantveṣāṁ pretakāryāṇi sarvaśaḥ ,yathā cānāthavatkiṁciccharīraṁ na vinaśyati.
english: (Yudhiṣṭhira said to them:) "You all should thoroughly perform the funeral rites for these (deceased), and ensure that no body is neglected or perishes as if it were an orphan's."
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 26. (Yudhiṣṭhira said to them:) "You all should thoroughly perform the funeral rites for these (deceased), and ensure that no body is neglected or perishes as if it were an orphan's."

sanskrit: शासनाद्धर्मराजस्य क्षत्ता सूतश्च संजयः ।सुधर्मा धौम्यसहित इन्द्रसेनादयस्तथा ॥२७॥
transliteration: śāsanāddharmarājasya kṣattā sūtaśca saṁjayaḥ ,sudharmā dhaumyasahita indrasenādayastathā.
english: By the command of Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), Vidura, the charioteer Saṃjaya, Sudharman accompanied by Dhaumya, and similarly, those headed by Indrasena (undertook the tasks).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 27. By the command of Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), Vidura, the charioteer Saṃjaya, Sudharman accompanied by Dhaumya, and similarly, those headed by Indrasena (undertook the tasks).

sanskrit: चन्दनागुरुकाष्ठानि तथा कालीयकान्युत ।घृतं तैलं च गन्धांश्च क्षौमाणि वसनानि च ॥२८॥
transliteration: candanāgurukāṣṭhāni tathā kālīyakānyuta ,ghṛtaṁ tailaṁ ca gandhāṁśca kṣaumāṇi vasanāni ca.
english: Sandalwood and aloe wood, as well as Kāliyaka woods, ghee, oil, fragrances, and silk cloths and garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 28. Sandalwood and aloe wood, as well as Kāliyaka woods, ghee, oil, fragrances, and silk cloths and garments.

sanskrit: समाहृत्य महार्हाणि दारूणां चैव संचयान् ।रथांश्च मृदितांस्तत्र नानाप्रहरणानि च ॥२९॥
transliteration: samāhṛtya mahārhāṇi dārūṇāṁ caiva saṁcayān ,rathāṁśca mṛditāṁstatra nānāpraharaṇāni ca.
english: Having collected costly heaps of timbers, and also the crushed chariots and various weapons from there.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 29. Having collected costly heaps of timbers, and also the crushed chariots and various weapons from there.

sanskrit: चिताः कृत्वा प्रयत्नेन यथामुख्यान्नराधिपान् ।दाहयामासुरव्यग्रा विधिदृष्टेन कर्मणा ॥३०॥
transliteration: citāḥ kṛtvā prayatnena yathāmukhyānnarādhipān ,dāhayāmāsuravyagrā vidhidṛṣṭena karmaṇā.
english: Having carefully prepared the pyres, they, undisturbed, cremated the foremost kings with rituals prescribed by sacred texts.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 30. Having carefully prepared the pyres, they, undisturbed, cremated the foremost kings with rituals prescribed by sacred texts.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं च राजानं भ्रातॄंश्चास्य शताधिकान् ।शल्यं शलं च राजानं भूरिश्रवसमेव च ॥३१॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ ca rājānaṁ bhrātṝṁścāsya śatādhikān ,śalyaṁ śalaṁ ca rājānaṁ bhūriśravasameva ca.
english: King Duryodhana and his more than a hundred brothers; Śalya, Śala, and King Bhūriśravas as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 31. King Duryodhana and his more than a hundred brothers; Śalya, Śala, and King Bhūriśravas as well.

sanskrit: जयद्रथं च राजानमभिमन्युं च भारत ।दौःशासनिं लक्ष्मणं च धृष्टकेतुं च पार्थिवम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: jayadrathaṁ ca rājānamabhimanyuṁ ca bhārata ,dauḥśāsaniṁ lakṣmaṇaṁ ca dhṛṣṭaketuṁ ca pārthivam.
english: O descendant of Bharata, (among them are) King Jayadratha, Abhimanyu, Lakṣmaṇa (the son of Duḥśāsana), and King Dhṛṣṭaketu.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 32. O descendant of Bharata, (among them are) King Jayadratha, Abhimanyu, Lakṣmaṇa (the son of Duḥśāsana), and King Dhṛṣṭaketu.

sanskrit: बृहन्तं सोमदत्तं च सृञ्जयांश्च शताधिकान् ।राजानं क्षेमधन्वानं विराटद्रुपदौ तथा ॥३३॥
transliteration: bṛhantaṁ somadattaṁ ca sṛñjayāṁśca śatādhikān ,rājānaṁ kṣemadhanvānaṁ virāṭadrupadau tathā.
english: Also (there are) Bṛhanta, Somadatta, and the Sṛñjayas, who number more than a hundred. Similarly, King Kṣemadhanvan, and Virāṭa and Drupada.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 33. Also (there are) Bṛhanta, Somadatta, and the Sṛñjayas, who number more than a hundred. Similarly, King Kṣemadhanvan, and Virāṭa and Drupada.

sanskrit: शिखण्डिनं च पाञ्चाल्यं धृष्टद्युम्नं च पार्षतम् ।युधामन्युं च विक्रान्तमुत्तमौजसमेव च ॥३४॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍinaṁ ca pāñcālyaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pārṣatam ,yudhāmanyuṁ ca vikrāntamuttamaujasameva ca.
english: And Śikhaṇḍin, the Pāñcāla prince; and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, son of Pṛṣata; and the valorous Yudhāmanyu; and Uttamaujas himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 34. And Śikhaṇḍin, the Pāñcāla prince; and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, son of Pṛṣata; and the valorous Yudhāmanyu; and Uttamaujas himself.

sanskrit: कौसल्यं द्रौपदेयांश्च शकुनिं चापि सौबलम् ।अचलं वृषकं चैव भगदत्तं च पार्थिवम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: kausalyaṁ draupadeyāṁśca śakuniṁ cāpi saubalam ,acalaṁ vṛṣakaṁ caiva bhagadattaṁ ca pārthivam.
english: And Kausalya, and the sons of Draupadī, and also Śakuni, son of Subala. Furthermore, there are Acala, Vṛṣaka, and King Bhagadatta.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 35. And Kausalya, and the sons of Draupadī, and also Śakuni, son of Subala. Furthermore, there are Acala, Vṛṣaka, and King Bhagadatta.

sanskrit: कर्णं वैकर्तनं चैव सहपुत्रममर्षणम् ।केकयांश्च महेष्वासांस्त्रिगर्तांश्च महारथान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: karṇaṁ vaikartanaṁ caiva sahaputramamarṣaṇam ,kekayāṁśca maheṣvāsāṁstrigartāṁśca mahārathān.
english: And indeed, Karna, the son of Vikartana, along with his impatient son, and the Kekayas, who were great archers, as well as the Trigartas, who were mighty charioteers...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 36. And indeed, Karna, the son of Vikartana, along with his impatient son, and the Kekayas, who were great archers, as well as the Trigartas, who were mighty charioteers...

sanskrit: घटोत्कचं राक्षसेन्द्रं बकभ्रातरमेव च ।अलम्बुसं च राजानं जलसंधं च पार्थिवम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: ghaṭotkacaṁ rākṣasendraṁ bakabhrātarameva ca ,alambusaṁ ca rājānaṁ jalasaṁdhaṁ ca pārthivam.
english: Ghatotkaca, the lord of the Rākṣasas and indeed the brother of Baka, and King Alambusa, and King Jalasandha...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 37. Ghatotkaca, the lord of the Rākṣasas and indeed the brother of Baka, and King Alambusa, and King Jalasandha...

sanskrit: अन्यांश्च पार्थिवान्राजञ्शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।घृतधाराहुतैर्दीप्तैः पावकैः समदाहयन् ॥३८॥
transliteration: anyāṁśca pārthivānrājañśataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ ,ghṛtadhārāhutairdīptaiḥ pāvakaiḥ samadāhayan.
english: O King (rājan), they completely consumed in blazing fires - fed by streams of ghee oblations - hundreds, and then thousands, of other kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 38. O King (rājan), they completely consumed in blazing fires - fed by streams of ghee oblations - hundreds, and then thousands, of other kings.

sanskrit: पितृमेधाश्च केषांचिदवर्तन्त महात्मनाम् ।सामभिश्चाप्यगायन्त तेऽन्वशोच्यन्त चापरैः ॥३९॥
transliteration: pitṛmedhāśca keṣāṁcidavartanta mahātmanām ,sāmabhiścāpyagāyanta te'nvaśocyanta cāparaiḥ.
english: And for some of those great souls (mahātman), ancestral rites (pitṛmedhāḥ) were performed. They sang with Sāman chants, and others lamented them.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 39. And for some of those great souls (mahātman), ancestral rites (pitṛmedhāḥ) were performed. They sang with Sāman chants, and others lamented them.

sanskrit: साम्नामृचां च नादेन स्त्रीणां च रुदितस्वनैः ।कश्मलं सर्वभूतानां निशायां समपद्यत ॥४०॥
transliteration: sāmnāmṛcāṁ ca nādena strīṇāṁ ca ruditasvanaiḥ ,kaśmalaṁ sarvabhūtānāṁ niśāyāṁ samapadyata.
english: During the night, a profound bewilderment (kaśmala) arose among all creatures, caused by the chanting of Sāman and Ṛc verses, and by the sounds of women's lamentations.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 40. During the night, a profound bewilderment (kaśmala) arose among all creatures, caused by the chanting of Sāman and Ṛc verses, and by the sounds of women's lamentations.

sanskrit: ते विधूमाः प्रदीप्ताश्च दीप्यमानाश्च पावकाः ।नभसीवान्वदृश्यन्त ग्रहास्तन्वभ्रसंवृताः ॥४१॥
transliteration: te vidhūmāḥ pradīptāśca dīpyamānāśca pāvakāḥ ,nabhasīvānvadṛśyanta grahāstanvabhrasaṁvṛtāḥ.
english: Those smokeless, brilliantly blazing, and brightly shining fires appeared in the sky, resembling planets enveloped by thin clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 41. Those smokeless, brilliantly blazing, and brightly shining fires appeared in the sky, resembling planets enveloped by thin clouds.

sanskrit: ये चाप्यनाथास्तत्रासन्नानादेशसमागताः ।तांश्च सर्वान्समानाय्य राशीन्कृत्वा सहस्रशः ॥४२॥
transliteration: ye cāpyanāthāstatrāsannānādeśasamāgatāḥ ,tāṁśca sarvānsamānāyya rāśīnkṛtvā sahasraśaḥ.
english: And those helpless (anātha) individuals who were present there, having come from diverse regions, Vidura, having gathered all of them, made them into thousands of piles (for proper rites).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 42. And those helpless (anātha) individuals who were present there, having come from diverse regions, Vidura, having gathered all of them, made them into thousands of piles (for proper rites).

sanskrit: चित्वा दारुभिरव्यग्रः प्रभूतैः स्नेहतापितैः ।दाहयामास विदुरो धर्मराजस्य शासनात् ॥४३॥
transliteration: citvā dārubhiravyagraḥ prabhūtaiḥ snehatāpitaiḥ ,dāhayāmāsa viduro dharmarājasya śāsanāt.
english: Vidura, unperturbed, piled abundant wood, prepared (lit. "heated") with fats, and cremated them in accordance with the command of Dharmarāja (Yama, the King of natural law).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 43. Vidura, unperturbed, piled abundant wood, prepared (lit. "heated") with fats, and cremated them in accordance with the command of Dharmarāja (Yama, the King of natural law).

sanskrit: कारयित्वा क्रियास्तेषां कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।धृतराष्ट्रं पुरस्कृत्य गङ्गामभिमुखोऽगमत् ॥४४॥
transliteration: kārayitvā kriyāsteṣāṁ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ puraskṛtya gaṅgāmabhimukho'gamat.
english: After causing their rites to be performed, Yudhishthira, the king of the Kurus, went towards the Ganga, with Dhritarashtra leading the way.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 26 Verse 44. After causing their rites to be performed, Yudhishthira, the king of the Kurus, went towards the Ganga, with Dhritarashtra leading the way.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ते समासाद्य गङ्गां तु शिवां पुण्यजनोचिताम् ।ह्रदिनीं वप्रसंपन्नां महानूपां महावनाम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,te samāsādya gaṅgāṁ tu śivāṁ puṇyajanocitām ,hradinīṁ vaprasaṁpannāṁ mahānūpāṁ mahāvanām.
english: Vaishampayana said: They, having reached the Ganga, which was auspicious, fitting for the virtuous (puṇyajana), a river with deep pools, abundant with high banks, possessing vast wetlands, and great forests.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: They, having reached the Ganga, which was auspicious, fitting for the virtuous (puṇyajana), a river with deep pools, abundant with high banks, possessing vast wetlands, and great forests.

sanskrit: भूषणान्युत्तरीयाणि वेष्टनान्यवमुच्य च ।ततः पितॄणां पौत्राणां भ्रातॄणां स्वजनस्य च ॥२॥
transliteration: bhūṣaṇānyuttarīyāṇi veṣṭanānyavamucya ca ,tataḥ pitṝṇāṁ pautrāṇāṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ svajanasya ca.
english: After taking off their ornaments, upper garments, and wraps, then, for the fathers, grandsons, brothers, and their other relatives...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 2. After taking off their ornaments, upper garments, and wraps, then, for the fathers, grandsons, brothers, and their other relatives...

sanskrit: पुत्राणामार्यकाणां च पतीनां च कुरुस्त्रियः ।उदकं चक्रिरे सर्वा रुदन्त्यो भृशदुःखिताः ।सुहृदां चापि धर्मज्ञाः प्रचक्रुः सलिलक्रियाः ॥३॥
transliteration: putrāṇāmāryakāṇāṁ ca patīnāṁ ca kurustriyaḥ ,udakaṁ cakrire sarvā rudantyo bhṛśaduḥkhitāḥ ,suhṛdāṁ cāpi dharmajñāḥ pracakruḥ salilakriyāḥ.
english: And for the sons, and the revered elders, and the husbands, all the Kuru women, weeping and greatly distressed, performed the water rites. Also, those who understood natural law (dharma) performed water rites for their friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 3. And for the sons, and the revered elders, and the husbands, all the Kuru women, weeping and greatly distressed, performed the water rites. Also, those who understood natural law (dharma) performed water rites for their friends.

sanskrit: उदके क्रियमाणे तु वीराणां वीरपत्निभिः ।सूपतीर्थाभवद्गङ्गा भूयो विप्रससार च ॥४॥
transliteration: udake kriyamāṇe tu vīrāṇāṁ vīrapatnibhiḥ ,sūpatīrthābhavadgaṅgā bhūyo viprasasāra ca.
english: As water offerings were being made by the wives of the heroes, the Gaṅgā became a sacred bathing place and flowed widely again.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 4. As water offerings were being made by the wives of the heroes, the Gaṅgā became a sacred bathing place and flowed widely again.

sanskrit: तन्महोदधिसंकाशं निरानन्दमनुत्सवम् ।वीरपत्नीभिराकीर्णं गङ्गातीरमशोभत ॥५॥
transliteration: tanmahodadhisaṁkāśaṁ nirānandamanutsavam ,vīrapatnībhirākīrṇaṁ gaṅgātīramaśobhata.
english: That bank of the Gaṅgā, crowded with the wives of heroes, appeared like the great ocean - devoid of joy and festivity.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 5. That bank of the Gaṅgā, crowded with the wives of heroes, appeared like the great ocean - devoid of joy and festivity.

sanskrit: ततः कुन्ती महाराज सहसा शोककर्शिता ।रुदती मन्दया वाचा पुत्रान्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuntī mahārāja sahasā śokakarśitā ,rudatī mandayā vācā putrānvacanamabravīt.
english: Then, O great king, Kuntī, suddenly emaciated by grief, weeping with a subdued voice, spoke these words to her sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 6. Then, O great king, Kuntī, suddenly emaciated by grief, weeping with a subdued voice, spoke these words to her sons.

sanskrit: यः स शूरो महेष्वासो रथयूथपयूथपः ।अर्जुनेन हतः संख्ये वीरलक्षणलक्षितः ॥७॥
transliteration: yaḥ sa śūro maheṣvāso rathayūthapayūthapaḥ ,arjunena hataḥ saṁkhye vīralakṣaṇalakṣitaḥ.
english: He who was that hero, the great archer, the leader among the chiefs of charioteers, and adorned with the marks of a warrior (vīralakṣaṇa), was slain by Arjuna in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 7. He who was that hero, the great archer, the leader among the chiefs of charioteers, and adorned with the marks of a warrior (vīralakṣaṇa), was slain by Arjuna in battle.

sanskrit: यं सूतपुत्रं मन्यध्वं राधेयमिति पाण्डवाः ।यो व्यराजच्चमूमध्ये दिवाकर इव प्रभुः ॥८॥
transliteration: yaṁ sūtaputraṁ manyadhvaṁ rādheyamiti pāṇḍavāḥ ,yo vyarājaccamūmadhye divākara iva prabhuḥ.
english: O Pāṇḍavas, he whom you consider to be the son of a charioteer, the son of Rādhā, that mighty one shone in the midst of the army like the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 8. O Pāṇḍavas, he whom you consider to be the son of a charioteer, the son of Rādhā, that mighty one shone in the midst of the army like the sun.

sanskrit: प्रत्ययुध्यत यः सर्वान्पुरा वः सपदानुगान् ।दुर्योधनबलं सर्वं यः प्रकर्षन्व्यरोचत ॥९॥
transliteration: pratyayudhyata yaḥ sarvānpurā vaḥ sapadānugān ,duryodhanabalaṁ sarvaṁ yaḥ prakarṣanvyarocata.
english: He who formerly fought against all of you and your followers, and who, leading the entire army of Duryodhana forward, shone resplendently.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 9. He who formerly fought against all of you and your followers, and who, leading the entire army of Duryodhana forward, shone resplendently.

sanskrit: यस्य नास्ति समो वीर्ये पृथिव्यामपि कश्चन ।सत्यसंधस्य शूरस्य संग्रामेष्वपलायिनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yasya nāsti samo vīrye pṛthivyāmapi kaścana ,satyasaṁdhasya śūrasya saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinaḥ.
english: Whose equal in valor is not to be found anywhere, even on earth; of the one whose vows are true, the hero who never flees in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 10. Whose equal in valor is not to be found anywhere, even on earth; of the one whose vows are true, the hero who never flees in battles.

sanskrit: कुरुध्वमुदकं तस्य भ्रातुरक्लिष्टकर्मणः ।स हि वः पूर्वजो भ्राता भास्करान्मय्यजायत ।कुण्डली कवची शूरो दिवाकरसमप्रभः ॥११॥
transliteration: kurudhvamudakaṁ tasya bhrāturakliṣṭakarmaṇaḥ ,sa hi vaḥ pūrvajo bhrātā bhāskarānmayyajāyata ,kuṇḍalī kavacī śūro divākarasamaprabhaḥ.
english: Perform the water offering for him, your brother whose deeds are unfailing. For he is indeed your elder brother, born of the sun-god (bhāskara) in me. He possessed earrings and armor, was a hero, and shone with splendor equal to the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 11. Perform the water offering for him, your brother whose deeds are unfailing. For he is indeed your elder brother, born of the sun-god (bhāskara) in me. He possessed earrings and armor, was a hero, and shone with splendor equal to the sun.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु पाण्डवाः सर्वे मातुर्वचनमप्रियम् ।कर्णमेवानुशोचन्त भूयश्चार्ततराभवन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu pāṇḍavāḥ sarve māturvacanamapriyam ,karṇamevānuśocanta bhūyaścārtatarābhavan.
english: Upon hearing their mother's distressing words, all the Pāṇḍavas, lamenting solely for Karṇa, became even more miserable.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 12. Upon hearing their mother's distressing words, all the Pāṇḍavas, lamenting solely for Karṇa, became even more miserable.

sanskrit: ततः स पुरुषव्याघ्रः कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।उवाच मातरं वीरो निःश्वसन्निव पन्नगः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa puruṣavyāghraḥ kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,uvāca mātaraṁ vīro niḥśvasanniva pannagaḥ.
english: Then, that hero, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī and a tiger among men, spoke to his mother, sighing like a serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 13. Then, that hero, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī and a tiger among men, spoke to his mother, sighing like a serpent.

sanskrit: यस्येषुपातमासाद्य नान्यस्तिष्ठेद्धनंजयात् ।कथं पुत्रो भवत्यां स देवगर्भः पुराभवत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yasyeṣupātamāsādya nānyastiṣṭheddhanaṁjayāt ,kathaṁ putro bhavatyāṁ sa devagarbhaḥ purābhavat.
english: How could that divine child (devagarbha), whose range of arrows no one could withstand but Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), have been born from you?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 14. How could that divine child (devagarbha), whose range of arrows no one could withstand but Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), have been born from you?

sanskrit: यस्य बाहुप्रतापेन तापिताः सर्वतो वयम् ।तमग्निमिव वस्त्रेण कथं छादितवत्यसि ।यस्य बाहुबलं घोरं धार्तराष्ट्रैरुपासितम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yasya bāhupratāpena tāpitāḥ sarvato vayam ,tamagnimiva vastreṇa kathaṁ chāditavatyasi ,yasya bāhubalaṁ ghoraṁ dhārtarāṣṭrairupāsitam.
english: How could you have concealed, like fire covered by a cloth, the one by whose mighty arms we were completely tormented, and whose formidable arm-strength was relied upon by the Dhārtarāṣṭras?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 15. How could you have concealed, like fire covered by a cloth, the one by whose mighty arms we were completely tormented, and whose formidable arm-strength was relied upon by the Dhārtarāṣṭras?

sanskrit: नान्यः कुन्तीसुतात्कर्णादगृह्णाद्रथिनां रथी ।स नः प्रथमजो भ्राता सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।असूत तं भवत्यग्रे कथमद्भुतविक्रमम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: nānyaḥ kuntīsutātkarṇādagṛhṇādrathināṁ rathī ,sa naḥ prathamajo bhrātā sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,asūta taṁ bhavatyagre kathamadbhutavikramam.
english: No other charioteer among warriors could prevail against Karna, the son of Kunti. He was our elder brother, the foremost among all weapon-bearers. How did you, revered mother, first give birth to such a one of astonishing prowess (vikrama)?
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 16. No other charioteer among warriors could prevail against Karna, the son of Kunti. He was our elder brother, the foremost among all weapon-bearers. How did you, revered mother, first give birth to such a one of astonishing prowess (vikrama)?

sanskrit: अहो भवत्या मन्त्रस्य पिधानेन वयं हताः ।निधनेन हि कर्णस्य पीडिताः स्म सबान्धवाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: aho bhavatyā mantrasya pidhānena vayaṁ hatāḥ ,nidhanena hi karṇasya pīḍitāḥ sma sabāndhavāḥ.
english: Oh, by the concealment of your secret counsel (mantra), we have been ruined! Indeed, by the death of Karna, we, along with all our kinsmen, are greatly afflicted.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 17. Oh, by the concealment of your secret counsel (mantra), we have been ruined! Indeed, by the death of Karna, we, along with all our kinsmen, are greatly afflicted.

sanskrit: अभिमन्योर्विनाशेन द्रौपदेयवधेन च ।पाञ्चालानां च नाशेन कुरूणां पतनेन च ॥१८॥
transliteration: abhimanyorvināśena draupadeyavadhena ca ,pāñcālānāṁ ca nāśena kurūṇāṁ patanena ca.
english: By the destruction of Abhimanyu, and by the slaughter of Draupadi's sons, and by the annihilation of the Pañcālas, and by the downfall of the Kurus...
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 18. By the destruction of Abhimanyu, and by the slaughter of Draupadi's sons, and by the annihilation of the Pañcālas, and by the downfall of the Kurus...

sanskrit: ततः शतगुणं दुःखमिदं मामस्पृशद्भृशम् ।कर्णमेवानुशोचन्हि दह्याम्यग्नाविवाहितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śataguṇaṁ duḥkhamidaṁ māmaspṛśadbhṛśam ,karṇamevānuśocanhi dahyāmyagnāvivāhitaḥ.
english: Compared to all that, this sorrow, a hundred times greater, has intensely afflicted me. Indeed, lamenting for Karna alone, I am burning as if in fire, yet not placed (ahita) within it.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 19. Compared to all that, this sorrow, a hundred times greater, has intensely afflicted me. Indeed, lamenting for Karna alone, I am burning as if in fire, yet not placed (ahita) within it.

sanskrit: न हि स्म किंचिदप्राप्यं भवेदपि दिवि स्थितम् ।न च स्म वैशसं घोरं कौरवान्तकरं भवेत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: na hi sma kiṁcidaprāpyaṁ bhavedapi divi sthitam ,na ca sma vaiśasaṁ ghoraṁ kauravāntakaraṁ bhavet.
english: Indeed, nothing would have been unattainable, even if it were situated in heaven. Nor would there have been such a terrible and destructive calamity that brought about the end of the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 20. Indeed, nothing would have been unattainable, even if it were situated in heaven. Nor would there have been such a terrible and destructive calamity that brought about the end of the Kauravas.

sanskrit: एवं विलप्य बहुलं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।विनदञ्शनकै राजंश्चकारास्योदकं प्रभुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: evaṁ vilapya bahulaṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,vinadañśanakai rājaṁścakārāsyodakaṁ prabhuḥ.
english: Having thus lamented extensively, King Yudhishthira (the king of natural law (dharma)), O King, slowly crying out, performed the water oblations for him (Karna), being the lord (prabhu).
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 21. Having thus lamented extensively, King Yudhishthira (the king of natural law (dharma)), O King, slowly crying out, performed the water oblations for him (Karna), being the lord (prabhu).

sanskrit: ततो विनेदुः सहसा स्त्रीपुंसास्तत्र सर्वशः ।अभितो ये स्थितास्तत्र तस्मिन्नुदककर्मणि ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato vineduḥ sahasā strīpuṁsāstatra sarvaśaḥ ,abhito ye sthitāstatra tasminnudakakarmaṇi.
english: Then, suddenly, men and women alike, all those who were present there on all sides during that water ritual (karma), cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 22. Then, suddenly, men and women alike, all those who were present there on all sides during that water ritual (karma), cried out.

sanskrit: तत आनाययामास कर्णस्य सपरिच्छदम् ।स्त्रियः कुरुपतिर्धीमान्भ्रातुः प्रेम्णा युधिष्ठिरः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tata ānāyayāmāsa karṇasya saparicchadam ,striyaḥ kurupatirdhīmānbhrātuḥ premṇā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Then, the intelligent lord of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, out of love for his brother, caused Karna's women to be brought, along with their retinue.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 23. Then, the intelligent lord of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, out of love for his brother, caused Karna's women to be brought, along with their retinue.

sanskrit: स ताभिः सह धर्मात्मा प्रेतकृत्यमनन्तरम् ।कृत्वोत्ततार गङ्गायाः सलिलादाकुलेन्द्रियः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa tābhiḥ saha dharmātmā pretakṛtyamanantaram ,kṛtvottatāra gaṅgāyāḥ salilādākulendriyaḥ.
english: With those women, that righteous-souled (dharmātmā) man, after immediately performing the funeral rites, emerged from the waters of the Gaṅgā, his senses agitated.
search_text: Mahabharata Strī Parva Chapter 27 Verse 24. With those women, that righteous-souled (dharmātmā) man, after immediately performing the funeral rites, emerged from the waters of the Gaṅgā, his senses agitated.
